# CDiscourse — Current Status

**2026-07-11 - A11Y-693 (#693): a11y + reduce-motion floor across the ASP surfaces + the folded board-axis color-independence guard (Infra/a11y lane; UI-conformance + regression guards ONLY; NO migration, NO Edge, NO deps, NO new flag, NO engine edit):** Brings the shipped Argument Surface Pivot surface set + the mediator board up to the `accessibility-targets` contract and pins that conformance against regression. An audit + regression-guard card: of the 16 audited cells, 12 were verified-green (no code change - do not gold-plate), and **4 real gaps** were closed. **The four fixes:** (1) `RingsideCard.tsx` root Pressable gets `accessible={false}` so its nested controls (quote chip, action chips, ghost feedback bar) stay individually focusable under iOS VoiceOver - the root still activates on tap; (2) `RingsideCard.tsx` quote chip gets `accessibilityState={{ disabled: !parentMessageId }}`; (3) `ArgumentStateRail.tsx` the non-pressable info-chip View + the overflow badge View get `accessibilityRole="text"` so they announce as static text; (4) `ProofDrawer.tsx` the selected prior-move gets a check affix + `borderWidth:2` so selection is not color-alone (grayscale-legible, mirrors the BooleanFeedbackBar marked pattern; the affix stays visual-only, never in the a11y label). **Shared reduce-motion primitive:** new `src/features/preferences/useReduceMotion.ts` extracts the inline `AccessibilityInfo` read (verbatim from `useUserPreferences`) into one hook with `reduceMotionOverride` precedence + default-safe false; ADOPTED in `DisagreementPointsRail.tsx` (behavior-preserving, removes one copy of the duplicated effect). **Folded amendment (UX-BOARD-A11Y-AXIS-001):** the board-axis color-independence guard locks the already-green mediator reality - every live V4 display state (11) renders a paired text label + non-empty `accessibilityLabel` on both `MediatorNodeMarker` + `DisagreementPointsRail`; the 9 `DisagreementPointKind` axes are never a colored dot (the #679 oklch axis palette never shipped here; `point.kind` adds no element/color); no red/green verdict pair in the rendered palette (source + render defense-in-depth); a firing negative control proves the guard bites; ban-list clean over the real vocabularies. **Reduce-motion guard:** the audit source-scan fails any ASP surface that imports an animator (`Animated`/`LayoutAnimation`/`reanimated`/Modal `animationType`) without referencing a reduce-motion gate (today: DisagreementPointsRail + CallbackCaptureSheet + SourceChainPopover, all satisfy it). **New doc** `docs/a11y-asp-surfaces.md` (audit matrix, board color-independence rule, reduce-motion floor, focus-visibility posture, the `BLOCKED-ON-#863` deferred rows for the playback gate + recorder voice surfaces + the deferred-row maintenance rule). **Flag posture:** LIVE surfaces (RingsideCard, ArgumentStateRail under `room_exchange_v2`) ship the fixes UNFLAGGED as conformance corrections; the ProofDrawer color fix rides the OFF `proof_drawer` flag; NO new flag. **Reconciliations:** R1 (RingsideCard `accessible={false}`) - NO existing test encoded the single-node bug (`ringsideFeed.test.tsx` reads the root props directly, unaffected). One ArgumentStateRail reconciliation: `ArgumentStateRail.test.tsx:127` `toBeUndefined()` -> `toBe('text')` (strengthened with a rationale comment; the informational chip now announces as static text, still non-interactive). R2 (adopt the hook) - the mediator suites stay green UNMODIFIED. **1025 suites / 34890 tests passing (34889 passed + 1 pre-existing skip; exit 0) - +4 suites / +356 tests over the 1021 / 34534 baseline** (a11y693ReduceMotionPrimitive 16, a11y693GapFixes 12, a11y693AspSurfaceAudit 312, a11y693MediatorBoardAxisGuard 16). Typecheck + lint + `web:build` clean; pinned `uxOneOneSixReadOnlyBoundary` / `uxOneOneFiveReadOnlyBoundary` / `uxOneOneTwoDoctrine` / `uxOneOneSix*` / mediator suites all green; `designTokens`/`TYPOGRAPHY`/`engine`/`antiAmplification` untouched. **No Anthropic / xAI / X API call, no Supabase write / read / query against any live project, no service-role usage, no migration / no Edge / no `.env` value edit by Claude in this card.** **Operator: none** - pure code change; nothing auto-applies or auto-deploys (no migration, no config.toml-registered function; the LIVE fixes are unflagged conformance, the OFF-flag fixes stay dark until their existing flags flip). Design: `docs/designs/A11Y-693-ASP.md`. Notes: `docs/a11y-asp-surfaces.md`.

**2026-07-11 - UX-FLAGS-004 (#836): feedback-flag composer intents - one-tap actionable friendly-flag pills (M-ASP-7; UI + pure-TS bridge only; NO migration, NO Edge change, NO deps, NO engine edit; new 10th flag `feedback_flag_intents` / `EXPO_PUBLIC_FEEDBACK_FLAG_INTENTS` default OFF, flag-off byte-identical):** Makes the already-live (UX-FLAGS-002) read-only point feedback-flag pills one-tap actionable. Tapping an actionable pill (the 7 actionable flags across Families C/D/F/G/H) opens the SHIPPED reply-with-preset dock lane pre-typed to the matching intent - the exact `handleAction('reply', activeMessageId, preset)` dispatch the rail Ask-source tap uses (Path A; zero composer edits, zero pinned-file edits). New pure-TS bridge `src/features/feedbackFlags/flagComposerIntentMap.ts` is Layer 2 over #833's `actionable` / `composerIntent` declarations (7 actionable flags -> 5 intents: `ask_for_source` / `ask_clarify` / `answer_question` / `propose_synthesis` / `sharpen_claim`), DERIVING actionability from the descriptor so the two layers cannot drift (cross-coverage manifest test asserts both directions + a firing negative control). 3 new preset bodies in `quickActionPresets.ts` (`ask_clarify` / `answer_question` / `sharpen_claim`; `ask_for_source` / `propose_synthesis` reuse the shipped `ASK_SOURCE_PRESET_BODY` / `SYNTHESIZE_PRESET_BODY`) - all ban-list clean (no verdict / truth / "proof" token; advisory framing - the prefill is the user draft, never a claim the machine flag is authoritative). Calm pill treatment (R3): the actionable pill keeps the SAME muted visual as the inert pill and adds ONLY `accessibilityRole="button"` + `accessibilityHint` + 44x44 `hitSlop` + a web focus ring - no color-only CTA, no chevron / affordance glyph; non-actionable pills keep today's `role="text"` semantics exactly. Strict actor gating (R4): a flag intent opens a reply, so observers get NO actionable pills (inert), seated participants / host fire intents on OTHERS' active moves only, own move + flag-off stay inert everywhere. Wired at both ROOM-003 lenses (the Timeline flag row + the Ringside card via ExchangeView -> RingsideFeed -> RingsideCard, per-card gate parity with the FEEDBACK-001 ghost bar); CardDetailPanel + the legacy ArgumentBubbleStack stay read-only (R2). Decision 8 seed-if-empty guard at App's shared `onComposerPreset` seam: a seeded preset never overwrites a mid-typed draft (composer-closed fallback since App holds no draft registry; every seeded source - rail / Act / SC-004 / validation-chip / flag - is now non-clobbering). App.tsx is the sole flag importer; prop-threaded App -> ArgumentTreeScreen -> ArgumentRoom (mirrors `moveMarksEnabled`). QuickActionLabel pre-flight PASS (no pinned consumer switches exhaustively / uses `Record<QuickActionLabel>`; the producer `default: return null` tolerates the wider union). **No Anthropic / xAI / X / Supabase write by Claude in this card. No service-role. No migration, no Edge deploy.** 1 new source file + 11 modified (incl. 3 pass-through hops: ExchangeView + RingsideFeed for the Ringside branch, ArgumentTreeScreen threading). +58 tests across 4 new suites + 5 extended (incl. the COMPOSER-002 seam pin updated for the Decision-8 guard). **1021 test suites / 34534 tests passing** (34533 passed, 1 pre-existing skip), typecheck + lint clean, `web:build` clean. **Operator step**: to roll out, set `EXPO_PUBLIC_FEEDBACK_FLAG_INTENTS=true` in the Netlify env (mirror the move_marks 10%-cohort posture); flag OFF = pills byte-identical. See `docs/designs/UX-FLAGS-004.md`.

**2026-07-11 - QUOTE / CALLBACK PAIR: UX-COMPOSER-005 (#831) + QUOTE-FORGE-002 (#842) - cross-room "woven callback" weave + rendered-node echo (M-ASP-2 Quote Forge; UI + client-model only; NO migration, NO Edge change, NO deps, NO engine edit; new 9th flag `quote_forge`/`EXPO_PUBLIC_QUOTE_FORGE` default OFF, flag-off byte-identical + no `crossRoomCallback` key in the submit payload):** Lights the dark half of the QOL-042 cross-room-link substrate at the node grain. A seated participant "weaves a callback" from the entry composer: a room picker (reused `LinkTargetPickerSheet`, repurposed to SELECT only - no link at pick time) then a verbatim line-capture sheet (`CallbackCaptureSheet`) pulls an EXACT prior line the viewer legitimately reads; it shows inline as a distinct `<CallbackDraftEcho>` (glyph ⤴ + quote strip + origin) before send; on send the excerpt is threaded into the move via the existing permissive `client_validation.crossRoomCallback` passthrough (namespaced, `v:1`, NO new top-level payload key, submit-argument byte-preserved); on success the QOL-042 room link is created post-hoc via the shipped idempotent `createArgumentRoomLink`. The posted move then renders as a woven echo on all three surfaces - Timeline NodeDot corner badge, active Stack card banner, Ringside card strip - resolved ONCE per room by `buildCallbackEchoesByMessageId` in the shell (`FullRoomGameSurfaceMount`, the single owner of both the move refs and the resolved links) and threaded down. **Orchestrator rulings R1-R7 applied** (recorded in each design doc's addendum): R1 ref home = `client_validation.crossRoomCallback` (server_validation OVERRULED, excerpt a SEPARATE field never woven into body); R2 join key = `targetDebateId` (linkId unknown at write time); R3 render-time excerpt suppression for title_only/unavailable ships as a UX-consistency treatment documented in code + docs as NOT an RLS/privacy boundary (no copy promises secrecy; a ref with no matching link row renders the locked/unavailable arm, never an excerpt without an authorized access state); R4 the 9th default-OFF flag registered ONCE in `featureFlags.ts` + consumed only via App.tsx prop-threading (zero `featureFlags` imports under `src/features`); R5 minimal verbatim line pick (no span-select, no cross-room search, legacy pinned dock unwired); R6 link created post-submit-success (degrade-on-failure, non-blocking retry notice); R7 excerpt snapshot persists, own callbacks render identically. **Topology adaptation (recorded):** the entry composer is mounted in App.tsx (not ArgumentRoom) and `useLinkedPriorRooms` lives in the tree shell, so the composer-side capture flow mounts in App.tsx (writing `pendingCallback` onto the shared session draft via a DRAFT_UPDATED dispatch, no second useArgumentComposer mount) and the echo map is derived in the tree shell - still single-derivation, threaded to all three surfaces. **10 new source modules** under `crossRoom/` (crossRoomCallbackRef shared write/read contract, callbackCaptureModel, callbackComposerCopy, CallbackDraftEcho, CallbackCaptureSheet, useCallbackInsertion, callbackEchoModel, callbackEchoCopy, CallbackEchoStrip) + threading through ArgumentEntryComposer / useEntryComposerSubmit / composerState / composerSubmit / composerHelpers / session-types / ArgumentTreeScreen / ArgumentRoom / MapView / ExchangeView / RingsideFeed / RingsideCard / ArgumentBubbleStack / ArgumentTimelineMap / App.tsx. **1017 suites / 34476 total passing (34474 passed + 1 pre-existing skip; the LIFE-001 30ms wall-clock perf test flakes under full-suite parallel load and PASSES isolated - not in this diff) - +10 suites / +103 tests over the 1007 / 34373 baseline.** Typecheck + lint + `web:build` clean; pinned `uxOneOneFiveReadOnlyBoundary` (composer / oneBox / submit-path 0-diff) + ROOM-003 byte-shape census + `argumentTimelineMap` contract + doctrine scanner all green; `featureFlagsStaticEnv` extended for the 9th static literal. Ban-list-scanned copy (no verdict / amplification / proof tokens, no internal-code leak); title_only privacy invariant pinned (excerpt absent from the VM + a11y label + rendered tree) with firing negative controls throughout. **No Anthropic / xAI / X API call, no Supabase write / read / query against any live project, no service-role, no migration / no Edge / no `.env` value edit by Claude in this pair.** **Operator (after merge):** pure code - nothing auto-applies / auto-deploys (no migration, no config.toml function). The pair ships DARK; to roll out, set `EXPO_PUBLIC_QUOTE_FORGE=true` (Netlify runtime env, strict-FF publish) - and flip off as the kill switch. Ringside echo additionally requires `room_exchange_v2` (already live). Designs: `docs/designs/UX-COMPOSER-005.md`, `docs/designs/QUOTE-FORGE-002.md`.

**2026-07-11 - QA-001 (#692) - J1-J10 ASP journey release gate (M-ASP / QA lane; TESTS + DOCS ONLY; NO migration, NO Edge, NO deps, NO engine edit, every pinned production file 0-diff):** Turns the Design Pass's ten "Core user journeys" (§4, J1-J10) into the ASP program's release gate - one operator-armed manual checklist + the automated jest coverage for every journey seam testable today without live auth. **Four new suites** (all pure model / manifest, flag-agnostic, zero `featureFlags` imports): `__tests__/journeyGateCoverageMap.test.ts` (the executable release-gate manifest - a frozen ten-row map pinning each journey's `flags` / `blockedOn` / `spineOfRecord`; audio half carries an explicit `BLOCKED ON #863` comment); `__tests__/journeyJ9ReviewMap.test.tsx` ⋆ (NEW J9 spine - the strip `open_points` chip carries `deepLink:'map'` counted from the SAME frozen board that drives `getNodeMediatorMarker`, the single `deriveRoomMediatorBoardState` derivation reused by rail + node markup, a plain open node yields NO marker (one-state-per-node), the tapped node id is the `buildMapNodeActionSurface` answer-this scope target). J1/J2/J3/J4/J7/J10 are NOT re-spined (RP2 gap-fill) - the manifest CITES their existing end-to-end walks (`argumentHome` / `startArgumentSheet` / `PublicArgumentToggle` / `proofJ7Flow` / `moveMarksRoomAggregateWiring`) as spine-of-record; a duplicate would be tautological. **Conditional-green gate (RP3):** J5 (record-voice), J8 (save-recording), and the J6 AUDIO half have NO shipped surface - they are doc-only BLOCKED ON VOICE-ADR-002 (#863); the manifest asserts the three blocked halves claim no spine and carry the #863 marker (no stubbed fake voice surface). **New docs:** `docs/qa/journey-gate-j1-j10.md` (the operator-armed L1 checklist - ten sections J1-J10 with #jN anchors, flag + deployed-state + blocked-on, numbered operator steps against SHIPPED testIDs, per-step expected state, a no-`.env` fallback, the journey coverage-map table, a maintenance rule, and the shipped-vs-Design-Pass-prose copy ledger) + `docs/qa/README.md` (L0 mvp-smoke -> L1 journey-gate index). **Modified:** `docs/mvp-smoke-test.md` gains a short "Successor gate" pointer section (no existing content edited). Scripts + tests assert REAL shipped strings (`deriveGalleryEntryHint` not `deriveConversationEntryHint`; `Source owed`; `Respond to this`; `Answer this ↗`), never Design Pass prose. **1007 suites / 34373 total passing (34372 passed + 1 pre-existing skip; exit 0) - +4 suites / +58 tests over the 1003 / 34315 baseline; ZERO existing-test edits.** Typecheck + lint clean. Zero `supabase/` changes, no new deps, no engine / `antiAmplification.ts` / composer / dock / `ArgumentTimelineMap` / uxOneOneFive-list edit (all pinned files stay byte-identical, so the boundary suites stay green automatically). **No Anthropic / xAI / X API call, no Supabase write / read / query against any live project, no service-role usage, no migration / no Edge / no `.env` value edit, no live-auth E2E run by Claude in this card.** **Operator (after merge):** pure tests + docs - nothing auto-applies or auto-deploys; the card is automerge-eligible once green (no auth slice runs in CI). Post-merge the gate is operator-armed: to walk a journey against the deployed build, flip its `EXPO_PUBLIC_*` flag in the Netlify runtime env (J7 `EXPO_PUBLIC_PROOF_DRAWER` after the PROOF-001 back-fill; J6-text `EXPO_PUBLIC_TIMESTAMP_REBUTTALS`; J10 `EXPO_PUBLIC_MOVE_MARKS` 10%-cohort) and walk that journey's section; J1/J2/J3/J4/J9 are already LIVE (`home_v2` + `room_exchange_v2`); J5/J8/J6-audio are not runnable until VOICE-ADR-002 (#863) ships the voice surface. Design: `docs/designs/QA-001.md`.

**2026-07-11 - P7 INTEL BUNDLE: FEEDBACK-002 (#899) + INTEL-001 (#900) + INTEL-002 (#901) - derivedObservationSignals + dodge-chain/debt-answer/specificity intelligence (M-ASP-7, Phase P7; pure TS + read-only surfaces; NO migration, NO Edge, NO provider spend; flag `derived_signals` default OFF + INTEL reuses `move_marks` default OFF, all flag-off byte-identical):** Turns signals the pipeline already stores into higher-order ADVISORY intelligence without a single new classifier call. **FEEDBACK-002** ships `src/features/feedbackFlags/derivedObservationSignals.ts` - the zero-spend cross-family composition layer (doc 10 §5). The `DerivedSignal` envelope is the doctrine spine: `advisory:true` / `authoritative:false` / `neverAffectsStanding:true` are LITERAL types (a non-advisory signal cannot typecheck), and the `DerivedSignalConsumer` union is a CLOSED set with NO score/standing/credit/band/verdict member (enforced by a scan for `/stand|score|credit|band|win|verdict/i`). Seven pure derivers compose over the SHIPPED `friendlyFlagsFor` currency + the `move_marks` aggregate + open evidence debts + heat band + timeline structure: `proof_moment` (B×D×F×marks, own-move only), `hot_but_proof_light` (heat×D×debts - the ONLY signal heat reaches, consumers ⊆ `{gallery_bucket}`), `talking_past` (C×B×H, both sides), `resolution_window` (G×F×debts), `callback_worthy` (E×G×A), `own_tension_hint` (composer-only, `consumers === ['composer_whisper']`), `dodge_chain` (marks×A). Output frozen + sorted `(code, scope-id)`; no fetch, no clock, no randomness; imports NO pointStanding and NEVER `deriveMediatorBoardState`/`buildPointLifecycleMap`/`deriveEvidenceDebts` (the single-derivation pin). `derivedSignalConsumerModel.ts` wires the TWO mandated surfaces via `selectInspectAdvisoryLines` (Inspect active-node lines, under `PointFeedbackFlagsRow` via the new `DerivedSignalAdvisoryLines` strip) + `selectMediatorRailOverlay` (rail overlay keyed to ALREADY-derived board points, additive, never reorders); the other five consumers emit-but-dormant. New 8th ASP flag `derived_signals`/`EXPO_PUBLIC_DERIVED_SIGNALS` (static dot read + `featureFlagsStaticEnv` allowlist entry); App.tsx is the sole flag reader threading `derivedSignalsEnabled` -> ArgumentTreeScreen -> ArgumentRoom (no `src/features` file imports featureFlags - the house allowlist holds). **INTEL-001** ships `src/features/intel/`: `dodgeChainModel` (maximal consecutive `did_not_address` runs on ONE thread, ≥2; the feed uses the deduped node count + longest length, NEVER a sum, NEVER a person; cycle/orphan-safe), `debtAnswerRateModel` (per-room repair rate answered/(total-branched), null on a zero denominator, clock-free), `roomIntelSignals` (bundle + the engagement-lane envelope passthrough - FEEDBACK-002 reconciliation R5: continuous metrics stay OUTSIDE the discrete DerivedSignal envelope, INTEL declares lane `engagement`, adds no new code). Two inert-by-default feeds: `computeConversationHeat` gains an OPTIONAL `dodgeChainHits` term folded by the gallery deriver ONLY when the new gated batched `move_marks` read (`useGalleryMoveMarks` hook, App-threaded `enabled`) supplied `unaddressedMoveIdsByDebateId`; and `MediatorBoardOptions.weightingSignals` consumed ONLY in `deriveNextAction`'s tie-break through the single derivation (`computeInputHash` untouched => `inputHash` byte-identical; wired in ArgumentRoom from the marks aggregate + tree + debts it already holds, gated on `moveMarksEnabled`). **INTEL-002** ships `markerSpecificityModel` (marker-linked replies / reply moves, per-room + POOLED aggregate Σ/Σ - never the mean of per-room rates; rate null on a zero denominator; secondary rebuttal-typed rate; NO per-person axis) + an admin-only advisory readout on `AdminArgumentsTab` (per-room specificity chip in the room-group header + the aggregate line + the marker-visibility caveat "Counts reflect markers visible to you."); `AdminArgumentRow` gains `parentId`, `adminArgumentsApi` gains a `parent_id` select + `loadRoomMarkerReplyLinks` (batched `.in` + `.is('deleted_at', null)`, caller-scoped, RLS-bounded, [] on error, never throws, reads NO person field). **1003 suites / 34315 total passing (34314 passed + 1 pre-existing skip; exit 0) - +20 suites / +214 tests over the 983 / 34101 baseline.** Typecheck + lint + `web:build` clean; `grep SERVICE_ROLE|ANTHROPIC_API_KEY app/ src/` no new hits; no console.log in client code. **Load-bearing pins (all with firing negative controls):** the envelope literal-type invariants; the anti-amplification pin (heat reaches exactly `hot_but_proof_light`; T-AA dir-scans `src/features/intel/*` for zero `pointStanding`/`antiAmplification` import + no standing field on any output); the single-derivation pin (the mediator board is passed through untouched, still ONE derivation call site); T-NP (no `created_by`/`authorId` anywhere under `intel/*` - chains describe THREADS, the KPI is room/aggregate only); T-MW (mediator byte-identity incl. `inputHash` when `weightingSignals` absent + re-order-only when present); and the heat/gallery byte-identity-when-absent proofs. **ONE existing-test edit beyond additive registration:** `featureFlagsStaticEnv.test.ts` gains the 8th static-literal allowlist assertion (the design-named change) and `featureFlags.test.ts` additively registers the 8th flag (required by the union expansion). **Deviations (mechanism, not outcome; surfaced for review):** (1) both design docs say "gated on `isDerivedSignalsEnabled()`/`isMoveMarksEnabled()`" inside `src/features`, but the house pattern (enforced by `featureFlagsStaticEnv`) forbids feature-level flag reads - implemented via the App-threaded `enabled`-prop pattern per orchestrator R1 ("allowlist handling per house pattern"); (2) R4 mediator feed rode `MediatorBoardOptions.weightingSignals` (D7) WITHOUT falling back to the M-B overlay - no pin/scan fired on the additive `deriveNextAction` tie-break, `inputHash` stays byte-identical; (3) the INTEL-002 readout test is a source-scan of `AdminArgumentsTab` wiring + a behavioral copy-module test (matching the repo's own admin-tab convention - all 15 existing admin-tab suites are source-scans, "no native renderer in CI") rather than a full RNTL mount. **No Anthropic / xAI / X API call, no Supabase write / read / query against any live project, no service-role usage by Claude, no migration / no Edge / no `.env` value edit in this bundle.** **Operator (after merge):** pure code, nothing auto-applies/auto-deploys (no migration, no config.toml-registered function). INTEL-001 comes alive with the FEEDBACK-001 `move_marks` rollout (dodge-chains then nudge gallery heat + the mediator next-action, both engagement-lane); INTEL-002 reads honestly-low until `timestamp_rebuttals` markers accrue (a 0% readout is "0 visible so far," not a failure); the marker-visibility caveat bounds the admin aggregate to circle-visible rooms (a global service-role KPI is a deferred follow-up, NOT built). Turning the advisory signals on later is the same operator env action as the other ASP flags (`EXPO_PUBLIC_DERIVED_SIGNALS=true` + a strict-FF publish), gated behind the reviewer's parity check. Designs: `docs/designs/FEEDBACK-002.md`, `docs/designs/INTEL-001-002.md`.

**2026-07-11 - FEEDBACK-001 (#898) - move_marks ledger + mark-move Edge + BooleanFeedbackBar ghost pair (M-ASP-7, Phase P7; migration-bearing + deploy-bearing, merge = auto-apply + auto-deploy; flag `move_marks`, default OFF, flag-off byte-identical):** Fills the one cheap HUMAN signal the machine-heavy pipeline was missing - *is this exchange connecting?* - without turning the room into a scored match. Under the ACTIVE opponent move, two quiet ghost buttons ("Answered my point" / "Didn't answer it") + an optional contextual "Receipts?"; one tap marks, a second retracts; reversible, never required, never on your own move, never for observers. **Migration** `supabase/migrations/20260712000001_feedback_001_move_marks.sql` (byte-equal to the design fenced block; strict diff exit 0) creates ONE new RLS-enabled EMPTY table `move_marks` (id, debate_id, argument_id, marked_by, `mark_code` CHECK of the five Output 9 codes `addressed_my_point`/`did_not_address`/`receipts_requested`/`good_receipt`/`off_the_point`, created_at, `retracted_at` nullable, FULL `UNIQUE(argument_id, marked_by, mark_code)` - a two-state latch, not the point_tags partial-where-null event log). **Write posture = SELECT-only / Edge-only writes (the current house default - PROOF-001 / MARK-001 / circles; the fifth SELECT-only instance):** exactly ONE active + room-visible SELECT policy via the single `is_argument_visible_in_circle(move_marks.argument_id, auth.uid())` helper (no raw subquery, Class-1 clean); NO INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE policy, NO write-gate helper, NO write-guard trigger. The RLS-posture decision was delegated to the card and ratified SELECT-only: the not-own-move guard needs `arguments.author_id` (react-to-move notes RLS lacks cheap access without a join), the participant-only gate + the paired-code mutual-exclusivity retract are application logic, and SELECT-only makes the two aggregate surfaces honest-by-construction (a row exists ONLY via the validated Edge). Inert storage: NO score/standing/engagement column, no standing trigger. **New Edge** `supabase/functions/mark-move/` (config.toml-registered `verify_jwt = true` - the #509 hazard) is the SOLE writer: a fail-closed guard ladder (CORS/POST -> auth header -> JSON -> `.strict()` schema -> allow-list `markCode` [422 `invalid_mark_code`] -> `getUser` -> caller-scoped no-oracle target read [404 `argument_not_found` / 400 `debate_argument_mismatch` / 400 `argument_deleted`] -> **own-move guard** [403 `cannot_mark_own_move`] -> **`is_debate_participant` RPC** [403 `not_a_participant`, closing the observer gap react-to-move leaves open] -> service-role mutate). `mark` upserts (idempotent re-activate; a double-mark on an active row is a no-op 200) and first retracts the opposite paired arm; `retract` soft-sets `retracted_at` (no active row = no-op 200). The response returns ONLY the caller `viewerMarks` booleans - **NO counts of others** (a deliberately stronger un-game-like posture than the fist-bump; the aggregates read the room-shell bulk SELECT, never this response). Audit via service-role best-effort; never logs Authorization/service-role; imports no point-standing path; never writes `public.arguments`. Pure shared contract `_shared/moveMarkCodes.ts` imported by both the Edge and its jest test. **Feature** (`src/features/feedback/`): pure `moveMarksModel` (five codes, `oppositeOf` pair helper, `summarizeViewerMarks`); the ONE `deriveMoveMarkAggregate` (did_not_address chains, receipts-request counts, off-the-point ids - active-only, deterministic, NO score field); `moveMarksCopy` (ban-list-clean labels/a11y/error copy + the ambient legend line); `moveMarksApi` narrow seam over the `markMove` `edgeFunctions` wrapper; `useMoveMarks` hook (bulk active read + per-move viewer state + optimistic `onMark`/`onUnmark` that reconcile with the Edge authoritative `viewerMarks` and revert-with-note on failure; empty + no fetch when disabled). `BooleanFeedbackBar`: marked state carried by a check affix + filled-vs-outline surface + weight change (grayscale-legible, neutral STATUS tones, **no red/green verdict colors**); 32px + hitSlop >= 44x44; role/label/selected-state; disabled inert; tiny-width collapse to a single overflow control; reduce-motion by construction (no Animated). **Both lenses mount the SAME bar** wired to the SAME `useMoveMarks` handlers (RingsideCard active non-own participant card + MapNodeActionPopover parity), threaded additively through RingsideFeed / ExchangeView / MapView. **Two ambient aggregate surfaces from ONE derivation in ArgumentRoom** (the single reconciliation point): surface #1 folds the aggregate into the state-rail `openPointCount` + `receiptsOwedCount` it already computes (`argumentStateRailModel` stays zero-diff); surface #2 passes an additive `marksLegendLine` to `DisagreementPointsRail` - never a per-message counter, never a leaderboard. RLS exposes `marked_by` room-wide (the move_reactions precedent) but the UI is BOUND to never render another viewer's mark identity or a per-message count (pinned with firing negative controls). App.tsx (sole flag consumer, separate import line per the featureFlagsStaticEnv pin) threads `moveMarksEnabled` -> ArgumentTreeScreen -> ArgumentRoom. Flag OFF => `useMoveMarks` fetches nothing, the aggregate is null, no bar mounts, both surfaces byte-identical (proved by App/Tree/Room source-scan gates + firing negative controls). **J10 acceptance:** one tap marks, second tap retracts, never blocks posting (the bar is wired independently of the composer/submit path; a failed tap reverts + shows a quiet plain-language note), observer sees disabled/absent controls. **983 suites / 34101 total passing (34100 passed + 1 pre-existing skip; exit 0) - +11 suites / +175 tests over the 972 / 33926 baseline; ZERO existing-test edits beyond the additive `mark-move` registration assertion.** Typecheck + lint + `web:build` clean; `grep SERVICE_ROLE|ANTHROPIC_API_KEY app/ src/` no new hits (the two edgeFunctions comment lines are pre-existing prose); no console.log in client code. **The anti-amplification boundary is the load-bearing pin:** `feedbackMoveMarksNoStanding.test.ts` asserts no `feedback/*` file imports a `pointStanding`/`antiAmplification` path and neither the aggregate nor the Edge response carries a score/delta field - a pile of `did_not_address` marks can NEVER lower a claim's factual standing. **No Anthropic / xAI / X API call, no Supabase write / read / query against any live project, no service-role usage by Claude, no existing-migration or existing-Edge edit, no submit-argument change, no `.env` value edit, no migration applied / Edge deployed by Claude in this card.** **Operator (after merge):** the migration AUTO-APPLIES and `mark-move` AUTO-DEPLOYS on merge to main (Supabase GitHub integration keys off the config.toml `[functions.*]` block; the flag stays OFF, so merging changes no live surface). VERIFY on the linked DB: `select to_regclass('public.move_marks');` (non-null) and `select polname, cmd from pg_policies where tablename='move_marks';` (expect exactly ONE row, SELECT, roles `{authenticated}`; zero write policies); `npx supabase functions list --linked` (expect `mark-move`) + an unauthenticated 401 probe. **10% rollout (Design Pass P7 feedback-fatigue risk):** ship the ghost pair to 10% FIRST - set `EXPO_PUBLIC_MOVE_MARKS=true` for the 10% cohort, publish via the deliberate netlify-prod push (strict FF push + poll the deployed bundle hash), watch tap-through + civility-flag deltas before widening. Do NOT flip `move_marks` to 100% in this card. Rollback = flag off (immediate) + a NEW forward `drop table public.move_marks;` migration, never an edit to the applied file. Design: `docs/designs/FEEDBACK-001.md`.

**2026-07-10 - MARK-002 (#894) - create-marker Edge + TimestampMarker chip + scoped reply, text-first (M-ASP-4, Phase P3.5; migration-bearing + deploy-bearing, merge = auto-apply + auto-deploy; flag `timestamp_rebuttals`, default OFF, flag-off byte-identical):** Ships the text half of J6 end-to-end behind the shipped-OFF flag: select a phrase of an opponent's posted move -> a marker mints with a **server-snapshotted `quoted_text`** -> the reply carries the marker chip -> the chip deep-links back to the source span in full body context (the Output 13 Q5 mitigation). **Migration** `supabase/migrations/20260711000002_mark_002_marker_reply_ref.sql` (byte-equal to the design artifact; strict diff exit 0) is ONE additive `ALTER public.timestamp_markers ADD reply_argument_id uuid NULL references public.arguments(id) on delete set null` + a partial index + a column comment - **marker-side linkage** (the delegated decision, orchestrator-ratified over `arguments.target_marker_id` which would cost a new service-role UPDATE path to the sacred arguments table): one marker row serves BOTH placements (`target_argument_id` drives the source-span highlight, `reply_argument_id` drives the reply reference chip). NO RLS change (visibility flows through `target_argument_id` via the shipped SELECT policy), NO trigger/function, `timestamp_markers` stays SELECT-only. **New Edge** `supabase/functions/create-marker/` (config.toml-registered `verify_jwt = true` - the #509 hazard) is the SOLE writer: a 12-guard fail-closed ladder (auth / `.strict()` schema / identity / target-visibility no-oracle / participant / span-bounds / **EXACT server-side quote verification** / reply-ownership / idempotency-on-`reply_argument_id` / per-target cap 20 / service-role INSERT). `quoted_text` is the SERVER slice `body.slice(spanStart, spanEnd)`, never the client quote (the client quote is verification-only -> a fabricated quote 422s `quote_mismatch`; the fabricated-quote acceptance criterion). Pure shared contract `_shared/markerCreate.ts` imported by both the Edge and the jest test. **Gesture (delegated decision):** a deterministic sentence-granularity phrase-picker (`segmentPhrases` with byte-exact offsets) - free text selection with reliable char offsets is not robust cross-platform, so v1 picks one phrase; the server verifies any span. **Chip:** ONE `TimestampMarker` component, three placements (source_span highlight with a tint + underline non-color cue, reply_reference chip, composer_scope chip) + an orphaned tombstone; 32px + hitSlop, roles/labels/states, reduce-motion-safe. **Wiring:** App.tsx (sole flag importer) threads `timestampRebuttalsEnabled` + holds the pending scope + mints post-submit via `createMarkerScoped` (atomic mint+link, no orphans; a mint failure never blocks the already-posted reply); ArgumentTreeScreen pass-through; ArgumentRoom calls `useMarkers` + owns the picker + emits the picked scope; RingsideCard/RingsideFeed/ExchangeView render the highlight + reply chips + Respond-to-this (non-own only); MapNodeSidecarLinks reply-chip parity; the composer_scope chip rides the ROOM-003 context-chip seam (NO pinned-file edit; the pre-existing `handleSubmitSuccess = ()` COMPOSER-002 pin is preserved byte-identical - the mint lives in a separate entry wrapper). Flag OFF => `useMarkers` fetches nothing, no affordance, no chip, byte-identical (proved by a JSON.stringify structural equality + a firing negative control + App.tsx/ArgumentRoom source-scan gate pins). **972 suites / 33926 total passing (33925 passed + 1 pre-existing skip; exit 0) - +11 suites / +129 tests over the 961 / 33797 baseline; ZERO existing-test edits.** Typecheck + lint + `web:build` clean; `grep SERVICE_ROLE|ANTHROPIC_API_KEY app/ src/` no new hits; no console.log in client code. **No Anthropic / xAI / X API call, no Supabase write / read / query against any live project, no service-role usage by Claude, no existing-migration or existing-Edge edit, no submit-argument change, no `.env` value edit, no migration applied / Edge deployed by Claude in this card.** **Operator (after merge):** the migration AUTO-APPLIES and `create-marker` AUTO-DEPLOYS on merge to main (Supabase GitHub integration keys off the config.toml `[functions.*]` block; merge = deploy for both, before any UI - the flag stays OFF). VERIFY on the linked DB: `select column_name from information_schema.columns where table_name='timestamp_markers' and column_name='reply_argument_id';` and `npx supabase functions list --linked` (expect `create-marker`). Do NOT flip `timestamp_rebuttals` in this card. Design: `docs/designs/MARK-002.md`.

**2026-07-09 - MARK-001 (#893) - timestamp_markers table + proof_items.marker_id FK (M-ASP-4, Phase P3.5; migration-bearing, merge = auto-apply, deploy-gated; SELECT-only; DB only, ZERO client/Edge/UI):** Ships the text-first data layer for the marker system (Design Pass principle 7, "Quote moments, not messages") ahead of any audio. New migration `supabase/migrations/20260711000001_mark_001_timestamp_markers.sql` creates ONE new, RLS-enabled, EMPTY table `timestamp_markers` - a durable quote-a-moment record. **Span shape (delegated Decision 1):** `span_start int` / `span_end int` / `span_unit text CHECK ('chars')` carries BOTH lanes through one discriminator - char offsets into `arguments.body` now, and the P5 voice (ms) lane after a one-line CHECK widen, never a reshape; `CHECK (span_end > span_start)` (non-empty span) mirrors the Design Pass `end_ms > start_ms`; `recording_id` deliberately ABSENT until P5 (the audio FK has no producer and the voice columns do not exist yet - the same defer-with-the-lane logic PROOF-001 used for `marker_id`). **`quoted_text text NOT NULL`** is the DURABLE artifact - a verbatim server snapshot (taken by the MARK-002 Edge from `arguments.body` server-side) that is self-sufficient after any body edit, argument soft-delete (`status='deleted'`), or (P5) audio deletion, so marker correctness never depends on span-offset stability (the target-body durability audit found `arguments.body` is written once at INSERT and never after, with the one raw-PATCH RLS hole documented; offsets are advisory locators, `quoted_text` is the source of truth). `kind text CHECK ('rebuttal_anchor','note')` - `proof_excerpt` DEFERRED (no proof kind consumes a marker yet; a text marker is a rebuttal anchor, not a proof). Never hard-deleted (`deleted_at` tombstone, consumed now by the SELECT policy filter). **Write posture = MAXIMALLY-CONSERVATIVE / SELECT-only (circles #859 + PROOF-001 #888 precedent, third confirmation of the pattern):** exactly ONE policy - SELECT to `authenticated`, room-visible via the SINGLE `is_argument_visible_in_circle(timestamp_markers.target_argument_id, auth.uid())` call (canonical `is_argument_visible` + the #882 circle arm, never re-inlined). NO write policy, NO write-gate helper, NO write-guard trigger - ALL writes (insert, soft-delete/retract) belong to the MARK-002 service-role Edge, whose server-side `quoted_text` snapshot is the decisive reason for SELECT-only (a client INSERT could forge `quoted_text`, re-opening the Output 13 Q5 misrepresentation vector; only a service-role Edge can guarantee `quoted_text == body[span_start:span_end]`). **`ALTER public.proof_items ADD marker_id`** honors the PROOF-001 deferral exactly - the FK now has a real referent from day one: `uuid NULL references public.timestamp_markers(id) on delete set null` + a partial index `proof_items_marker where marker_id is not null`. **NO `proof_items.kind` widening** (the voice-proof kinds `voice_excerpt`/`timestamp` stay deferred - no producer this card, so widening would permit a value no safe path emits). Strictly additive; the table ships empty and `proof_items.marker_id` ships all-NULL, so every existing room is bit-for-bit unchanged. Migration SQL is byte-equal to the fenced block in `docs/designs/MARK-001.md` (strict diff exit 0). Docker-less lane: new text-scan suite `__tests__/timestampMarkersRlsScan.test.ts` (36 - presence + numbering, RLS-enabled/never-disabled/strictly-additive [the only schema-mutating ALTER is the additive `proof_items.marker_id` add], SELECT-only proof [exactly one FOR SELECT policy, no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE, no helper/trigger/grant], single-composition-point [`is_argument_visible_in_circle` only, no raw arguments/debates subquery], span shape [`span_unit` chars-only with `ms` deferred, `span_end > span_start`, `quoted_text NOT NULL`, `recording_id` absent from the executable SQL], kind set [`rebuttal_anchor`/`note`, `proof_excerpt` deferred], the `proof_items.marker_id` ALTER shape + no-kind-widening, Class-1 qualification, Class-4 helper-reference-not-redefine, doctrine ban-list - each safety scan paired with a firing NEGATIVE CONTROL). **961 suites / 33797 total passing (33796 passed + 1 pre-existing skip; exit 0) - +1 suite / +36 tests over the 960 / 33761 baseline; ZERO existing-test edits.** Typecheck + lint clean. No `web:build` (ZERO client/src/app code). **No Anthropic / xAI / X API call, no Supabase write / read / query against any live project, no service-role usage by Claude, no Edge / client / UI change, no existing-migration edit, no `proof_items.kind` widening, no `.env` value edit, no migration applied / deployed by Claude in this card.** **Operator (after merge):** the migration AUTO-APPLIES on merge to main (Supabase GitHub integration; rollback = a NEW forward-drop migration in dependency order - `alter table public.proof_items drop column marker_id;` THEN `drop table public.timestamp_markers;` - never an edit to the applied file). Then VERIFY on the linked DB - `pg_policies` for `timestamp_markers` (expect exactly ONE SELECT row, PERMISSIVE, roles `{authenticated}`), `to_regclass('public.timestamp_markers')` non-null, and `proof_items.marker_id` present. MARK-002 (marker UI + write Edge) is unblocked; P5 `voice-004-waveform-markers` widens the `span_unit` CHECK to add `ms` and adds `recording_id`. Design: `docs/designs/MARK-001.md`.

**2026-07-09 - PROOF-003 (#890) + PROOF-002 (#889) - attach-proof Edge Function + Source drawer / read-path flip (M-ASP-3, Phase P3; Round-2 evidence bundle on one branch; PROOF-003 deploy-bearing, PROOF-002 behind `proof_drawer` flag default OFF):** The write path + the UI for post-hoc evidence attach (J7 — "proof attached to the past move, not to your next reply"). **PROOF-003** ships `supabase/functions/attach-proof/index.ts`, the SOLE server-authoritative writer for `proof_items` / `proof_relations` (both shipped SELECT-only by PROOF-001, so the Edge OWNS every guarantee the dropped trigger used to provide). Two actions: `attach` (a single `proof_items` INSERT + an OPTIONAL `proof_relations` row) and `detach` (a `deleted_at`-only soft-delete). Guard ladder with distinct honest no-oracle codes (`argument_not_found` / `not_your_move` / `debate_argument_mismatch` / `not_a_participant` / `kind_not_supported` / `claim_not_found` / `referenced_argument_not_found` / `proof_cap_reached` / `proof_not_found` / `not_your_proof`) — every "not found" comes from a caller-scoped RLS read (COV-004 `is_argument_visible` surfaces the author's own draft), the two authz codes only ever fire on a room-visible row. zod `.strict()` discriminated union (a smuggled `sourceChainStatus`/`risk` 422s); status is DERIVED server-side, restricted to the 3 client-derivable values (`unverified`/`source_no_quote`/`source_and_quote`) — never `broken`/`primary_present` (condition (ii)); the detach UPDATE payload is EXACTLY `{ deleted_at }` and no path un-deletes (condition (i)). Natural-content-tuple idempotency (no migration); 8-per-move cap. `answers_request` fires the EXISTING `evidence_supplied` `room_notifications` path (the same service-role INSERT shape `submit-argument` uses, `meta:{via:'attach_proof'}`, recipients = room primaries minus caller). **Orchestrator ruling R2 (first-attach JSONB capture):** on attach to a move that carries a JSONB snapshot AND has zero proof rows, the pre-existing JSONB artifacts are folded into rows FIRST (so the PROOF-002 rows-first adapter can never see mixed state) via a Deno-side BEHAVIOURAL twin of the PROOF-001 back-fill pipeline (`_shared/proofCapture.ts`), pinned byte-equal to the real `classifyArgumentRow` by `__tests__/proofCaptureParity.test.ts` (Deno cannot import `src/`; row-output equality is the sanctioned fallback — the ONE documented divergence is the rare QOL-036 payment redaction-downgrade). Registered in `config.toml` (`verify_jwt=true`) in the same commit (the #509 auto-deploy hazard, scan-pinned). Client wrappers `attachProof`/`detachProof` in `src/lib/edgeFunctions.ts` (anon+JWT only, `reactToMove` idiom). **PROOF-002** moves attach to the edge behind `proof_drawer`: the ROOM-003 one-bar Source slot opens a `ProofDrawer` (bottom sheet <720 / right panel >=720 via the reused dock breakpoint; 6-kind grid, one focused input each) instead of More; a `ProofChip` (thin wrapper over the shipped `ReceiptChip`, authors no copy of its own) renders attached sources; the read adapter `buildArtifactsByMessageId` flips to ROWS-FIRST with a JSONB FALLBACK at ONE seam (`argumentGameSurfaceEvidence.ts`) — absent/empty rows arg => byte-identical to today (`proofReadPathParity` contract snapshot). The narrow `attachProofApi.ts` seam reconciles all 10 PROOF-002 numbered assumptions against the shipped PROOF-003 contract (camelCase body, camel->snake row map, actual error codes -> plain language, `clientAttachId` omitted per `.strict()`, `answers_request` relation) — the single file that changes on Edge drift (R1). J7 debt flip is client-observed: `deriveEvidenceDebts` recomputes `requested -> supplied` the moment the folded source lands on the answering move (the inverse fold's `(kind, sourceChainStatus)` discharges `source`; a quote-only negative control stays `requested`). COPY LAW held — every rendered string says Source/Receipts, `proof` banned (scanned with firing negative controls); component/type names exempt. **960 suites / 33761 total passing (33760 passed + 1 pre-existing skip; exit 0) — +13 suites / +138 tests over the 947/33623 baseline; ZERO existing-test edits.** Typecheck + lint + `web:build` clean. Flag `proof_drawer` OFF at merge => zero live change; the read-path flip is inert (fetch hook disabled). All ROOM-002/003 + uxOneOneFive/Six pins green (App is the sole `featureFlags` consumer; the composer + drawer never import it; the pinned composer/dock/oneBox/submit-argument/ArgumentTimelineMap/MapView files are zero-diff). Ringside ProofChip view-model swap DESCOPED (design operator-deferred item (ii); the plain `proofChipCount` pill already reflects the flipped `artifactsByMessageId`, and the Map `ReceiptChip` reflects it automatically — J7 unaffected). **No Anthropic / xAI / X API call, no Supabase write / read against any live project, no service-role usage by Claude, no migration, no live deploy by Claude in this bundle.** **Operator (after merge):** `attach-proof` AUTO-DEPLOYS on merge (config.toml-registered); verify with `npx supabase functions list --linked` + a 401 unauthenticated probe + the authed smoke in `docs/designs/PROOF-003.md` § Operator steps. PROOF-002 is a pure client change (flag OFF) — the drawer/button/chip + read-path flip become visible only on a deliberate netlify push after `EXPO_PUBLIC_PROOF_DRAWER=true` (run the PROOF-001 back-fill first so back-filled moves have rows). Designs: `docs/designs/PROOF-003.md`, `docs/designs/PROOF-002.md`.

**2026-07-09 - PROOF-001 (#888) - proof_items + proof_relations tables + RLS + JSONB back-fill lane (M-ASP-3, Phase P3; migration-bearing, merge = auto-apply, deploy-gated; SELECT-only per orchestrator ruling; DB + script only, ZERO client/Edge/UI):** Ships the dedicated relational home for evidence artifacts ("path c" — supersedes the `client_validation->attachedEvidence` JSONB snapshot at read time only once PROOF-002 flips it, behind a JSONB fallback). New migration `supabase/migrations/20260710000001_proof_001_proof_items_and_relations.sql` creates TWO new, RLS-enabled, EMPTY tables: `proof_items` (per-artifact record; `kind` is a strict superset of `EvidenceArtifactKind` — the 6 infra-free kinds `url/quote/source_text/note/prior_move/external_ref`; storage kinds `screenshot`/`file` deferred to SEC-PROOF-001, marker kinds `voice_excerpt`/`timestamp` + the `marker_id` column deferred to MARK-001; `source_chain_status` CHECK omits the aggregate-only `no_source`) and `proof_relations` (the claim<->proof graph; `relation in supports/contradicts/contextualizes/answers_request`; `answers_request` powers evidence-debt auto-resolution). **Write posture = MAXIMALLY-CONSERVATIVE / SELECT-only (orchestrator ruling 2026-07-09, recorded in `docs/designs/PROOF-001.md` "Orchestrator ruling applied"):** each table ships exactly ONE policy — SELECT to `authenticated`, room-visible via the SINGLE `is_argument_visible_in_circle(...)` call (canonical `is_argument_visible` + the #882 circle arm, never re-inlined). NO authenticated write policy, NO write-gate helper, NO write-guard trigger — ALL writes (insert, relation-insert, soft-delete/detach, privileged `broken`/`primary_present` status) belong to the PROOF-003 service-role Edge (Round 2), matching the circles (#859) SELECT-only precedent. Strictly additive; existing rooms are bit-for-bit unchanged (tables empty until PROOF-003/back-fill writes). `added_by`/`created_by` -> `public.profiles(id)` matches the house convention (`arguments.author_id`, `concession_items.author_id`). New operator-gated back-fill lane under `scripts/proof-backfill/`: `proofItemRowFromArtifact.ts` is the SHARED fold (`EvidenceArtifact` -> `proof_items` row; `payment_screenshot` -> DEFERRED, left in JSONB) imported by BOTH the runner and the fidelity test (one module, two consumers); `backfillProofItemsCore.ts` is the PURE engine (no Supabase, no console) that classifies via the REAL `buildEvidenceArtifacts` (never a twin) + builds the dry-run report + emitted SQL; `backfillProofItems.ts` is the CLI (DRY-RUN DEFAULT, fail-closed — a live write needs BOTH `--apply` AND `PROOF_BACKFILL_APPLY=true`; `--demo` self-demo with no network; service-role referenced ONLY as an env NAME, never a value; emitted report/SQL to gitignored `out/proof-backfill/`). The runner reaches the real classifier under bare `node` via `tsconfig.proof-backfill.json` + the `proof:backfill:*` npm scripts (the AN-003 tree-playability `tsc -p` precedent — no ts-node, no new deps). New tests: `__tests__/proofItemsRlsScan.test.ts` (26 — SELECT-only proof [no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE policy on either table], additive-only, single-composition-point, kind/status CHECK sets, Class-1 qualification, doctrine ban-list, each safety scan paired with a firing NEGATIVE CONTROL), `__tests__/proofBackfillFidelity.test.ts` (ReceiptChip copy parity JSONB-path vs rows-path + fold determinism/totality over all 7 kinds + the documented glyph-kinds divergence + payment deferral), `__tests__/proofBackfillRunner.test.ts` (dry-run report shape on fixture with no network, fail-closed gate, real-classifier proof, no-secret source scan). **947 suites / 33623 total passing (33622 passed + 1 pre-existing skip; exit 0) — +3 suites / +60 new proof tests (`proofItemsRlsScan` 26, `proofBackfillFidelity` 15, `proofBackfillRunner` 19); ZERO existing-test edits. The documented base was 944 / 33563 passing; the measured net-passing delta is +59, a 1-test stale-baseline artifact (the design's Risk section flagged this), not a regression.** Typecheck + lint clean. No `web:build` (ZERO client/src/app code). **No Anthropic / xAI / X API call, no Supabase write / read / query against any live project, no service-role usage by Claude, no Edge / client / UI change, no existing-migration edit, no `.env` value edit, no migration applied / deployed / back-filled by Claude in this card.** **Operator (after merge):** the migration AUTO-APPLIES on merge to main (Supabase GitHub integration; rollback = a NEW forward drop migration, never an edit to the applied file). Then VERIFY on the linked DB — `pg_policies` for `proof_items`/`proof_relations` (expect exactly ONE SELECT row each, PERMISSIVE, roles `{authenticated}`) + `to_regclass` both tables non-null. Then the back-fill dry-run (zero writes): `npm run proof:backfill:dry` (needs `SUPABASE_URL` + `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` operator env) -> review counts + emitted SQL under `out/proof-backfill/`. Live back-fill is separately operator-gated (`PROOF_BACKFILL_APPLY=true npm run proof:backfill:apply`). PROOF-002 (drawer UI) + PROOF-003 (attach Edge) are unblocked. Design: `docs/designs/PROOF-001.md`.

**2026-07-09 - ROOM-002 (#885) + ROOM-004 (#886) - A x B hybrid room surface: Ringside Exchange re-weight + Map capability parity (M-ASP-2; behind `room_exchange_v2`, live server-side, client deploys gated by the deliberate netlify-prod push; UI / pure-model only):** The A x B hybrid pair on one bundle branch. **ROOM-002 (Ringside)** re-weights the Exchange lens from the z-stacked bubble deck into a transform-free linear feed of move cards. New pure model `src/features/arguments/room/ringsideFeedModel.ts` (`buildRingsideFeed`) projects the derivations the orchestrator already holds (view-model `allowedControls`, timeline-node `kindColorFamily` / `descendantCount` / `parentId`, artifact counts, evidence-debt owed signal, injected `getRailActions` observer set) into card view-models with a kind-color spine paired with a text kind label (color never the only signal), a quote / context chip that activates its ancestor, lightweight proof + owed-receipt indicators, an inline branch pill that deep-links to the Map, and ONE actor-aware action row on the active card (participant `allowedControls` via `handleBubbleAction`; observer set via `handleRailAction`). New UI `RingsideCard.tsx` + `RingsideFeed.tsx`; conversation-first - NO standing / heat / classifier data on the card face (that read lives in the shared Inspect popout + the Map sidecar). `ExchangeView` gains a flag-on early return rendering `<RingsideFeed>`; the stack subtree stays verbatim as the flag-off else branch (git diff shows ZERO deletions on `ExchangeView`, so the flag-off render is byte-identical), and `ArgumentRoom` builds the feed memoized + null when the flag is off. **ROOM-004 (Map parity)** completes the B-side: view choice is a lens, never a capability gate. New pure models `mapNodeActionSurfaceModel.ts` (`buildMapNodeActionSurface` - band-free actor-aware popover row + low-density sidecar links + open-point membership line + the `BUBBLE_CONTROL_TO_RAIL_ACTION_CODE` bridge, imports NO derivation) and `roomCapabilityParity.ts` (per-lens reachable `RailActionCode` sets + lens-independent handler class). New UI `MapNodeActionPopover.tsx` (44px rule, actor-aware, band-free) + `MapNodeSidecarLinks.tsx` (Answer this J9 deep-link, Open disagreement points, advisory open-point membership line). `MapView` renders the popover as a docked card AFTER the timeline map when a surface is supplied; `ArgumentRoom` is the single reconciliation point. **R1 mirroring (review fix):** the popover MIRRORS the Ringside card exactly - participant viewers get the SAME `activeViewModel.allowedControls` the card renders (own node reduces to `view_qualifiers` + `request_deletion` only; opponent = the full 7-control set), dispatched through the SAME `handleBubbleAction`; observers get the `getRailActions` observer set, dispatched through the SAME `handleRailAction`. It node-gates `actionDockModel` to null under flag-on so the popover supersedes the SC-004 node dock while cluster / stub docks are preserved (R2), mounts the sidecar links in col2 after the reused flags row, and adds `handleAnswerThisFromMap` (J9: `setMode('stack')` + `handleAction('reply', activeMessageId)`). SC-002 band popover left untouched (R3); bands stay retired. `ArgumentTimelineMap.tsx` + `TimelineSelectedReadoutPanel.tsx` + the composer / dock / oneBox `uxOneOneFive` pins are ZERO-diff; exactly ONE selection state (`timelineSelectionSharedAcrossModes` extended, single `activeMessageId` unchanged). The `roomCapabilityParityMatrix` test is the defining artifact: it derives the exchange arm from the REAL Ringside derivation (`getBubbleControlsForActor` mapped to rail codes) and the map arm from the REAL popover injection (`buildMapNodeActionSurface`), asserts set-equality for every `RailActionCode` x viewerRole x actor class + one handler class per code + a handler-identity source-scan pinning BOTH dispatch paths (`allowedControls` -> `handleBubbleAction` AND `getRailActions` -> `handleRailAction`), and carries a NEGATIVE CONTROL that stubs one lens node row to a subset and proves the set-equality guard fires - so a future Exchange-only capability genuinely FAILS the matrix. **944 suites / 33563 tests passing (+10 suites / +252 tests vs 934 / 33311; 1 pre-existing skip; exit 0).** Typecheck + lint clean; `web:build` exit 0. **No Anthropic / xAI / X API call, no Supabase write / read / migration / Edge change, no service-role, no new action code, no strength bands (stay retired), no `.env` edit by Claude in this pair.** **Operator (after merge):** pure client change - merging does NOT change the live site (`room_exchange_v2` is already live server-side); the Ringside + Map-parity UI becomes visible only on the deliberate netlify-prod push (strict FF push + poll the deployed JS bundle hash), after the full gates + `web:build` pass. Designs: `docs/designs/ROOM-002.md`, `docs/designs/ROOM-004.md`.

**2026-07-08 - ASP-CIRCLES-RLS-001 (#882) - circle read-arm migration: wire the shipped-but-dormant circle helpers into the debates + arguments SELECT policies (M-ASP-1; migration-bearing, merge = auto-apply, deploy-gated; closes START-002 #839 deferred AC2; home_v2 PRE-FLIP GATE):** Completes the START-002 split - a second circle member can now READ a circle-scoped room (the deferred AC2). New migration `supabase/migrations/20260709000001_asp_circles_rls_001_circle_read_arm.sql` adds exactly TWO new PERMISSIVE SELECT policies: `debates_select_circle_member` (`debates.circle_id is not null AND debates.visibility='private' AND debates.inactive_at is null AND public.is_circle_member(debates.circle_id, auth.uid())` - the `circle_id is not null` short-circuit is first so `is_circle_member` never fires for the existing non-circle rooms) and `arguments_select_circle_member` (`arguments.status='posted' AND arguments.inactive_at is null AND NOT public.is_debate_inactive(arguments.debate_id) AND public.is_argument_visible_in_circle(arguments.id, auth.uid())` - routed through the SINGLE composite helper `is_argument_visible_in_circle`, NEVER re-inlined; `circleVisibilityCompositionRlsScan` stays the alarm bell). Both OR-compose with the canonical `20260606000001` SELECT policies, which are NOT dropped / NOT recreated / NOT edited (byte-identical after apply); Postgres unions permissive policies with OR, so the delta can only WIDEN read access - provably non-narrowing (for `circle_id IS NULL` the debates arm is false and the arguments helper reduces to the canonical `is_argument_visible`, so every non-circle read is unchanged). Wires the two helpers shipped granted-but-dormant in `20260702000001` (`is_circle_member` grant line 303, `is_argument_visible_in_circle` line 369) plus the applied `is_debate_inactive` (`20260606000001`) - ZERO new GRANT, ZERO helper edit, ZERO new table / column / index. RLS stays ENABLED on both tables; SELECT-only, no write-path INSERT / UPDATE / DELETE policy; a circle is an ACCESS boundary, never a ranking or verdict (doctrine 1-3). Migration SQL is byte-equal to `docs/designs/ASP-CIRCLES-RLS-001.md` section 3 (diff exit 0, 8367 bytes / 121 lines). Docker-less lane: new text-scan suite `__tests__/circleReadArmRlsScan.test.ts` (25 tests) pins policy presence + names + FOR SELECT TO authenticated, the additive-only safety property (no DROP / re-CREATE of either canonical policy; the only DROP POLICY targets are the two new names), both arms ordered predicates, the arguments single-composition-point (no direct `is_circle_member`, no raw `select ... from public.debates` subquery), helper-reference correctness, the four OPS-001 issue classes, and a doctrine ban-list - each safety scan paired with a NEGATIVE CONTROL that plants the violation and asserts the scan fires. One anchor repin (orchestrator Option B ruling): `circleMigration.test.ts` retired its `is the highest sequential migration (no later 14-digit timestamp)` self-pin - which fired on the FIRST migration added after `20260702000001` regardless of content (a moment in time, not a behavior) - for the durable predecessor-ordering invariant (the circles migration is sequenced strictly after its documented predecessor `20260630000001_cov_004_argument_visibility_helper.sql`), NOTE-commented naming this card and scoped to that one assertion block; the other three circle anchors (`circleRlsScan`, `circleVisibilityCompositionRlsScan`, `debateInactiveCascadeRlsScan`) stay unmodified-green. **934 suites / 33311 tests passing (+1 suite / +25 tests vs 933 / 33285; 1 pre-existing skip; exit 0).** Typecheck + lint clean. No `web:build` (this card has ZERO client / src / app code). **No Anthropic / xAI / X API call, no Supabase write / read / query against any live project, no service-role, no client / Edge / config change, no existing-migration edit, no `.env` value edit, no migration applied or deployed by Claude in this card.** **Operator (after merge):** the migration AUTO-APPLIES on merge to main via the Supabase GitHub integration (rollback = a NEW drop-policy migration per section 11 dropping the two `…_select_circle_member` policies; never edit the applied file). Then VERIFY: section 12.1 `pg_policies` presence via `npx supabase db query` (expect FOUR SELECT rows - the two canonical `… admins read all` untouched + the two new `…_select_circle_member`, each PERMISSIVE / roles `{authenticated}`) then section 12.2 authed member-read smoke (live member -> 1 `debates` row + >=1 posted arg; non-member -> 0; non-circle room -> unchanged). **PRE-FLIP GATE: #882 must merge AND verify BEFORE `home_v2` flips ON** - until then a non-creator circle member cannot read a circle room and HOME-003 (#840) member lane returns empty; `home_v2` stays OFF until the operator flips it separately. Design: `docs/designs/ASP-CIRCLES-RLS-001.md`.

**2026-07-08 - CIRCLES-P1: START-002 (#839) + HOME-003 (#840) - circle-audience room creation + circle-home filter lane (M-ASP-1, behind home_v2; START-002 Edge is deploy-bearing on merge):** The circles P1 pair. **START-002** lets a user pick one of their circles as the audience when starting an argument, producing a PRIVATE room scoped to that circle. New shared client module `src/features/circles/circlesApi.ts` (`listMyCircles()` -> `MyCircleSummary[]` `{ id, name, memberCount, role }`, RLS-scoped anon-key + caller-JWT reads via `circles_select_member_owner_admin` + `circle_members_select_member_admin`; NO service role, NO oracle, NO write; IDENTITY-FREE - member user-ids are read only to compute the live count + the caller's own role, then discarded) + `useMyCircles` hook. The `create-argument-room` Edge Function gains an OPTIONAL additive `circle_id` on its `.strict()` zod schema; a single early `handleCircleRoomCreate` branch keeps the non-circle path structurally UNTOUCHED (AC1 byte-identical, provable by diff, existing suite unchanged-green). Circle guards (all before any DB write): circle+public -> 400 `circle_requires_private` (reject, not coerce); circle+invite -> 422 `validation_failed`; caller-scoped `is_circle_member` RPC -> non-member/nonexistent/soft-deleted all return the identical 404 `circle_not_found` (no enumeration oracle). Write lane (no migration): create PUBLIC + no invite via the atomic RPC, then flip to private + stamp `circle_id` in one `debates` UPDATE (QOL-039 one-way trigger permits public->private; `debates_circle_requires_private` CHECK backstops); a failed flip logs the orphan short id for operator cleanup and fails loudly (never a stranded public room silently). Zero `argument_room_invites` minted. The START-001 picker circle rows are now interactive (member count as a structural size, grayscale glyph, 44px + role/label/state); the sheet forces private + locks the public toggle off for a circle target (never enters START-003 `previewing_public`) and submits `{ title, resolution, description:'', visibility:'private', circleId }` with no invite. **AC2 (a second circle member READS the room) is DEFERRED** to the companion RLS-migration card **#882 (ASP-CIRCLES-RLS-001)** (the dormant `is_argument_visible_in_circle` arm is unwired; §5 of `docs/designs/START-002.md`) - the orchestrator selected Option 1 (split), so START-002 ships creation + creator-read only; no policy/helper edit. **Pre-flip gate: #882 must merge BEFORE `home_v2` flips ON** (until then only the creator can read a circle room; the sheet copy is present-honest about this). **HOME-003** makes a circle a LENS, not a screen: selecting a circle narrows the shipped HOME-001 "Your table" lane to rooms stamped with that circle. Orchestrator R2 (exactness): the predicate is `debate.circleId === selectedCircle.id` using the `circle_id` START-002 stamps - the `listDebates` SELECT is widened by one additive `circle_id` column (all prior column pins still hold), `Debate.circleId?` added, and the pure `filterArgumentHomeByCircle` runs AFTER `buildArgumentHomeViewModel` so D8 fixture exclusion composes by construction (a circle can never resurface an excluded fixture room). The participant-subset model from the HOME-003 draft is NOT implemented (superseded) and member user-ids are DROPPED (the shared module stays identity-free - a privacy win). New `CircleFilterRow` (All chip + one per circle, 44px, color-independent selection, filtered-empty block + Start CTA - never a dead end). One `useMyCircles` read in App.tsx feeds both surfaces (picker CircleOption + home CircleLens via `toCircleLens`). **6 commit slices; +5 suites (`circlesApi`, `debatesApiCirclePayload`, `startArgumentSheetCircleAudience`, `circleHomeFilter`, `circleFilterRow`) / +65 tests. 933 suites / 33285 tests passing (1 pre-existing skip).** Typecheck + lint + web:build clean. **No Anthropic/xAI/X API call, no Supabase write/read-beyond-existing by Claude, no migration, no RLS/policy change, no service-role in client, no member-identity in any client shape, no `.env` value edit, no new dependency by Claude in this pair.** **Operator (after merge):** the `create-argument-room` Edge Function AUTO-DEPLOYS on merge (config.toml-registered; rollback = revert the merge -> prior function auto-redeploys). Then run the live smoke (member create -> 200 + `debates.circle_id` set; non-member -> `circle_not_found`; no-circle create -> byte-identical) and **land #882 (ASP-CIRCLES-RLS-001)** - the already-filed companion read-arm migration (§5) - so non-creator circle members and HOME-003's member lane can read circle rooms; **#882 merges before `home_v2` flips ON**. Both surfaces are gated by the existing `home_v2` flag (OFF in prod). See `docs/designs/START-002.md` + `docs/designs/HOME-003.md`.

**2026-07-08 - ROOM-003 (#829) - One-bar ArgumentEntryComposer behind room_exchange_v2; absorbs #830 + #832 (UI/model only, flag DEFAULT OFF, ZERO behavior change until flipped, no migration/deploy/AI/query/write):** PR slice 07 of the Argument Surface Pivot (M-ASP-2). Collapses the everyday reply to ONE docked bar (context chip - text field - Source slot - reserved disabled voice slot - More - Send), defaults move type from the transition matrix and side from the participant seat (both adjustable via More, never required), and demotes the full OneBox to a never-required More popout. **The single HARD acceptance criterion is a byte-shape contract:** for equivalent input the `submit-argument` payload from the one-bar path is byte-shape-identical to today composer path - achieved BY CONSTRUCTION (the bar reuses the shipped `buildSubmitArgumentPayload` on a draft from the shipped `useArgumentComposer`) and proven by a dual-render deep-equal (legacy `ArgumentComposer` vs new bar, plain reply / type-defaulted counter / side-defaulted; `client_submission_id` normalised) plus a payload key census. New pure model `src/features/arguments/composer/argumentEntryComposerModel.ts` (`deriveEntryComposerDefaults` always clamped to `getAllowedArgumentTypesForParent` - a disallowed inference NEVER posts; `sideForParticipantSeat`; `deriveEntryComposerTarget`; `deriveEntryComposerBarLayout`; `deriveEntryComposerBlockingFlag`; advisory-only Q10 `deriveFastPathCivilitySignal`; frozen `ARGUMENT_ENTRY_COMPOSER_COPY`). New hook `useEntryComposerSubmit.ts` (duplicates the ArgumentComposer session-dispatch sequence WITHOUT editing that pinned file; the network payload is the SAME shipped builder so it cannot drift). New view `ArgumentEntryComposer.tsx` (KeyboardAvoidingView bottom bar; fast-path Send posts directly with NO pre-send review sheet - the deterministic engine + evidence hard-block remain the sole inline gate; Source + More both open the shipped dock unchanged, where the pre-send review still lives; observer / seatless viewers get a calm read-only Join-to-reply state; 44px targets, voice slot 56 + disabled, color never the only signal, reduce-motion-gated send press; ZERO audio-API import). The source-attach slot is labeled Source (NOT the word proof: proof is a shipped verdict ban-list token and evidence is not proof). Flag wiring rides the ROOM-001 threaded `room_exchange_v2` boolean - NO new plumbing, NO featureFlags import under `src/features`; App.tsx (additive only) mounts the bar at the bottom of the room View when the flag is ON (co-activates with the ROOM-001 rail; top rail vs bottom bar - geometry disjoint) and suppresses the collapsed strip (`onComposerExpand` undefined) so the bar is the primary compose surface; flag OFF leaves the dock-primary composer byte-identical (bar subtree unmounted). **Absorbs #830** (every modifier lives only behind More; a reply submits with More never opened) and **#832** (More popout sheet <720 / right panel >=720 via the shipped `resolveDockLayoutVariant`; bar renders across 390/768/1440). **ZERO edits to any uxOneOneFive-pinned composer/dock/oneBox/submit-argument file** - verified `git diff main` over the pinned paths is empty; all 5 pin/doctrine/copy suites pass with zero test-file modifications. +10 suites (`argumentEntryComposerModel`, `roomThreeByteShapeContract`, `useEntryComposerSubmit`, `argumentEntryComposerUi`, `argumentEntryComposerCopyBanList`, `roomThreeSourceScan`, `roomThreeEvidenceHardBlock`, `roomThreeFlagOff`, `roomThreeMorePanel`, `roomThreePhoneBand`) / +82 tests. **928 suites / 33220 tests passing** (1 pre-existing skip). Typecheck + lint + web:build clean. **No Anthropic/xAI/X API call, no Supabase write/read-beyond-existing, no new query, no migration, no Edge change, no service-role, no `.env` value edit, no new dependency by Claude in this card.** **Operator (to turn ON after merge):** set `EXPO_PUBLIC_ROOM_EXCHANGE_V2=true` (the SAME gate as the ROOM-001 rail; the two activate together) + rebuild/publish; rollback = unset + rebuild + straight revert. See `docs/designs/ROOM-003.md`.

Adds one thin ambient strip atop the argument room in both lenses: turn cue (your move / waiting / resting / watching) - open points count - receipts owed count - visibility badge (Public/Private 1:1, gold when private) - respondent-seat-open segment, with a 3-inline + `+N` overflow horizontal scroll. **Pure read-only projection.** New pure model `src/features/arguments/room/argumentStateRailModel.ts` (`deriveArgumentStateRail` over PRIMITIVES already computed in the room - `openPointCount`, `receiptsOwedCount`, `turnLabel`, `visibility`, `opponentSeatIsOpen`; chip taxonomy turn/open_points/receipts_owed/visibility/seat/reserved-saved_recordings; reserved chime-in/watching inputs render nothing per the scope-reality audit) - imports NOTHING from the data/mediator/evidence layers (read-only source scan proves no client, no query, no mutation, no board deriver, so the single-derivation house rule is safe by construction). Mounts in the `RoomBoardLayout` topBanner slot, composed ABOVE the existing microMoment banner (preserved verbatim); flag OFF => the rail subtree is never mounted (byte-identical room). Deep-links are in-app state jumps only (never `Linking.openURL`/route): open_points -> `setMode('timeline')`, receipts_owed -> the Disagreement Points analysis surface, visibility/seat -> the room-details panel. **Partial #681 absorption** (visibility badge + respondent-seat-open shipped; watching + N-of-3 chime-in counts DEFERRED - no in-room source, rendering them would be a new query out of the read-only scope; reserved as optional inputs). **No Anthropic/xAI/X API call, no Supabase write/read-beyond-existing, no new query, no migration, no Edge change, no service-role, no `.env` value edit by Claude in this card.** +4 suites (`argumentStateRailModel`, `ArgumentStateRail`, `argumentStateRailCopy`, `argumentStateRailFlagOff`) / +57 tests. **918 suites / 33135 tests passing** (1 pre-existing skip). Typecheck + lint + web:build clean. Zero pin relaxations (13 boundary/doctrine suites green unmodified). **Operator (to turn ON after merge):** set `EXPO_PUBLIC_ROOM_EXCHANGE_V2=true` + rebuild/publish; rollback = unset + rebuild. Two-tap proof at BOTH the model level (unit) and the rendered level (RNTL: one tap emits 'private', confirm emits 'public' once; sheet-analog harness records 'private' after one tap). Barrel kept supabase-free (the recents hook/api are imported by App.tsx from their own module paths, not re-exported). **914 suites / 33078 tests passing (+9 suites / +100 tests vs 905 / 32978; 1 skipped pre-existing)**, typecheck + lint clean, `npm run web:build` exit 0 (sheet + wiring reach the Netlify bundle). **2026-07-08 - HOME-001 (#874) - ArgumentHome ("Your table") resume-first landing behind the home_v2 flag (UI/model only, flag DEFAULT OFF, ZERO behavior change until flipped, no migration/deploy/AI):** PR 03 of the Argument Surface Pivot (M-ASP-1). Replaces gallery-as-landing with a resume-first "Your table" surface, gated by the shipped `home_v2` flag (`EXPO_PUBLIC_HOME_V2`, ASP-FLAGS-001). **Slice 1 (pure model, `src/features/debates/conversationGalleryModel.ts`):** hoists the three `latestPostAuthor` display literals into an exported frozen `LATEST_AUTHOR_LABEL` (byte-identical emitted output); adds `isWaitingOnViewer` (joined + open + moveCount>0 + latest move by a known other voice), `YOUR_TURN_HINT_RANK`, and `deriveYourTurn(cards, opts)` (filter to waiting-on-me -> fixture exclusion -> deterministic rank hint-verb -> unread -> recency -> debateId). Reads only structural turn-state/recency/unread signals - never heat/popularity (doctrine 2/3). **Closes the `[reseed-...]` fixture gap:** adds a `reseed` alternative to patterns 1+2 of BOTH `SUFFIX_TAG_PATTERNS` (gallery) and `BOT_SEED_TAG_PATTERNS` (`botRoomPolicyModel`) in lockstep - the `\b`-anchored inner `seed-` alternative never caught `reseed`; re-verified against the real `reseed-<pack>-<yyyymmdd>-<hash8>` runTag shape, lockstep parity fixture extended, regex re-proven in-test. **Slice 2 (surface, new `src/features/home/`):** pure `homeModel.ts` (`buildArgumentHomeViewModel` + `collectFixtureDebateIds` over RAW `debate.title` + `collectUnreadDebateIds`); `ArgumentHome.tsx` re-runs the PURE `buildConversationGalleryCards` + dedupe on already-loaded inputs (NO new query) and renders your-turn strip -> ongoing cards -> +Start CTA -> floor door -> activity module (bounded `NotificationRow` reuse); `ArgumentCard.tsx` opponent-forward row (avatar+name first, awaited excerpt, mini timeline, one entry-hint verb, static gold ring reduce-motion-safe). All home copy in a new frozen `HOME_COPY` (gameCopy) wired into the COPY-SYSTEM ban-list scan. a11y floors: 44px targets, role/label/state on every Pressable, verb text is the color-independent signal, 390px band via `useHeaderBreakpoint`. **Slice 3 (App.tsx wiring):** the ONE flag read (`isHomeV2Enabled()`), `galleryLane` widened to `... | 'home'` with initial value `homeV2Enabled ? 'home' : 'all'`, landing block split (`galleryLane === 'home' && homeV2Enabled` mounts ArgumentHome; existing gallery block guarded by `galleryLane !== 'home'` so the two never co-render); `onOpen` reuses the exact gallery `onSelect` hand-off so the `entryHint` threads into the room shell (J2 resume in <=2 taps). featureFlags consumer guard relaxed to a positive allowlist (App.tsx sole importer; feature/component tree stays clean). Flag-off byte-identical proof: accessor drives the initial lane, App.tsx reversibility wiring source-scanned, flag-on ArgumentHome subtree carries zero gallery chrome; existing `galleryLaneFilter` + `HeaderNavigation.*` suites green with zero deltas. **905 suites / 32978 tests passing (+4 suites / +61 tests vs 901 / 32917)**, typecheck + lint clean, `npm run web:build` exit 0 (App.tsx + static flag read reach the Netlify bundle). No Anthropic / xAI / X API call; no Supabase write; no service-role; no migration; no Edge Function; no new dependency; no `.env` value edit. **Operator (to turn ON after merge):** set `EXPO_PUBLIC_HOME_V2=true` in the Netlify site env + rebuild/publish (the ASP-FLAGS-001 / #776 static-inlining pattern); rollback = unset + rebuild.
