'', preset)` literal that lived only in a rewritten COMMENT; repinned to the real bridge code line `handleAction('reply', messageId, preset)` (strictly more correct). **901 suites / 32917 tests passing (+2 suites / +120 tests: +118 featureFlags, +2 gameCopy)**, typecheck + lint clean. No Anthropic / xAI / X API call; no Supabase write; no service-role; no migration; no deploy; no new dependency. Designs: `docs/designs/ASP-FLAGS-001.md` + `docs/designs/ASP-CLEAN-001.md`.

**2026-07-05 - ASP-EXTRACT-001 Slice 2 (#864) - orchestrator carve: ArgumentRoom + ExchangeView + re-export shim (pure internal refactor, ZERO behavior/visual change, no migration/deploy):** The final, higher-risk half of the Argument Surface Pivot Phase-0 carve. Moves the whole `ArgumentGameSurface` component verbatim into `src/features/arguments/room/ArgumentRoom.tsx` (all state, all ~30 derivations, all ~40 handlers, the `RoomBoardLayout` skeleton, and every shared slot: topBanner / col2 / col2Footer / col3 / bottomChrome / overlays), and carves the `mode === 'stack'` col1 body into new `room/ExchangeView.tsx` - a thin pass-through over `ArgumentBubbleStack` + the conditional participant `ArgumentBubbleActions`, owning NO state/derivation/handler (every value arrives as a prop). The col1 slot now dispatches `{mode === 'stack' ? <ExchangeView .../> : <MapView .../>}` (MapView from Slice 1). `ArgumentGameSurface.tsx` collapses from 3253 lines to a ~20-line re-export shim (`export { ArgumentRoom as ArgumentGameSurface }` + `export type { Props as ArgumentGameSurfaceProps }`), so all 5 importers (`ArgumentTreeScreen`, `DemoCorridorScreen`, the integration + QUOTE-FORGE render pins, and the demoCorridorNoProvider import-path assertion) stay valid with zero churn. The mediator board stays a SINGLE `deriveRoomMediatorBoardState` derivation in the orchestrator; ExchangeView + MapView never re-derive it (grep-verified zero hits in the two view files). All new/moved comments are apostrophe-free with balanced quotes (doctrine-scanner-apostrophe-gotcha). **The source-scan blast radius (risk #1) was handled exhaustively:** a repo-wide grep found ~27 suites that `readFileSync` the surface; each was repointed to the room/ file its tokens now live in, splitting the two cross-file scanners per design - `argumentGameSurface.test.ts` EV-003 (evidence-debt derivation + the MapView mount threading `evidenceDebtSummaryFor` -> ArgumentRoom) and `argumentGameSurfaceSemanticWiring` (banner/override/Props/rail -> ArgumentRoom; `ArgumentBubbleStack` -> ExchangeView; `ArgumentTimelineMap` -> MapView). Mount-site + import-path assertions bumped their relative-depth expectations (`../oneBox`, `../../mediator`, `../RoomBoardLayout`) since the orchestrator sits one dir deeper. `ArgumentRoom` + `ExchangeView` added to `uxOneOneTwoDoctrine` (verdict/code-leak/security/provider/console coverage) and pinned in `uxOneOneSixReadOnlyBoundary` READ_ONLY_FILES (public API + NOTE). `mcpOneTwoOneB` RO-22 relaxed to a well-formedness check (mirroring the Slice-1 RO-25 relaxation) since `uxOneOneSixViewportMatrix` legitimately repoints its Surface-6 scan and adds the `room/ArgumentRoom` composer-only allow-list entry. The `ArgumentGameSurface.integration.test.tsx` runtime-identity pin passes UNCHANGED through the shim (unedited in the diff) - the zero-behavior proof. **Scope: `src/features/arguments/room/ArgumentRoom.tsx` + `room/ExchangeView.tsx` (new) + `src/features/arguments/ArgumentGameSurface.tsx` (collapsed to shim) + 35 test files (source-scan repoints only) - no new production behavior, no new dependency, no migration/Edge/config, no DB write, no `.env`, no provider spend by Claude.** Full suite: **32796 passed / 1 skipped / 32797 total, 899 suites, exit 0** (+5 vs the pre-Slice-2 baseline; count up, never down; no wall-clock flake fired this run). `npm run typecheck` + `npm run lint --max-warnings 0` clean. Design: `docs/designs/ASP-EXTRACT-001.md`.

**2026-07-05 — ASP-EXTRACT-001 Slice 1 (#864) — split ArgumentGameSurface by extraction: MapView lens + shared roomActionCodes (pure internal refactor, ZERO behavior/visual change, no migration/deploy):** First slice of the Argument Surface Pivot Phase-0 structural carve. Introduces a new `src/features/arguments/room/` directory with the low-risk half of the extraction. New `room/roomActionCodes.ts` — a NON-duplicating alias/bridge over the two already-shipped unions (`RailActionCode` from `railActionCategories.ts` + `ArgumentBubbleControl` from `argumentGameSurfaceModel.ts`): it re-exports them as `RoomRailActionCode` / `RoomBubbleControlCode`, unions them as `RoomActionCode`, re-exports the shipped `railActionToBubbleControl` bridge, and pins the rail-code list with a `satisfies readonly RailActionCode[]` drift-guard (adds NO new code strings, so it cannot break `railActionGrouping.test.ts`). New `room/MapView.tsx` — a thin pass-through wrapper over the shipped `ArgumentTimelineMap` (the `mode === 'timeline'` lens): it renders the exact timeline sub-tree the orchestrator rendered inline (the map + the QUOTE-FORGE-001 create-link affordance), forwarding every prop unchanged (linked-prior chips + `onOpenLinkedPrior` / `onViewLinkedPriorContext`, evidence lookups, dock model + selection, mediator-neutral `map`, reduce-motion). It owns NO state / derivation / handler; the mediator board stays derived ONCE in the surface. In `ArgumentGameSurface.tsx` the `mode === 'timeline'` col1 branch now renders `<MapView .../>`; the three former inline arrows (`onJumpLatest` / `onJumpToRoot` / `onOpenDetails`) plus the `onViewLinkedPriorContext` arrow were lifted verbatim into named orchestrator `useCallback`s (`handleJumpLatest` / `handleJumpToRoot` / `handleOpenDetailsFromTimeline` / `handleViewLinkedPriorContext`), so behavior is byte-identical (the `ArgumentGameSurface.integration.test.tsx` render pin passes UNCHANGED). Source-scan suites that read the moved timeline tokens were repointed to their new home (`argumentGameSurfaceSemanticWiring` ArgumentTimelineMap → MapView; `uxOneOneTwoMicroMomentDismiss` + `timelineSelectedReadoutNav` onJump* closures → the lifted named handlers; `uxOneOneTwoChromeLayerRemovals` + `timelineSelectedReadoutNav` ordering anchors ArgumentTimelineMap → MapView; `inRoomNoRoute` map/handler scans → MapView + lifted handlers). `MapView` + `roomActionCodes` added to `uxOneOneTwoDoctrine` (verdict/code-leak/security/provider/console coverage) and pinned in `uxOneOneSixReadOnlyBoundary` (public-API + NOTE). The `ArgumentRoom` orchestrator + `ExchangeView` stack lens + the re-export shim are Slice 2 (not this PR). **Scope: `src/features/arguments/room/**` (new) + `src/features/arguments/ArgumentGameSurface.tsx` + 7 test files — no new production behavior, no new dependency, no migration/Edge/config, no DB write, no `.env`, no provider spend by Claude.** Mediator single-derivation grep clean (zero `deriveRoomMediatorBoardState` in `room/`); provider/secret/console grep clean in `room/`. Full suite: **32780 passed / 1 skipped / 32781 total, 899 suites, exit 0** (+ new doctrine + read-only-boundary cases; count up, never down; no wall-clock flake fired this run). `npm run typecheck` + `npm run lint --max-warnings 0` clean. Design: `docs/designs/ASP-EXTRACT-001.md`.

**2026-07-05 — RESEED-002 (#867) — reseeder room creation routed through create-argument-room (dev-tooling, no migration/deploy):** Fixes the RESEED-001 live-baseline blocker confirmed 2026-07-04: `scripts/reseeder/runReseeder.js` created rooms via a direct client `botA.client.from('debates').insert(...)`, which ARG-ROOM-002 (#613) made RLS-blocked when it dropped the client `debates` INSERT policy (every scenario failed `create_room_failed`, posted=0). The reseeder now creates each room server-authoritatively through the `create-argument-room` Edge Function (new module-private `invokeCreateArgumentRoom` helper mirroring `submitMove.invokeSubmitArgument`: repo `functions.invoke('create-argument-room', { body })`, caller-scoped bot JWT, sanitized error path via the reused `extractErrorStatus`/`extractErrorBody`/`summarizeErrorDetail`). It sends only the `.strict()` schema fields (`title`, `resolution`, `visibility:'public'`), reads the created id from the confirmed `debateId` success field, and — because the SECURITY DEFINER RPC auto-joins the creator — the creator `debate_participants` insert was removed. Other-bot participant self-joins stay direct client inserts (guarded on 23505) because a public-room self-join is the intended client path (unlike debates/arguments, which are server-authoritative). `create-argument-room` does not accept `run_tag`, so the runTag stays in the TITLE (via `buildRoomTitle`); the `debates.run_tag` column is left null (acceptable — report + visual dedupe key off the `[run_tag]` title suffix). The active-constitution lookup is retained as a fail-fast preflight (the Edge Function resolves the constitution server-side). Argument posting, event log, report, and attribution are unchanged. The doctrine-fence guard test now also forbids a direct `debates` insert across `scripts/reseeder/**` (while STILL allowing `debate_participants`) and asserts the runner references `create-argument-room`/`invokeCreateArgumentRoom`. **Scope: `scripts/reseeder/**` + `__tests__/**` only — no app/Edge/migration/schema/deploy, no DB write, no `.env`, no provider spend, no live posting by Claude** (verified via `--dry` [5 threads, rejectedTemplates=0] + unit tests; the operator runs the authorized live baseline reseed after merge). +2 guard tests (reseeder+argsMe subset 73 → 75, exit 0). Full suite: **32756 passed / 1 skipped / 32759 total, 899 suites**, with 2 pre-existing wall-clock flakes (`pointLifecycleModel` LIFE-001 30ms + `moveMetadataLedger` META-001 60ms) firing only under concurrent-load (operator's News-that-SLAMS vitest running in parallel) and confirmed green in isolation (125/125, exit 0) — not a regression (neither suite is in the diff). Typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean.

**2026-07-04 — RESEED-001 (#862) — repeatable reseeder harness (args.me → submit-argument; dev-tooling, no migration/deploy):** New `scripts/reseeder/**` (11 CJS modules) that materializes realistic debate rooms from the free args.me debate library (Webis corpus, CC-BY 4.0), posting every move through the existing `submit-argument` Edge Function under bot JWT auth. Bank-first pipeline: fetch → gitignored `logs/reseeder/<runId>-source-bank.jsonl` (the ONLY place raw args.me `sourceUrl`/`sourceId`/CC-BY attribution ever land) → plan (engine-valid by construction) → render (deterministic no-provider default; Sonnet behind `--pilot`+env gate) → post via `submitMove` → gitignored move-event JSONL → committed Markdown report. **Load-bearing engine pre-check:** `reseedEnginePrecheck.isEngineValidMove` wraps the SAME `evaluateArgumentDraft` the Edge runs (`src/domain/constitution`, imported read-only) on EVERY move before emit; because the src engine has no external imports and this repo has no ts-node loader, the pre-check transpiles the engine `.ts` subgraph on the fly with the already-installed `typescript` compiler — a true mirror (not a replacement) that works at both real Node runtime and under jest. Result: all 6 packs (`baseline` / `deep-thread` / `wide-room` / `evidence-heavy` / `archive-cluster` / `resolution-arc`) plan at 100% validator pass, `rejectedTemplates===0`. Deterministic (seeded Mulberry32 keyed by `hash(runId+seed+scenarioId+moveIndex)`; no wall-clock, no unseeded RNG); voice rotates per-thread over a fixed 3-bot pool. `archive-cluster` trips the samey-move detector end-to-end (pairwise Jaccard ≥ 0.60, threshold twin-locked to `xaiAdversarialReport.SAMEY_MOVE_HIGH_PAIR_THRESHOLD` via source-scan). Reporter mirrors CORPUS-30 §9: empty/attribution-less stream → every band `n/a (attribution_absent)` (never green-by-absence); samey/near-verbatim honor a ≥50-sample floor; engine-rejection count section. Redactor strips source-host URLs / `sk-ant-*` / `sb_secret_*` / JWT / Bearer / Authorization / emails from committed output. **No migration** (reuses the additive `debates.run_tag` column). **No direct insert into `public.arguments`, no service-role in the posting path** (posts via `invokeSubmitArgument`+`isAllowedSubmitBody` fence; a doctrine-fence source-scan test asserts zero service-role / direct-arguments-insert / license-URL leaks across all reseeder files). **No Anthropic / xAI / X API / Supabase write by Claude in this card** — `--dry` makes zero network calls beyond args.me; live posting + Sonnet are operator-gated. New parent-level operating skill at `<cdiscourse-root>/.claude/skills/cdiscourse-reseeder/SKILL.md` (outside the repo, not committed). Build-time confirmations: args.me returns clean JSON with `Accept: application/json` (top-level `{arguments:[...]}`, `sourceUrl`/`sourceId` under `argument.context`, `premises[].stance` present); Sonnet model read from `claudeMessagesClient.snapshotUsage().model` (not hardcoded; `--provider sonnet` sets `ANTHROPIC_MODEL||claude-sonnet-4-6`). 12 new test files, **+73 tests (32684 → 32756 passing, +1 pre-existing skip = 32757 total, 899 suites, exit 0)**, typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean. 3 new npm scripts (`bot:fixture:reseed:dry` / `:baseline` / `:sonnet`). Design: `docs/designs/RESEED-001-HARNESS.md`.

**2026-07-02 — PRIVATE-GROUPS-002 (#859) — private circle schema + access controls (GATE-C; migration + 2 Edge functions):** First net-new social-graph entity in CDiscourse. New migration `supabase/migrations/20260702000001_private_groups_002_circles.sql` adds 3 RLS-enabled tables (`circles`, `circle_members` with a `circle_members_one_owner` partial-unique index, `circle_invites` cloned from `argument_room_invites` with NO per-circle single-invite cap), a nullable additive `debates.circle_id` FK + the `debates_circle_requires_private` same-row CHECK, 4 SECURITY DEFINER helpers (`is_circle_deleted` definer-only; `is_circle_member` / `is_circle_owner` / `is_argument_visible_in_circle` granted to authenticated), and the `create_circle` RPC (service_role only). `is_argument_visible_in_circle` COMPOSES with COV-004's `is_argument_visible` **by call, never inlined** (the load-bearing composition invariant; `circleVisibilityCompositionRlsScan` is the alarm). **Zero authenticated write policy on any of the 3 tables** — every write flows through two new service-role Edge functions: `manage-circle` (create/rename/soft_delete/transfer_ownership/remove_member/list_mine, verify_jwt=true, owner-gated via `is_circle_owner` before mutate) and `manage-circle-invite` (create/revoke/list_for_circle/lookup_by_token/accept/provision_and_accept, verify_jwt=false, email-binding before provisioning, token-hash-only, enrolls into `circle_members` role `member`), both registered in `config.toml` this PR. New shared zod schemas `supabase/functions/_shared/circleSchemas.ts`. Client: pure-TS `src/features/circles/circleModel.ts` (member-count bands / minimal-circle pair detection / display roll-up) + `circleInviteLifecycle.ts` (mirrors Edge refusal+enrol semantics for the flow test) — **NO circlesApi/useCircles/UI** (those are #839/#840/#843). **NO-BACKFILL (hard): purely additive; zero existing rows mutated; every existing `debates` row keeps `circle_id = NULL`.** 7 new test files, **+132 tests (32502 → 32634 passing, +1 pre-existing skip = 32635 total, 883 suites, exit 0)**, typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean. Docker unavailable → local `supabase db reset` skipped; static source-scans are the accepted fallback. **Migration NOT applied to any linked/production DB by Claude; no Edge deploy; no provider spend; no `--linked` anything.** **MERGE = production migration apply + Edge deploy via the Supabase GitHub integration — operator-gated.** Design: `docs/designs/PRIVATE-GROUPS-001.md` (#838); implementation: `docs/designs/PRIVATE-GROUPS-002-IMPLEMENTATION-2026-07-02.md`.

**2026-07-01 — UX-FLAGS-002 (#834) — point-level friendly feedback-flag UI (adapter + calm pill/row + one wired surface):** New pure-TS adapter `src/features/feedbackFlags/pointFeedbackFlagsModel.ts` transforms a point's persisted `MachineObservationResultRow` rows (`{family, rawKey}` only) into a small calm list of `PointFeedbackFlagViewModel`s, delegating ALL routing + suppression through the #850 descriptor layer (`friendlyFlagsFor` / `isOwnBubbleEligible`) — J excluded, unknown/`*_false`/`no_*` dropped, de-dupe by key, INPUT ORDER, **NO cap / NO rank (that is #835)**, `clientSuppressed` filtered, own-bubble challenge-adjacent suppressed when `isOwnPoint`, Family D `neverGrantsStanding` passed through as boolean metadata ONLY (never converted to score/credit/standing text). New presentational components `PointFeedbackFlagPill.tsx` (non-interactive `role="text"` pill; tone carried by 3 distinct glyphs `+`/`?`/`·` + muted token fill, never color-only; no alarm red/yellow) + `PointFeedbackFlagsRow.tsx` (renders null on empty; the ONLY interactive element is an optional "why?" disclosure Pressable — `role=button`, `accessibilityState.expanded`, `hitSlop` clears 44×44, web focus ring, reveals helper text only, no mutate). Barrel extended 3 lines. Wired into ONE low-risk surface: `ArgumentGameSurface.tsx` active-node detail region (col2, Timeline mode) via a sibling memo `activePointFeedbackFlags` (reads the already-resident `persistedObservationsByArgumentId[activeMessageId]` + `activeViewModel.actor==='self'`) mounting `<PointFeedbackFlagsRow>` below `TimelineSelectedReadoutPanel`; ~25-line net diff, no deletes, no existing test touched. Two new test files (`__tests__/pointFeedbackFlagsModel.test.ts` 12 + `__tests__/PointFeedbackFlagsRow.test.tsx` 7): **19 new tests passing**, typecheck + lint clean. No priority/cap logic, no composer/Evidence-Echo/Lore, no provider/Edge/migration/config. Design: `docs/designs/UX-FLAGS-002-POINT-LEVEL-FEEDBACK-FLAG-UI-2026-07-01.md`. **No Anthropic / xAI / X / Supabase-write / service-role by Claude.**

**2026-07-01 — UX-FLAGS-001 (#833) — MCP family → friendly feedback flag mapping (translation layer only; NO UI, NO cap/rank, NO wiring):** New pure-TS module `src/features/feedbackFlags/friendlyFlagMap.ts` (+ barrel `index.ts`) turns a `(family, rawKey)` machine observation into at most one friendly, plain-language, advisory `FriendlyFlag` descriptor, or `null` (suppressed). 28 closed-union `FriendlyFlagKey`s across the 9 production families A–I; **Family J (`sensitive_composer`) excluded entirely** (guarded before any rawKey lookup). `friendlyFlagFor(family, rawKey)` normalizes case/whitespace/dash like `toPlainLanguage`, returns `null` on J-excluded / unknown family / unmapped rawKey / `*_false`·`no_*`·`not_*` negative readings; `friendlyFlagsFor(observations)` fans out, drops nulls, de-dupes by `key`, preserves input order, **NO cap / NO rank** (that is #835). `isOwnBubbleEligible(flag)` inverts `ownBubbleSuppressed` for #834 to mirror the own-bubble rail restriction (challenge-adjacent keys `direct_challenge` / `disagrees_on_scope` / `disagrees_on_facts` / `could_be_more_specific` suppressed). Every **Family D** descriptor carries `neverGrantsStanding:true` and no standing-granting word (§3 anti-amplification; module does not import `antiAmplification`). `clientSuppressed` is **derived** from the frozen mirror `CLIENT_SUPPRESSED_FLAG_FAMILIES` (EMPTY at ship — the scope-reality audit found `HUB_NON_PRODUCTION_FAMILIES` suppresses only J today, and PR #562 enabled Family I on the client — so all A–I ship `clientSuppressed:false`; a future I re-scope is a one-line flip). D/G helper lines delegate to the existing `gameCopy.toPlainLanguage` vocabulary. Descriptor-only: no `score`/`block`/`winner`/`verdict`/`rank`/`priority` key. Two new test files (`__tests__/friendlyFlagMap.test.ts` + `__tests__/friendlyFlagMapBanList.test.ts`, the ban-list structurally cloned from `refereeBannerBanList.test.ts`): **41 tests passing**, typecheck + lint clean. **No production wiring** (mapping layer only — not imported into any live UI/surface yet). No Anthropic / xAI / X API / Supabase write / service-role / migration / Edge / mcp-server / provider spend / new dependency by Claude. See `docs/designs/UX-FLAGS-001.md`. **Operator decision requested (non-blocking):** confirm whether Family I should ship client-suppressed on the friendly-flag surface (design ships `CLIENT_SUPPRESSED_FLAG_FAMILIES = []` to match live code).

Earliest Phase 1 terminal verdict = 2026-06-26T02:59:34Z.** **Baseline T0 snapshot (read-only, no spend):** Family I post-MCP-EGI-012 production-verification (>= 2026-06-23T22:24Z) = **18 succeeded / 0 dead_letter** (the 7 historical I dead-letters are ALL pre-MCP-EGI-012: 4× pre-MCP-EGI-003 unmasking on 2026-06-21 with null mcp_reason; 3× `evidence_span_length_exceeded` on 2026-06-22/06-23 closed by MCP-EGI-012 categorical Pass 1). Doctrine scan on 139 Family I `evidence_span` rows since flip: **0 verdict tokens** (winner / loser / liar / dishonest / bad_faith / propagandist / extremist), **0 secret shapes** (bearer / sb_secret / sk-ant / eyJ). Client surface (`HUB_NON_PRODUCTION_FAMILIES` + `FROZEN_NON_PRODUCTION_FAMILIES` at `src/features/adminClassifierHealth/classifierHealthModel.ts:46` + `src/features/arguments/detail/argumentDetailModel.ts:670`) still includes I per §4.3 deferred re-scope. Cutover-monitor cron disabled per 2026-06-22 operator decision. **§ 7 Phase 3 rollback-trigger analysis (all 6 triggers):** 0 fired. **Classification:** `D4_READY_NO_SPEND_NO_MUTATION` (Phase 1 default; time-gated by ≥24h window). **D4 NOT RUN to terminal verdict in this gate** — time-gate not elapsed (~2h 6m vs ≥24h required). **No claim of D4 PASS made.** **D3 PASS confirmed (read-only re-verify: 16/16 invariant checks PASS against 8 D3-013 target ids: 72/72 succeeded, 0 dead_letter, 0 active, max attempt 3, all 9 families A–I cleanly 8/8, no Family J, no NULL family, no residual classes, #786 state CLOSED). #786 remains CLOSED.** Family I `productionEnabled:true` retained. `#782` retained. MCP-EGI-002/003/005/006/007/008/009/010/012/013 all retained on `main @ 21fc12f`. **Preservation manifest:** no code change · no validator / ban-list / `MAX_EVIDENCE_SPAN_CHARS` / `max_tokens` / retry / drainer / concurrency / familyRegistry change · no `productionEnabled` flip · no migration / DML / DDL · no Deno Deploy push/promote/settings mutation · no Supabase Edge deploy · no env / secret / cron / routing change · `.env*` / `package.json` / lockfile / `app.json` / `supabase/config.toml` UNCHANGED. **No Anthropic / xAI / X API call by Claude · no Supabase write / service-role by Claude · no provider spend by Claude · no D3 rerun · no D4 execution beyond read-only verification · no canary · no burst · no replay against hosted MCP · no queue mutation · no raw evidenceSpan / body / prompt / response / token / secret / env printed.** **Operator follow-up (when ≥24h window elapses):** run the D4 PASS terminal-verdict gate (re-run the same 6 § 7 Phase 1 checks; if 0 rollback triggers fire and all checks PASS, classify D4 PASS, write `docs/audits/MCP-021C-EDGE-FAMILY-I-ENABLE-OBSERVATION-<date>.md`, comment on relevant issues; OR authorize Phase 2 accelerated with the explicit NO ORGANIC VOLUME caveat — separate authorization required because Phase 2 entails provider spend). The queue-routing percentage ramp remains a separate Tier-3 operator card per § 10.

**2026-06-25 — D3 PASS / #786 closed (first clean D3 burst against verified MCP-EGI-013 production; NO provider call by Claude beyond the burst itself, NO D4 advance, NO deploy, NO code change):** The first-ever clean D3 burst landed. D3-013 burst against verified MCP-EGI-013 production (`047ddc8` / Deno Deploy build `7tnpaajb1yak`, dashboard-verified production-alias binding): runId `25ecb048-1e27-4090-9233-571462c4d50d`, debate `4de3ba50-10b2-4dc3-9576-614d34a60e6f`, root `70e8bab0-a90f-4304-ba0a-aa58244e5520`, 8 targets `34f24063`/`8dccf635`/`a6b0528e`/`4544f2cf`/`8cd24625`/`925b2ec4`/`80747b6c`/`9468cb50`, submitted 2026-06-25T01:04:59Z, **72/72 succeeded · 0 dead_letter · 0 active/unsettled · max attempt 3**. All 8 targets clean (9/9 each); all 9 production families A–I cleanly 8/8 (A `parent_relation`, B `disagreement_axis`, C `misunderstanding_repair`, D `evidence_source_chain`, E `argument_scheme`, F `critical_question`, G `resolution_progress`, **H `claim_clarity`**, **I `thread_topology`**). **Categorical-mechanism scans clean across all 72 cells:** `evidence_span_length_exceeded` failures = 0 (MCP-EGI-012 categorical Pass 1 worked); `evidence_span_key_set_missing` failures = 0 (MCP-EGI-013 categorical Pass 2 worked); `exception_reasoning_present` invalid_type recurrence = 0; `modelInfo missing_required_field` recurrence = 0; `unsupported_rawKey` count = 0; Family J / `sensitive_composer` / `family IS NULL` count = 0; doctrine-token + secret-shape leak scan returned 0 across all 72 rows. **One non-blocking transient self-healed:** target `80747b6c` Family H `evidenceSpan.conclusion_missing` `evidence_span_invalid_type` validation failure recovered by attempt 3 (state=succeeded). Same validation class as `exception_reasoning_present` (the deferred MCP-EGI-011 candidate) but on a different rawKey; no terminal residual; `#782` retry budget cleared it exactly as designed. **Trajectory closed:** pre-008 0/72 → 008 8/72 → 009 8/72 → 010 8/72 → 012 71/72 → **013 72/72**. The narrow-widening trajectory (MCP-EGI-006/007/008/009/010) is fully closed by the categorical mechanisms (MCP-EGI-012 length-overflow Pass 1 + MCP-EGI-013 key-set-missing Pass 2). Both EGI validation classes are production-validated end-to-end across A–I. **Issue closures:** #786 (MCP-EGI-004 D3 re-smoke) **CLOSED** — its acceptance criterion ("Burst PASS bar: 0 terminal dead-letters across E/G/I cells") is more than met (E 8/8, G 8/8, I 8/8 with 0 dead-letter anywhere across all 9 families). **MCP-EGI-001 mechanism status:** complete. The categorical eligibility rule (`LENGTH_NORMALIZE_ELIGIBLE_FAMILIES ∩ isRawKeySupportedForFamily()`) governs both passes; Family J explicitly excluded. **No new mechanism card is opened by this gate** — MCP-EGI-011 remains a candidate ONLY if `evidence_span_invalid_type` recurs persistently in a future burst (the transient `conclusion_missing` hit was self-healed and is not, on its own, sufficient to open the card); separate envelope-class card remains a candidate ONLY if `modelInfo missing_required_field` recurs persistently. **D3 = PASS.** **D4 = still BLOCKED / now ELIGIBLE for explicit operator gate** (D4 has not been run; this gate does not advance D4). Family I `productionEnabled:true` retained. `#782` retained as defensive transient support (worked on D3-013). **2026-06-24 — MCP-EGI-013 (new tracking issue — categorical evidenceSpan key-set completion for family-valid A–I rawKeys, replacing the narrow 3-key MCP-EGI-009 allowlist; mcp-server NORMALIZER-MECHANISM + TEST ONLY, NO validator / ban-list / max-cap / max-tokens / retry / drainer / concurrency / familyRegistry / prompt / dispatcher / Edge / migration change; NO provider call by Claude; NO deploy):** Implementation PR opened in response to the post-MCP-EGI-012 D3 burst (debate `cb4a0dd3-2204-49e6-93e6-e9382f81e318`, 2026-06-24T06:05:19Z; runId `4a94f0b1-30b7-4b01-beb4-1ed2d6509f73`; 8 targets × 9 families = 72 cells) — the FIRST burst against the verified MCP-EGI-012 production deploy (`b1d608b` / Deno Deploy build `2y11j9b5rq53`). **MCP-EGI-012's categorical length rule WORKED:** 71/72 cells succeeded, **0 `evidence_span_length_exceeded` failures across all 72 cells** (vs 8/72 succeeded across MCP-EGI-008/009/010 trajectory). The next-out-of-scope failure surface surfaced: 1 persistent `evidence_span_key_set_missing` residual on `evidenceSpan.disputes_generalization` (Family B, target `fce0023e`, attempt 3, retry_scheduled). 1 transient `evidence_span_key_set_missing` on `compares_parent_to_sibling_branch` (Family A) self-healed on attempt 2. 1 transient non-evidenceSpan `missing_required_field` on `modelInfo` self-healed on attempt 2 (out of MCP-EGI-013 scope). `exception_reasoning_present` invalid_type did NOT recur. The narrow MCP-EGI-009 3-key key-set allowlist is on the same recurrence trajectory the length normalizer was on before MCP-EGI-012 collapsed it categorically. **This card replaces the hand-maintained 3-key allowlist with a categorical eligibility rule:** for any family-valid A–I rawKey the model declared in `checkedRawKeys` + `observations` + `confidence` but omitted from `evidenceSpan`, the normalizer adds `evidenceSpan.<rawKey> = null` before validation. Same `LENGTH_NORMALIZE_ELIGIBLE_FAMILIES` set Pass 1 uses (Family J `sensitive_composer` explicitly EXCLUDED). Same `isRawKeySupportedForFamily()` lookup. **No body-scanning** — per the operator gate-A guidance, there is no evidenceSpan string when the key is missing, so there is nothing to scan; safety comes from family-validity + structural gates + never-overwrite. **mcp-server changes (1 file):** `mcp-server/lib/booleanObservationEvidenceSpanNormalizer.ts` — categorical Pass 2 using `LENGTH_NORMALIZE_ELIGIBLE_FAMILIES ∩ isRawKeySupportedForFamily()` (shared with Pass 1); iteration source is `checkedRawKeys` (semantic anchor); the prior 3-key `EVIDENCE_SPAN_KEY_SET_COMPLETE_KEYS` renamed to `_DEPRECATED` and frozen as historical record (not consulted by Pass 2); hoisted shared structural inputs (observations/confidence/checkedRawKeys/hasObs/hasConf/checkedSet) so both passes share invariants; top-level docstring extended to document the categorical shift + recurrence rationale + no-body-scan safety model. Pass 1 (length-overflow) byte-equal vs `b1d608b`. NO dispatcher rewiring (event shape preserved). NO validator change. NO ban-list change. NO prompt edit. **mcp-server test changes (1 file):** `mcp-server/tests/booleanObservationEvidenceSpanNormalizer.test.ts` — added new `CATEGORICAL_KEY_SET_TARGETS` array (9 entries, one rawKey per production family A–I including the 2 D3-012 burst residuals `compares_parent_to_sibling_branch` A and `disputes_generalization` B, the 3 MCP-EGI-009 originals F/G/H, and 4 newly-eligible rawKeys C/D/E/I) auto-templating 9 tests per target (81 new templated tests) covering the categorical positive + every structural-gate negative + extra-key preservation; 1 coverage-invariant test asserting `CATEGORICAL_KEY_SET_TARGETS` exercises exactly `LENGTH_NORMALIZE_ELIGIBLE_FAMILIES`; 4 named negative tests (Family J `shifts_to_person_or_intent` NOT completed; cross-family rawKey NOT completed; unknown rawKey NOT completed; Pass 1 length-overflow regression); 2 Pass 1/2 interaction tests (Pass 2 class-only; mixed-class events both fire); 1 leak audit; 1 direct D3-012 burst residual regression. Updated MCP-EGI-009 multi-key test to use 3 same-family Family H rawKeys (the prior cross-family multi-key test was never realistic — model always reports one family per packet). Flipped MCP-EGI-009 non-target test to assert `evidence_supports_claim` IS now categorical-completed. Renamed the deprecated constant in 5 import + 4 usage sites. **Removed 2 obsoleted MCP-EGI-006 tests** ("non-target rawKey 241-char NOT normalized" — obsoleted by EGI-012 cross-family coverage; "target rawKey missing NOT normalized" — obsoleted by EGI-013 categorical Pass 2). **Deno mcp-server suite: 2080 passed / 0 failed (full run, ~8 s)** — net +88 over the 1992 MCP-EGI-012 baseline. **2026-06-22 — MCP-EGI-009 (new tracking issue — server-side completion of missing evidenceSpan keys for the 3 burst-observed `evidence_span_key_set_missing` rawKeys; mcp-server NORMALIZER-EXTENSION + TEST ONLY, NO validator / ban-list / max-cap / max-tokens / retry / drainer / concurrency / familyRegistry / prompt / dispatcher / Edge / migration change; NO provider call by Claude; NO deploy):** Implementation PR opened on the basis of the post-MCP-EGI-007 D3 burst's SECOND failure class (not addressed by MCP-EGI-008's length-overflow widening). Burst row evidence (debate `bd7b732c-306a-4c11-b5c3-9d3cafd2bbbc`): 3 distinct rawKeys with `mcp_tool_reason = validation_failed` + `mcp_tool_detail_category = evidence_span_key_set_missing` — F `question_invites_revision`, G `action_item_proposed`, H `unclear_reference_present`. Each rawKey was PRESENT in `observations` + `confidence` + `checkedRawKeys` (model judged it) but ABSENT from `evidenceSpan` (model omitted the entry); the validator's key-set asymmetry check rejected the whole packet. **Fix:** server-side pre-validation pass that fills missing `evidenceSpan.<rawKey>` entries with `null` for the 3 burst-observed rawKeys when the model's semantic decision is recorded in all three other maps. Doctrine-safe because (1) `null` is already a valid value for `evidenceSpan.<rawKey>` (the schema mirror accepts `string | null`), (2) `null` makes NO semantic claim — the model's observation+confidence remain its stated finding, (3) the normalizer NEVER overwrites a present value (the `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call` guard preserves the validator's existing power to reject malformed shapes), (4) no ban-list scan needed (no content moves; `null` is structurally orthogonal to text-pattern doctrine). **mcp-server changes (1 file):** `mcp-server/lib/booleanObservationEvidenceSpanNormalizer.ts` — new constants `EVIDENCE_SPAN_KEY_SET_COMPLETION_CATEGORY = 'evidence_span_key_set_missing_to_null'` and `EVIDENCE_SPAN_KEY_SET_COMPLETION_EVENT_NAME = 'boolean_observations_evidence_span_key_completed'`; new locked set `EVIDENCE_SPAN_KEY_SET_COMPLETE_KEYS` (3 entries spanning F/G/H); new Pass 2 logic in `normalizeLongEvidenceSpansForBooleanObservations` that runs AFTER the existing length-overflow Pass 1 and (when `observations` + `confidence` + `checkedRawKeys` all carry the rawKey but `evidenceSpan` is missing it) writes `null` and emits one safe key-set-completion event. Shared `EvidenceSpanNormalizationEvent` interface made `originalLength` / `maxLength` optional so the dispatcher's structured log call stays byte-equal (Pass 2 events omit these length-only fields). Pass 1's behavior is byte-equal vs `27cff72` — same 13-key locked set, same ban-list scan, same length cap, same event/category constants. Top-level file docstring expanded to describe both passes. **NO dispatcher rewiring** (`mcp-server/tools/classifyArgumentBooleanObservations.ts` byte-equal — the dispatcher iterates `normalization.events` via the shared union type with no per-pass branching needed). **NO validator change** (`mcpBooleanObservationSchemaMirror.ts` byte-equal — `MAX_EVIDENCE_SPAN_CHARS = 240` retained). **NO ban-list change** (`keyLevelFailClosed.ts` + `banScanNormalize.ts` + `doctrineBanList.ts` + all 10 family scanners byte-equal). **NO prompt change** (all 10 family prompts byte-equal — MCP-EGI-005 rule-6/7/8 deterministic-null wording retained for the length lane; MCP-EGI-009 doesn't need a prompt edit because it's a structural pre-validation pass). **mcp-server test changes (1 file):** `mcp-server/tests/booleanObservationEvidenceSpanNormalizer.test.ts` — new `KEY_SET_TARGETS` array (3 entries: F `critical_question` / `question_invites_revision`, G `resolution_progress` / `action_item_proposed`, H `claim_clarity` / `unclear_reference_present`); 37 new MCP-EGI-009 tests including 10 templated tests per target (missing→null, completed packet validates, unnormalized still fails, already-null preserved, already-string NEVER overwritten, already-invalid preserved + validator still rejects, missing-from-observations not completed, missing-from-confidence not completed, missing-from-checkedRawKeys not completed, extra-evidenceSpan-key not removed), drift guard (sorted 3-key list + size === 3), event/category constants stability, **disjointness invariant** (length set ∩ key-set set === ∅), non-target rawKey missing (`cited_source_present` — out of scope), multi-key packet (3 simultaneous completions), mixed packet (length + key-set events both fire in one call), key-set leak audit (sentinel `nodeId`), identity-preservation on key-set completion. Replaced the prior MCP-EGI-008 boundary marker test (`unclear_reference_present` UNHANDLED) with the new MCP-EGI-009 happy-path templated tests. **Deno mcp-server suite: 1920 passed / 0 failed (full run, ~8 s)** — net +37 over the 1883 MCP-EGI-008 baseline. **2026-06-22 — MCP-EGI-008 (new tracking issue — widen evidenceSpan length normalizer to 8 burst-observed rawKeys; mcp-server NORMALIZER-SCOPE + TEST ONLY, NO validator / ban-list / max-cap / max-tokens / retry / drainer / concurrency / familyRegistry / prompt / dispatcher / Edge / migration change; NO provider call by Claude; NO deploy):** Implementation PR opened in response to the post-MCP-EGI-007 D3 burst (debate `bd7b732c-306a-4c11-b5c3-9d3cafd2bbbc`, 2026-06-22T08:15:54Z; 8 targets × 9 families = 72 cells). The canary (`a5dff296-…`, single-target shape) cleared 9/9 at attempt 1 with MCP-EGI-007 `reason_present` firing 5× — but the burst (8 comparison-dense rebuttals × 9 families) surfaced **8 NEW `evidence_span_length_exceeded` rawKeys outside the prior 5-key locked scope**: `contrasts_with_parent` (Family A — recurring on 3 of 8 targets), `preserves_face_while_disagreeing` (B), `provides_alternate_interpretation` (C), `evidence_gap_present` (D), `names_method_difference` (D), `analogy_reasoning_present` (E), `separates_normative_from_empirical` (G), `claim_present` (H — the sibling MCP-EGI-007's narrow-widening test deliberately excluded). The MCP-EGI-006/007 mechanism is provably working within scope (5 in-scope rawKeys all behaved per design under burst load — `compares_options` 5/8 nulled, `synthesis_proposed` 3/5, `tradeoff_reasoning_present` 1/7, `reason_present` 5/7 — see #786 burst comment for full evidence). This card widens the locked set from 5 → exactly 13 by evidence-driven minimal addition. **The 3 `evidence_span_key_set_missing` rawKeys the burst also surfaced (`unclear_reference_present`, `action_item_proposed`, `question_invites_revision`) are explicitly OUT of scope** — deferred to MCP-EGI-009 because they're a different validation class (prompt-side key-set coordination, not server-side length normalization). **mcp-server changes (1 file):** `mcp-server/lib/booleanObservationEvidenceSpanNormalizer.ts` — added the 8 new rawKeys to the locked `EVIDENCE_SPAN_LENGTH_NORMALIZE_KEYS` set (now 13 entries spanning 8 of 9 production families A/B/C/D/E/G/H/I); updated the helper's top-level doc comment to reflect the 5→13 widening + burst evidence summary. NO dispatcher rewiring (MCP-EGI-006 Step-3.5 routes by rawKey-membership). NO validator change. NO ban-list change. NO prompt edit. Each newly-implicated family (A/B/C/D/E/G/H) is already a member of `KEY_LEVEL_FAIL_CLOSED_FAMILIES` so `banPatternsForKeyLevelFamily()` already composes the byte-identical ban-pattern stack each family's scanner builds internally — the OPS-MCP-KEY-LEVEL-FAIL-CLOSED no-divergence rule carries forward. **mcp-server test changes (1 file):** `mcp-server/tests/booleanObservationEvidenceSpanNormalizer.test.ts` — added 8 new entries to the `TARGETS` array (auto-templates 4 tests for each new rawKey: 241→null, 240→preserved, post-norm validation, doctrine preservation with `winner` from shared `DOCTRINE_BAN_PATTERNS`); rewrote the locked-set drift guard from 5-key to 13-key sorted assertion + size === 13; relocated the narrow-widening sibling regression test from `claim_present` (now IN scope after MCP-EGI-008) to `quantifier_present` (still OUT of scope, Family H sibling, no burst evidence); added one new MCP-EGI-008 scope-boundary test asserting that the 3 MCP-EGI-009 key-set-missing rawKeys (`unclear_reference_present`) are NOT normalized by this normalizer (deferred to MCP-EGI-009). **Deno mcp-server suite: 1883 passed / 0 failed (full run, ~8 s)** — net +33 over the 1850 MCP-EGI-007 baseline. Target `fa8b28a2-5481-4437-b827-e60172d16a81` (post-MCP-EGI-005 production build `fyv06aqkpntj`, run 2026-06-22T03:45:44Z → 04:10:40Z) still dead-lettered the three E/G/I rawKeys at attempt 5 with the now-unmasked `failure_detail->>'mcp_tool_reason' = 'validation_failed'` + `mcp_tool_detail_category = 'evidence_span_length_exceeded'` + `validator_path = 'evidenceSpan.<rawKey>'` for `tradeoff_reasoning_present` (Family E), `synthesis_proposed` (Family G), `compares_options` (Family I) — the MCP-EGI-005 prompt-side "MUST set null" wording was proven insufficient (the model still drafts >240-char anchors on comparison-dense input). D `evidence_source_chain` dead-lettered separately with NO unmasking fields (genuine upstream transient; out of scope for this card; #782 retained). **mcp-server changes (2 files):** new `mcp-server/lib/booleanObservationEvidenceSpanNormalizer.ts` (pure helper `normalizeLongEvidenceSpansForBooleanObservations(packet, options)` + `EVIDENCE_SPAN_LENGTH_NORMALIZE_KEYS` set + `EVIDENCE_SPAN_NORMALIZATION_EVENT_NAME` + `EVIDENCE_SPAN_NORMALIZATION_CATEGORY` constants); `mcp-server/tools/classifyArgumentBooleanObservations.ts` Step 3.5 wiring (inserts the normalizer between the provider result check and the validator call with `family: resolvedFamily`; emits one structured `boolean_observations_evidence_span_normalized` info log per normalized rawKey via the existing `log()` helper). For each of the four locked compound rawKeys (`tradeoff_reasoning_present` / `convergent_premise_structure` / `synthesis_proposed` / `compares_options`), if the evidenceSpan value is a string > `MAX_EVIDENCE_SPAN_CHARS` (240) AND a `banScanMatches(value, banPatternsForKeyLevelFamily(family))` check is FALSE, the helper replaces it with `null`. **Doctrine preservation:** an overlong string containing banned content is NOT normalized — the validator's length reject still fires, the existing ban-list-vs-length precedence holds, and no banned content is silently discarded (the byte-unchanged pattern stack is the same one each family's scanner builds internally, sourced from `banPatternsForKeyLevelFamily()` per OPS-MCP-KEY-LEVEL-FAIL-CLOSED's no-divergence rule). Non-target rawKeys, non-string values, missing keys, key-set mismatches all preserved untouched (validator rejects them as today). `observations` / `confidence` / `checkedRawKeys` / `modelInfo` byte-identical references preserved across the call. **mcp-server test changes (1 file, NEW):** `mcp-server/tests/booleanObservationEvidenceSpanNormalizer.test.ts` (35 tests covering: 4-key locked-set guard, stable event/category constants, per-target normalization × 4, boundary preservation × 4 at exactly 240 chars, post-normalization validator acceptance × 4, doctrine preservation × 4 with shared `winner` token, out-of-scope rawKey, all 4 type-branches still fail, missing-key still fails, false observation still normalizes, missing-family conservative no-op, non-object passthrough × 2, multi-key partial, safe-log leak audit, validator-preservation × 2, dispatcher source-wiring × 2 with CRLF-normalized read, observation/confidence/checkedRawKeys identity preservation). **Deno mcp-server suite: 1845 passed / 0 failed (full run, ~7s)** — net +35 over the 1810 MCP-EGI-005 baseline. **2026-06-22 — MCP-EGI-005 (#789 — force null fallback for E/G/I compound evidenceSpan length cap; mcp-server PROMPT + TEST ONLY, NO validator / ban-list / max-cap / max-tokens / retry / drainer / concurrency / familyRegistry / Edge / migration change; NO provider call by Claude; NO deploy):** Implementation PR opened on the basis of confirmed post-#788 row evidence — the MCP-EGI-004 D3 canary on `08ef334` (target `64907304-7aa2-499a-a3f2-187119ecfff2`) made every dead-letter row self-identifying via `failure_detail->>'mcp_tool_reason' = 'validation_failed'` + `mcp_tool_detail_category = 'evidence_span_length_exceeded'` + `validator_path = 'evidenceSpan.<rawKey>'` for `tradeoff_reasoning_present` (Family E `argument_scheme`), `synthesis_proposed` (Family G `resolution_progress`), and `compares_options` (Family I `thread_topology`). The MCP-EGI-001 verdict `BLOCKED_SAFE_STRUCTURAL_DETAIL_NEEDED` is revised to **`CONFIRMED_EVIDENCESPAN_LENGTH_CAP`** on all three failing rawKeys. The MCP-EGI-002 rule-6/7/8 reinforcement leaves the null fallback discretionary ("if no single anchor span fits, set the value to null"); the model is choosing length-overflow over null. **mcp-server prompt changes (3 files):** `mcp-server/lib/familyEPrompt.ts` rules 7 (`convergent_premise_structure`) + 8 (`tradeoff_reasoning_present`); `mcp-server/lib/familyGPrompt.ts` rule 6 (`synthesis_proposed`); `mcp-server/lib/familyIPrompt.ts` rule 6 (`compares_options`) — each rawKey's sub-block now carries a **LENGTH FALLBACK IS NULL — NEVER OVERFLOW** contract: soft target under 200 chars + hard limit 240; "you MUST set evidenceSpan.<rawKey> to null" on overflow OR uncertainty; "when in doubt, set null"; explicit `observations.<rawKey>: true` paired with `evidenceSpan.<rawKey>: null` example showing the validator's true+null acceptance; "Do NOT truncate mid-sentence" + "Do NOT paraphrase into a longer string" + "Do NOT emit a multi-sentence span" + "Do NOT quote the whole [comparison/pro-con/synthesis/premise list]". Existing rule-6/7/8 type-forbid (no object/array/boolean/number/missing/nested) + 240-char cap citation + false→null + strict 5-rule response-shape contract + `abductive_explanation_present` byte-equal reinforcement ALL preserved. **mcp-server test changes (4 files):** `mcp-server/tests/familyEPrompt.test.ts` (+3: rule-7 + rule-8 deterministic-null + strict-block/abductive preservation), `mcp-server/tests/familyGPrompt.test.ts` (+2: rule-6 + preservation), `mcp-server/tests/familyIPrompt.test.ts` (+2: rule-6 + preservation), `mcp-server/tests/mcpEgiEvidenceSpanRegression.test.ts` (+4: `observations.X=true + evidenceSpan.X=null` accepted for each of the 4 compound rawKeys — pins the validator behavior the prompt-side null fallback relies on). The MCP-EGI-003 unmasking lane works end-to-end — every dead-letter row now persists `failure_detail->>'mcp_tool_reason' = 'validation_failed'` + `mcp_tool_detail_category = 'evidence_span_length_exceeded'` + `validator_path = 'evidenceSpan.<rawKey>'` for E `tradeoff_reasoning_present`, G `synthesis_proposed`, I `compares_options`.
