**2026-06-21 — MCP-EGI-003 (#785 — Edge unmasking of hosted-MCP `validation_failed` into safe structural `failure_detail` fields; Edge / TEST / DOC ONLY, NO mcp-server change, NO migration, NO validator / ban-list / retry / drainer / concurrency / familyRegistry / prompt change; NO provider call by Claude; NO deploy):** Implementation PR opened for MCP-EGI-003 following the **MCP-EGI-004 D3 canary FAIL** on `26fbeb1` (post-MCP-EGI-002 deploy `ahrj6fnbectc`). Target `7bf37011-fbca-4de7-b24f-a9ea6060966a`: 6/9 succeeded (A/B/C/F/H attempt 1; D attempt 3 via #782 extended budget), 3/9 dead-lettered at attempt 5 — E `argument_scheme`, G `resolution_progress`, I `thread_topology` — all `mcp_api_error` / `provider_server_error` / `retry_attempts_exhausted`. The persisted `failure_detail` did not carry the discriminator needed to pick the next lane; this card adds it. **Edge changes (5 files):** `supabase/functions/_shared/booleanObservations/booleanObservationFailureSubreason.ts` — new closed-enum `McpToolDetailCategory` (14 values: `evidence_span_length_exceeded` / `evidence_span_invalid_type` / `evidence_span_key_set_missing` / `evidence_span_key_set_extra` / `confidence_key_set_*` / `confidence_invalid_value` / `observation_invalid_value` / `observation_key_missing_from_checked` / `schema_version_mismatch` / `missing_required_field` / `flag_count_too_high` / `doctrine_ban_list` / `unknown_validation_failed`); new `ALLOWED_MCP_TOOL_REASONS` closed allowlist (9 values: `validation_failed` / `not_implemented` / `unsupported_family` / `unsupported_raw_key` / `invalid_source_subset` / `fixture_load_failed` / `key_missing` / `timeout` / `rate_limited`); pure `mcpToolDetailToCategory(detail)` mapper that NEVER returns the raw string (the `expected mcp-021.…; got <X>` shape is matched by PREFIX only so the X value is discarded); path allowlist widened from a literal set to `isAllowedDetailPath()` that accepts `<top>.<identifier>` for `top ∈ {evidenceSpan, observations, confidence}` and identifier `/^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_]{0,79}$/`; new `detailCategory` field on `BooleanObservationFailureDetail` + `FailureDetailInput`; plumbed through `buildFailureDetail`. `booleanObservationMcpAdapter.ts` — `isServerErrorEnvelope` branch now derives `detailCategory` from `extracted.detail` via the closed-enum mapper and passes it through (raw string never persisted). `classifierRunRowFailureDetail.ts` — new `mcp_tool_reason` + `mcp_tool_detail_category` fields on the persisted `RunRowFailureDetail` jsonb (snake_case for SQL); new `mcpToolReason` + `mcpToolDetailCategory` inputs; closed-allowlist enforcement via locally re-derived `RUN_ROW_ALLOWED_MCP_TOOL_*` sets (self-contained pattern preserved). `classifierDrainerCore.ts` + `classifyArgumentCore.ts` — both call sites of `buildRunRowFailureDetail` now forward the two new fields from the adapter's detail. **mcp-server UNCHANGED. No migration** (jsonb additive). **Tests:** `__tests__/mcpEgiZeroZeroThreeFailureDetailUnmasking.test.ts` (NEW, 42 tests — mapper × 14 cases, path widening × 9 cases, builder plumbing × 13 cases, leak/source scans × 3, cross-file allowlist drift × 2); `__tests__/mcpOneTwoOneCServerErrorEnvelope.test.ts:388-397` (SEV-15b updated to assert the new MCP-EGI-003 invariant: `extracted.detail` may be read but only as input to `mcpToolDetailToCategory()`, never as a `buildFailureDetail` free-text input); `_helpers/booleanObservationFailureSubreasonDeno.ts` + `_helpers/classifierRunRowFailureDetailDeno.ts` bridges extended. **Jest full suite: 32,166 passed / 1 skipped / 0 failed across 859 suites (24.5s)** — net +42 over the pre-EGI-003 baseline (32,124). `npm run typecheck` clean, `npm run lint --max-warnings 0` clean. **Preservation manifest:** validator (`validateMcpBooleanObservationResponse`) UNCHANGED · `MAX_EVIDENCE_SPAN_CHARS = 240` UNCHANGED · doctrine ban-list scanners UNCHANGED · family prompts UNCHANGED · `FAMILY_E/G/I_MAX_TOKENS = 1500` UNCHANGED · `DRAINER_*` retry policy / backoff / concurrency UNCHANGED (#782 retained) · `familyRegistry` UNCHANGED · `failure_reason='mcp_api_error'` / `failure_sub_reason='provider_server_error'` UNCHANGED (additive only) · retry-disposition logic UNCHANGED (lookups key off `subReason` which is byte-equal) · submission engine UNCHANGED · `mcp-server/**` UNCHANGED · no migrations · no env / secret / cron / routing change · `.env*` / `package.json` / lockfile / `app.json` / `supabase/config.toml` UNCHANGED. **No Anthropic / xAI / X API call by Claude · no Supabase write / service-role · no provider spend · no replay against hosted MCP · no Deno Deploy push/promote · no merge.** D3 still **FAIL** pending operator merge + Edge auto-redeploy via the Supabase GitHub integration + next operator MCP-EGI-004 canary. D4 still **BLOCKED**. Family I `productionEnabled:true` retained. **Operator follow-up:** (1) merge PR (Edge auto-deploys on merge — `classifier-drainer` + `classify-argument-boolean-observations` config.toml-registered); (2) re-run MCP-EGI-004 canary (#786 lane) against the new Edge revision; the row now distinguishes `mcp_tool_reason='validation_failed' + mcp_tool_detail_category=<category>` (prompt residual) from both fields absent (genuine provider 5xx → #409 lane). Design doc: `docs/designs/MCP-EGI-FAILURE-DETAIL-UNMASKING-2026-06-21.md`.

**2026-06-21 — MCP-EGI-004 D3 canary on `26fbeb1` (post-MCP-EGI-002 deploy `ahrj6fnbectc`) — FAIL.** Target `7bf37011-fbca-4de7-b24f-a9ea6060966a`: 6/9 succeeded (A/B/C/F/H att 1; D att 3 via #782 extended budget — exactly the case #782 was sized for), 3/9 dead-lettered at att 5 — E `argument_scheme`, G `resolution_progress`, I `thread_topology` — all `mcp_api_error` / `provider_server_error` / `retry_attempts_exhausted`. 0 `mcp_validation_failed`, 0 `unsupported_rawKey`, 0 Family J, 0 `family IS NULL`; doctrine/leak scans clean (27 result rows, 0 verdict/secret tokens, max evidence_span len 198 ≤ 240). Burst NOT RUN (canary FAIL gates burst). `failure_detail` lacks the validator-detail discriminator → cannot tell from the row whether the inner cause is (1) hosted-MCP `validation_failed` (MCP-EGI-002 prompt rewrite insufficient), (2) genuine Anthropic 5xx persisting > 26 min, or (3) a different MCP-server internal error. Next gate per MCP-EGI-001 §10 ordering: **#785 / MCP-EGI-003 unmasking BEFORE any further prompt iteration**. D3 still FAIL. D4 still BLOCKED. Family I `productionEnabled:true` retained. #782 retained. MCP-EGI-002 (#787 / `26fbeb1`) retained — not rolled back. Detail in [cDiscourse#786 comment](https://github.com/kyleruff1/cDiscourse/issues/786#issuecomment-4763588210); safe Deno correlation IDs surfaced for operator log inspection.

**2026-06-21 — MCP-EGI-002 (#784 — prompt-only hardening of Families E/G/I evidenceSpan response shape; mcp-server PROMPT + TEST ONLY, NO validator / ban-list / retry / drainer / concurrency / familyRegistry / Edge / migration change; NO provider call by Claude; NO deploy):** Implementation of MCP-EGI-001 (#783) prompt-hardening lane following Gate-A approval. Targets the four rawKeys whose `evidenceSpan.<rawKey>` validation_failed on the 2026-06-21 D3 production smoke after Anthropic HTTP 200, masked by the Edge as `provider_server_error`: `compares_options` (Family I / `thread_topology`), `convergent_premise_structure` and `tradeoff_reasoning_present` (Family E / `argument_scheme`), `synthesis_proposed` (Family G / `resolution_progress`). **mcp-server prompt changes (3 files):** `mcp-server/lib/familyEPrompt.ts` — appended rule-7 + rule-8 per-rawKey shape reinforcement for `convergent_premise_structure` and `tradeoff_reasoning_present` (existing rule-6 abductive reinforcement and strict 5-rule contract block kept byte-equal). `mcp-server/lib/familyGPrompt.ts` — replaced the weak two-rule coordination hint with Family-E-style STRICT RESPONSE-SHAPE CONTRACT (rules 1–5: key-set equality across `checkedRawKeys` / `observations` / `confidence` / `evidenceSpan`; include every requested rawKey exactly once; evidenceSpan value type is `string ≤ 240 chars OR null`; null evidenceSpan for false observations; self-check before emitting) plus rule-6 per-rawKey reinforcement for `synthesis_proposed`. `mcp-server/lib/familyIPrompt.ts` — same strict contract + rule-6 reinforcement for `compares_options`. All new rule-6 blocks enumerate the four candidate live shapes (length > 240 / value-type / missing entry / extra entry) without claiming which one occurred live — the validator `detail` discriminator is structurally suppressed by the Phase-1 leak-safety posture in `booleanObservationMcpAdapter.ts:261-274`, so the live rejected shape remains unconfirmed and the prompt hardens against all four simultaneously. New prompt text introduces no banned doctrine/verdict tokens (per-family ban-list scans + new MCP-EGI-002 banned-token tests stay green). **mcp-server test changes (4 files):** `mcp-server/tests/familyEPrompt.test.ts` — added 3 tests (rule-7 / rule-8 / rule-6 byte-equal preservation). `mcp-server/tests/familyGPrompt.test.ts` — relaxed the weak-phrase observations/confidence coordination assertion to the strict-contract pattern + added 7 MCP-EGI-002 tests (strict contract block, key-set equality, value-type forbids, null-for-false, self-check, synthesis_proposed reinforcement, banned-verdict scan). `mcp-server/tests/familyIPrompt.test.ts` — same shape (relaxed + 7 new MCP-EGI-002 tests). `mcp-server/tests/mcpEgiEvidenceSpanRegression.test.ts` — NEW cross-family validator regression file: 41 tests covering each of the 4 EGI rawKeys × 10 variants (short string / null / 240-char string accepted; 241-char / object / array / boolean / number / missing entry / extra entry rejected with `path = evidenceSpan.<rawKey>`) + 1 cap-preservation test. **Deno test suite: 1799 passed / 0 failed (full mcp-server run, 6s).** Targeted subset: Family E prompt 36/36, Family G prompt 31/31, Family I prompt 31/31, MCP-EGI regression 41/41 (102 / 102 across the four files). **Jest full suite: 32,124 passed / 1 skipped / 0 failed across 858 suites (31.0s).** `npm run typecheck` clean, `npm run lint --max-warnings 0` clean. **Preservation manifest:** `validateMcpBooleanObservationResponse` UNCHANGED · `MAX_EVIDENCE_SPAN_CHARS = 240` UNCHANGED · doctrine ban-list scanners UNCHANGED · `FAMILY_E/G/I_MAX_TOKENS = 1500` UNCHANGED · `DRAINER_*` retry policy / backoff / concurrency UNCHANGED (#782 retained) · `familyRegistry` UNCHANGED · `submit-argument` / Edge adapter / failure-detail builder UNCHANGED · submission engine UNCHANGED · no migrations · no env / secret / cron / routing change · `.env*` / `package.json` / lockfile / `app.json` / `supabase` tree UNCHANGED. **No Anthropic / xAI / X API call by Claude · no Supabase write / service-role · no provider spend · no replay against hosted MCP · no Deno Deploy push/promote · no merge.** D3 still **FAIL** pending operator `npx supabase functions deploy` + Deno Deploy `mcp-server/` push + MCP-EGI-004 (#786) operator-gated re-smoke. D4 still **BLOCKED**. Family I `productionEnabled:true` retained. **Operator follow-up:** (1) merge PR (commit-only, no Edge deploy bearing); (2) push/promote `mcp-server/` to Deno Deploy via the `deploy/civildiscourse/cdiscourse-mcp-server` integration (separate operator action); (3) run the MCP-EGI-004 (#786) canary + burst D3 re-smoke (PASS bar: 9/9, 0 dead-letter). Design doc: `docs/designs/MCP-EGI-EVIDENCESPAN-VALIDATION-FIX-2026-06-21.md`.

**2026-06-21 — AUTH-GOOGLE-SSO-LAYOUT-001 (#780 — email-first Sign In order + remove stray gold accent rule + divider copy "or continue with SSO"; UI / COPY / TEST ONLY, NO Supabase / migration / Edge / RLS / service-role / secret / provider call by Claude / deploy):** Three Sign In surface changes, all UI/copy/test scoped. **Change 1 — email-first reorder (`src/features/auth/AuthScreen.tsx`):** the SSO provider region (the official Google button when the gate is enabled; the "coming soon" notice when disabled) + its divider previously rendered ABOVE the email/password form; they now render BELOW the email **Sign In** button and ABOVE the **"Don't have an account? Sign up"** toggle. Final render order top→bottom: SUPABASE-not-configured warning · value-prop (brand lockup + tagline) · Email field · Password field · Display-name field (signup only) · displayError ErrorNotice · **Sign In** button · provider **divider** · provider **region** · **Sign-up** toggle (now the very last element). JSX-order-only — the gate (`resolveGoogleAuthEnabled()`, default OFF), the Google button (official PNG, decorative `accessible={false}`, single `accessibilityLabel` from `CONTINUE_WITH_GOOGLE_LABEL`), `signInWithGoogle`, and all email/password logic are byte-unchanged. **Change 2 — removed the stray gold accent rule:** deleted the `<View style={styles.valuePropAccent} testID="auth-value-prop-accent" />` element (the small 40px gold rule under the lockup — purposeless/asymmetric per operator) AND the now-unused `valuePropAccent` StyleSheet entry; the `valueProp` `gap` already spaces the lockup + tagline so no replacement spacer is needed. **Change 3 — divider copy (`src/features/auth/authProviderSlotModel.ts`, pure TS):** renamed the constant `PROVIDER_EMAIL_DIVIDER_LABEL = 'or continue with email'` → `PROVIDER_SSO_DIVIDER_LABEL = 'or continue with SSO'` (the divider now leads INTO the SSO options, not the email form); updated the `dividerLabel` field assignment, doc comments, and all importers/tests. Also made `PROVIDER_UNAVAILABLE_COPY` position-neutral (dropped the now-inaccurate directional word "below" — the email form is now ABOVE this notice; copy is "Social sign-in is coming soon. Use your email and password to continue." and still carries the `coming soon` substring + no verdict tokens / snake_case / banned slogans). The model stays pure (no React/Supabase). **Tests:** `__tests__/authProviderSlotModel.test.ts` (constant rename + expected value `'or continue with SSO'`; copy-safety + default + future-consumability stay green), `__tests__/authScreenProviderRegion.test.tsx` (divider-label assertion → `'or continue with SSO'`; +2 NEW render-order tests proving the SSO divider + region appear AFTER the Sign In button and the Sign-up toggle is last; Google button + email/password + source-scan guards unchanged), `__tests__/uxBrand001GoldAccent.test.ts` (the accent-rule assertion now asserts `auth-value-prop-accent` + `valuePropAccent` are ABSENT; the gold-lead assertion stays). **858 suites / 32,123 tests (32,122 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped, 0 failed; +2 tests over the 858 / 32,121 AUTH-GOOGLE-SSO-BRAND-001 baseline — the two new render-order tests; LIFE-001/META-001 wall-clock perf flakes did not surface this run).** typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean · `npm run web:build` exits 0 (771 modules; the official `google-continue-light.png` (13.3 kB) still bundles, all three branding assets present). No Anthropic / xAI / X API call by Claude · no Supabase write / service-role / secret · no migration / Edge / MCP / RLS · no Supabase config write · no `.env*` / `package.json` / lockfile / `app.json` / `supabase` / `mcp-server` change · no new dependency · no Facebook/Apple · no `signInWithGoogle` / callback / provisioning / invite / gate change · no deploy. **Operator follow-up:** none new — the SSO region is observable live once `EXPO_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_AUTH_ENABLED=true` is set in Netlify env (the existing #746 step) and netlify-prod is republished. (This card's design is specified in the card itself; no separate design doc.)

**2026-06-21 — AUTH-GOOGLE-SSO-BRAND-001 (#778 — official Google mark on the "Continue with Google" button; UI / ASSET / TEST ONLY, NO Supabase / migration / Edge / RLS / service-role / secret / provider call by Claude / deploy):** Replaces the generic text `<Button>` Google affordance on the Sign In screen with the OFFICIAL Google "Sign in with Google" web button image (light theme, rounded, continue-with variant — multicolor G mark + Roboto wordmark baked in by Google). **Asset:** copied the official Google web light rounded continue-variant asset (the `light_web_light_rd_ctn` PNG at 4x density; 756×160 px, aspect 4.725, 13,348 bytes, sha256 `2c8884ed…`) byte-for-byte to `assets/branding/google-continue-light.png` — UNMODIFIED (no recolor / resize / redraw, per Google's brand guidelines). **Component (`src/features/auth/AuthScreen.tsx`):** the `providerRegion.hasVisibleProvider` branch now renders a `Pressable` (onPress = the unchanged `signInWithGoogle` wrapper; `accessibilityRole="button"`; `accessibilityLabel={CONTINUE_WITH_GOOGLE_LABEL}` = "Continue with Google"; `minHeight:44` + hitSlop ≥44px touch target; centered; `testID="auth-provider-google-button"`) wrapping an `Image` of the asset (`require('../../../assets/branding/google-continue-light.png')` — same path form as the existing `lockup-horizontal.png` require; explicit `width:227 × height:48` preserving the 4.725 aspect since RN Web ignores `aspectRatio`; `resizeMode="contain"`; decorative `accessible={false}`; `testID="auth-provider-google-icon"`). The visible "Continue with Google" text is now baked into the image (no separate `<Text>` node). The gate (`resolveGoogleAuthEnabled()`, default OFF), the "coming soon" notice on the OFF path, the "or continue with email" divider, the email/password form, the mode toggle, and `signInWithGoogle.ts` / callback / provisioning / invite are ALL unchanged. **Tests (`__tests__/authScreenProviderRegion.test.tsx`):** the enabled-state assertions move off `getByText('Continue with Google')` (text is now image-baked) onto `getByLabelText('Continue with Google')` + the `auth-provider-google-icon` image testID; added a guard that the image is decorative (`accessible===false`, `resizeMode==='contain'`) and that the Pressable carries the role + name + `minHeight≥44`. The default-OFF tests (no Google affordance, "coming soon" notice present), the never-reaches-a-provider guard, the email/password tests, the no-Facebook/no-Apple guards, and the source-scan allow-list (`signInWithOAuth` only in `signInWithGoogle.ts`; AuthScreen contains no `oauth` / no `Continue with Google` literal) all stay green. **Web-build asset-bundling proof (the authoritative require-path check — jest mocks the require):** `npm run web:build` exits 0 and emits `assets/branding/google-continue-light.<hash>.png (13.3 kB)`, byte-identical to source (sha256 match) — the `../../../assets/branding/` require path resolves in the real Metro/Netlify bundle. **858 suites / 32,121 tests (32,120 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped, 0 failed; +1 test over the 858 / 32,120 AUTH-GOOGLE-SSO-GATE-FIX-001 baseline in `authScreenProviderRegion`; note the pre-existing LIFE-001/META-001 wall-clock perf flakes that pass isolated, unrelated to this diff).** typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean · `npm run web:build` exits 0. No Anthropic / xAI / X API call by Claude · no Supabase write / service-role / secret · no migration / Edge / MCP / RLS · no Supabase config write · no `.env*` / `package.json` / lockfile / `app.json` / `supabase` / `mcp-server` change · no new dependency · no font added · no Facebook/Apple · no `signInWithGoogle` / callback / AuthScreen-gating / invite / provisioning change · no deploy. **Operator follow-up:** none new — the official button is observable live once `EXPO_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_AUTH_ENABLED=true` is set in Netlify env (the existing #746 step) and netlify-prod is republished. (This card's design is specified in the card itself; no separate design doc.)

**2026-06-21 — AUTH-GOOGLE-SSO-GATE-FIX-001 (#776 — static Expo env access for the Google SSO gate; ONE-LINE CLIENT FIX + TESTS + DOC ONLY, NO Supabase / migration / Edge / RLS / service-role / secret / provider call by Claude / deploy):** Fixes the confirmed live defect where the "Continue with Google" button never rendered even with the Netlify flag set to `true`. Root cause: `src/features/auth/googleAuthGate.ts` `resolveGoogleAuthEnabled()` read the flag via DYNAMIC computed access `process.env[GOOGLE_AUTH_ENABLED_FLAG]`. `babel-preset-expo` inlines `EXPO_PUBLIC_*` ONLY for STATIC member expressions (`process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_X`); the dynamic bracket form is left un-inlined → `undefined` in the web bundle (no Node `process.env`) → the gate was always false. Jest passed because Node has a real `process.env`. **Fix (one line):** `const fromEnv = process.env[GOOGLE_AUTH_ENABLED_FLAG];` → `const fromEnv = process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_AUTH_ENABLED;` (static dot access). The `SUPABASE_CONFIGURED` guard, the `readRuntimeEnv()` runtime-env-shim fallback (a real-object access, kept for the Cloud-Run shim path), the shim-over-env precedence, and the strict `value === 'true'` default-off check are all unchanged. The `GOOGLE_AUTH_ENABLED_FLAG` constant remains as the `readRuntimeEnv()[...]` key (not a `process.env[` access). **Tests:** extended `__tests__/googleAuthGate.test.ts` with a static-read describe block (flag `'true'` → enabled; `'false'`/`''`/`'1'`/`'TRUE'`/unset → disabled; `SUPABASE_CONFIGURED=false` → disabled) and added a SOURCE-SCAN guard `__tests__/googleAuthGateStaticEnv.test.ts` (jest cannot observe Metro inlining): asserts the source contains the static literal `process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_AUTH_ENABLED` and contains NO dynamic `process.env[` access (`/process\.env\[/`). **Web-build inlining proof:** `EXPO_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_AUTH_ENABLED=true npm run web:build` → compiled `resolveGoogleAuthEnabled` inlines `fromEnv` to the literal `"true"` (gate returns true); a cache-cleared default build (`/tmp/metro-cache` purged, no flag) inlines `fromEnv` to `void 0` (gate stays false). Both bundles show 0 occurrences of static `process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_AUTH_ENABLED` (fully inlined) and 0 occurrences of dynamic `process.env[`. **858 suites / 32,120 tests (32,118 passed; +1 suite / +9 tests over the 857 / 32,111 AUTH-GOOGLE-SSO-005 baseline; 1 pre-existing skipped; 1 pre-existing META-001 wall-clock perf flake in `moveMetadataLedger.test.ts` that passes 49/49 isolated, unrelated to this diff).** typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean · both `npm run web:build` runs exit 0. No Anthropic / xAI / X API call by Claude · no Supabase write / service-role / secret / provider secret · no migration / Edge / MCP / RLS · no Supabase config write · no `.env*` / `package.json` / lockfile / `app.json` / `supabase` / `mcp-server` change · no Facebook/Apple · no `signInWithGoogle` / callback / AuthScreen-wiring / invite / provisioning change · no deploy. **Operator follow-up:** none new — the gate is observable live once `EXPO_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_AUTH_ENABLED=true` is set in Netlify env (the existing #746 step) and netlify-prod is republished so the corrected bundle ships. (This card had no separate design doc; root cause + fix are specified in the card itself.)

**2026-06-21 — AUTH-GOOGLE-SSO-005 (#748 — preserve invite/room intent through Google SSO; CLIENT AUTH-STATE + TEST + DOC ONLY, NOT GATE-C, NO Supabase config write / migration / Edge / RLS / service-role / secret / provider call by Claude / deploy):** Converts the invite-through-Google resume from incidental to deterministic + tested. The invite intent is captured to the dedicated AsyncStorage key (= localStorage on web → survives the full-page Google redirect) at the invite-link cold start, long before any Google button is clickable; a Google `?code=` return lands on `/auth/callback`, exchanges to a session (`exchangeCodeForSession` → `{status:'success'}`, NOT `needs_password` which is invite-email-only), and the `InviteRedeemGate` resumes. **One small client hardening edit** in `App.tsx`: the inline `onDone={() => setAuthCallback({active:false,url:''})}` on `<AuthCallbackScreen>` is promoted to a named `handleAuthCallbackDone = React.useCallback(async …)` that flips the flag FIRST (synchronous, never delayed by the async load) then re-reads the persisted intent via the ALREADY-imported `loadPendingInviteIntentFromStorage(now)` and dispatches `SET_PENDING_INVITE_INTENT` so the gate sees a live intent on the next render regardless of cold-start timing. Idempotent with the retained cold-start branch-2 re-read (both dispatch the same slice; the gate's auto-accept awaits `lookupInviteByToken`); stale (>24h) / malformed intents are dropped inside the load helper; the token is never logged; no access/refresh/provider token is read or stored. **Room-intent (returnTo debateId) is DEFERRED** (design Q2 proved it unreachable today — `signInWithGoogle` is invoked only from `AuthScreen.tsx`, the gate out-ranks `AuthScreen` while an intent is live, and no signed-out room-target store exists); a follow-up card owns it when an inline room/gallery Google sign-in exists. `signInWithGoogle.ts` is UNCHANGED (design Q3: the intent always persists at cold start, so no flush-before-redirect; keeping the wrapper intent-agnostic preserves its single-file `signInWithOAuth` allow-list). `consumeAuthCallback` / `parseAuthCallbackUrl` / `AuthCallbackScreen` / `InviteRedeemGate` / `inviteApi` / `sessionState` all UNCHANGED — acceptance stays the existing server-side `acceptRoomInvite` → `manage-room-invite` Edge invoke (no new authorization surface, no direct `acceptRoomInvite` bypass, no seat/capacity/chime-in change). One optional pure helper added: `decideInviteResume({intent, signedIn, viewerEmail})` in `pendingInviteIntent.ts` (+ barrel export) — a TESTABLE MIRROR of the gate-mount decision (`resume:true` only for a non-null fresh intent; `autoAcceptEligible = signedIn && !!viewerEmail`), it does NOT replace the gate's own effect and carries no email (no enumeration). **Three new suites:** `__tests__/inviteResumeAfterOAuth.test.ts` (the persisted intent survives a simulated redirect, stale/malformed dropped, normal sign-in captures nothing, the blob is token-free of OAuth secrets, the `decideInviteResume` matrix, and a synthetic bare `?code=` parses→consumes to `success`); `__tests__/appInviteResumeOAuthWiring.test.ts` (App.tsx source-scan: named `handleAuthCallbackDone` wired via `onDone={handleAuthCallbackDone}`, flag-flip + re-read + dispatch present, cold-start fallback RETAINED, routing priority preserved, no token logged / no OAuth token stored / no service-role / no Google client secret); `__tests__/InviteRedeemGate.oauth.test.tsx` (RTL: the gate resumes off ANY established session — the Google-account shape — firing `acceptRoomInvite({token})` once → `onAccepted`; email mismatch → plain MismatchPanel with no invited address; byte-identical signed-out render; expired/room_closed render their panels, never auto-accept). One existing assertion updated in lockstep: `authCallbackRouting.test.ts` "flips the flag off via onDone" now pins the named-callback structure (design-authorized; the flag-flip property is unchanged). `consumeAuthCallback.test.ts` NOT extended — the bare-`?code=`-no-`type`→`success` case is ALREADY covered (`consumeAuthCallback.test.ts:90-93`), so per the design no duplicate was added. **857 suites / 32,111 tests (32,110 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped; +3 suites / +28 tests over the 854 / 32,083 AUTH-GOOGLE-SSO-003 baseline).** typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean · `npm run test` exit 0 · `npm run web:build` exit 0. No Anthropic / xAI / X API call by Claude · no Supabase write / service-role / secret / provider secret / OAuth token stored · no migration / Edge / MCP / RLS · no Supabase config write · no `.env*` / `package.json` / lockfile / `app.json` change · no Facebook/Apple · no seat/capacity/chime-in/mediator/submission semantics change · no deploy · NO live OAuth call in tests (mocked). **Operator follow-up:** none to merge (not GATE-C; automerge-eligible when green). The resume is observable live only once `EXPO_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_AUTH_ENABLED=true` is set in Netlify env (the existing #746 operator step) + the hosted provider (#745, done); no NEW operator action is introduced. See `docs/designs/AUTH-GOOGLE-SSO-005.md`.

**2026-06-21 — AUTH-GOOGLE-SSO-003 (#746 — gated "Continue with Google" button + `signInWithGoogle` wrapper; CLIENT UI + WRAPPER + GATE + TEST + DOC ONLY, NOT GATE-C, NO Supabase config write / migration / Edge / RLS / service-role / secret / provider call by Claude / deploy):** The UI half of the operator-ratified (ADR #743) Google-only SSO scope change. Three new files + three modified, all auth-UI/wrapper/gate/test/doc scoped. **Gate** `src/features/auth/googleAuthGate.ts` — pure decision `resolveGoogleAuthEnabled()` returns true ONLY when `SUPABASE_CONFIGURED && EXPO_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_AUTH_ENABLED === 'true'` (exact-string), reading the PUBLIC flag via `readRuntimeEnv()` (window shim) then `process.env` — the same precedence as `supabase.ts`. **Default OFF** (unset / `'false'` / `'1'` / `'TRUE'` / `''` → false), so merging #746 does NOT change the live Sign In surface; the operator sets `EXPO_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_AUTH_ENABLED=true` in Netlify env to go live post-#745 (the hosted provider is already enabled). **Wrapper** `src/features/auth/signInWithGoogle.ts` — the ONLY file under `src/` permitted to contain `signInWithOAuth` (single-file source-scan allow-list). Calls `supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({ provider: 'google', options: { redirectTo } })` where `redirectTo = buildAuthRedirectUrl({ kind: 'magic_link', route: '/auth/callback', runtimeOrigin: resolveRuntimeOrigin(), isDev: getIsDev() })` → resolves to `<currentOrigin>/auth/callback` (NOT the `dev.cdiscourse.com` fallback; works on localhost / Netlify dev / preview / prod with no code change). Guards `SUPABASE_CONFIGURED` (safe no-op `{ ok:false, error:'config_missing' }`), reuses the now-exported `mapAuthError` + the `AuthResult` shape, never throws, never logs the provider response / access / refresh tokens; an invalid origin degrades to `options` omitted (Supabase falls back to Site URL) rather than blocking sign-in. **No new `'oauth'` `AuthRedirectKind`** — explicit `route` per the merged architecture design §1; `buildAuthRedirectUrl` unchanged. **No callback change** (design-verified: a bare `?code=` already → `{kind:'code', type:null}` → `exchangeCodeForSession` → `success`; `parseAuthCallbackUrl` / `consumeAuthCallback` / `AuthCallbackScreen` untouched). **`AuthScreen.tsx`** wires it with zero re-layout: `const googleEnabled = resolveGoogleAuthEnabled()` → `resolveAuthProviderSlotRegion(googleEnabled ? { enabledSlots: [{ id:'google', order:0, enabled:true }] } : undefined)`; in the `hasVisibleProvider` branch the empty `<View testID="auth-provider-region" />` placeholder is replaced by a real `Button` (label = the imported `CONTINUE_WITH_GOOGLE_LABEL` constant, `variant="secondary"`, `onPress={() => { void signInWithGoogle(); }}`, `testID="auth-provider-google-button"`, a11y label "Continue with Google" via the Button `label`→`accessibilityLabel` mapping, `minHeight:48 ≥ 44`). The email/password form + toggle are byte-identical; AuthScreen contains no `signInWithOAuth` / no `oauth` substring / no `'Continue with Google'` literal, so its three existing source guards stay green. **`authApi.ts`** — additive `export function mapAuthError` (1-word, no logic change). **`Button.tsx`** — additive optional `testID?: string` passthrough to the Pressable (omitted = byte-identical for every existing caller; needed so the gated affordance is locatable). The `noLocalhostInProdAuthPaths` static scan is a free regression net over the new wrapper (it hard-codes no origin → passes). **Two new suites:** `__tests__/googleAuthGate.test.ts` (13 — default-OFF for unset / every non-`'true'` value, false when `SUPABASE_CONFIGURED` false, true only when flag `'true'` AND configured, shim-over-`process.env` precedence) and `__tests__/signInWithGoogle.test.ts` (12 — `provider:'google'` once; `redirectTo` ends `/auth/callback` using the resolved current origin and is NOT `dev.cdiscourse.com`; invalid origin → `options` omitted but still initiates; `SUPABASE_CONFIGURED` false → no provider call; Supabase error / rejection → mapped, never throws; no token logged across happy + error paths). `__tests__/authScreenProviderRegion.test.tsx` extended (+7) with an enabled-state describe (real gate forced ON via the runtime-env shim flag: button renders by testID + text + a11y label; no "coming soon" notice; pressing it calls the mocked `signInWithGoogle` wrapper, NOT `supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth` directly; email/password still works; no Facebook/Apple) and the src/-walk guard relaxed from `offenders === []` to the single-file allow-list `['src/features/auth/signInWithGoogle.ts']` + a positive control. **854 suites / 32,083 tests (32,082 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped; +2 suites / +32 tests over the 852 / 32,051 DEV-SEED-001 baseline).** typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean · `npm run test` exit 0 · `npm run web:build` exit 0. No Anthropic / xAI / X API call by Claude · no Supabase write / service-role / secret / provider secret · no migration / Edge / MCP / RLS · no Supabase config write · no `.env*` / `package.json` / lockfile / `app.json` change · no Facebook/Apple UI · no invite/room redemption (#748) · no deploy. **Operator follow-up to go live (post-#745, NOT run by Claude):** set `EXPO_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_AUTH_ENABLED=true` in Netlify env + redeploy; until then the Sign In surface stays email-only. See `docs/designs/AUTH-GOOGLE-SSO-003.md`.

**2026-06-21 — DEV-SEED-001 (#772 — fix local/preview seed profile FK failure; SEED-FILE + TEST ONLY, NO migration / RLS / auth-behavior / production mutation / deploy):** `supabase/seed.sql` inserted three dev profiles with hardcoded UUIDs (`00000000-…-0001/0002/0003`) into `public.profiles` without seeding matching `auth.users` rows, but `public.profiles.id REFERENCES auth.users(id)` (`20260516000001_initial_schema.sql:26`) — so every fresh DB (`supabase db reset` locally, the Supabase preview on each migration PR) raised `profiles_id_fkey` (SQLSTATE 23503) and aborted. Fix seeds three minimal GoTrue-shape `auth.users` rows FIRST (`instance_id`, `aud`/`role` = `'authenticated'`, `*@dev.local` emails, `encrypted_password = crypt('devpassword', gen_salt('bf'))` — a documented throwaway dev password, not a secret; `email_confirmed_at`/`created_at`/`updated_at = now()`; `raw_app_meta_data` email provider; `raw_user_meta_data.display_name` mirroring the profile names), with `ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING`. **Trigger interaction handled:** inserting `auth.users` fires `on_auth_user_created` → the (now-coalescing) `handle_new_user()` auto-creates each profile as role `'user'`, which would silently demote Mod (Dev); so the explicit profiles statement is changed from `DO NOTHING` to an **UPSERT** (`ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET display_name = EXCLUDED.display_name, role = EXCLUDED.role`) so the dev names + Mod's `'moderator'` role win over the trigger defaults. Final seeded state: Alice/Bob role `user`, Mod role `moderator`, names `Alice (Dev)`/`Bob (Dev)`/`Mod (Dev)`. The "DO NOT apply to staging or production" header is retained; no migration, RLS, or trigger touched. New additive `__tests__/devSeed001SeedAuthUsers.test.ts` (11 — static SQL scan: `auth.users` before `public.profiles`, the three dev UUIDs in both blocks, `ON CONFLICT` present, the Mod upsert resolves to `role = 'moderator'`, header retained, no `service_role`/provider-secret/static-bcrypt literal, single `auth.users` INSERT with no UPDATE/DELETE/TRUNCATE). **852 suites / 32,051 tests (32,049 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped, +1 suite / +11 tests over the 851 / 32,040 AUTH-GOOGLE-SSO-004 baseline; the LIFE-001 `pointLifecycleModel` 30ms wall-clock perf test flakes under full-parallel load — passes 76/76 isolated, unrelated to this seed-only delta).** typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean · `npm run test` exit 0 on the seed suite · `npm run web:build` exit 0 (775 modules, no new asset bytes). No Anthropic / xAI / X API call by Claude · no Supabase write / service-role · no migration / Edge / MCP · no OAuth / provider call · no package / `app.json` change · no deploy. **Real-PG validation deferred to the Supabase PREVIEW when the PR opens** (no Docker in this session) — the fix + test are static-correct; the preview reset exercises the FK + trigger + upsert against real Postgres.

**2026-06-20 — AUTH-GOOGLE-SSO-004 (#747 — OAuth profile provisioning / metadata coalescing; MIGRATION-BEARING, GATE-C merge = apply; NO provider call / NO Supabase mutation / NO RLS change / NO service-role / NO UI):** Server-side fix for the one real Google-SSO provisioning gap. The original `handle_new_user()` trigger (`20260516000001_initial_schema.sql:37-49`) read ONLY `raw_user_meta_data ->> 'display_name'` — the key the email/password path sets — so a Google identity (whose human name lands under `full_name`/`name`/`given_name`+`family_name`) provisioned a profile row with a NULL `display_name`. New append-only migration `supabase/migrations/20260620000001_auth_google_oauth_display_name_coalesce.sql` `CREATE OR REPLACE`s the function body to COALESCE the display name across `display_name → full_name → name → concat_ws(' ', given_name, family_name) → split_part(NEW.email,'@',1) → 'Member'` (each candidate `nullif(btrim(regexp_replace(<x>,'\s+',' ','g')),'')`-normalized), capped `left(…, 60)` to match `ContactInfoSection.DISPLAY_NAME_MAX`. PRESERVED verbatim: `RETURNS trigger / LANGUAGE plpgsql / SECURITY DEFINER / SET search_path = public`, the `INSERT INTO public.profiles (id, display_name, role)` with `'user'`, and **`ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING`** (the idempotency + no-clobber spine; conflict target is `id`, never email → no enumeration, no same-email takeover). The existing `on_auth_user_created` trigger is NOT touched (CREATE OR REPLACE keeps it bound). New pure-TS twin `src/features/auth/coalesceProviderDisplayName.ts` (`coalesceProviderDisplayName(metadata, email?)` + `DISPLAY_NAME_DB_CAP = 60` + `GENERIC_DISPLAY_NAME_FALLBACK = 'Member'`) is the unit-testable executable spec the migration mirrors and that #748's optional client self-heal can reuse so client + trigger never diverge — not wired into runtime in this card. **Email/password empty-string delta characterized + confirmed REACHABLE:** the signup display-name field is labeled "(optional)", `validateAuthInput` requires only email + password, and `AuthScreen` passes `displayName.trim() || undefined` which `authApi` stores as `display_name: ''` — so a blank-name email/password signup lands with `''` today (stored as `''`); under the new trigger that `''` normalizes to NULL and falls through to the email local-part. Strict improvement, the one intended behavioral delta, documented in the migration header + the design doc. Two new additive suites: `__tests__/authGoogleDisplayNameCoalesceMigration.test.ts` (static SQL scan — shape, coalesce coverage + priority order, normalization, `left(…,60)`, `ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING`, no-schema/trigger-drift negatives, doctrine negatives incl. no `service_role`/`DROP POLICY`/secret, ban-list-clean `'Member'`, append-only + stamp-collision) and `__tests__/coalesceProviderDisplayName.test.ts` (every priority/fallback/normalization/cap/never-throw/never-empty case + cap-constant parity scan of the migration). **851 suites / 32,040 tests (32,039 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped; +2 suites / +48 tests over the 849 / 31,992 baseline this session).** typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean · `npm run test` exit 0 · `npm run web:build` exit 0. No Anthropic / xAI / X API / Supabase write by Claude. Migration written but NOT applied — operator merges (= applies) per GATE-C; live Google sign-in acceptance is operator-verified post-#745. See `docs/designs/AUTH-GOOGLE-SSO-004.md`.

**2026-06-20 — CIVILDISCOURSE-LOW-HALT-UX-COPY-BATCH-002 (issues #740 / #754 / #758 / #759 / #760 — coordinated UI/copy/docs/test batch; UI/COPY/DOCS/TEST ONLY, NO provider call / OAuth / Supabase / RLS / Edge / migration / MCP / app.json / native dep / deploy):** Five low-halt items landed together. **#740 + #760 (MERGED, email-only default):** new single canonical pure model `src/features/auth/authProviderSlotModel.ts` (no React/Supabase/network/mutation) owns the Sign In provider-slot contract — a future-reserved disabled Google slot (`enabled:false`), the canonical `FIRST_RUN_PROVIDER_SLOT_ORDER = ['google','apple','facebook']`, `anyProviderEnabled`/`hasVisibleProvider` (both false today), a future-framed `PROVIDER_UNAVAILABLE_COPY` ("Social sign-in is coming soon. Use your email and password below to continue."), a `PROVIDER_EMAIL_DIVIDER_LABEL` ("or continue with email"), and a future-reserved `CONTINUE_WITH_GOOGLE_LABEL` constant for #746 (NOT rendered while disabled). `AuthScreen.tsx` renders the provider-slot region ABOVE the email form: the default (email-only) path shows the "coming soon" notice (a `<Text>`, NOT a Pressable/Button) + the divider, then the UNCHANGED email/password form. **NO visible/enabled "Continue with Google" button this batch; NO `signInWithOAuth` anywhere in `src/` (source-scan enforced); email/password byte-identical.** #746 lights a slot via `resolveAuthProviderSlotRegion({enabledSlots})` with zero re-layout. **#754 UX-COPY-SYSTEM-002 (CLOSE_BY_DOC):** new `docs/designs/CIVILDISCOURSE-COPY-SYSTEM.md` (verbatim copy-system standard: allowed/banned vocabulary, room/seat/invite grammar, mediator-not-judge, SSO-READY auth language, voice-FUTURE, timeline-is-structure-not-feed, no-copied-slogan) + new additive `__tests__/copySystemBanList.test.ts` (consolidated ban-list guard over the doc §10 scan set — room/seat/visibility/observer/invite/mediator/brand constants — with positive + negative controls and the §3.6 carve-outs `block`/`opponent`/`hot` pinned; STATUS_COPY excluded per §10 scope, as it carries the shipped both-sides phrase "You might both be wrong"). NO shipped copy constant mutated. **#758 UX-BOARD-MOBILE-DEPTH-001 (CLOSE_AFTER_IMPL):** new `docs/designs/UX-BOARD-MOBILE-DEPTH-001.md` + the additive 390px reading-depth fix — `bodyFull` field added to `SidecarSection_WhatThisMoveSays` (the 280-char `bodyExcerpt`/`isTruncated`/`fullBodyLength`/`truncateAtWordBoundary` pins stay BYTE-IDENTICAL) + a read-only "Show full body" disclosure toggle in `ArgumentReplySidecar.tsx` modeled byte-for-byte on the existing "Show details" control (NO TextInput/editable/onChangeText/onAction/dispatch; no new component prop). The full selected-node body is now reachable in ≤2 taps at 390px (was an unbounded truncation dead-end). **#759 UX-ROUTE-SEAT-INVITE-COPY-001 (PARTIAL):** `DebateListScreen.tsx` action label routed through the shared `accessView.actionLabel` (`deriveGalleryActionLabel` policy) so the list row and the Conversation Gallery no longer drift (member → "Continue →"; non-member+open → "Observe →"; otherwise → "Open →"). The OD-5 "Opponent" seat-word slice stays deferred (follow-up comment on #759). **848 suites / 31,968 tests (31,967 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped; +4 suites / +67 tests over the 844 / 31,901 UX-ROOM-1V1-CHIMEIN-001A baseline; the LIFE-001 `pointLifecycleModel` 30ms + META-001 `moveMetadataLedger` 60ms wall-clock perf tests flake under full-parallel load — both pass 125/125 isolated, unrelated to this UI/copy/docs delta).** typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean · `npm run test` exit 0 (clean re-run) · `npm run web:build` exit 0 (775 modules; same two branding assets, no new asset bytes). **2026-06-20 — UX-ROOM-1V1-CHIMEIN-001A (issue #737, child of #680 — 1:1-first room-state UI/copy + pure display model; the Layer-A safe-now slice of `docs/designs/UX-ROOM-1V1-CHIMEIN-001.md` §5; UI/COPY + PURE-MODEL ONLY, NO backend / schema / RLS / Edge / migration / capacity / chime-in contribution path; OD-1 NOT resolved):** Adds the 1:1-first room display layer the v4 product rule needs, using only distinctions the current code already makes. New pure-TS `src/features/debates/oneToOneRoomModel.ts` (NO React/Supabase/fetch import; frozen, deterministic) exports a `RoomOneToOneDisplayState` union (`private_invited_access` · `public_respondent_seat_open` · `public_principal_voices_established` · `public_chime_in_available_dormant` · `public_chime_in_full_dormant` · `observer_reading` · `unknown`) + `deriveRoomOneToOneDisplayState(input)` (consumes ONLY already-derived inputs: room `visibility`, the `RoomContract` open/established second-principal-seat state, viewer active/observer flags, and — for the two `*_dormant` states only — the EXISTING GAME-005 `PublicRoomSeatMap.openChimeInSeatCount`; returns `'unknown'` when inputs are insufficient) + `buildRoomOneToOneViewModel` (label/subcopy; `chimeAffordanceVisible` is ALWAYS false in this card) + `buildOneToOneSeatLineViewModel` (principal-voices vs observers separation, reusing `SEAT_CLAIM_COPY.readersNote`) + `chimeInAllowed(roomType)=roomType==='public'` (the private-no-chime guard) + `_forbiddenOneToOneTokens`. **The two chime states are DORMANT — informational classification only; this card renders NO active chime control** (the chime-in CONTRIBUTION path stays GATE-C, design §6). Copy relabels (all ban-list clean): `ROOM_CONTRACT_COPY` room-type → **"Public 1:1" / "Private 1:1"**, `seatOpen`/`turnOpenSeat` → **"Respondent seat open"** (the open seat is the second PRINCIPAL/respondent seat, NEVER a "chime-in"); **`seatOpponent` ("Opponent") left byte-identical** (deferred OD-5, not on this card's ban-list); `ROOM_ACCESS_COPY.public_open_line` → **"Respondent seat open — observe or take it."**, `private_member_line` → **"Private 1:1 — you are in this argument."**; `ROOM_VISIBILITY_COPY` create-form helpers gain 1:1 framing. Two new frozen `gameCopy.ts` blocks: `ROOM_ONE_TO_ONE_COPY` (state labels/subcopy) + DORMANT `POINT_SCOPED_CHIME_IN_COPY` (referenced by tests/constants only, NOT rendered). **OD-1-SAFE wording (OVERRIDES the design draft's pre-OD-1 strings):** private rooms use "Private 1:1" / "Invited access." / "No public chime-ins." — they NEVER claim "no observers" / "invited parties only" / "only the person you invite", because whether private rooms have observers is the UNRESOLVED operator decision OD-1 and shipped code allows private observers. **App.tsx visibility-wiring bug FIXED:** `useRoomContract` now passes `options: { roomType: currentDebate?.visibility }` — previously omitted, so the header seat strip defaulted every room (incl. private) to "Public 1:1"; pure display-data threading (`visibility` is already loaded), no persistence/capacity/seat-claim change. `RoomContractSeatStrip.roomTypeGlyph` now keys off `ROOM_CONTRACT_COPY.privateRoom` (drift-proof). New `__tests__/oneToOneRoomModel.test.ts` (+38) covers every state derivation, the `'unknown'` fallback, respondent-seat-is-not-chime-in, the OD-1-safe private copy (forbidden phrases asserted absent), principal/observer separation, dormant-copy-but-no-active-control, App.tsx visibility-wiring source-scan, and a ban-list scan over all new/changed copy. `roomContractModel`/`roomContractSeatStrip`/`roomContractDoctrine`/`roomAccessModel`/gallery+debate-list visibility tests updated in lockstep (read the relabeled constants — auto-follow). **844 suites / 31,901 tests (31,900 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped; +1 suite / +38 tests over the UX-ROOM-CHROME-002 baseline 843 / 31,862).** typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean · `npm run test` exit 0 · `npm run web:build` exit 0 (774 modules; only the two existing branding assets, no new asset bytes, favicon unchanged). **No Anthropic / xAI / X API / Supabase write / service-role / migration / Edge / `app.json` / dependency / package / lockfile / `.env` change by Claude in this card.** Deferred: `seatOpponent` relabel (OD-5), the active "Take the respondent seat" CTA (binds seat-claim semantics — GATE-C/#681), and ALL chime-in contribution controls (Layer B/C, operator-gated). See `docs/designs/UX-ROOM-1V1-CHIMEIN-001.md` § "UX-ROOM-1V1-CHIMEIN-001A implementation map".

**2026-06-20 — UX-ROOM-CHROME-002 (issue #734 — signed-in masthead lockup/nav spatial balance; signed-in TOP CHROME ONLY, UI/UX only, NO behavior / topology / data / API / dependency change):** The signed-in shell masthead (`App.tsx` ~778, the navSlot-bearing `<AppHeader>`) rendered the gold lockup in the UX-ROOM-CHROME-001 **compact** variant (`min(48, widthFit)` ≈ 48 px / ≈203 px wide on every band), which under-filled the top-left brand zone against the `flex:1` nav band (operator screenshot: a tiny floating block, not one composed region). This card adds an ADDITIVE `balanced` variant to `src/components/AppHeader.tsx`: new `BALANCED_LOGO_HEIGHT_BY_BAND` (phone **48** / tablet **88** / wide **112** — named band constants, not inline magic px) + new exported pure `resolveSignedInMastheadLogoHeightPx(band, width)` = `min(BALANCED_LOGO_HEIGHT_BY_BAND[band], widthFit)` (width-capped; non-positive width → wide default since `resolveBand(0)==='wide'`), new `balanced?: boolean` prop (default `false`; precedence `balanced → compact → prominent`), new `rootWithNavBalanced` root (slim, `alignItems:'center'` to COMPOSE the lockup + nav companion + right slot as one centered row; phone still reflows to a column) + `navSlotBalanced` (centers the nav vertically, drops the compact top-inset). Tagline omitted (like compact); brand still announced via the logo aria; `resizeMode="contain"` + `width = height × LOGO_ASPECT_RATIO` so aspect is ALWAYS preserved. **The `compact` + prominent resolver paths (`resolveMastheadLogoHeightPx`), `rootWithNav`, `rootWithNavCompact`, the constants, and the single asset require are byte-equivalent.** `App.tsx` swaps the signed-in shell `compact` → `balanced` (line-405 transient `<AppHeader>` stays prominent; AuthScreen / `signInLockupModel` / favicon / `app.json` / `assets/branding/*` bytes FROZEN). Larger + proportional lockup, board still dominant (balanced header `balH + 8` → wide 120 / tablet 96 / phone 56, far shorter than the old 296 px prominent shell); width-cap proven across 320/360/390/414/600/768/1024/1366/1920 (rendered width 203/372.2/473.7 px ≤ available at every width — no horizontal overflow). **NEW `__tests__/uxRoomChrome002MastheadBalance.test.tsx` (21)** — per-band balanced height; tablet+wide `>48` & `<288`; phone `>=48`; no-overflow at all 9 widths; non-positive → wide default; compact `<=48` + prominent `===288` regression (byte-equivalent); balanced render (logo aspect preserved, navSlot reachable, tagline omitted, distinct from compact/prominent); banned-copy scan = none; frozen-surfaces (AuthScreen/favicon/asset) untouched. **`__tests__/uxRoomChrome001CompactHeaderAndMediatorReadout.test.tsx` updated additively (+2)** — the App.tsx-wiring section now asserts the signed-in shell carries `balanced` (supersedes the prior `compact` wiring), `no AppHeader carries the retired compact prop`, `exactly one carries balanced`; the compact + prominent resolver-math assertions are unchanged. **843 suites / 31,862 tests (31,862 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped; +1 suite / +23 tests over the UX-IMPASSE-002 baseline 842 / 31,841).** typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean · `npm run test` exit 0 · `npm run web:build` exit 0 (766 modules; only `civic-discourse-logo.png` + existing `lockup-horizontal.png`, no new asset bytes, favicon unchanged). No xAI / Anthropic / X API / Supabase write / service-role / migration / Edge / MCP / provider / submit / route / persistence change; no dependency / package / lockfile / .env / app.json change; no banned copy (winner/loser/score/verdict/truth/wrong/dishonest/bad-faith/manipulative); "Mediator readout" untouched. See `docs/designs/UX-ROOM-CHROME-002.md`.

**2026-06-20 — UX-IMPASSE-002 (issue #710 — surface the two latent structured-impasse subtypes `value_tradeoff → "Different priorities"` + `key_detail_unavailable → "Key detail unavailable"`; DISPLAY-MAPPING + COPY ONLY, NO derivation / persistence / migration / Edge / MCP / classifier / room / submit change):** The mediator board already DERIVES thirteen internal `MediatorStateCode`s but DISPLAYED only nine — `value_tradeoff` folded to **Open** (no chip) and `key_detail_unavailable` folded into **Evidence blocked**, even though both have deterministic producers that fire on real data today (value axis / `disputes_value_weighting`; `flags_context_limit`). This card surfaces those two as their own v4 display states. In `src/features/mediator/mediatorBoardTypes.ts`: `V4MediatorStateCode` + `ALL_V4_MEDIATOR_STATE_CODES` gain both members (now **eleven**); `V4_PRIMARY_STATE_PRIORITY` inserts `key_detail_unavailable` at **#3** (just below a declined `evidence_blocked`) and `value_tradeoff` at **#10** (just above `open`) — final order `[structured_impasse, evidence_blocked, key_detail_unavailable, accounts_differ, definition_not_shared, scope_mismatch, missing_mechanism, needs_evidence, narrowed, value_tradeoff, open]`, identical to `ALL_V4_MEDIATOR_STATE_CODES`; `V4_DISPLAY_STATE_BY_CODE` flips both entries to **identity** (only `off_point → scope_mismatch` + terminal `resolved_or_settled` still collapse). In `mediatorPlainLanguage.ts`: `MEDIATOR_STATE_COPY.value_tradeoff` → **`'Different priorities'`** (label parity — node chip / rail badge / distribution segment all read `plainLanguageForMediatorState(displayCode)`), operator Lead+Help on `MEDIATOR_STATE_HELPER` for both surfaced states, `VALUE_TRADEOFF_DISPLAY_COPY` / `KEY_DETAIL_UNAVAILABLE_DISPLAY_COPY` updated to the operator copy (now "surfaced by #710"), new dormant `ACCOUNTS_DIFFER_DISPLAY_COPY` + `no_current_pathway` aligned, both marked `// DEFERRED (#710 follow-up)`. In `nextMovesForState.ts`: new `NEXT_MOVE_COPY.name_tradeoff = 'Name the tradeoff'` + rationale + two `STATE_MOVE_SPECS` entries — `value_tradeoff` dominant "Name the tradeoff" (`name_tradeoff` step, available), `key_detail_unavailable` reuses the evidence_blocked shape, dominant "Branch the provable part" (`narrow_or_branch` available) + "Name what kind of record would test this point" (`await_record`, guidance). **`deriveMediatorBoardState.ts` is NOT in the diff** (no derivation change; `point.state` + the 13-code vocabulary unchanged); **evidence_blocked + structured_impasse copy byte-identical**; `accounts_differ` + `no_current_pathway` stay DEFERRED (no producer / not a state). **NEW `__tests__/uxImpasse002LatentSubtypes.test.ts` (34)** — identity mapping, map totality over 13, the 11-entry priority at the exact indices == ALL_V4, end-to-end producer→display, deferred subtypes never surface, label parity (node chip == distribution segment), no chip soup, Inspect + next-move ban-list clean, evidence_blocked byte-identical regression, fallback (no signal → open / exhausted → structured_impasse), next-move dominant labels. **INVERTED the deferral-proof assertions** in `uxImpasse001Copy` / `mediatorBoardState` / `mediatorPrecedence` (the §6-named files) plus `uxNextMove001SuggestMyNextMove` / `nodeMediatorMarkers` / `uxImpasse001Rail` / `uxMediator003EvidenceDetail` / `uxMediator005DisagreementSheet` (additional deferral-proof blocks for the same two states; required to keep those suites green). **842 suites / 31,841 tests (31,839 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped; +1 suite / +37 tests over the UX-BOARD-RAIL-004 baseline 841 / 31,804; the LIFE-001 `pointLifecycleModel` 30ms wall-clock perf test flakes under full-parallel load — passes 76/76 isolated, unrelated to this display-only delta).** typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean · `npm run web:build` exit 0 (773 modules). No xAI / Anthropic / X API / Supabase write / service-role / migration / Edge / MCP / provider / submit / route / persistence change; no dependency / app.json / lockfile / .env change; no banned copy (every surfaced string is a point-state structural description). See `docs/designs/UX-IMPASSE-002.md`.

**2026-06-19 — UX-BOARD-RAIL-004 SN-1 (issue #730 — group the three bottom-chrome surfaces into one calm board-bottom region; placement/grouping ONLY, NO behavior / data / semantics / topology / A-B-placement change):** New PURE presentational wrapper `src/features/arguments/BoardBottomChrome.tsx` (~70 lines, RN `View` only — zero hooks/handlers/state/derivation/network/text) groups the three loose bottom-chrome surfaces (`OpenIssuesRail` · `SeatAvailabilityStrip` · `ArgumentSideActionRail`) inside ONE container with a single top **geometry border** (`borderTopWidth: BORDER_WIDTH.sm` + `borderTopColor: SURFACE_TOKENS.border` — the same token pair `RoomBoardLayout.columnDivider` uses, never color-only) + a board-consistent gutter (`SPACING.m`/`SPACING.s`), `testID="board-bottom-chrome"`. `ArgumentGameSurface.tsx` now passes the `bottomChrome` slot THROUGH `<BoardBottomChrome>…</BoardBottomChrome>` instead of as a bare fragment — every `<OpenIssuesRail>` / `<SeatAvailabilityStrip>` / `<ArgumentSideActionRail>` subtree stays textually in-file with identical props, testIDs, the public-rooms-only seat guard, the `isAnyPanelOpen` OR-terms, and the source order Open Issues → seat → Side Action. **Single `deriveRoomMediatorBoardState(` stays ===1**; no logic above the `return` changed; no other slot (col1/col2/col3/col2Footer) touched; RAIL-003 segment nav (`DisagreementPointsRail.tsx`) UNTOUCHED. The A/B Open-Issues placement, the side-action col-fold, and the mutex-rewire remain DEFERRED (§6 of the design). **NEW `__tests__/uxBoardRail004BottomChrome.test.tsx` (11)** — `board-bottom-chrome` contains the three real child testIDs; all three reachable at phone/tablet/wide; top boundary is a real `borderTopWidth` (geometry, not color-only); wrapper renders no text / no hooks / no derivation; surface wires through the wrapper with single-derivation ===1 + child source-order preserved. **`__tests__/uxBoardRail002Topology.test.tsx` extended additively (+12)** — bottomChrome slot still mounts at all bands and resolves through `board-bottom-chrome`; column count (1/2/2/3/3) + col1/col2/col3 placement unchanged. **841 suites / 31,804 tests (31,803 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped; +1 suite / +22 tests over the RAIL-003 baseline 840 / 31,782).** typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean · `npm run test` exit 0 · `npm run web:build` exit 0 (773 modules). No xAI / Anthropic / X API / Supabase write / service-role / migration / Edge / MCP / provider / submit / route / persistence change; no package install; no dependency; no banned copy (the wrapper renders zero text). See `docs/designs/UX-BOARD-RAIL-004.md`.

**2026-06-19 — UX-BOARD-RAIL-003 (issue #728 — Disagreement Points distribution-segment navigation; DisagreementPointsRail UI interaction ONLY, NO board topology / mediator derivation / room semantics / submit change):** The distribution strip in `src/features/mediator/DisagreementPointsRail.tsx` becomes a **local navigation control** over the rail's own row list. Behavior: **FOCUS + SCROLL, not a hard filter.** Each distribution-bar segment AND each legend item is now a `Pressable` (`accessibilityRole="button"`, `accessibilityState={{ selected }}`, `TOUCH_TARGET.hitSlopAll` on the 18px bar / `minHeight:44` Pressable wrap on the legend) — a11y label `Jump to <plainLabel> points: <count> of <total>` (unselected) / `Showing <plainLabel> points…` (selected), all built from the ban-list-clean `plainLabel`, never the snake_case `displayState`. A new local `selectedSegment: V4MediatorStateCode | null` state drives it: on press → find the first live point whose `v4DisplayStateFor(state)` matches (chronological order preserved, `firstMatchingPointId`), scroll the rail's OWN `ScrollView` (`disagreement-points-rail-scroll`, via a new `scrollRef` + per-row offsets captured through `onLayout` on each row wrapper) to that row with `scrollTo({ y, animated: !reducedMotion })`, and set `showAll` so a matching point past the 6-row cap is revealed. **Selected/in-view treatment is non-color-only:** the selected segment gets a top accent rule + a `▸` glyph in its count; the selected legend item gets an underline rule + `▸`; a `Showing: <plainLabel>` header anchor (`disagreement-points-rail-showing`, role=header) names the focused group as text; each matching row widens its left accent rule + renders a `▸ In view` marker (`disagreement-points-rail-inview-<id>`). A dashed **"Show all points"** reset (`disagreement-points-rail-show-all`) clears the selection (every row reads as in view again) and returns to the top; collapsing the rail also clears it. **Reduced motion** → `animated:false` scroll; selection still applies. **Preserved invariants:** priority order stays `V4_PRIMARY_STATE_PRIORITY` (never count-rank; `buildDisagreementDistribution` untouched), counts subordinate (no rank/percentage), `onJump` "View in timeline →" + state badges + "Move forward:" + impasse/bridge/evidence rows + empty/unavailable states + dormant chime-in slot all unchanged, sheet (phone) vs pane (tablet/wide) presentation preserved. 3 new ban-list-clean copy atoms in `mediatorRailCopy.ts` (`showAllPoints`, `showingPrefix`, `inViewMarker`). **NEW `__tests__/uxBoardRail003SegmentNav.test.tsx` (20)** — segments+legend interactive/role=button/named-as-jump-not-scoring, ≥44px targets, selection focuses + scrolls to first matching row (ScrollView mock captures `scrollTo`), non-color-only markers, v4 priority order kept (not count-rank), counts subordinate, onJump+badge+"Move forward:" intact, reset clears, dormant chime-in inert, sheet+pane preserved, ban-list clean. Regression green: `uxBoardRail002Topology` / `uxMediator005*` / `uxBoardReadability001` / `DisagreementPointsRail` (115/115). **840 suites / 31,782 tests (31,781 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped; +1 suite / +20 tests over the QUICK-BRAND-LOCKUP-003 baseline 839 / 31,762).** typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean · `npm run test` exit 0 · `npm run web:build` exit 0 (bundle produced, 768 modules). Proof board topology untouched: `RoomBoardLayout` / `ArgumentGameSurface` not in diff; single `deriveRoomMediatorBoardState` stays single (rail is a pure consumer; the only scroll model touched is the rail's OWN ScrollView); one active-node chip unchanged. No xAI / Anthropic / X API / Supabase write / service-role / migration / Edge / MCP / provider / submit / route / persistence change; no package install; no `v4DisplayStateFor` / display-map / state-code change; no rail relocation. See `docs/designs/UX-BOARD-RAIL-003.md`.

**2026-06-19 — QUICK-BRAND-LOCKUP-003 (issue #724 — isolated b/w bird + larger gold wordmark CivilDiscourse lockup; brand asset + aspect re-cut + test wiring + favicon freeze, UI/asset only, NO schema / Edge / scoring / AI / secrets / app.json / native-icon / resolver-logic change):** The horizontal CivilDiscourse lockup was re-cut from the gold/cream **duotone 960×342** lockup to an **isolated black/white bird + a LARGER gold wordmark** (full "C" preserved) + gold glow, exported at **1400×331** 8-bit RGBA (PNG colorType 6, 235,931 B). The operator placed the new PNG at both canonical lockup paths BEFORE this card; both `assets/branding/lockup-horizontal.png` (Sign In hero) and `assets/branding/civic-discourse-logo.png` (app masthead) are **byte-identical** (confirmed at the Metro layer too — both bundle at the SAME content hash `50b0ac5c…`, 236 kB each). **Aspect ratio change 960/342 (≈2.807) → 1400/331 (≈4.230):** the new lockup is WIDER per unit height. Updated `LOGO_ASPECT_RATIO` (`src/components/AppHeader.tsx`) and `SIGNIN_LOCKUP_ASPECT_RATIO` (`src/features/auth/signInLockupModel.ts`) from `960 / 342` → `1400 / 331`, plus recomputed comment figures (prominent width 808→1218, compact 135→203, intrinsic height 342→331, fit threshold ≳832→≳1242, 390-phone height 130→86). **`resolveMastheadLogoHeightPx` LOGIC + CONSTANTS UNCHANGED** (PROMINENT 288, MIN_PHONE 64, MAX_PHONE 160, compact 48, header budget 24): its `min(PROMINENT_288, widthFit)` already returns prominent-where-it-fits + width-fits otherwise. The wider aspect simply moves the prominent-288 threshold from viewport ~1024 to ~1280 (288×4.230≈1218 needs available ≥1218 → viewport ≳1242; 1280 → 288 ✓, 1024 → width-fits to 236). Test assertions that pinned the prominent 288 at 1024 are updated to 1280 (a correct prominent-where-it-fits threshold update, NOT a weakening — every `height × aspect ≤ available` no-overflow assertion is retained and still passes). Eight test files updated to the new aspect; `quickBrandLockup002.test.ts` dim assertions 960×342→1400×331 + size ceiling 200KB→500KB; **NEW `__tests__/quickBrandLockup003.test.ts`** (13) — card-named authoritative guard for 1400×331 RGBA + byte-identical, aspect 1400/331, the prominent-288 threshold (1280→288, 1024→<288) with no-overflow at every band + the phone floor, and the favicon/native FREEZE. **Favicon FREEZE verified zero-diff vs main** — `app.json` `expo.web.favicon` still `./assets/branding/civildiscourse-favicon.png`, favicon PNG unchanged 512×512, native icon / adaptive-icon / splash + `civildiscourse-mark.png` byte-unchanged, `dist/index.html` favicon link unchanged `<link rel="icon" href="/favicon.ico" />`. Zero stale *active* `960/342` references remain (only provenance comments + the historical 002 design doc). **839 suites / 31,762 tests (31,760 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped; +13 tests / +1 suite over the QUICK-BRAND-LOCKUP-002 baseline; the LIFE-001 `pointLifecycleModel` 30ms wall-clock perf test flakes under full-parallel load — passes 76/76 isolated, unrelated to this asset-only delta).** typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean · `npm run web:build` exit 0 (both lockups bundle at one hash; favicon path unchanged in dist). No xAI / Anthropic / X API / Supabase write / service-role / migration / Edge / deploy; `app.json` + `civildiscourse-favicon.png` + native icons + `civildiscourse-mark.png` byte-unchanged; no resolver-logic change; no Sign In copy / room / mediator / board / submit change; no package install. See `docs/designs/QUICK-BRAND-LOCKUP-003.md`.

**2026-06-19 — QUICK-BRAND-LOCKUP-002 (issue #722 — new gold/cream duotone CivilDiscourse lockup; brand asset + aspect re-cut + test wiring + favicon freeze, UI/asset only, NO schema / Edge / scoring / AI / secrets / app.json / native-icon change):** The horizontal CivilDiscourse lockup was re-cut from the gold **800×260** art to a gold/cream **duotone 960×342** lockup (source `civil_discourse.png` → `#C6A15B` gold + `#F5EDE0` cream + soft gold glow, 8-bit RGBA / PNG colorType 6, ~164 KB). The operator placed the new PNG at both canonical lockup paths BEFORE this card; both `assets/branding/lockup-horizontal.png` (Sign In hero) and `assets/branding/civic-discourse-logo.png` (app masthead) are **byte-identical** (confirmed at the Metro layer too — both bundle at the same content hash). **Aspect ratio change 800/260 (≈3.077) → 960/342 (≈2.807):** the new lockup is NARROWER per unit height, so rendered width at any height is SMALLER — overflow risk only decreases. Updated `LOGO_ASPECT_RATIO` (`src/components/AppHeader.tsx`) and `SIGNIN_LOCKUP_ASPECT_RATIO` (`src/features/auth/signInLockupModel.ts`) from `800 / 260` → `960 / 342`, plus recomputed comment figures (prominent width 886→808, compact 148→135, intrinsic height 260→342, fit threshold ≳910→≳832, 768-tablet fit 241→265, 390-phone height 118→130). Seven test files updated to the new aspect; the `SIGNIN_LOCKUP_ASPECT_RATIO > 3` floor relaxed to `> 2.5`. **NEW `__tests__/quickBrandLockup002.test.ts`** (16) — both lockups parse to 960×342 colorType 6 RGBA via IHDR + byte-identical; rendered aspect is 960/342 (constant + AppHeader source + resolver behavior); **favicon FREEZE** — `app.json` `expo.web.favicon` still `./assets/branding/civildiscourse-favicon.png`, the favicon PNG unchanged 512×512, native icon / adaptive-icon / splash paths untouched, no icon repointed at a lockup. Zero stale *active* `800/260` references remain (only provenance comments). **838 suites / 31,749 tests (31,748 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped; +2 suites / +17 tests over the 836 / 31,732 UX-BOARD-READABILITY-001 baseline).** typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean · `npm run test` exit 0 · `npm run web:build` exit 0 (both lockups bundle; `dist/index.html` favicon link unchanged `<link rel="icon" href="/favicon.ico" />`). No xAI / Anthropic / X API / Supabase write / service-role / migration / Edge / deploy; `app.json` + `civildiscourse-favicon.png` + native icons + `civildiscourse-mark.png` byte-unchanged; no Sign In copy / room / mediator / board / submit change; no package install (`.tmp/` scratch tool never staged). See `docs/designs/QUICK-BRAND-LOCKUP-002.md`.

**2026-06-19 — UX-BOARD-READABILITY-001 (issue #718 — mediator board readability + density polish; signed-in room style/spacing/copy-presentation ONLY, NO topology / room / mediator / submit semantics / rail nav / data / API change):** Fixes the inverted type hierarchy where the most load-bearing text on each room surface was the SMALLEST. **Room chrome** (`DebateDetailHeader.tsx`): room title 13/14/15 → **16/17/18** (phone/tablet/wide, file-local literals + explicit lineHeight = fontSize+6); `containerPaddingVertical` (4/6/8) + `rowMinHeight` (36/38/42) UNCHANGED so the strip-height cap (≤48/56/64, `uxOneOneTwoCompactStripHeight`) holds; phone chip floor 10 → 11; Leave glyph/label lifted one step. **Disagreement rail** (`DisagreementPointsRail.tsx`): title → `popoutHeading` (13/18, drop uppercase/letterSpacing); `nextStep` → `popoutBody` (12/16); `jumpHint`/`evidenceLine`/`blockedPathLine`/`activeWord` 10 → 11 (`chipLabel`); `rowMain` padding s→m + gap 2→4; `distributionBar` 14→18; header string `'Disagreement points · N total'` + `expandedRootPane` border block byte-identical/intact. **Selected-node centre** (`TimelineSelectedReadoutPanel.tsx`): `bodyLine` 11→**13** + lineHeight 18 + #e2e8f0 + **numberOfLines 1→2** (the readable centre); `kindLine` demoted to eyebrow (#94a3b8); `parentExcerptLine` distinguished (#94a3b8/lh15); `compactBody` gap 1→3; 5 line-style names + `marginTop:8` + no-row/no-rail-import preserved. **Mediator inspect** (`MediatorNodeInspectDetail.tsx` stateLabel → popoutHeading; `SelectedNodeInspectDrawer.tsx` section marginBottom s→m). **Mediator readout** (`ArgumentScoreTracker.tsx`): title 10→11 sentence-case (visible `'Mediator readout'` + aria byte-identical, only `textTransform` dropped); tile spacing 8→10 / gap 6→8 / minWidth 160→180. **Timeline** (`ArgumentTimelineMap.tsx`): node pills 9→11, chipText 8→10, bandLabel 10→11, timestamp #64748b→#94a3b8; presentation-only relabels `Root`→`Opening`, `detached`→`Off-thread` (model/a11y strings UNCHANGED); band overlay opacity LEFT at 0.6 (pinned by `uxOneOneTwoChromeLayerRemovals`, not an authorized relaxation — see design "Implementer note"); ≥6 hitSlopAll sites + info-icon hitSlop preserved, no `left:4,right:4` (uxMobile001). **AIG dock** (`ArgumentGameSurface.tsx`): `menuTriggerRow` paddingBottom 4→8, badge 10→11, `styles.body` padding 8→12; board-menu-trigger-* testIDs + Act/Inspect/Go + col1/col2 order verbatim; board derived ONCE. **Layout** (`RoomBoardLayout.tsx`): additive `paddingHorizontal:12` gutter on tablet/wide columns; paneColumn 380 + 1px columnDivider UNCHANGED (uxBoardRail002Topology). **Bubble card** (`ArgumentBubbleCard.tsx`): micro-type 10→11. **Two operator-authorized relax-with-NOTE boundary edits:** removed `DebateDetailHeader.tsx` from `uxOneOneFiveReadOnlyBoundary` (dated NOTE), and relaxed the `bodyLine numberOfLines={1}` pin in `uxOneOneTwoReadoutCompactMode` to `{2}` (dated NOTE). **Tests:** new `__tests__/uxBoardReadability001.test.tsx` (27 — title ≥16/band, bodyLine ≥13 + numberOfLines 2, rail title distinct tier, old game copy absent + 'Mediator readout' present, ban-list/snake_case scan over changed visible strings, topology pane 380 + divider intact, Act/Inspect/Go reachable, rail mounted, no NodeLabelStrip JSX, bubble micro-type ≥11). **836 suites / 31,732 tests (31,731 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped; +1 suite / +26 tests over the 835 / 31,706 UX-ROOM-CHROME-001 baseline).** typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean · `npm run test` exit 0 · `npm run web:build` exit 0 (bundle produced, "Supabase is not configured" expected). No xAI / Anthropic / X API / Supabase write / service-role / migration / Edge / MCP / provider / submit / route change; no topology/breakpoint/pane-width/divider change; Sign In / AuthScreen untouched. Deferred: CollapsedComposerStrip mainLabel (zero-diff, low value); distribution-segment navigation → UX-BOARD-RAIL-003; column re-flow / breakpoint / pane-width / divider topology → UX-BOARD-RAIL-003/004; admin-table TABLE_WIDTH overflow. See `docs/designs/UX-BOARD-READABILITY-001.md`.

**2026-06-19 — UX-ROOM-CHROME-001 (issue #716 — compact signed-in room chrome + de-game mediator-board copy; UI/copy/test only, NO backend / migration / RLS / Edge / MCP / provider / classifier / submit / engine change, NO deploy):** Two small, local, safe-now changes so the signed-in room reads as a slim mediator surface, not a tall game scoreboard. **(1) Compact signed-in masthead** — `src/components/AppHeader.tsx` gains ONE additive `compact?: boolean` prop (default `false` → byte-identical to today: the prominent ~288 px gold lockup, width-capped per band, so every existing caller + pinned test stays green). When `compact === true` the SAME gold lockup (`DEFAULT_LOGO` — no new asset import) renders at a COMPACT height via the new `COMPACT_LOGO_HEIGHT_PX = 48` constant; `resolveMastheadLogoHeightPx(band, viewportWidth, compact=false)` honors it on every band as `min(48, widthFit)` (still width-capped → rendered width = height × `LOGO_ASPECT_RATIO` never exceeds the available header width, no horizontal overflow), the anchored tagline is omitted, and the oversized prominent header padding is tightened via a new `rootWithNavCompact` style whose `minHeight` tracks the compacted logo (not the 296 px prominent minHeight). The logo `accessibilityLabel="CivilDiscourse"` / aria, `navSlot`, and `rightSlot` all keep working in compact. `App.tsx` passes `compact` ONLY to the signed-in shell masthead (the `navSlot={<AppPrimaryNav…/>}` `<AppHeader>`); the bare/transient `<AppHeader>` (unconfigured / invite / callback states) stays prominent; AuthScreen / the Sign In hero are untouched. **(2) De-game the score-tracker copy** — `src/features/arguments/ArgumentScoreTracker.tsx` visible title `"Where the points stand · gameplay analysis"` → **`"Mediator readout"`** (and its `accessibilityLabel` `"where the points stand"` → `"mediator readout"`); the header doc comment is reframed to mediator-readout language. The per-participant standing bands, trend arrows, sparklines, `testID="argument-score-tracker"`, component name, and `argumentScoreModel` are all UNCHANGED (rename deferred). **Tests:** new `__tests__/uxRoomChrome001CompactHeaderAndMediatorReadout.test.tsx` (22 — compact-vs-prominent height math across 320/360/390/414/768/1280, no-overflow width-fit, compact render keeps logo+aria & omits tagline, prominent default unchanged, App.tsx wiring (exactly one compact site; bare/transient + AuthScreen excluded), Sign In tagline intact + QUICK-COPY-001 subline/footer absent, "Mediator readout" rendered + ban-list scan over visible output for gameplay/points stand/score/scoreboard/ranking/leaderboard/winner/loser, RoomBoardLayout topology + no-new-dep regression); `__tests__/uxCopyOneCivilDiscourseRebrand.test.ts` updated (the retired "Where the points stand · gameplay analysis" assertion → "Mediator readout"). **835 suites / 31,706 tests (31,705 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped; +1 suite / +23 tests over the 834 / 31,683 UX-BOARD-RAIL-002 baseline).** typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean · `npm run test` exit 0 · `npm run web:build` exit 0 (bundle produced, logo asset resolves). No xAI / Anthropic / X API / Supabase write / service-role / migration / Edge / deploy. Deferred (out of scope): ArgumentScoreTracker / `argumentScoreModel` rename; Sign In hero redesign; new logo asset; RoomBoardLayout column-count / breakpoint rewrite; DisagreementPointsRail relocation; rail / Open-Issues / Side-Action / chime-in changes; standing-band-label copy (doctrine-compliant strength bands, LEFT untouched). See issue #716.

**2026-06-19 — UX-BOARD-RAIL-002 (issue #714 — persistent mediator board topology; UI/layout/test only, NO backend / migration / provider / submit / classifier / engine change):** Turns the single `flex:1` vertical room column into a band-driven 1 / 2 / 3-column board. **NEW `src/features/arguments/RoomBoardLayout.tsx`** — a PURE presentational flex grid (RN `View`/flex only, zero hooks/handlers/derivation, no new dependency, no `<Text>` of its own). Phone (<600) -> 1 column (today's vertical stack, byte-identical); tablet (600-1279) -> 2 columns (left spine `col1`+`col2`+`col2Footer` . 380 px right pane `col3`); wide (>=1280) -> 3 columns (`col1` spine `flex:1.2` . `col2` readout+footer `flex:1` . 380 px `col3` pane). Column count is driven by the already-resident `headerBand` (passed in as a prop -- no second `useHeaderBreakpoint`, no new breakpoint helper); column boundaries carry a 1px geometry border (`borderLeftWidth`, not color-only); the re-flow across a band boundary is an instant React render (reduce-motion-safe by construction). **`ArgumentGameSurface.tsx`** is a pure render-tree re-parent into the wrapper's slots (O-1 order col1 . col2 . col2Footer . col3 . bottomChrome . overlays) -- JSX moved only, NO hook/handler/state/prop change: the single `deriveRoomMediatorBoardState` derivation (one call site) is consumed by all three columns and never re-derived; col1 JSX stays before col2 in source order (`uxOneOneTwoChromeLayerRemovals` indexOf pins); exactly one `mediator-node-marker-active` chip; the `inspectVisible && activeMessageId ? (<SelectedNodeInspectDrawer` gate + the single `activeMessageId` selection state unchanged; Act/Inspect/Go + the three popouts re-parent into `col2Footer` with all testIDs/handlers preserved. **`DisagreementPointsRail.tsx`** gains ONE additive prop `presentation?: 'sheet' | 'pane'` (default `'sheet'` = byte-identical phone bottom sheet; `'pane'` = expanded-by-default docked column child that ignores `isAnyPanelOpen`, suppresses the entry animation, drops the bottom-overlay cues, and carries a left geometry border via a new `expandedRootPane` style). The bottom-rail mutual-exclusion OR-terms are UNCHANGED (DEFER -- the pane ignores `isAnyPanelOpen`, so correctness does not depend on the rewire). **+42 new tests** (`__tests__/uxBoardRail002Topology.test.tsx` 37; `__tests__/DisagreementPointsRail.test.tsx` +5 pane/sheet cases). **834 suites / 31683 passing (+1 skipped, pre-existing)**, typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean, `npm run web:build` exit 0. No xAI / Anthropic / X API / Supabase write / service-role / migration / Edge / deploy. See `docs/designs/UX-BOARD-RAIL-002.md`.

**2026-06-19 — UX-FEEDBACK-001 (issue #690 — restrained STATIC current-state progress notes; display-only, NO gamification, NO transition language, NO persistence):** Adds a small, restrained, STATE-REFLECTIVE feedback layer that makes a disagreement feel quietly satisfying when its STRUCTURE is in a more-resolved shape — never a reward, rating, score, like, vote, ranking, popularity, streak, badge, applause, or transition moment. The reward is clarity, not applause. Operator RE-LOCKED scope: **STATIC current-state CONFIRMATION ONLY** (a point IS anchored, a claim IS narrowed, a concession IS preserved, a source path IS identified, the next useful move IS this) — never temporal/transition framing (no "cleaner now"/"clearer"/"moved forward"/"clarified"/"just"/"improved"; the board is a STATELESS re-projection with no before→after delta). **NEW `src/features/mediator/feedbackForMediatorProgress.ts`** (pure TS, no React/I/O): `feedbackForMediatorProgress(displayState, ctx?)` → one `MediatorProgressNote | null`, total over the nine v4 display states, deterministic.
