Precedence: selection surface + anchored non-root node → **"Point anchored."**; inspect surface concession (on a `narrowed` point, `clusterState === 'conceded'`) → **"Concession preserved."**; `narrowed` → **"Claim narrowed."**; an identified source path (a debt on the point with `!isOpen && !isBlocked`) → **"Source path identified."**; a non-empty `nextMovesForState` set → **"Next useful move: <dominant move>"**; `structured_impasse` → **null (cross-ref UX-IMPASSE-001 — no second impasse render)**; all other / insufficient → **null** (restraint by default). Exports `_forbiddenFeedbackTokens()` = the mediator ban-list + a TRANSITION guard (`just`/`now`/`cleaner`/`clearer`/`clarified`/`moved forward`/`improved`/…) + an applause/rating guard (`like`/`vote`/`score`/`ranking`/`leaderboard`/`popularity`/`applause`/`trophy`/`badge`/`streak`/`confetti`/…) + a person-attribution guard. **NEW `src/features/mediator/MediatorProgressNote.tsx`** (read-only presentational): renders ONE note as informational `<Text>` — NOT a `Pressable`, NO `onPress`, NO tap-to-rate chip, NO "Save feedback" button; restrained gold (`dignified`) / indigo (`progress`) / neutral tone carried by a left-rule GEOMETRY so color is never the only signal (grayscale-legible); no confetti/badge/trophy/Image/animation; `null` note → `null` render. **`src/features/mediator/index.ts`:** exports the helper + note (component re-exported as `MediatorProgressNoteView` to avoid a data-type name collision). **`src/features/arguments/ArgumentGameSurface.tsx` (host mount only):** derives `activeNodeProgressSelectionNote` (default-visible "Point anchored." cue near the selected-node readout) + `activeNodeProgressInspectNote` (the narrowed/concession/source-path/next-move note in the existing Inspect "Move forward:" region) from the ALREADY-derived board (single-derivation invariant; never re-derived); imports nothing from the engine/pointStanding/argumentScoreModel; no new gate, no new mount site, no new primary chip (one-chip invariant holds), no topology/rail/timeline change, no persistence, no submit-path change. **Doctrine:** structural-acknowledgement only, person-neutral, advisory, engine is the sole gate; no truth/verdict/winner/loser/score/heat/popularity/like/credibility/intent token; insufficient signal → null. **Tests:** new `__tests__/feedbackForMediatorProgress.test.ts` (20 — totality/determinism, exact operator-locked copy, restraint default, NO transition/temporal phrasing, ban-list clean, gate-independence/no-write, frozen output) + `__tests__/uxFeedback001ProgressNote.test.tsx` (35 — render + null render, NO tap-to-rate/Save/like affordance, tone-via-geometry, ban-list rendered strings, 320–414px, host wiring source-scan, NO new primary chip, NO topology/rail/timeline change, NO persistence/submit, impasse not duplicated). **833 suites / 31,642 tests (31,641 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped; +2 suites / +55 tests over the 831 / 31,585 UX-IMPASSE-001 baseline).** `web:build` exits 0 (`dist/index.html` produced, no resolve errors) · typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean. NO transition-event detection · NO before→after diff · NO rating/score/like/vote/ranking/popularity/leaderboard system · NO new primary state chip · NO board topology / rail relocation / timeline geometry / scroll / virtual-list change · NO persistence/migration/RLS/Supabase/service-role · NO classifier/MCP/provider/Family J/K · NO submit-path / room / seat / chime-in change · NO route/model/table/type rename · NO app.json / package / dep · NO deployment · NO netlify-prod · no Anthropic/xAI/X API call by Claude. Deferred: the past-tense/"just happened" transition moments (Definition named / per-response "moved it forward" / "just resolved") → `UX-FEEDBACK-DELTA-001` (needs a turn-delta the board does not carry — do not synthesize); the interactive tap-to-rate clarity chip set + "Save feedback" → operator-gated clarity-annotation persistence slice (GATE-C: private annotation, RLS, never a public tally / never counted as popularity / never fed to standing); standalone board destination → UX-BOARD-RAIL-002. See `docs/designs/UX-FEEDBACK-001.md`.

**2026-06-19 — UX-IMPASSE-001 (issue #689 — dignified structured-impasse states; COPY-ONLY, display-only, no display-map flip / no topology / no persistence):** Makes the structured-impasse family of mediator states read as a calm, dignified, *complete* destination — never a failure, deadlock, defeat, or verdict — while keeping the door open to reopen. A language + display clarification over the shipped mediator stack; no new screen, no board-topology change, no classifier, no persistence. **`src/features/mediator/mediatorPlainLanguage.ts`:** aligned `MEDIATOR_STATE_HELPER.structured_impasse` to "The disagreement is preserved. No available next move would test this point further yet." and `MEDIATOR_STATE_HELPER.narrowed` to "The disagreement is smaller now. Continue on the remaining point." (concession framed as PROGRESS, not defeat). New frozen `IMPASSE_SUBTYPE_COPY` block keyed by the v4 DISPLAY state it dresses (`structured_impasse` / `evidence_blocked` (KEEP shipped #003) / `narrowed` / `no_current_pathway` — the last folds into the single Structured-impasse chip per Q4, no chip soup) carrying ban-list-clean lead/help/next; every `chip` mirrors `MEDIATOR_STATE_COPY` (chip labels UNCHANGED so UX-MEDIATOR-002/005 label tests stay green). New **dormant** constants `VALUE_TRADEOFF_DISPLAY_COPY` ("Different priorities") + `KEY_DETAIL_UNAVAILABLE_DISPLAY_COPY` ("Key detail unavailable"), intentionally **UNWIRED** (commented `// UX-IMPASSE-002 (#710): dormant — … surfacing requires a v4DisplayStateFor map flip (deferred).`) — not referenced from any render path. **`src/features/mediator/mediatorRailCopy.ts`:** new `impassePreserved` + `impasseReopen` rail-row copy. **`src/features/mediator/DisagreementPointsRail.tsx`:** the rail row for a `structured_impasse` point whose pathway has no available step (`anyAvailable === false` → today an EMPTY "Move forward:") now renders the dignified preserve + reopen line in the existing row body — COPY on the existing surface row, no new row, no relocation, no topology change; guarded so it never renders on a non-impasse point or on an impasse point that still has an available step. **`nextMovesForState.ts` audited — zero functional diff** (impasse/narrowed labels already match §4: "Preserve the disagreement" / "Reopen with a source, definition, or narrower claim" / "Continue on the smaller point" / "Concede the resolved part"). **`V4_DISPLAY_STATE_BY_CODE` UNCHANGED — `value_tradeoff`→`open`, `key_detail_unavailable`→`evidence_blocked` (no new primary chip for either); `accounts_differ` left unproduced (no producer).** **Doctrine:** impasse is a structural state, never a truth/verdict; engine is the sole gate; the board never blocks posting; person-neutral advisory copy; insufficient signal collapses to Open (never a stronger impasse); ban-list clean (no deadlock/failure/verdict/winner/loser/score/credibility/intent/tone tokens). **Tests:** new `__tests__/uxImpasse001Copy.test.ts` + `__tests__/uxImpasse001Rail.test.tsx` (33 tests — §4 verbatim copy + dignity + ban-list over every visible line; narrowed-as-progress; Evidence-blocked #003 byte-identical regression guard; DEFERRAL PROOF that value_tradeoff/key_detail are NOT surfaced as new primary chips (map unchanged, no chip renders); dormant constants exist but unwired; insufficient→Open; rail empty "Move forward:" → dignified preserve/reopen line; non-impasse + available-pathway guards; single jump Pressable + testIDs intact). **831 suites / 31,585 tests (31,584 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped, +2 suites / +33 tests over the 829 / 31,551 UX-NEXT-MOVE-001 baseline).** `web:build` exits 0 (`dist/index.html` produced, no resolve errors) · typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean. No `V4_DISPLAY_STATE_BY_CODE` change · no board/timeline/rail-structure topology / lane / branch / column / scroll / virtual-list change · no new screen · no precedence (001) logic change · no classifier/MCP/provider · no persistence/migration/Supabase/service-role · no submit-path / room / seat / chime-in change · no route/model/table/type rename · no app.json / package / dep · no deployment · no netlify-prod · no Anthropic/xAI/X API call by Claude. Deferred: surfacing `value_tradeoff` as "Different priorities" + `key_detail_unavailable` distinctly (UX-IMPASSE-002 #710, one-line map flip + chip-label test) · `accounts_differ` recollection classifier/persistence (future card) · standalone full-screen impasse destination / board topology (UX-BOARD-RAIL-002) · "Save resolution"/"Set reopen trigger" persisted writes (GATE-C) · tappable next-move chooser (UX-NEXT-MOVE-002). See `docs/designs/UX-IMPASSE-001.md`.

**2026-06-19 — UX-NEXT-MOVE-001 (issue #688 — "What would move this forward?" selected-node guidance; LOCAL, display-only, no GATE-C):** Fills the reserved `SelectedNodeInspectDrawer` **"Move forward:"** slot (UX-SELECTED-NODE-001 §17 deferral) with a small read-only **"What would move this forward?"** card showing the ordered structural next moves for the active node's v4 display state — converting the already-derived mediator state into next MOVES, never into a conclusion. **New pure helper `src/features/mediator/nextMovesForState.ts`:** `nextMovesForState(state)` is a deterministic, total switch over the nine `V4MediatorStateCode`s returning a dominant-first `ReadonlyArray<NextMove>` (`{ id, label, rationale, stepCode, available, isDominant }`) per the operator-locked §3 map (needs_evidence→"Ask for a source"/"Add evidence" · evidence_blocked→"Mark evidence unavailable"/"Branch the provable part"/"Name what kind of record would test this point" · definition_not_shared→"Define the key term" · scope_mismatch→"Narrow the claim"/"Branch the provable part"/"Respond to the exact point"/"Accept the narrower scope" · missing_mechanism→"Add the missing link"/"Ask for the mechanism" · narrowed→"Continue on the smaller point"/"Concede the resolved part" · accounts_differ→"Separate memory from records"/"Name what could verify it" · structured_impasse→"Preserve the disagreement"/"Reopen with a source, definition, or narrower claim" · open→"Respond to the exact point"/"Ask a clarifying question"). The `stepCode` mirrors the shipped `pathwayForState` codes (the dominant/first-available move shares ONE source with the Act target); `available:false` (`await_record` for evidence_blocked / accounts_differ / impasse) renders as guidance copy, never a live action. Insufficient/unknown state collapses to the neutral Open set. Plus `_forbiddenNextMoveTokens()` (mediator ban-list + person-attribution tokens). Pure TS — no React/Supabase/fetch/MCP/clock/randomness. **New read-only card `src/features/mediator/MediatorNextMovesCard.tsx`:** title exactly **"What would move this forward?"** (`accessibilityRole="header"`) + a token-free structural lead (O-2 rephrase: "These are actions that could move the shape of the disagreement forward — not conclusions.") + dominant-first move rows (label + one-line structure-only rationale). **Guidance-only (O-1): `onSelectMove` OPTIONAL, undefined by default** → rows render as guidance labels (no tappable chooser — that is UX-NEXT-MOVE-002); when supplied + the move is actionable, the row is a `Pressable` (role=button, label, `hitSlop` 12 → ≥44×44) that routes the `stepCode` to the host's EXISTING handler only (no new action semantics); unavailable rows are always non-pressable guidance. RN primitives only; reuses `designTokens` (no new hex); renders `null` for an empty list. **`ArgumentGameSurface.tsx` (~16 lines):** a memoized `activeNodeNextMoves = nextMovesForState(v4DisplayStateFor(activeNodeMediatorMarker.code))` (board derived ONCE, never re-derived; terminal `resolved_or_settled` → empty) fills the previously-unset `moveForward` slot with `<MediatorNextMovesCard moves={…}>` when non-empty (self-suppresses otherwise); ships guidance-only (no `onSelectMove` passed). The shipped inline "What would help next:" line in `MediatorNodeInspectDetail` is KEPT (O-3 — headline vs full set; zero churn). **Doctrine:** structural MOVES only — no truth/verdict/winner/loser/score/heat/popularity/credibility/intent token (ban-list test over all nine states' labels+rationales + the rendered card + the lead); reads only the display state (never standing/tone/temperature/engagement); imports nothing from the engine and gates nothing (the deterministic Constitution engine stays the sole acceptance gate); deterministic (same input deep-equals same output); §10a — surfaces machine Observations as moves, no sensitive composer-only mark reaches the card. **Tests:** new `__tests__/uxNextMove001SuggestMyNextMove.test.tsx` (44 tests — full nine-state §3 map dominant+alternates ordering, exactly-one-dominant-at-index-0, determinism, the four superset collapses via `v4DisplayStateFor`, insufficient→Open fallback, ban-list + snake_case + explicit §4-token exclusion + exact title, no-chip-soup/no-who-is-right, render dominant-emphasis + unavailable-as-guidance, guidance-only onSelectMove wiring, shared-source stepCode proof, 320/360/390/414 no-overflow + 44px hitSlop, host drawer-slot wiring source-scan, NO board/rail/timeline-topology change proof, sensitive-mark hidden). All shipped mediator/selected-node regression suites stay green (`uxMediator00*`, `uxSelectedNode001`, `nodeMediatorMarkers`, `MediatorNodeMarker`, `MediatorNodeInspectDetail`, `mediatorBoardState`, `mediatorPrecedence`, `suggestedMovesModel` — 263 tests). **829 suites / 31,552 tests (31,550 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped, +44 tests / +1 suite over the 828 / 31,508 UX-SELECTED-NODE-001 baseline; the LIFE-001 `pointLifecycleModel` 30ms wall-clock perf test flakes under full-parallel load — passes 76/76 isolated, unrelated to this pure-TS-UI delta).** `web:build` exits 0 (`dist/index.html` produced, 769 modules bundled, no resolve errors) · typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean. No board topology / rail placement / persistent-column change (`DisagreementPointsRail`/`ArgumentSideActionRail`/`OpenIssuesRail`/timeline mounts unchanged, absent from the diff) · no `deriveMediatorBoardState`/`mediatorBoardTypes`/`mediatorPlainLanguage`/`suggestedMovesModel` (ST-002) change · no precedence (001) / node-chip (002) / rail-structure (005) change · no discrete tappable chooser (UX-NEXT-MOVE-002) · no new persisted next-move state / GATE-C persistence · no new classifier/MCP/provider · no submit-path / room / seat / chime-in change · no supabase / schema / RLS / auth / migration / persistence · no app.json / package / dep · no deployment · no netlify-prod · no Anthropic/xAI/X API call by Claude. Deferred: the discrete tappable next-move chooser with new action semantics (UX-NEXT-MOVE-002) · the persisted "mark evidence unavailable" write (GATE-C card) · board topology / rail placement (UX-BOARD-RAIL-002) · impasse-specific treatment (UX-IMPASSE-001). See `docs/designs/UX-NEXT-MOVE-001.md`.

**2026-06-18 — UX-SELECTED-NODE-001 (issue #687 — selected-node "center of the room" treatment; LOCAL-ONLY scope, no GATE-C):** Composes the already-shipped mediator + readout surfaces into one coherent selected-node anatomy — a strictly LOCAL, in-the-selected-card delta. The global / room-level **left-rail recommendation is SUPERSEDED → UX-BOARD-RAIL-001 (#706)** (design doc amended at the top); this card touches NO room/timeline/board-column topology. **Delta:** (1) a restrained **GOLD selected-node card halo + an in-card LEFT-ACCENT stripe** on the `TimelineSelectedReadoutPanel` (`BRAND.accent.gold`/`goldSoft`/`goldBorder` — existing UX-BRAND-001 tokens, no new hex; distinct from the indigo `GLOW.activePath` system; the VG-004 timeline-node `GLOW.selectedHalo` cream token is left UNTOUCHED — it is VG-004-pinned). (2) the v4 **"Responding to this point"** anchor (top-anchor; O-2) with the structural `#N (kind)` sub-label (NEVER a synthesized display name, §7 R3) + the **parent excerpt** (`parentBodyPreview`, ≤120 chars — already on the SC-003 view-model, surfaced in compact for the first time; omitted at the root). (3) a read-only **"Go to parent point"** jump (reuses `setActiveMessageId`, the same local-state selection path the 005 rail uses). (4) exactly **ONE primary state chip** (no chip soup; unchanged from UX-MEDIATOR-002). (5) new pure presentational wrapper **`src/features/mediator/SelectedNodeInspectDrawer.tsx`** (O-4) sectioning the four already-mounted Inspect siblings — `MediatorNodeInspectDetail` · `NodeLabelInspectGroups` · `InspectOpenIssueDetail` · `MetadataDiffInspector` — into the v4 drawer's four named sections ("Why this state · Other structure notes · Move forward · History"); the siblings + every testID are composed, never modified. (6) **Act-dominant** Act/Inspect/Go dock (gold accent fill + heavier weight on Act; Inspect + Go stay ≥44px `minHeight`; routing/popout/key-badge gating untouched). **Doctrine:** read-only projection; engine remains sole gate; person-neutral structural copy; no truth/verdict/winner/loser/score/heat/popularity; sensitive composer-only Observations reach neither the node nor the drawer (boundary preserved); ban-list clean (snake_case + verdict). **Tests:** new `__tests__/uxSelectedNode001CenterOfRoom.test.tsx` (38 tests — anchor+excerpt present/root-omit/no-name; gold halo + in-card accent; go-to-parent read-only + ≥44px; four-section drawer + header roles + clean omit; drawer wiring preserves all four siblings; Act-dominant + 44px; NO room/timeline/board-rail topology in the diff; no chip soup; ban-list; sensitive-mark hidden; 320/360/390/414 no overflow). RECONCILED (intent preserved, not weakened): `uxOneOneFiveReadOnlyBoundary` (relaxed the `TimelineSelectedReadoutPanel.tsx` zero-diff path with a documented NOTE — the operator-authorized pattern; the panel's contract stays pinned by `uxOneOneTwoReadoutCompactMode` + `timelineReadoutBanList` + the new suite), `uxOneOneTwoChromeLayerRemovals` + `timelineReadoutNoRoute` (the mount gained the read-only `onGoToParent` prop; no action/submit/route callback), `uxOneOneFiveACallSiteWiring` (NodeLabelInspectGroups is now in the gated drawer's `structureNotes` slot). **828 suites / 31,508 tests (31,507 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped), +4 suites / +74 tests over the 824 / 31,433 baseline.** `web:build` exits 0 (`dist/index.html` produced, 767 modules bundled, no resolve errors) · typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean. No room-level rail / `DisagreementPointsRail` / `ArgumentSideActionRail` / `OpenIssuesRail` relocation · no timeline flex/width/geometry/topology change · no board-column layout · no scroll/virtual-list change · no precedence (001) / node-chip-selection (002) / rail-structure (005) change · no classifier/MCP/provider/persistence/migration/Supabase/service-role · no room/seat/chime-in/submit-path change · no route/model/table/type rename · no app.json / package / dep · no deployment · no netlify-prod · no Anthropic/xAI/X API call by Claude. Deferred: multi-chip next-move (UX-NEXT-MOVE-001) · impasse treatment (UX-IMPASSE-001) · cross-device QA matrix (UX-FEEDBACK-001 / UX-RESPONSIVE-V4-001 / UX-ACCESSIBILITY-001) · chime-in History data (UX-ROOM-1V1-CHIMEIN-001) · room-level rail/board topology (UX-BOARD-RAIL-001 #706). See `docs/designs/UX-SELECTED-NODE-001.md`.

**2026-06-18 — UX-MEDIATOR-005 (issue #686 — Disagreement Points rail v4 delta; FULL operator-confirmed delta; UI + tests only, no GATE-C):** EXTENDS the shipped `DisagreementPointsRail` (PRs #644-648, already mounted at `ArgumentGameSurface.tsx:2321`) — does NOT rebuild it and makes **no `ArgumentGameSurface` change** (the mount + once-derived `mediatorBoard` already exist). **(O-1) v4 badge projection:** each rail row's state badge now projects `point.state` through UX-MEDIATOR-001's `v4DisplayStateFor` (new `v4RowBadgeLabel` helper in the rail) so the row vocabulary matches the UX-MEDIATOR-002 node chip (`off_point`→"Scope mismatch", `key_detail_unavailable`→"Blocked evidence path"); `point.state` is unchanged for Inspect; a display projection of `resolved_or_settled` shows no badge. `'Definition needed'` is KEPT (UX-MEDIATOR-004 deferral). **(O-2) "Move forward:" lead-in:** the row "next step" lead-in renamed from `whatHelps` ("What would help next?") to new `moveForward: 'Move forward:'` in `mediatorRailCopy.ts`; the deprecated `whatHelps` key is kept exported (no consumer break — the only other "What would help next" string is the separate `MediatorNodeInspectDetail`, untouched). **State-distribution bar:** new pure helper `src/features/mediator/mediatorDistribution.ts` (`buildDisagreementDistribution` + `totalDistributionCount` + `DisagreementDistributionSegment`, exported via `index.ts`) buckets the already-selected live points by v4 display state, live-filters terminal/suppressed (`resolved_or_settled`), and orders by `V4_PRIMARY_STATE_PRIORITY` (NEVER count/votes/heat). The rail renders a flex-weighted `<View>`-segment bar (`disagreement-points-rail-distribution`) that is **NOT color-only** — each segment carries a count + `accessibilityLabel` ("Needs evidence: 4 of 12") and a compact text legend names every bucket for grayscale/screen-reader parity; the impasse segment reuses the rail's existing `SURFACE_TOKENS.focusRing` emphasis (no new hex). **(O-4a) Dormant chime-in slot:** the row reads an OPTIONAL `anchor.contributionKind` (no `PointAnchor` type change — read via a narrow extension interface) and renders `↳ chime-in` (`disagreement-points-rail-chimein-<id>`) ONLY when it equals `'chime_in'`; no such data ships today, so no row renders it. Never synthesized from absent data (doctrine §4); the chime-in DATA is owned by the unshipped UX-ROOM-1V1-CHIMEIN-001. Header now reads "Disagreement points · N total" (live count, no hard-coded 12). **(O-5)** rail stays collapsed-by-default; the shipped phone-sheet (`variant === 'sheet'`, <720) / tablet-side-panel (`variant === 'side'`, ≥720) surfaces are unchanged — no layout rewrite, no desktop right-column, no pan gesture. **Doctrine:** structure board, not a scoreboard — no winner/loser/score/heat/popularity; ordering is structural; one primary state per row (no chip-soup — the rail mounts no `NodeLabelStrip`/`AnnotationChipStrip`); no sensitive composer-only Observations in rows; read-only projection, engine remains sole gate; ban-list clean. **Tests:** new `__tests__/uxMediator005DisagreementSheet.test.tsx` (25 tests — distribution helper sum/priority-order/superset-projection/live-filter/empty; one-row-per-point + overflow; anchor jump; v4 badge projection; "Move forward:" present + old lead-in absent; "· N total" count framing; distribution bar segments + a11y labels + legend; no chip-soup; dormant chime-in default-off + on-with-data + principal-off; 320/360/390/414 sheet touch-safety; reduce-motion dismissible; no sensitive marks; ban-list + new-copy clean). All shipped mediator/rail suites stay green (`DisagreementPointsRail`/`disagreementPointsRail{Bridge,Evidence}`/`mediatorBoardState`/`mediatorPrecedence`/`nodeMediatorMarkers`/`MediatorNode*`/`uxMediator002NodeMarkup`/`definitionScopeBridgeDisplay`/`evidenceDebtDisplay`/`roomMediatorAdapter`). **825 suites / 31,433 tests (31,432 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped), +1 suite / +25 tests over the 824 / 31,408 baseline.** `web:build` exits 0 (`dist/index.html` produced, 766 modules bundled, no resolve errors) · typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean. No `ArgumentGameSurface`/layout/precedence-model/node-chip change · no room/seat/invite/chime-in semantics (slot is render-only) · no new classifier/MCP · no persistence/submission-gate change · no supabase / app.json / package / dep / migration / deploy / netlify-prod · no Anthropic/xAI/X API call by Claude. See `docs/designs/UX-MEDIATOR-005.md`.

**2026-06-18 — UX-MEDIATOR-002 (issue #683 — one-primary-state-chip node markup + Inspect; "collapse the chip soup"; UI-model + tests only, no GATE-C):** Collapses the per-node DOUBLE-MOUNT default view (the `MediatorNodeMarker` state chip stacked above the `NodeLabelStrip` Observation/Allegation chips + overflow) into **exactly ONE primary state chip per node**, with all the relocated detail living in the already-shipped Inspect overlay. **One-chip default (`ArgumentGameSurface.tsx`):** `NodeLabelStrip` is UNMOUNTED from the default view (and no longer imported); the active node renders a single `MediatorNodeMarker` chip, selected ONCE from the already-derived board (`activeNodeMediatorMarker = useMemo(getNodeMediatorMarker(mediatorBoard, activeMessageId))` — single-derivation invariant preserved, board consumed never re-derived). **O-1 (v4 vocabulary):** `getNodeMediatorMarker` (`nodeMediatorMarkers.ts`) now projects the selected internal code through UX-MEDIATOR-001's `v4DisplayStateFor` for the chip `code`/`label` (priority/selection still runs on the internal 13-code set); `off_point`→"Scope mismatch", `key_detail_unavailable`→"Blocked evidence path", `value_tradeoff`→display-suppressed (no chip); `point.state` unchanged for Inspect. The `'Definition needed'` visible string is KEPT (the rename is UX-MEDIATOR-004). **O-2 (chip-adjacent Inspect caret):** a `Pressable` (role `button`, label "Inspect this point", `accessibilityState.expanded`, `hitSlop` 12 → ≥44×44 effective) beside the chip opens the existing Inspect overlay via the shipped `setInspectVisible` plumbing (mutual-exclusion closes Act/Go); the chip itself stays non-interactive read-only `text`. **O-3 (new component `src/features/mediator/MediatorNodeInspectDetail.tsx`):** a small read-only block mounted ABOVE `NodeLabelInspectGroups` inside the same `inspectVisible && activeMessageId` gate, showing the mediator state label + helper sentence (`helperForMediatorState`) + the next-useful-move pathway (first AVAILABLE pathway step). **O-4:** the Disagreement Points rail is UNTOUCHED (deferred to UX-MEDIATOR-005). **Doctrine (§10a):** the one default chip is the machine Observation; Inspect keeps the "Machine Observations" / "User Allegations" groups separate + labelled (never generic "tags"); sensitive composer-only Observations (`shifts_to_person_or_intent` etc.) appear on NEITHER the chip NOR Inspect (Inspect's surface filter already excludes them — unchanged). No verdict/person/heat/popularity copy; ban-list clean. **Tests:** new `__tests__/uxMediator002NodeMarkup.test.tsx` (one-chip-default mount contract, caret a11y, Inspect-reveals-detail, nothing-deleted/uncapped, sensitive-on-neither-surface, v4-label + "Definition needed" deferral, ban-list) + new `__tests__/MediatorNodeInspectDetail.test.tsx`; reconciled `__tests__/nodeMediatorMarkers.test.ts` (O-1 projection) + the `__tests__/uxOneOneFiveACallSiteWiring.test.ts` "Edit B" block (was double-mount → now single-chip-default + content-in-Inspect, per design §5). **824 suites / 31,408 tests (31,407 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped), +2 suites / +30 tests over the 822 / 31,378 baseline.** `web:build` exits 0 (`dist/index.html` produced, 764 modules bundled, no resolve errors) · typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean. No precedence-model change (`deriveMediatorBoardState`/`mediatorBoardTypes` untouched beyond consuming `v4DisplayStateFor`) · no rail redesign · no `'Definition needed'`→"Definition not shared" rename (UX-MEDIATOR-004) · no `NodeLabelStrip` deletion (unmounted, file + tests + export intact) · no chime-in · no persistence · no MCP/classifier activation · no new state codes · no submission-gate/room-seat change · no supabase / app.json / package / dep / migration / deploy / netlify-prod · no Anthropic/xAI/X API call by Claude. See `docs/designs/UX-MEDIATOR-002.md`.

**2026-06-18 — UX-MEDIATOR-001 (issue #682 — deterministic mediator-state precedence; pure-TS model + tests only, no GATE-C):** RECONCILIATION of the shipped mediator projection (PRs #644-648), not a rebuild. `deriveMediatorBoardState` already collapses lifecycle + evidence-debt + persisted machine observations into exactly ONE `MediatorStateCode` per disagreement point; this card re-orders `decidePointState` to match the published v4 precedence and adds the total 13→9 display map. **`decidePointState` refactor (`src/features/mediator/deriveMediatorBoardState.ts`):** the early-return cascade is restructured into three phases — (1) resolution dominance short-circuit (a settled point is terminal), (2) `buildPointStateCandidates` (each candidate is an independent boolean keyed off the SAME deterministic / observation inputs as before — the per-signal detection is moved VERBATIM into one predicate), (3) Gate A (impasse demotion) + an ordered picker over a single explicit total order. This fixes two divergences from the v4 table: **D1** impasse was too LOW (now highest, but Gate A demotes it whenever any non-impasse, non-`open` candidate has an available resolution pathway → v4 "Impasse + any path remains → not impasse"); **D2** narrowed was too HIGH (now `needs_evidence` and every more-specific state outrank `narrowed` → v4 "Needs evidence + Narrowed → Needs evidence"). The internal 13-code `point.state` is UNCHANGED (Inspect / downstream consumers untouched); a parallel projection collapses it for display. **New types (`mediatorBoardTypes.ts`):** `V4MediatorStateCode` (the nine v4 display states), `ALL_V4_MEDIATOR_STATE_CODES`, `V4_PRIMARY_STATE_PRIORITY` (impasse → evidence_blocked → accounts_differ → definition_not_shared → scope_mismatch → missing_mechanism → needs_evidence → narrowed → open), and the total `V4_DISPLAY_STATE_BY_CODE` 13→9 map (`key_detail_unavailable`→`evidence_blocked`, `value_tradeoff`→`open`, `off_point`→`scope_mismatch`, `resolved_or_settled` terminal). **New helper:** `v4DisplayStateFor(code)` (O-3 — a pure lookup, NOT a new `DisagreementPoint` field, so the JSON round-trip surface is unchanged) exported via `index.ts`. **Copy (O-1):** `missing_mechanism` label `'Missing mechanism'` → **`'Missing link'`** in `mediatorPlainLanguage.ts` (ban-list clean, no fallacy term); the visible `'Definition needed'` → "Definition not shared" rename is DEFERRED to UX-MEDIATOR-004 (marker/rail copy come from `mediatorRailCopy.ts`, untouched → those tests stay green). **Doctrine:** the mediator state is a READ-ONLY projection; the deterministic engine (`src/domain/constitution/engine.ts`) remains the sole acceptance gate; unknown/insufficient inputs → `open` (never a stronger/accusatory state); `accounts_differ` is never synthesized in v1; no person/intent/honesty/verdict/fallacy language. **Tests:** new `__tests__/mediatorPrecedence.test.ts` (one-primary-state-from-many-candidates, full v4 priority order, every conflict row incl. Gate A both branches, idempotence/deep-equal, uncertainty fallback, irrelevant role/seat/voice/transcript inputs stay weak, no gate-shaped export); extended `__tests__/mediatorBoardState.test.ts` (total 13→9 map, `v4DisplayStateFor` identity + collapses, "Missing link" label, one-state-per-node). All 16 existing mediator/rail/marker suites stay green. **822 suites / 31,378 tests (31,377 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped), +1 suite / +30 tests over the 821 / 31,348 baseline (the LIFE-001 wall-clock perf test flakes under full-parallel load — passes isolated + on re-run; unrelated to this pure-TS delta).** Typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean. No UI change (ArgumentGameSurface / MediatorNodeMarker / DisagreementPointsRail / NodeLabelStrip untouched — one-chip markup is UX-MEDIATOR-002) · no visible "Definition needed" rename (UX-MEDIATOR-004) · no chime-in · no persistence · no MCP/classifier activation · no submission-gate change · no room/seat/invite semantics · no supabase / app.json / package / dep / migration / deploy / netlify-prod · no Anthropic/xAI/X API call by Claude. See `docs/designs/UX-MEDIATOR-001.md`.

**2026-06-18 — UX-BRAND-ASSETS-001 (issue #678 — CivilDiscourse horizontal lockup on the Sign In hero ONLY; UI/asset/test only, no GATE-C):** Fulfills the UX-COPY-001 handoff ("logo asset integration → UX-BRAND-ASSETS-001 #678"). **Asset:** the cream "CivilDiscourse" horizontal lockup (swan-on-rock outline + wordmark, cream/light on transparent, designed for a dark field) is now git-tracked at `assets/branding/lockup-horizontal.png` (128,937 B ≈ 129 KB; intrinsic aspect ≈ 1499×388). The 2.3 MB header scene `civic-discourse-logo.png` and `AppHeader.tsx` are UNTOUCHED. **AuthScreen (`src/features/auth/AuthScreen.tsx`):** the Sign In hero's standalone VISIBLE "CivilDiscourse" TEXT wordmark (the old `valuePropBrand` `<Text>` / `auth-value-prop-brand` testID) is REPLACED by the lockup `<Image>` (testID `auth-brand-lockup`) on a dark `BRAND.surface.app.bg` (#08060F) backing band (testID `auth-brand-lockup-backing`) so the cream art reads like the on-black reference. The Image carries `accessibilityRole="image"` + `accessibilityLabel={AUTH_FIRST_RUN_COPY.brand}` so screen readers still announce "CivilDiscourse" — there is NO duplicate visible text wordmark. The tagline + three-beat value line + mediator footer remain rendered as TEXT below. **New pure-TS model `src/features/auth/signInLockupModel.ts`:** `resolveSignInLockupWidthPx(viewportWidth)` clamps the rendered width to the card's available width (viewport − 72 px chrome budget) and caps it at 320 px; `SIGNIN_LOCKUP_ASPECT_RATIO` (≈1499/388) + `resizeMode="contain"` + `maxWidth:'100%'` guarantee the mark never causes horizontal overflow or a mobile edge gutter; a non-positive / non-finite width (SSR first paint) falls back to the cap. **Tests:** new `__tests__/signInLockupModel.test.ts` (clamping at 320/360/390/414/600/768/1024 + SSR safety + monotonicity) and `__tests__/uxBrandAssets001SignInLockup.test.ts` (lockup Image present from the asset, aria label = brand, no duplicate visible text wordmark, tagline+three-beat still text, no stale "CDiscourse", dark backing band, AppHeader/header-logo/useHeaderBreakpoint NOT in this card's `git diff main`, committed asset is the ~129 KB cream PNG and the header logo is still >2 MB). Existing `uxCopy001FirstRun` + `uxCopyOneCivilDiscourseRebrand` brand contracts stay green unchanged (they key on `AUTH_FIRST_RUN_COPY` + preserved testIDs); the only AuthScreen assertion adjusted is the visible-wordmark expectation (now the image's aria). The `darkSurfaceTokens` near-white-hex scan stays green (no raw hex literals added to AuthScreen — the backing references `BRAND.surface.app.bg` by name). **817 suites / 31,289 tests passing (31,288 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped), +2 suites / +2 tests over the 815 / 31,287 AuthScreen-isolated baseline (see final full-suite count below).** Typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean. No AppHeader/header-logo change · no 2.3 MB swap · no `app.json` / native-icon / slug / package / env / domain / provider rename · no package install · no `.tmp` staged · no room/seat/chime-in/mediator/speech/MCP/supabase change · no deployment · no netlify-prod · no Anthropic/xAI/X API call by Claude. See `docs/designs/CIVILDISCOURSE-V4-UX-OVERHAUL-INDEX.md`.

**2026-06-18 — UX-COPY-001 (issue #676 — visible CivilDiscourse rebrand + first-run clarity; UI/copy/test only, no GATE-C):** Adopts the v4 copy system across the visible product. **Tagline swap:** `BRAND.taglineText` (`src/lib/designTokens.ts`) "Just get to the bottom of it" → **"A high-trust room for hard conversations."** — flows through the token to `AppHeaderTagline.tsx`, `AppHeader.tsx`, and `AboutScreen.tsx`; the leading-ellipsis "`...`" fragment prefix (tied to the retired fragment-style copy) was removed from the AppHeader + About render so the full sentence reads cleanly. **First-run / sign-in (folds UX-FIRST-RUN-001):** `AuthScreen.tsx` value-prop now shows the brand **CivilDiscourse** + the primary tagline + the three-beat line **"Mark the point. Respond clearly. See what remains unresolved."** + the mediator-not-a-judge footer **"A mediator, not a judge. We surface the structure of a disagreement — never who’s right."** New reusable copy module `src/lib/brandCopy.ts` (`PRODUCT_NAME`, `PRIMARY_TAGLINE`, `FIRST_RUN_SUBLINE`, `PRINCIPLE_MARK_THE_POINT`, `MEDIATOR_NOT_A_JUDGE`, `WHAT_REMAINS_UNRESOLVED`, `WHAT_WOULD_MOVE_THIS_FORWARD`, `AUTH_FIRST_RUN_COPY`). No voice copy (voice is not shipped). **De-score (copy only, scoring logic untouched):** `ArgumentScoreTracker.tsx` ("Score tracker appears…" → "This appears…"; "Standings · gameplay analysis" → "Where the points stand · gameplay analysis"; a11y label de-scored), `ComposerValidationPanel.tsx` (__DEV__ "Score" → "Topic fit"), `gameCopy.STATUS_COPY` ("Currently ahead" → "More support so far"; "More supported" → "Better supported point"). **Soften:** `gameCopy.CONCESSION_COPY.onlyMostlyWrong` "I'm only MOSTLY wrong" → "I overstated part of this"; feedback "an honest move" → "a clarifying move". **Brand collapse (visible CDiscourse → CivilDiscourse):** `corridorModel.ts`, `inviteCopy.ts`, `InviteRedeemGate.tsx`, `InviteCredentialStep.tsx`, `DevEnvironmentBanner.tsx`, `gameCopy.ts` legal/counseling disclaimers. **`app.json`:** `name` + `web.name` (user-visible OS/PWA/tab DISPLAY label) "CDiscourse" → "CivilDiscourse"; **`slug` (`expo-scaffold`) UNCHANGED** (deploy/EAS/OTA identity — deferred to a separate operator-gated migration; no `eas.json`, no `updates`/`extra.eas.projectId` coupling, so the name change is display-only-safe). **DEFERRED handoffs:** logo asset integration → UX-BRAND-ASSETS-001 #678 (no 2.3 MB import this card); mediator "Definition needed" → "Definition not shared" label rename → UX-MEDIATOR-004 #685. **Tests:** new `__tests__/uxCopyOneCivilDiscourseRebrand.test.ts` (65 tests: visible brand, retired-tagline absence, high-trust tagline present, no voice copy, display title not scaffold, ban-list-clean changed copy, internal-allowlist guard); extended `appHeaderTagline`, `uxOneOneTypographyBaseline`, `uxCopy001FirstRun`, `gameCopy`, `inviteCopy`; relaxed `uxOneOneFiveReadOnlyBoundary` for the two de-scored files (documented NOTE pattern). **817 suites / 31,287 tests passing (31,286 passed, 1 pre-existing skipped), +1 suite / +65 tests over the 816 / 31,222 baseline.** Typecheck + lint (`--max-warnings 0`) clean. No Anthropic / xAI / X API call by Claude · no Supabase write · no service-role · no migration · no deploy · no slug/package/env/domain/provider rename · no room/seat/chime-in/mediator-logic/speech/MCP change. See `docs/designs/CIVILDISCOURSE-V4-UX-OVERHAUL-INDEX.md`.

**2026-06-13 — EMAIL-TRANSPORT-002 (Option B — server-side new-user provisioning on the app-owned `/invite` route; GATE-C / DENO-DEPLOY-BEARING):** Lets a brand-new invitee complete the invite round-trip on the CDiscourse-owned `/invite/<token>` redemption route WITHOUT depending on GoTrue honoring a `redirect_to` against the hosted Redirect-URLs allow-list (issue #637; design `docs/designs/EMAIL-TRANSPORT-002.md`; GATE-A review `docs/reviews/EMAIL-TRANSPORT-002.md`). **Option B chosen (not A):** a live Management-API read of the hosted Auth config returned `mailer_autoconfirm: false` → email confirmations are ON, so Option A's synchronous-session `signUp` is not viable; the operator authorized the Option-B pivot. **(1) New Edge action `provision_and_accept` on `supabase/functions/manage-room-invite/index.ts`** — UNAUTHENTICATED (the token + typed email + typed password ARE the auth, like `lookup_by_token`). Security spine in exact order: look up invite by `token_hash` → run the same live-status checks as `accept` (not-found / revoked / expired / already-accepted) → **email-binding enforced BEFORE provisioning** (`typedEmail.toLowerCase() === invitee_email_lower` else `403 invite_email_mismatch` — a wrong email never mints an account; the binding is never weakened) → room-open check (also before provisioning) → service-role `auth.admin.createUser({ email_confirm: true })` (a confirmed user, usable immediately, no email round-trip; already-existing address → neutral `409 account_exists` so the client routes to sign-in — NOT enumeration, the caller typed their own email+password and the unauthenticated `lookup_by_token` shape is unchanged) → shared `enrolAndFlipInvite` (refactored out of `handleAccept`, reused by both; enrols the opposite seat + flips the invite, idempotent via `23505`) → **returns NO session / JWT / token** (`{ debateId, status, enteredAsParticipant, intendedSeat }`); the client then signs in normally with the password it set. The password is passed ONLY into `createUser`, never logged. **(2) Schema** `provision_and_accept` (token + email + password, min-6/max-128) added to `_shared/inviteSchemas.ts` + the importing Jest mirror in `__tests__/inviteSchemas.test.ts`. **(3) Client wrapper** `provisionAndAcceptInvite({token,email,password})` in `inviteApi.ts` (routes through `supabase.functions.invoke`, no service-role, never logs the password). **(4) Pure model** `src/features/invites/inviteCredentialModel.ts` — `validateInviteCredentialForm` (composes `validateInviteEmailInput` + `validateNewPassword`), `mapProvisionOutcomeToStep` / `mapSignInOutcomeToStep` (every Edge/Auth code → a plain-language UI state; NO raw provider message ever surfaced — doctrine §9), `credentialCopyForMode`. **(5) In-place UI** `src/features/invites/InviteCredentialStep.tsx` — the app-owned "Create your account" step rendered INSIDE the `/invite` gate; owns `provision_and_accept` + the subsequent `signInWithEmailPassword`; the "I already have an account" affordance toggles a sign-in sub-mode. **(6)** `InviteRedeemGate.tsx` signed-out pending branch is now a TWO-path panel ("Create account & join" / "I already have an account — sign in") that mounts the credential step in-route (the token never leaves `/invite`, never co-mingled onto `/auth/callback?invite=`); on credential success the gate's existing auto-accept fires off the live session (idempotent). The session-expired-mid-accept `unauthorized` path keeps the single-path AuthScreen route via the new `SessionExpiredPrompt` (behavior preserved). `App.tsx` `handleInvitePromptSignIn` now consumes `{invitedEmail, preferSignUp}` (the fallback AuthScreen route). **(7) Copy** `INVITE_CREDENTIAL_COPY` bundle (verdict-free; added to the `inviteCopyDoctrine` ban-list scan) + `account_exists`/`provision_failed`/`weak_password` plain-language codes. **NO migration, NO RLS change, NO new Edge function, NO new dependency, NO live email send, NO hosted-config mutation; the service-role stays inside `manage-room-invite` (none in client); no-enumeration preserved (the gate render is byte-identical regardless of invitee account state — asserted directly).** +3 NEW suites (`inviteCredentialModel`, `InviteCredentialStep`, `InviteRedeemGate.newuser`) + assertions added to `inviteSchemas` / `manageRoomInviteEdgeCases` (no-JWT, email-binding-before-provision, no-session-returned, no-token/password-log) / `inviteCopyDoctrine`; the `handleAccept` source-scan checks re-pointed to the shared `enrolAndFlipInvite` helper (intent unchanged, never weakened). **Full jest: 797 suites / 31065 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 31066 total), up from 794 / 30994 (+3 suites / +71 tests), exit 0.** `npm run typecheck` exit 0; `npm run lint` exit 0; secret scan over the changed client files clean. **OPERATOR DEPLOY (GATE-C — merge IS deploy):** `manage-room-invite` is `config.toml`-registered, so the squash-merge auto-redeploys it via the Supabase GitHub integration (the new action is inert until the client calls it); the operator may also run `npx supabase functions deploy manage-room-invite --linked`. Manual smoke (operator): fresh alias → `/invite/<token>` → "create my account" → enrolled, invite `accepted`, `debate_participants` row present (verify the DB before cleanup). Claude never deploys / sets a secret / flips a config / runs a live smoke. **Built on `feat/EMAIL-TRANSPORT-002-newuser-credential-route`, NOT pushed/merged by the implementer.** See `docs/designs/EMAIL-TRANSPORT-002.md`.

**2026-06-13 — EMAIL-TRANSPORT-001 (CDiscourse-owned product email module + Resend adapter + gated transport; GATE-C / DENO-DEPLOY-BEARING, lands DORMANT):** Standardizes CDiscourse's transactional email into a shared, swappable, gated Deno module + a behavior-preserving refactor of the existing-user invite send (issue #635; design `docs/designs/EMAIL-TRANSPORT-001.md`; runbook `docs/runbooks/email-provider-setup.md`). **Product lane (Lane B) only this card; Auth lane (Lane A) = docs + the already-branded `invite.html` template; other Auth templates are a documented follow-up.** **(1) New shared Deno module `supabase/functions/_shared/email/`:** `emailProvider.ts` (the swappable `EmailProvider` interface + `getEmailProvider` factory — Resend default, Postmark a documented `null` swap path not built this card; audit-safe `EmailSendResult` carrying ONLY `status` + `providerStatusClass`, never recipient/body/key), `resendProvider.ts` (THE ONLY place the provider key + the Bearer-scheme auth header live; `POST https://api.resend.com/emails` via Deno `fetch()`, body drained-not-echoed, 4xx/5xx/throw -> `failed_sanitized` + class, never blocks the user action; **no nodemailer, no SMTP socket**), `emailTemplates.ts` (PURE `renderArgumentRoomInviteEmail(input)` -> `{subject, html, text}`; CDiscourse brand, "You've been invited to an argument", public/private capacity copy, "Open invitation" CTA -> the app-controlled `/invite/<token>` route with the token ONLY inside the href, plain-text fallback, >=44px CTA, HTML-escaped user input), `emailSchemas.ts` (Deno `npm:zod@4` validators) + the importable Jest twin `src/features/email/emailSchemasMirror.ts` (Node zod; an improvement over the inline-redeclare pattern), `safety.ts` (`sanitizeLine` / `escapeHtml` / `assertNoBannedTokens` over the 17-token doctrine ban-list incl. `challenger`/`opponent` / `sanitizeRoomContext` / `isPlausibleEmail`), `sendTransactionalEmail.ts` (the single Edge-facing seam: master-gate -> provider+FROM -> recipient -> ban-list scan -> `provider.send`; resolves sender identity from env; returns the audit-safe result). **(2) Behavior-preserving refactor** of `supabase/functions/room-notifications/index.ts`: `maybeSendInviteEmail` now renders via the shared module + dispatches through `sendTransactionalEmail`, mapping the audit-safe status back to the unchanged QOL-038 `InviteEmailStatus` union (`sent | not_configured | queued`); the inline Resend fetch + Bearer header are GONE from this file (moved to `resendProvider.ts`). The branch-independent `resolveInviteNotificationStatus` (no-enumeration) is unchanged. **(3) Gate composition (documented behavior change):** the product lane now requires BOTH `CDISCOURSE_EMAIL_TRANSPORT_ENABLED` (NEW master, default OFF) AND `INVITE_EMAIL_ENABLED` (per-feature, default OFF) — `isInviteEmailEnabled()` composes them. The new-user Auth bridge `INVITE_AUTH_BRIDGE_ENABLED` stays INDEPENDENT (Lane A, not subsumed). **(4)** `config.toml` documenting comment block for the master email gate + the Custom-SMTP-is-a-hosted-action note (the `[auth.email.smtp]` block stays commented; the branded `invite.html` registration is unchanged). **(5)** `scripts/auth/sendInviteSmoke.js` gains a pure, no-network Lane-B dry-posture predictor (`predictProductEmailStatus` / `describeProductEmailPosture`) surfaced in the redacted plan; presence-only fingerprints, NO new send capability, NO service-role. **Lands DORMANT:** master gate default OFF -> `sendTransactionalEmail` returns `skipped_gate_off` with NO network on merge. **Source-scan re-point (GATE-A watch-item):** the `Bearer ${apiKey}` / `api.resend.com` / drain assertions FOLLOWED the code from `room-notifications` to `resendProvider.ts` in `roomNotifications.email.safety.test.ts` / `roomNotifications.email.test.ts` / `roomNotifications.edge.test.ts` — re-pointed, never dropped or weakened. **No xAI / Anthropic / X API / Supabase write by Claude; no service-role; no migration; no RLS; no new dependency (zod@4 already installed); no live email send anywhere; no hosted-config mutation.** +13 NEW suites (`emailProviderFactory`, `emailMasterGate`, `emailGateReconciliation`, `renderArgumentRoomInviteEmail`, `emailCopyDoctrine`, `emailNoTokenLeak`, `emailSafetySanitizers`, `resendProviderRequest`, `emailNoLogLeak`, `roomNotificationsEmailRefactor`, `emailSchemasMirror`, `authTemplatesBranding`, `emailTransportSmokeGuards`). **Full jest: 794 suites / 30994 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 30995 total), up from 781 / 30847 (+13 suites / +147 net tests), exit 0.** `npm run typecheck` exit 0; `npm run lint` exit 0. **OPERATOR DEPLOY (GATE-C — merge IS deploy):** `room-notifications` is `config.toml`-registered, so the squash-merge auto-redeploys it (inert — gates default OFF); going live is a SEPARATE operator sequence per `docs/runbooks/email-provider-setup.md` (DNS -> secrets `CDISCOURSE_EMAIL_FROM`/`CDISCOURSE_EMAIL_REPLY_TO`/`CDISCOURSE_EMAIL_PROVIDER` -> Custom SMTP -> seed smoke -> `CDISCOURSE_EMAIL_TRANSPORT_ENABLED=true`+`INVITE_EMAIL_ENABLED=true`). Claude never flips a gate / sets a secret / pushes hosted SMTP/templates / runs a live smoke. See `docs/designs/EMAIL-TRANSPORT-001.md`.

**2026-06-13 — ARG-ROOM-008 (Re-surface the create-time invite copy-link; STANDARD-GATED, client + tests ONLY — ZERO `supabase/**`):** Closes the ARG-ROOM-003 user-exposure gate (issue #625; design `docs/designs/ARG-ROOM-008.md`). ARG-ROOM-003's create surface defaults to **Private**, and private **requires** one invite — but `createDebate` mapped to a `Debate` and **discarded** the one-time raw `inviteLink` the `create-argument-room` Edge returns to the creator. With invite email OFF (QOL-040 default) + ARG-ROOM-002's one-pending-invite-per-room index (the in-room `InvitePanel` can't mint a second link), the default-path private invitee was **unreachable**. This card stops discarding the link and renders it ONCE on the create surface. **The Edge already returns `inviteLink` — ZERO `supabase/**` file: no migration, no Edge, no RLS, no config.toml.** **Chosen threading: option (a)** — widen `createDebate`'s success to a new `CreatedRoom { debate; inviteLink: string | null }` (in `types.ts`) carried ALONGSIDE the `Debate`, threaded through `useDebates().create` → the `onCreate` props of `ConversationGalleryScreen` / `DebateListScreen` / `StartArgumentPage` (all callers updated, all compiling). **Why (a) not (b):** calling `createArgumentRoom` directly from the page would force re-implementing `createDebate`'s body (the room-row reload AND the ARG-ROOM-004 `notifyCreateTimeInvite` fire-and-forget call), risking the 004 path; (a) keeps the 004 notify block byte-unchanged inside `createDebate` and only stops *discarding* the link, with the synchronous return value as the box trigger. **The box (`StartArgumentPage`):** on a create that returned a link (private always; public-with-invite) the page sets local `createdInvite` state and renders an early-return success interstitial that REPLACES the form, **deferring** the landing hand-off until "Continue" (navigating straight in would strand the invitee — the link is the only client-side moment that token exists); a create with no link (public, no invite) navigates immediately (no regression). It **REUSES** the shipped `INVITE_PANEL_COPY.copyLinkButton`/`copyLinkSuccess` affordance, renders the raw link as **selectable monospace `<Text>`** (no clipboard import; long-press to copy, mirroring `InvitePanel`), and adds 3 generic strings to `ARGUMENT_ROOM_CREATE_COPY` (auto-scanned by the existing `argumentRoomCreateCopyDoctrine` ban-list). "Continue" clears `createdInvite` (link **not re-exposed**) then fires the deferred `onCreated`. **Doctrine pins (test-proven):** inviter-only (box absent on initial render; reachable only by the just-created creator), one-time (Continue clears it, no re-expose affordance), never logged (no `console.*` static scan + a behavioural spy over all console methods across the full flow asserting the raw token never appears), never persisted (in-memory React state only, mirrors `InvitePanel.lastInviteLink`; static scan bans `AsyncStorage`/`SecureStore`/`localStorage`/`MMKV`/…), no enumeration (generic copy + ban-list over enumeration tokens). a11y: copy + Continue controls are `role="button"` + label + `minHeight:44` (+`hitSlop` on copy); link in selectable `<Text>`; color not the only signal. **No xAI / Anthropic / X API / Supabase write by Claude; no service-role; no new dependency; the 004 notify path + existing `createDebate` callers are unaffected.** +1 NEW suite (`startArgumentInviteLinkBox`, +13 tests) + updated `StartArgumentPage` / `startArgumentVisibilityInvite` fakes + `debatesApi.visibility` assertions for the result-shape. **Full jest: 781 suites / 30847 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 30848 total), up from 780 / 30834 (+1 suite / +13 tests), exit 0.** `npm run typecheck` exit 0; `npm run lint` exit 0. **Operator follow-up: none** (standard-gated, mergeable when green). See `docs/designs/ARG-ROOM-008.md`.

**2026-06-13 — ARG-ROOM-007 (Live-smoke matrix / end-to-end verification harness; STANDARD-GATED — ZERO `supabase/**`):** Delivers the operator verification protocol for the public/private argument-room invite feature: a 12-check live-smoke matrix + a DRY-RUN-DEFAULT JWT harness + a committable report template (issue #618; design `docs/designs/ARG-ROOM-007-LIVE-SMOKE-MATRIX.md`; review `docs/reviews/ARG-ROOM-007-design-review.md`). **Verification only — adds ZERO product code: no migration, no Edge, no RLS, no `config.toml`, no production `src/`.** **(1) Pure matrix** `scripts/arg-room-live-smoke/smokeMatrix.js` — the frozen 12 core checks (public/private create · cap-5 6th-refused `23514` · reserved-seat accept · observer-into-full · wrong-user `403 invite_email_mismatch` · new-/existing-user callback · no-enumeration · no-token-leakage) + 3 regression re-checks (MAX1/SELF/DOOR, already proven LIVE by the 002 smoke). **(2) Pure plan** `plan.js` — dry-by-default; live requires BOTH `--live` AND env `CDISCOURSE_ALLOW_ARG_ROOM_LIVE_SMOKE=1` (mirrors `sendInviteSmoke.js`); `planCheckExecution` degrades #5/#7 to `accounts_insufficient → covered_by:[002-B6, roomCapacityModel-parity]` when <6 accounts and #9/#10/#11 to `dependency_not_deployed` when 004 is not deployed (never a misleading PASS/FAIL — review #2/#3); `buildRedactedPlan` carries present+length fingerprints only. **(3) Pure report** `report.js` — `renderReport` (ban-list clean), `scanForSecretLeak` (raw-token/JWT/auth-prefix/sha-256 scan; whitelists the caller's own token + the creator-only `inviteLink`), `_forbiddenReportTokens` MIRRORS `_forbiddenArgumentRoomCreationTokens` (parity-pinned — review #1). **(4) CLI** `runArgRoomLiveSmoke.js` — I/O only behind the armed gate; self-contained env reader (leaves `scripts/bot-fixtures/`/#623 untouched); rooms created ONLY via the `create-argument-room` Edge; the single direct `debates` insert is the deliberate R3 door-refusal probe; **NO service-role, NO `Authorization`/`Bearer` header by hand, NEVER sets `INVITE_EMAIL_ENABLED`/the auth-bridge gate.** **(5)** `docs/testing-runs/2026-06-13-arg-room-live-smoke.md` template (mirrors the 002 smoke doc) + `.env.bot-tests.example` keys (`CDISCOURSE_BOT_D/E_*`, `CDISCOURSE_ALLOW_ARG_ROOM_LIVE_SMOKE`) + `package.json` scripts `smoke:arg-room:dry` / `smoke:arg-room:live`. **Account honesty:** checks #5/#7 need ≥6 distinct JWT logins (only 3 required + C optional exist); the harness flags this rather than skipping silently — provision `BOT_D/E` (option A) or accept the test-covered fallback (option B). +37 tests (`__tests__/argRoomLiveSmoke.test.ts` — matrix completeness, dry/arm gating, redaction, ban-list parity, secret-leak scan, source guards). **Full jest: 780 suites / 30834 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 30835 total), up from 779 / 30787 (+1 suite / +37 tests), exit 0.** `npm run typecheck` exit 0; `npm run lint` exit 0. **Operator follow-up:** the live run is a SEPARATE operator-armed step AFTER 004 deploys (`npm run smoke:arg-room:live` with `CDISCOURSE_ALLOW_ARG_ROOM_LIVE_SMOKE=1`; disarm per the runbook). The harness NEVER self-arms a gate or a live send. Standard-gated → mergeable when green. See `docs/designs/ARG-ROOM-007-LIVE-SMOKE-MATRIX.md`.

**2026-06-13 — ARG-ROOM-006 item (g) (manage-room-invite create 23505 relabel; GATE-C, Edge-only, ONE `supabase/**` file, NO migration):** The deferred GATE-C slice from ARG-ROOM-002 (issue #617; design `docs/designs/ARG-ROOM-006-VISIBILITY-FEED-ACCESS.md` §5.3 / item g). ARG-ROOM-002 added the partial-unique index `argument_room_invites_one_live_per_room (debate_id) WHERE status='pending'` (one live invite per room, to ANYONE). Before that index, a `create`-action `23505` only fired the older per-address `argument_room_invites_one_live (debate_id, invitee_email_lower)` index, so the function's same-address re-read handled it idempotently. Now a second-invite `23505` to a **different** address means the room already holds its one live invite — the same-address re-read finds nothing and the path fell through to `internalError('invite_insert_failed')` (a **500 mislabel**). **Fix (one branch in `handleCreate`, `supabase/functions/manage-room-invite/index.ts`):** after the unchanged same-address idempotent re-read returns null, count pending invites for the room by `debate_id` + `status='pending'` ONLY via `.select('id', { count: 'exact', head: true })` (`head:true` reads NO row bodies → no email / token / who-was-invited leaked → **no enumeration**); if > 0, return a neutral `409 room_already_has_invite` ("This argument already has an invite waiting.") that names no one; otherwise the genuine `invite_insert_failed` 500 is preserved. **Companion (standard-gated):** one `ERROR_CODE_MAP` key `room_already_has_invite` in `src/features/invites/inviteCopy.ts` so the code round-trips through `plainLanguageForInviteError` (no raw code reaches the user, doctrine §9). `revoke`/`accept`/`lookup_by_token`/`list_for_debate` and the same-address idempotent reuse are byte-unchanged. **NO migration, NO RLS change, NO new Edge function, NO config.toml change** (the index already shipped in ARG-ROOM-002; `manage-room-invite` already registered). +8 tests across the existing suites (`inviteCopy` ×3 — neutral copy, distinct-from-fallback, no email/address enumeration; `manageRoomInviteEdgeCases` ×5 — source-scan of the relabel branch: debate_id-only count, neutral 409, no-enumeration message, genuine-failure preserved, same-address regression; plus `inviteCopyDoctrine` + `manageRoomInviteSafety` code-list extensions). **Full jest: 774 suites / 30743 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 30744 total), up from 774 / 30735 (30736 total) (+8 tests, no new suite), exit 0.** `npm run typecheck` exit 0; `npm run lint` exit 0. **OPERATOR DEPLOY (GATE-C — merge IS deploy):** `manage-room-invite` is `config.toml`-registered, so the squash-merge auto-redeploys it via the Supabase GitHub integration; the operator may also run `npx supabase functions deploy manage-room-invite --linked`. No migration to push. **GATE-C: heightened review + operator-gated merge; built on `feat/ARG-ROOM-006g-invite-collision-copy`, NOT pushed/merged by the implementer.** See `docs/designs/ARG-ROOM-006-VISIBILITY-FEED-ACCESS.md` § item (g).

**2026-06-13 — ARG-ROOM-006 items (a)–(f) (Visibility / feed / access integration; STANDARD-GATED, client + tests ONLY — ZERO `supabase/**`):** Surfaces what a viewer can SEE and DO once a room exists, making the two visibilities (QOL-039 RLS) and the four seat states (ARG-ROOM-002 cap trigger + ARG-ROOM-005 seat model) coherent and route-correct on the client (issue #617; design `docs/designs/ARG-ROOM-006-VISIBILITY-FEED-ACCESS.md`; review `docs/reviews/ARG-ROOM-006-design-review.md`). **It never re-enforces capacity and never re-implements/weakens privacy — it reads, routes, and renders.** **Item (g) (the `manage-room-invite` 23505 relabel) is EXCLUDED** — it is a separate GATE-C PR (touches `supabase/functions/**`); this card adds NO migration, NO RLS, NO Edge, NO `supabase/**` file. **(1) New pure model (engine discipline, frozen output):** `src/features/debates/roomAccessModel.ts` — `deriveRoomAccessView` (visibility + seat summary → `public_open`/`public_reserved`/`public_full`/`private_member`/`private_no_access` with badge / access line / `canObserve` always-true-when-readable / `canClaimSeat` never-true-when-full / `actionLabel`), `resolveRoomDeepLinkAccess` (item c — a requested id absent from the RLS-filtered set is `unavailable`, **IDENTICAL for private-no-access AND nonexistent — the no-enumeration guarantee by construction**; pure, no network, no new RLS), `feedVisibilityForCard` (item b — every private card hidden from public-discovery lanes; mirrors `classifyCardToSection`:1543, additive, does NOT weaken it), `deriveGalleryActionLabel` (the ONE action-label policy now SHARED by the gallery card + the access view — review [should] #1, no drift), `_forbiddenRoomAccessTokens`. The cap + seat math derive from the shared `roomActiveSeatCap`/`canJoinActive`/`openActiveSlots` primitives (the same ones ARG-ROOM-005's `seatClaimModel` consumes) — **no second seat literal**; a parity test pins `deriveRoomAccessView` full/open against `deriveSeatAvailability` across the count grid. `openStatus` is typed as the canonical `DebateStatus` (review [should] #2 — no `'settled'` drift). **(2) New copy:** `ROOM_ACCESS_COPY` in `gameCopy.ts` (public/reserved/full/member lines + the cause-neutral `unavailable_*` pair). **Open Question 1 RESOLVED as doctrine (review nit #3):** the deep-link `unavailable_*` copy is cause-neutral and **never asserts "it is private"** (that would confirm a private room exists at the URL = soft enumeration); the existing `ROOM_VISIBILITY_COPY.no_access_*` (which DOES assert it) stays reserved for the positive-knowledge member view. (The design draft's `unavailable_body` said "the link may be wrong"; reworded to drop the verdict-adjacent token `wrong`, consistent with the model ban-list — wording polish is ARG-ROOM-007's.) **(3) Read-only UI:** `ConversationGalleryScreen` + `DebateListScreen` each render a public/private access badge (text carries the meaning — color is not the only signal) + the plain-language access line, and the gallery now consumes the shared `deriveGalleryActionLabel` (identical Continue/Observe/Open output). New `src/features/debates/RoomUnavailableNotice.tsx` (Modal, reuses the core overlay pattern + InviteRedeemGate panel a11y) renders the cause-neutral notice; `App.tsx`'s notification deep-link handler now calls `resolveRoomDeepLinkAccess` and shows the notice instead of silently dropping when a room id is absent from the RLS list. **(4) Item (d) verified + pinned (no behavior change):** a new render test drives the shipped `InviteRedeemGate` mismatch path (mocked accept → `invite_email_mismatch` → MismatchPanel) and asserts the recovery copy names the VIEWER's email only, never the invitee's (the gate never receives it — no enumeration), with "Sign in as someone else" + the universal escape hatch present. **Doctrine pins (test-proven):** no verdict/amplification/person tokens in any emitted string (`_forbiddenRoomAccessTokens` + `ROOM_ACCESS_COPY` scan), no snake_case leak, observers stay uncapped (a "full" public card still reads observe-friendly — "full" is a seat fact), and the enumeration invariant (private-no-access ≡ nonexistent) is asserted directly. a11y: badge `accessibilityLabel` (reuses `ROOM_VISIBILITY_COPY.badge_private_a11y`), color-independent badges, the notice's dismiss is a 44-target button with role+label+hitSlop, headings exposed. **No xAI / Anthropic / X API / Supabase write by Claude; no service-role; no new dependency.** +5 NEW suites (`roomAccessModel`, `ConversationGalleryScreen.visibility`, `roomUnavailableNotice`, `InviteRedeemGate.mismatch`, `DebateListScreen.visibility`). **Full jest: 779 suites / 30787 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 30788 total), up from 774 / 30735 (+5 suites / +52 tests), exit 0.** `npm run typecheck` exit 0; `npm run lint` exit 0; `grep ANTHROPIC_API_KEY\|SERVICE_ROLE` over the changed client files is clean. **Operator follow-up: none for (a)–(f)** (standard-gated, mergeable when green). **Item (g) ships next as its own GATE-C PR** (`manage-room-invite create` 23505 → neutral 409 `room_already_has_invite` + the matching `ERROR_CODE_MAP` key) — operator-gated, never self-approved. See `docs/designs/ARG-ROOM-006-VISIBILITY-FEED-ACCESS.md`.

**2026-06-13 — ARG-ROOM-005 (Public participant slot claiming; STANDARD-GATED, client + tests only):** Makes public rooms usable beyond creation — a signed-in non-participant can claim an open public seat (active participant), the room enforces the cap of 5, the reserved invite seat is protected, and when no seat is free the flow degrades to the shipped **observe** affordance (issue #616; design `docs/designs/ARG-ROOM-005-PUBLIC-SLOT-CLAIMING.md`; review `docs/reviews/ARG-ROOM-005-design-review.md`). **SURFACES + gracefully handles the already-deployed ARG-ROOM-002 capacity trigger — it never re-enforces it. ZERO `supabase/**` files: no migration, no RLS, no Edge, no config.toml.** **(1) Pure model (new, engine discipline):** `src/features/debates/seatClaimModel.ts` — `isActiveParticipantSide` (twin of SQL `side<>'observer'`), `deriveSeatAvailability` (frozen open-slot / isFull / viewer-state view-model), `classifyJoinOutcome` (the `room_capacity_reached` **message token is authoritative** for `room_full`; a bare `23514` without the token is `unavailable`, review [should] #1; the trigger `DETAIL` cap=/active=/reserved= is **discarded** — no enumeration), `buildSeatAvailabilityViewModel` (verdict-free, counts only), `resolveJoinSideEffect`, `_forbiddenSeatClaimTokens`. New `roomCapacityModel.openActiveSlots` (companion of `canJoinActive`). The cap derives from the single source (`roomActiveSeatCap` → `PUBLIC_ROOM_SEAT_CAP`); **no second literal**. **(2) Graceful claim flow:** `joinDebate` widened (client-only) to `JoinDebateResult` carrying the classified outcome — STILL the single `debate_participants` INSERT (never touches `argument_room_invites` → a public join can't steal the reserved seat); `useDebates.join` returns `{ side, outcome }` and a full room degrades to observe instead of the generic error banner; `App.onJoinSide` routes `room_full` to the observe affordance (refresh count + polite announce), never a dead-end. New `useActiveParticipantCount` hook (one caller-scoped `head:true` count read of the existing `debate_participants` table, `side<>'observer'`; no new server object). **(3) Read-only surfacing:** new `SeatAvailabilityStrip.tsx` (open-slot count / "Room full — observe" + viewer-state line; counts only, never identities; role+label, ≥44px, color is never the only signal). `ArgumentSideActionRail` gains optional `canClaimActiveSeat` + `fullRoomNotice` props (SC-005 back-compatible) — Join For/Against render disabled (a11y `disabled` + dimmed + hitSlop preserved) with a polite observe nudge when full; Watch stays enabled; the locked `OBSERVER_ACTIONS`/`getRailActions`/`railActionToBubbleControl` contract is untouched. **(4) New copy:** `SEAT_CLAIM_COPY` in `gameCopy` (minimal, person-neutral seat facts — "full"/"observe" are never verdicts; ARG-ROOM-007 consolidates later). **The seven invariants** are proven 1:1 (pure-model / integration-mocked / live-provable): public+0 ⇒ creator+4 open; public+1 ⇒ creator+1 reserved+3 open; invitee can accept even when full-to-reserved; the 6th active is refused (`room_capacity_reached`); observer may join a FULL room; observer is never active; a public join never writes the invite table. PLUS a **no-differential-preview lock** (review [should] #2 — a public viewer always passes `knownReservedInviteCount=0`, so a hidden-reserved room and a genuinely-open room render identically) and the classifier-tightening negative test. **No xAI / Anthropic / X API / Supabase write by Claude; no service-role.** +6 suites (`seatClaimModel`, `seatClaimModel.doctrine`, `seatClaimWiring`, `useDebatesSeatClaim`, `SeatAvailabilityStrip`, `argumentSideActionRailSeatClaim`). **Full jest: 774 suites / 30735 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 30736 total), up from 768 / 30676 (+6 suites / +59 tests), exit 0.** `npm run typecheck` exit 0; `npm run lint` exit 0. **Operator follow-up: none** (standard-gated, mergeable when green). The optional GATE-C 002-follow-up (drop `reserved=` from the trigger DETAIL) remains OUT of scope (client-mitigated by never reading the DETAIL). See `docs/designs/ARG-ROOM-005-PUBLIC-SLOT-CLAIMING.md`.

**2026-06-13 — ARG-ROOM-004 (Create-time one-invite orchestration + email-link → seat bridge; GATE-C, lands DORMANT):** Wires the create-time invite → (gated) email send → `/auth/callback` → reserved-seat accept (issue #615; design `docs/designs/ARG-ROOM-004-INVITE-ACCEPTANCE-AND-EMAIL-TRANSPORT.md`; review `docs/reviews/ARG-ROOM-004-design-review.md`). REUSES the shipped pieces; rebuilds nothing. **(1) Bridge (client, pure):** new `src/features/invites/bridgedInviteToken.ts` — `extractBridgedInviteToken(url)` reads `?invite=<token>` off `/auth/callback` (path-gated, **query-only — never the implicit-flow `#access_token=…` fragment**, token-shape-gated, never throws) + `resolveColdStartInviteToken(url)` (the `/invite/<token>` path OR the `?invite=` bridge). `App.tsx`'s cold-start invite effect (the dispatch-safe one, NOT the render-phase `useState` initializer — review #2) now resolves via the combined resolver → build + persist + `SET_PENDING_INVITE_INTENT` → shipped #607/#608 set-password → auto-accept. `parseAuthCallbackUrl` is **un-widened** (proven by `parseAuthCallbackUrlBridgePurity`). **(2) Create-time send (Edge, gated, default OFF):** extends `room-notifications` `handleInvite` (the seam that ALREADY holds the existing-vs-new `listUsers` split) — existing-user → gated Resend (`INVITE_EMAIL_ENABLED`) with a **reconciled working link** (`buildInviteLinkFromOrigin`, closing the QOL-040 `inviteLink:null` gap); new-user → gated `INVITE_AUTH_BRIDGE_ENABLED` (**NEW, default OFF**) → `inviteUserByEmail(email,{ redirectTo:<origin>/auth/callback?invite=<token> })`. `notification` is a single branch-INDEPENDENT gate-posture predicate (`resolveInviteNotificationStatus`: dormant → `not_configured`, any gate armed → `queued`) so existing-vs-new is indistinguishable (review #3). **(3) Client trigger:** `notifyCreateTimeInvite` in `debatesApi.ts`, fired fire-and-forget from `createDebate` after a create-with-invite (extracts the raw token from the create-time `inviteLink`, passes it to `room-notifications`); best-effort — never blocks, never rolls back the room. **SEAM divergence from the design (documented):** the design named `create-argument-room`/`manage-room-invite handleCreate` as the send site, but ARG-ROOM-002's `create-argument-room` is contractually branch-free (`createArgumentRoomEdge.test.ts` forbids `listUsers`/`inviteUserByEmail`/existing-vs-new there). So the orchestration runs in `room-notifications` (the prompt-named REUSE target), triggered post-create. **No lift of `maybeSendInviteEmail`** (the design's `_shared/inviteEmail.ts` lift would break ~25 source-scan assertions in 3 room-notifications test files for zero functional gain) — it stays in place and is reused via the extended `handleInvite`. **Idempotency** relies on the shipped, unchanged `handleAccept` (same-redeemer early return + PK 23505 no-op; covered by `manageRoomInviteEdgeCases`). **No migration; no new Edge function; no config.toml change** (`room-notifications` already registered). **BOTH transport gates default OFF → a merge sends NO live email** (the whole point of GATE-C "land dormant, flip later"). **Operator steps (post-merge):** (a) merge auto-deploys `room-notifications` via the Supabase GitHub integration (dormant); (b) run the **dry** smoke `node scripts/auth/sendInviteSmoke.js --dry-run` + `npx jest sendInviteSmoke.bridge` to confirm the `?invite=` redirect resolves to a hosted-allow-listed `/auth/callback`; (c) to go live, set Edge secrets `INVITE_AUTH_BRIDGE_ENABLED=true` (new-user) and/or `INVITE_EMAIL_ENABLED=true`+`RESEND_API_KEY`+`INVITE_EMAIL_FROM` (existing-user) via `npx supabase secrets set …` — **never self-approved**. +6 suites (`extractBridgedInviteToken`, `parseAuthCallbackUrlBridgePurity`, `roomNotificationsCreateTimeInvite`, `notifyCreateTimeInvite`, `appInviteBridgeHandoff`, `sendInviteSmoke.bridge`). **Full jest: 768 suites / 30676 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 30677 total), up from 762 / 30617 (+6 suites / +59 tests), exit 0.** `npm run typecheck` exit 0; `npm run lint` exit 0. No new user-facing copy (reuses shipped `INVITE_REDEEM_COPY` `joiningTitle`). See `docs/designs/ARG-ROOM-004-INVITE-ACCEPTANCE-AND-EMAIL-TRANSPORT.md`.

**2026-06-13 — ARG-ROOM-003 (Visibility selector + one direct-invite on the LIVE create surface; client-only, standard-gated — NOT GATE-C):** Exposes the ARG-ROOM-001 binding creation matrix on `StartArgumentPage` (issue #614; design `docs/designs/ARG-ROOM-003-CREATE-ROOM-UX.md`; review `docs/reviews/ARG-ROOM-003-design-review.md`). New "Who can join" section between the declaration and the surface selector: a Public/Private radiogroup (default **Private**, lifting the QOL-039 radio semantics onto the dark in-file `surface*` styles), one direct-invite email field (no "add another"), a single validator-driven disabled-reason line, and a capacity explainer. The form drives button state + helper copy + capacity numbers off `deriveArgumentRoomCreation` (NO re-implemented rule): a 2+ comma/space paste in the single field is split + counted INSIDE the validator → the specific `too_many_direct_invites` copy (distinct from the generic invalid-email), and the cap (reconciled 5) + open-seat counts are interpolated from the validator output, never a second literal. **OQ#1 RESOLVED via ARG-ROOM-002's atomic `create-argument-room` Edge** — there is no "room created but invite failed" intermediate state, so the design's two-call retry / copy-link panel is dropped: submit threads an EXPLICIT visibility + the one optional `invite:{email}` through `createDebate` (extended to forward it) into the SAME atomic Edge call, then navigates uniformly (generic post-create, no account enumeration). **"Who can join" label reconciliation:** the radiogroup announces a JOIN-framed `accessibilityLabel` (`ARGUMENT_ROOM_CREATE_COPY.visibility_group_a11y` = "Who can join this argument"), NOT the reused read-access `ROOM_VISIBILITY_COPY.group_label` ("Who can see this argument"). New copy block `ARGUMENT_ROOM_CREATE_COPY` + `fillArgumentRoomCapacityCopy` in `gameCopy.ts`; `CreateDebateInput.invite?` added in `types.ts`; `createDebate` threads it in `debatesApi.ts` (no new StartArgumentPage prop, no App/gallery wiring change). a11y: radiogroup + radio roles/state, ≥44px (`minHeight 44` + `hitSlop`), ●/○ glyph (color-independent), labelled email field, polite-live disabled-reason text; no animation added (reduce-motion no-op). +2 NEW suites (`startArgumentVisibilityInvite`, `argumentRoomCreateCopyDoctrine`) + updated `StartArgumentPage` / `debatesApi.visibility`. **Full jest: 762 suites / 30617 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 30618 total), up from 760 / 30581 (+2 suites / +36 tests), exit 0.** `npm run typecheck` exit 0; `npm run lint` exit 0; `grep ANTHROPIC_API_KEY\|SERVICE_ROLE` over the new client files is clean. **No migration / Edge / deploy in this card** (rides on ARG-ROOM-002's deployed Edge). **Design gap (record-only):** with email delivery OFF (default), the atomic create returns the raw `inviteLink` once, but `createDebate` returns the mapped `Debate` (not the link), so the create-time link is not surfaced here — invite delivery is QOL-040's concern / a follow-up card. See `docs/designs/ARG-ROOM-003-CREATE-ROOM-UX.md`.

**2026-06-13 — ARG-ROOM-002 (Server-authoritative capacity + private-requires-invite + one-invite enforcement; MIGRATION + RLS + EDGE — GATE-C, deploy-bearing, WRITTEN-NOT-DEPLOYED):** Makes the ARG-ROOM-001 binding creation matrix TRUE AT THE DATABASE (issue #613; design `docs/designs/ARG-ROOM-002-BACKEND-VISIBILITY-CAPACITY-INVITES.md`; review `docs/reviews/ARG-ROOM-002-design-review.md`). Builds on the shipped seams (QOL-039 visibility + helpers, QOL-038 invites + `manage-room-invite` accept path, the `20260516000006` recursion-safe pattern) rather than rebuilding them. NEW append-only migration `supabase/migrations/20260613000001_arg_room_002_room_capacity_and_creation.sql`: four `SECURITY DEFINER STABLE` helpers (`room_active_seat_cap` private 2 / public 5 — derived from visibility, NO new column; `count_active_participants` side<>observer; `user_email_lower` reads `auth.users`; `count_reserved_invites` live-pending minus self minus already-seated-addressee, no double-count); a `BEFORE INSERT` trigger `enforce_room_capacity()` on `debate_participants` that rejects `active + reserved + 1 > cap` (fires for EVERY writer incl. the service-role accept path — RLS is bypassed by service-role, triggers are not — observers + already-seated pass through, NULL-cap defensive branch); the tighter partial-unique index `argument_room_invites_one_live_per_room (debate_id) WHERE status='pending'` (additive over the shipped per-address index); a tightened `debate_participants` INSERT policy (client self-join is PUBLIC-only via `is_debate_private(debate_id)=false`); the atomic `create_argument_room(...)` RPC (debate + creator + optional invite in ONE tx, re-asserts private⇒invite, `service_role`-only); and the DROP of the shipped `"debates: authenticated can create"` INSERT policy. NEW Edge Function `supabase/functions/create-argument-room/index.ts` (`verify_jwt=true`, registered in `config.toml`): standard shape (CORS, JWT via `createCallerClient`+`getUser`, Zod `.strict()` schema accepting ≤1 invite, mints the invite token in Deno + hashes before Postgres, calls the RPC via service-role, returns `{debateId, visibility, inviteId, inviteLink}` with the raw `inviteLink` to the creator ONCE), enforcing private⇒invite (400), self-invite (400), and no account enumeration (uniform response, `emailDomain`-only logs, no token/Authorization/SERVICE_ROLE logged). Client: NEW `createArgumentRoom` wrapper in `src/features/debates/debatesApi.ts`; `createDebate` REWIRED to route through the Edge path (no direct `debates` insert; signature kept for `useDebates`/`StartArgumentPage`). NEW pure twin `src/features/debates/roomCapacityModel.ts` (`roomActiveSeatCap`/`isCreationValid`/`openSlotsAfterCreate`/`canJoinActive` — imports the matrix caps, parity-tested against `publicSeatModel` 5/2 + the matrix for every row). gameCopy: 2 new `PLAIN_LANGUAGE_COPY` codes (`private_requires_invite`, `room_capacity_reached`) so denials round-trip through `toPlainLanguage` (no snake_case leak). **Design-review findings resolved:** [blocking] direct-insert door CLOSED (DROP the authenticated `debates` INSERT policy, NO replacement → the RPC is the sole creator; private+no-invite is unbypassable; refusal pinned by a text-scan test); [should] helper-grant oracle FIXED (`count_active_participants`/`count_reserved_invites`/`user_email_lower` revoked from PUBLIC, NOT granted to authenticated — definer trigger calls them as owner; grant-surface test pins it; `room_active_seat_cap` also definer-only); [should] co-landing RESOLVED (the migration + the Edge + the `createDebate` rewire all land in THIS one PR, so the creator-join moves to the service-role RPC with no stranding window);
