[should] the per-room index changes `manage-room-invite` create's `23505` meaning to "room already has a pending invite to anyone" — relabel owned by ARG-ROOM-006 (noted, this card does not touch `manage-room-invite`). **Docker/`db reset` DEFERRED** (not available in-session): heightened textual migration review + text-scan tests instead (migration shape, RLS policy + grant surface, OPS-001 four-class, append-only timestamp). +5 NEW suites (`argRoom002Migration`, `argRoom002RlsPolicy`, `createArgumentRoomEdge`, `roomCapacityModel`, `argRoom002Doctrine`) + rewired `debatesApi.visibility`. **Full jest: 760 suites / 30581 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 30582 total), up from the 755 / 30500 (30501 total) baseline (+5 suites / +81 tests), exit 0.** `npm run typecheck` exit 0; full `npm run lint` exit 0; `grep ANTHROPIC_API_KEY\|SERVICE_ROLE` over `src` is clean (the Edge fn uses `createServiceClient`, lives under `supabase/functions/`). **OPERATOR DEPLOY (GATE-C — merge IS deploy; operator-gated, do NOT automerge):** `npx supabase db push --linked` then `npx supabase functions deploy create-argument-room --linked` (the Supabase GitHub integration also auto-applies/redeploys on merge). **One-invite-per-room index precondition:** the migration's UNIQUE index fails if any room already has 2+ pending invites (the create surface never wired invites, so production should have zero); revoke surplus pending invites first if `db push` reports a unique-violation. **Operator follow-up (record-only, NOT in this card):** the 6 operator-gated `scripts/bot-fixtures/*` runners that do `botClient.from('debates').insert(...)` as the AUTHENTICATED bot user (`runStressBatch`, `runAiDrivenCorpus`, `runXaiAdversarialBotCorpus`, `runXaiAdversarialThreadCorpus`, `runMcpSmokeTest`) WILL be refused once the policy drop deploys — they must move to `create-argument-room` (or a service-role harness) in a follow-up card (live-gated dev scripts, not run in CI). See `docs/designs/ARG-ROOM-002-BACKEND-VISIBILITY-CAPACITY-INVITES.md`.

**2026-06-13 — ARG-ROOM-001 (Argument-room creation matrix — pure shared validator; PURE-TS, no migration/Edge/RLS/UI/network/provider/service-role):** The decision-only anchor of the public/private argument-room invite slate (roadmap `docs/roadmap-expansions/2026-06-13-public-private-argument-room-invites-roadmap.md`; issue #612) — one pure, deterministic, JSON-serializable validator answering "given a chosen visibility and an optional single direct invite, is this a valid room to create, and what is its seat accounting?" NEW `src/features/debates/argumentRoomCreationMatrix.ts` (pure TS, no React/Supabase/network/AI, no `async`/clock/randomness, frozen output — mirrors `engine.ts`; imports only shipped pure seams). `deriveArgumentRoomCreation(intent, opts?)` runs a fixed-order decision (visibility-normalise → raw `directInviteEmails.length > 1` → empty-token → single-field multi-address split → email shape → self-invite → valid) and returns a frozen `{ visibility, capacity 2|5, directInviteCount 0|1, reservedInviteSeats 0|1, openSlots 0|3|4, valid, reason?, normalisedDirectInviteEmail, maskedDirectInviteEmail }` (seat fields zeroed on every reject; `reservedInviteSeats === directInviteCount` for valid; `1 + reserved + openSlots === capacity`). Binding matrix encoded: private+no-invite → INVALID `private_requires_invite`; private+1 → VALID cap2/reserved1/open0; public+no-invite → VALID cap5/reserved0/open4; public+1 → VALID cap5/reserved1/open3; any 2+ → INVALID `too_many_direct_invites`. Ratified input shape is `directInviteEmails: string[]` (max length 1); reasons `private_requires_invite | too_many_direct_invites | invalid_email | self_invite`; field exposed as `openSlots`. REUSES (no new equivalents) `validateInviteEmailInput` (email shape, no new regex), `normaliseInviteeEmail` (storage + self-invite compare), `maskInviteeEmail` (echo), and `plainLanguageForInviteError` for the two overlapping reason copies (`self_invite`→`cannot_invite_self`, `invalid_email`) + its generic unknown-code fallback; authors ONLY the two genuinely new neutral strings. Capacity helpers `fitsPublicCapacity`/`fitsPrivateCapacity` (`active + reserved <= cap`). **6→5 reconcile (roadmap §4.1 operator decision 2):** `PUBLIC_ROOM_SEAT_CAP` reconciled 6→5 in `publicSeatModel.ts` (ONE source of truth — the matrix imports it as `PUBLIC_ACTIVE_PARTICIPANT_CAP` with a literal `: 5` annotation that doubles as a COMPILE-TIME reconcile guard, plus a runtime parity test); public chime-in capacity is now 5−2=3. Updated the cap-derived assertions in `__tests__/publicSeatModel.test.ts` (cap constant, the cap-fill + 7-chime-in-swarm fixtures, the `4 of 5 seats active` label) and `__tests__/publicRoomMetricsStrip.test.tsx` (`3 of 5 seats active`); the `{cap}` copy placeholder flows the rest. **Doctrine pins (test-proven):** decides capacity/validity, never correctness — no verdict string; a ban-list test scans every emitted copy for verdict/person tokens + the QOL-038 `BANNED_INVITE_FRAMING` (`challenger`/`opponent`) and asserts no raw snake_case reason code leaks; account-enumeration-safe (same shape regardless of invitee existence, never queries it); self-invite is best-effort (only when `creatorEmail` supplied — the Edge guard stays authoritative). Decision-only — NO migration/Edge/RLS/UI/insert (write-path enforcement is the sibling GATE-C card ARG-ROOM-002). NEW `__tests__/argumentRoomCreationMatrix.test.ts` (**+57 tests** — every matrix row, the five operator-ratified examples verbatim, invalid-email, self-invite [+ skip-when-no-creatorEmail], multi-address paste, normalisation/mask, visibility coercion, cross-branch invariants, capacity boundaries, plain-language reuse + ban-list, determinism/non-mutation/frozen, and the 6→5 parity). Barrel `src/features/debates/index.ts` re-exports the module (dir convention). **Full jest: 755 suites / 30500 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 30501 total), up from the 754 / 30443 (30444 total) baseline (+1 suite / +57 tests), exit 0.** `npm run typecheck` exit 0; full `npm run lint` exit 0 (this card's `chore(lint)` commit removed the dead `no-console` `eslint-disable`/`enable` pair at `scripts/auth/sendInviteSmoke.js:220`/`:283` — a #609 leftover that suppressed nothing because `eslint.config.js` scopes `no-console` to `.ts`/`.tsx` only, so it failed `--max-warnings 0`; comment-only, no behavior change); `grep ANTHROPIC_API_KEY\|SERVICE_ROLE` over the new module empty. **NO operator follow-up** — pure `src` TS change; no `db push`, no `functions deploy`, no Edge, no env var, no service-role, no new dependency. Merge is not a deploy. See `docs/designs/ARG-ROOM-001-CREATION-MATRIX-AND-MODEL.md`.

**2026-06-13 — AUTH-CALLBACK-CONSUMER-001 (Supabase invite callback + password-set flow; CLIENT-ONLY, no migration/Edge/hosted-config/provider/service-role):** Builds the missing consumer for `/auth/callback` — the route the branded Supabase invite email (AUTH-INVITE-BRANDED-SMOKE) redirects to. The app previously had no screen for that route (`detectSessionInUrl: false`, no `/auth/callback` anywhere), so an invited user landed nowhere; this card turns the redirect into a session (or a plain recoverable error) and routes an invited (passwordless) user to a **required set-password step**. NEW pure `src/lib/auth/parseAuthCallbackUrl.ts` (~245 lines, no `window`/`process`/React/Supabase, NEVER throws): `parseAuthCallbackUrl(url)` → a 5-variant discriminated union (`tokens` | `code` | `error` | `empty` | `unsupported`) with precedence **error > tokens > code > token_hash(unsupported) > empty**; `isAuthCallbackPath`, `redactAuthCallbackUrl` (`***`-redacts access/refresh/provider/id token + `token_hash` + `code` values; keeps the non-secret `error_code`), `describeAuthCallbackForDiagnostics` (no token value). NEW `src/features/auth/consumeAuthCallback.ts` (injected `AuthCallbackClient` — `supabase.auth` is structurally assignable; the ONLY side effect is that injected call, no `supabase` import, no `fetch`): primary fragment-token path → `setSession`; defensive `?code=` → `exchangeCodeForSession`; `error` → plain (recoverable→`expired` else `link_invalid`); `empty` → `getSession` probe → `already_session` or `link_invalid`. **Only `type=invite` returns `needs_password`** — `signup`/`magiclink`/`email_change`/missing-type establish the session and continue. NEW `authCallbackCopy.ts` (plain link-state `AUTH_CALLBACK_COPY` bundle + `plainLanguageForSetPasswordError`) and `AuthCallbackScreen.tsx` (six states: `checking → accepted | set_password → password_set | error_expired | error_generic`; reuses `Screen`/`Button`/`TextInputField`/`ErrorNotice`/`LoadingNotice`; submit disabled until `validateNewPassword` passes via `accessibilityState`; secure labelled input; the password is sent ONLY into `setInvitedUserPassword` and cleared after save; Continue/Return clear the URL via `window.history.replaceState` behind a `typeof window` guard then hand control back). MODIFIED `src/features/auth/authApi.ts` (+`validateNewPassword`, +`setInvitedUserPassword` wrapping `supabase.auth.updateUser({ password })`; reuses the existing `mapAuthError` + `SUPABASE_CONFIGURED` guard; sign-in/sign-up/reset wrappers byte-behaviour-unchanged) and `App.tsx` (synchronous `/auth/callback` capture in a `useState` initializer guarded by `typeof window`, a **highest-priority** screen branch above the invite gate / AuthScreen / shell, the bare `AppHeader` still docks; native = zero behaviour change). **Doctrine pins (test-proven):** `detectSessionInUrl` stays `false` (manual consumption; flipping it would race the manual path); no migration / Edge / RLS / SQL / hosted config / provider call / second auth client; no service-role / Anthropic key in any new client file; no password logged or persisted; tokens redacted in diagnostics; copy is link-state language only (no winner/loser/truth/verdict/raw-code/stack). 7 NEW test suites / **+147 tests** (`parseAuthCallbackUrl` — variants/precedence/redaction/never-throws-on-garbage; `consumeAuthCallback` — outcome mapping + injected-client-only + no-token-log source scan; `authCallbackSetPassword` — validator boundaries + `updateUser` wrapper + password-never-leaked; `AuthCallbackScreen` — six states + set-password form + disabled-until-valid + URL-clear-on-Continue/Return + config-missing short-circuit + no-token/raw-code-in-tree; `authCallbackCopy` — ban-list; `authCallbackRouting` — `invite → /auth/callback` pin + App.tsx capture wiring + `detectSessionInUrl` unchanged + existing-wrappers-intact; `authCallbackSmokeReadiness` — synthetic URL → parse → consume(mock) → `needs_password`, no-live-send). **Full jest: 754 suites / 30436 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 30437 total), up from the branch-base 747 / 30289 (30290 total) baseline (+7 suites / +147 tests), exit 0.** `npx tsc --noEmit` exit 0; scoped `eslint` on the 4 new + 2 modified src files + 7 test files clean; `grep SERVICE_ROLE\|ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` over the new client files empty. **Seed-smoke readiness = `READY_FOR_SEED_SEND`** (`docs/testing-runs/2026-06-13-auth-callback-consumer.md`) — the redirect URL is `https://<deployed-origin>/auth/callback`; the live G1 send stays operator-gated (G2 SMTP + G3 template + G4 redirect allow-list + armed `CDISCOURSE_ALLOW_INVITE_SEND_SMOKE`). **NO operator deploy for this card** — pure client change; no `db push`, no `functions deploy`, no hosted config, no env var, no service-role, no new dependency. **GATE-C: operator-gated merge (auth/session behaviour); stop at PR, no automerge.** See `docs/designs/AUTH-CALLBACK-CONSUMER-001.md`.

**2026-06-12 — DEMO-001 (Recruitable Debate Demo Corridor — first-disagreement walkthrough; DETERMINISTIC UI + FIXTURE, no provider/network/credential/migration/Edge):** A no-spend, no-provider **first-run corridor** that lets a cold viewer experience the Disagreement Contract loop in under three minutes using the **real shipped components** — not screenshots, not a video, not a parallel demo UI. It walks exactly **one disagreement** (a synthetic, neutral, unheated municipal **library weeknight-hours** debate): here is a claim → here is the disputed point → the real Referee Card reads `Source owed` → pick one of four plain moves (Ask for a source · Add evidence · Narrow the scope · Open a side issue) → the real surface re-derives the open task → a recruit-friendly closing screen. NEW `src/features/demoCorridor/` (7 files, ~1,050 lines): `corridorModel.ts` (pure-TS 7-step state machine — `CORRIDOR_STEPS`, the one-primary-action invariant, the deterministic `advanceCorridor` reducer, `resolveCorridorView`, the scripted `DEMO_MOVE_TRANSITIONS`, the frozen ban-list-clean `CORRIDOR_COPY`, `mapControlToDemoMove`, and `makeDemoBeforeSubmit` — no React/Supabase/network); `demoFixtureRoom.ts` (the bundled fixture — 5 states, all `ArgumentMessageInput[]` + tag maps + a `Debate`/`ArgumentRow` for the real composer; imports only surface prop TYPES); `DemoCorridorScreen.tsx` (route container — `useReducer`, mounts the REAL `ArgumentGameSurface` fed from the fixture, the guidance panel, the four-move menu, and the demo composer; pins the active node per beat via the existing `entryHint.entryHintForArgumentId` seam); `DemoCorridorGuidancePanel.tsx`, `DemoMoveMenu.tsx`, `DemoComposerPanel.tsx` (mounts the REAL `OneBox` with `onBeforeSubmit` returning `false` — the production submit chain is byte-untouched), `index.ts`. **The `source_owed` lever (pinned through the real derivation, not inspected):** the disputed sub-claim carries an OPEN `source_request`-tagged ask with no attached evidence, so the surface's pure `deriveEvidenceDebts → buildOpenIssue` chain yields `burden: 'source_owed'` / `axis: 'evidence'` on the active node; `add_evidence` supplies a source and the open source-debt count on that node drops 1→0. **The two interception seams are existing additive props (zero production submit-chain edits):** the surface's required `onAction` (a different value, not a code change) and the optional `OneBox.onBeforeSubmit` returning `false` (the RULE-004 pre-network hook) — `handlePostIntent` short-circuits before the network submit, so there is no post to block, reject, route, or delay (the deterministic Constitution engine stays the SOLE gate). MODIFIED `App.tsx` (~25 lines): `demoCorridorOpen` flag mirroring `aboutOpen`, a "See how it works" gallery-toolbar `Pressable` (testID `open-demo-corridor-trigger`, observer-first/non-modal), and a routed `<DemoCorridorScreen>` mount with `!demoCorridorOpen` added to the **arguments-tab** gallery/room/notifications guards only (account/admin/debug guards left byte-identical — the UX-001.2 App safeguard pins them; the corridor opens only from the arguments tab). **Open-question resolution (orchestrator-fixed, operator-overridable at the gate):** the "now you try" step uses the design's RECOMMENDED **stand-in participant** framing (`viewerRole='participant'`, `participantSide='affirmative'`, explicit entry copy "Step into one side of a live dispute…") so the four real moves are reachable without a separate scripted "Join" beat; the alternative strict-observer-with-inline-Join path adds ~15s and is the operator-overridable fallback. **One faithful deviation from the design's illustrative prediction (the design explicitly said to pin via test, not trust inspection):** the disputed node's derived `relationToParent` is `challenges` (a rebuttal challenging the root), not the design's inspection-guess of `asks_source`; the BINDING `burden === 'source_owed'` + `axis === 'evidence'` hold exactly and are pinned. The demo composer is **interactive** (the user picks a side / edits, then presses the real Post) rather than 1-tap — `quickActionToPreset` seeds a valid type+body for `ask_source`/`narrow`, while `add_evidence` needs a receipt and `branch` needs a chosen type (the design's own step-4b copy already says "Edit if you like, then post your move"). **Engine `allowPost` is pinned per preset against the fixture room (review §1):** `ask_source`/`narrow` are immediately Post-able — the SHIPPED `quickActionToPreset` seeds a valid type+body so the real engine returns `allowPost: true` as-offered — while `add_evidence`/`branch` reach `allowPost: true` only after the corridor-instructed completion (the receipt / the chosen type); each of the four moves' completed draft is proven Post-able by an `evaluateArgumentDraft`-per-preset test, **plus** an assembled regression that completes `ask_source`, presses the REAL enabled "Post move" button, and asserts the corridor advances to `issue_state_change` with `submitArgumentDraft` never called (the advance seam is now integration-pinned, not just unit-pinned). 7 NEW test suites / **+90 tests** (`demoCorridorModel` — progression/determinism/one-primary-action/scripted-table/replay-idempotence/`mapControlToDemoMove`/`makeDemoBeforeSubmit`; `demoCorridorFixture` — shape + `buildOpenIssue`-over-fixture `source_owed`/`evidence` + the post-move debt-count changes + ban-list/no-raw-code over all fixture strings; `demoCorridorCopy` — ban-list + no-snake_case over `CORRIDOR_COPY`/steps + the plain-move proof; `DemoCorridorScreen` — mounts the REAL `referee-card-view`/`open-issues-rail`/`one-box`, one primary per step, "Source owed" on the referee beat, picking a move opens the real composer, the network submit is never invoked, the closing screen + exit; `demoCorridorNoProvider` — source scan banning supabase/submit/Edge/`fetch(`/Anthropic/xai/service-role + the room-data-hook + submit-chain import-absence; `demoCorridorA11y` — roles/labels/state, ≥44×44, completable via buttons alone, four independently-focusable move options, reduce-motion renders static; `demoCorridorEngineValidity` (review §1) — per-preset `evaluateArgumentDraft` `allowPost` over each move's completed draft [`ask_source`/`narrow` as-offered, `add_evidence` + receipt, `branch` + chosen type] **plus** the assembled real-Post regression [completes `ask_source`, presses the REAL enabled Post, asserts advance to `issue_state_change` + `submitArgumentDraft` never called]). **Full jest (after the review §1 coverage fix): 745 suites / 30245 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 30246 total), up from the 738 / 30155 (30156 total) baseline (+7 suites / +90 tests), exit 0.** `npx tsc --noEmit` exit 0; scoped `eslint` on the 7 new src files + `App.tsx` + 6 test files clean; anchor suites green (REF-002/003/004 + `openIssuesRail` + `requestReview` + `actPopoutModel` + `boardActPopoutMountSite` + `uxOneOneFiveReadOnlyBoundary` + `uxOneOneTwoDoctrine`/`uxOneOneTwoAppSafeguard`/`uxOneOneTwoHiddenTabBar`); `grep ANTHROPIC_API_KEY\|SERVICE_ROLE src/features/demoCorridor/` empty. **NO operator follow-up** — pure `src` UI + fixture change; no migration, no `db push`, no `functions deploy`, no Edge, no env var, no service-role, no new dependency. Merge is not a deploy. The only follow-on is the REF-006 (#589) timed dogfood pass against the shipped corridor (a usability session, not a deploy). See `docs/designs/DEMO-001.md`.

**2026-06-12 — REF-006-RAIL (Open Issues rail for the Disagreement Contract loop; UI COMPOSITION, no persistence/Edge/migration/AI/provider):** Builds the room-wide **Open Issues rail** on top of the already-shipped per-node Disagreement Contract derivation — a **pure projection** over many nodes' already-built `OpenIssue` objects (REF-002's `buildOpenIssue` stays the SINGLE derivation home; the rail writes **no** derivation logic). NEW `src/features/arguments/openIssuesRail/openIssuesRailModel.ts` (~330 lines, pure TS, no React/Supabase/fetch/`Date.now`): the thin iterator `buildOpenIssuesLedger(candidates, options)` + `isOpenIssue` (the exact predicate `burden !== 'none' || state ∉ {answered, moved_on}`), `ledgerRank` (frozen seven-tier urgency `source→quote→clarification→reply→synthesis_ready→narrowed→conceded`), `compareLedgerCandidates` (rank asc → recency-desc → `issue.id` asc, fully deterministic), the frozen `OPEN_ISSUES_RAIL_COPY` chrome set, `RAIL_PROPOSITION_CAP=88`, and the Layer-1 host pre-filter vocabulary (`OPEN_ISSUES_RAIL_BUILD_CAP=48`, `CLOSED_LIFECYCLE_STATES`, `CANDIDATE_SIGNAL_TAGS` — frozen data the host consults; the iterator itself decides openness). NEW `OpenIssuesRail.tsx` (~430 lines): a collapsed-by-default chip ("Open issues · N") that expands into a compact panel (SC-005 dock chassis reuse — `resolveObserverDockVariant` / `resolveSheetMaxHeightPx`; side-anchored wide, capped ~28% bottom sheet narrow, never full-screen), each row showing plain state chip + non-color tone glyph + burden·axis line + verbatim contested-point excerpt + whole-row **Jump** + **Details** (Inspect) + the head 1–2 `nextBestMoves` chips, an in-panel "+N more" overflow reveal, the teaching empty state ("No open issues right now."), the active row distinguished by **geometry** (left bar + bold + "Currently active" word, grayscale-legible), reduce-motion-safe snap. NEW `index.ts` barrel. **Host wiring (`ArgumentGameSurface.tsx`, +~210 lines):** added O(1) `messageById` / `nodeByMessageId` / `recencyIndexById` maps (retro-de-quadratics `activeParentType` too); extracted the active-node assembly body into one shared `assembleRefereeCardInputForMessage(messageId, bannerSelection)` closure (active node keeps `refereeBanner`; candidates pass `bannerSelection: null` — byte-equivalent for the active node, pinned by the green REF-003/004 mount suites); a cheap Layer-1 candidate pre-filter superset → K-capped (≤48) per-candidate `buildRefereeCardInput → buildOpenIssue` build memoized on the data maps **but NOT `activeMessageId`** (the performance contract — changing the active node re-runs only the cheap `isActive` overlay + `buildOpenIssuesLedger`, never 48 rebuilds); generalized `enterBoxForActEntry(entryId, targetMessageId?, targetParentType?)` (existing call sites byte-identical); three handlers routing jump → `setActiveMessageId` + `issueStateToGoLens`, Details → the shipped Inspect popout + `InspectOpenIssueDetail` overlay, move → the SAME `enterBoxForActEntry` bridge; mounted `<OpenIssuesRail>` as a sibling immediately above `<ArgumentSideActionRail>` with two-state mutual exclusion (both bottom rails collapse the other). **Doctrine pins (test-proven):** ordering reads ONLY burden/state/recency-index/id (a member-access source scan bans `.heat/.popularity/.virality/.score/.standingBand/...`; a shuffle-determinism test); every emitted string is a frozen REF-002 atom or an `OPEN_ISSUES_RAIL_COPY` chrome string (ban-list scan over the 16 prohibited verdict/person tokens); no rendered string trips `looksLikeInternalCode` and the `issue.id`/`sourceCode`/`rawKey` never reach the text (row key is React-key + testID only); zero `userAllegations`, Family J re-asserted out upstream by REF-002; a11y per `accessibility-targets` (≥44×44 via `TOUCH_TARGET`, role/label/`accessibilityState` selected/expanded, tone glyph hidden from the SR tree, color-independent geometry, reduce-motion snap). 5 NEW test suites + 1 fixtures helper / **+114 tests** (`openIssuesRailModel.test.ts` — predicate, the frozen rank table, ordering+determinism, cap/overflow/omitted honesty, isActive, openTaskLine, proposition truncation, the Layer-1 vocab partition, purity + no-engagement-field scans; `OpenIssuesRail.test.tsx` — collapsed default, expand/collapse, jump/Inspect/move routing, move-chip-does-not-fire-jump, empty state, active highlight, observer empty-moves rows, overflow reveal, mutual-exclusion, `onExpandedChange`; `openIssuesRailBanList.test.ts`; `openIssuesRailNoRawCodes.test.ts`; `openIssuesRailA11y.test.tsx`). **Full jest: 738 suites / 30156 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 30157 total), up from the 733 / 30042 (30043 total) baseline (+5 suites / +114 tests), exit 0.** `npx tsc --noEmit` exit 0; scoped `eslint` on the 3 new + 1 modified src files + 5 test files + 1 fixture clean; anchor suites green (REF-002/003/004 suites, `actPopoutModel`, `goPopoutModel`, `boardActPopoutMountSite`, `uxOneOneTwoDoctrine`); `grep ANTHROPIC_API_KEY\|SERVICE_ROLE src/features/arguments/openIssuesRail/` empty. **Operator-deferred (recorded in `docs/designs/REF-006-RAIL.md`):** the `conceded` IssueState is KEPT in the ledger per the card's explicit bounded rule (`state ∉ {answered, moved_on}`) — borderline "resolved", one-line removable by adding `'conceded'` to `TERMINAL_DISPLAY_STATES`; recency direction is most-recent-first within a tier (oldest-owed-first deferred); display defaults (initial 8 rows, K=48 build ceiling) are tunable. **NO operator follow-up** — pure `src` UI change; no migration, no `db push`, no `functions deploy`, no Edge, no env var, no service-role, no new dependency. Merge is not a deploy. See `docs/designs/REF-006-RAIL.md`.

**2026-06-12 — REF-006 (Human usability smoke: 5-task first-user pass; DOCS-ONLY, no code/tests-as-code/migration/Edge/AI/provider spend):** Produces the **first human usability evidence** for the shipped disagreement loop (REF-002 model → REF-003 Referee Card → REF-004 Act/Inspect/Go routing → REF-005 Request-review, all merged to `main` at `b515da6`) as **two committed Markdown artifacts** — a facilitator-run protocol and a blank results template — plus this status entry. **Zero production code, zero tests-as-code, zero provider spend** (no Anthropic / xAI / X API call, no Supabase write beyond a participant's own ordinary moves, no service-role, no migration, no Edge). NEW `docs/testing-runs/REF-006-usability-smoke-protocol.md` (~245 lines) — instrument-vs-usability framing (verbatim, drift-locked: n ≤ 3 is a **directional first baseline only**, no metric, no pass/fail gate, no quantitative claim), recruitment note (1–3 first-time participants, no docs in hand, `P1`/`P2`/`P3` identifiers, anonymized quotes, no PII beyond volunteered), facilitator ground rules (read each script **verbatim** then stay quiet; **never** asks the participant to judge who is right or wrong; the only permitted non-answer is *"Whatever makes sense to you — there's no wrong move here."*), the **5 tasks** each with the reality-audited start-screen mapping + verbatim neutral script + capture rules (Tasks 2 and 4 carry **two valid entry paths** — Referee Card next-move button **or** Act, since the rail consolidated `Ask source`/branch into Act at `b515da6`; the facilitator records **which path** the participant found first), the **capture-field definitions** (the 10 fields with the **three token-free renames** + a **traceability map** back to the issue's originals: `askedSourceOnCorrectTarget → askedSourceOnIntendedTarget`, `refereeCardParaphraseCorrect → refereeCardParaphraseMatched`, `confusedByScoreHeatOrTruth → mixedUpScoreHeatWithStanding`; `rawCodeSeen` framed as a **failure capture** — any `snake_case`/family ID/`rawKey`/internal code reaching the participant fails the task), the **REF-005 label-split** facilitator note (`Request review` chip/composer vs `Send for review` Act entry — a documented cosmetic split, not a participant error), the **operator dry walk-through** 8-line checklist (reproduced inside the protocol; the named **pending acceptance step** — the card does not close until the operator confirms every task is executable on the current build), and the findings path (every stuck point + `suggestedCopyFix` becomes a **NEW issue**, never an inline fix). NEW `docs/testing-runs/REF-006-usability-smoke-results-template.md` (~135 lines) — run-metadata header (date, build SHA, participant count, dry-walk-through gate), per-participant per-task result rows (`pass`/`stuck` + verbatim-quote slot + the applicable capture fields, with a per-task field-applicability list so the facilitator never guesses), the rename footnote, the append-only **stuck-point ledger** (proposed-new-card column), and the **"their words vs our labels" vocabulary table** framed verbatim as **INPUT offered to META-1D (#79)** — #79's current scope stays the 26 META-001 codes; any extension to the REF rail/card vocabulary is **proposed on #79, not assumed here**. **Doctrine (cdiscourse-doctrine §1/§2/§9):** tasks are **procedural only** — the protocol never asks a participant to rule on the debate; the renamed fields drop the three `camelCase`-glued verdict tokens; the prohibited verdict/person tokens appear in both files **only** inside prohibition / failure-capture framing; every capture-field name and internal code is in backticks (participant-facing Task + script lines name **no** internal vocabulary — plain instructions only). Files deliberately live under `docs/testing-runs/` (not `docs/audits/**`) so the SMOKE audit-lint hook never fires. **Verification:** full `npx jest --silent` = **733 suites / 30042 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 30043 total), exit 0 — UNCHANGED from baseline** (docs-only diff provably cannot move the count); `npx tsc --noEmit` exit 0; manual ban-scan over both files = zero ordinary-prose hits for the 16 prohibited tokens (all `verdict`/`truth` occurrences are negations or the named-token-class) and zero naked `snake_case` (all 5 occurrences inside backticks). **NO operator deploy** — pure docs change; no `db push`, no `functions deploy`, no env var, no service-role, no new dependency. Merge is not a deploy. The one operator action is the **dry walk-through (acceptance criterion, not a deploy gate)**; the human run itself is operator-conducted. See `docs/designs/REF-006.md`.

**2026-06-12 — REF-005 (Request review / Mark concern — structured allegations; COMPOSER-LOCAL v1, no persistence/Edge/migration/AI):** Replaces two loose "flag / accuse" affordances with a **procedural allegation object**: a concern about another participant's move is only expressible as (a) a required target quote, (b) a bounded concern type (6 values), and (c) a bounded requested remedy (6 values) — never as free text on a person. **Persistence decision = Option (b): draft / composer-local only** (design §2, with quoted schema evidence). The live `public.argument_flags` table cannot carry the structured concern without a migration + RLS change — its SELECT policy makes any row readable by the move's author (the target) and every room participant, the exact "surfacing reads as accusation" failure §10a forbids; there is no visibility column, no home for `targetQuote`/`requestedRemedy` (the `payload jsonb` overload is disqualified), `flag_code` is a NOT NULL FK to a seeded registry, and no client write path exists. So **no `supabase/**`, no migration, no Edge, no RLS, no new `argument_flags` write/delete lands in-card** — the persisted table + moderator-only SELECT RLS + submission Edge + real queue are the named follow-up **REF-005B (GATE-C)**. NEW pure-TS `src/features/requestReview/requestReviewModel.ts` (the issue's verbatim `ReviewConcernType` / `ReviewRequestedRemedy` / `StructuredConcernDraft` + `canSubmitConcern` [all four fields required], `routeRemedy` [claim-level remedies → the shipped `enterBoxForActEntry` ActEntryIds `ask_source`/`ask_quote`/`branch_tangent`/`narrow`; moderator-queue remedies → a `moderator_queue` descriptor, **nothing hides automatically**], `deriveConcernVisibility`, `buildSubmittableConcern`, copy maps; type-only `ActEntryId` import, no React/Supabase/network/AI). **Doctrine pins (test-proven):** `deriveConcernVisibility` NEVER returns `public_after_review` for any of the 36 (type × remedy) combos (16 `composer_only` / 20 `moderator_visible` / 0 `public_after_review`); the two **person-directed** types (`about_person_not_claim`, `harassment_concern`) are ALWAYS `moderator_visible` — never public, never on the target's node (the §10a person-directed-never-public pin); the concern-type labels are *procedural* framing, and the 16 prohibited person/verdict tokens are unexpressible by any path (no free-text concern field exists — only bounded chips). NEW presentational `RequestReviewComposer.tsx` (3-step overlay: quote `TextInput` → 6 concern-type radio chips → 6 remedy radio chips + a live visibility readout; ≥44px targets, role/label/checked state, `announceForAccessibility` step transitions, inline `accessibilityLiveRegion` quote error, reduce-motion-safe by construction, cancelable at every step) + barrel. Wired into `ArgumentGameSurface.tsx` as a composer-side sibling overlay (like the deletion sheet): all `flag` entry points — the bubble chip, the timeline node popover, the action dock, and the board-Act direct entry — route through one `handleBubbleAction` interceptor / branch that opens the composer instead of firing the loose `handleAction('flag')` notify; on submit, claim-level remedies re-enter the SHIPPED disagreement loop via `enterBoxForActEntry`, moderator-queue remedies hand off to the EXISTING, unchanged "Send for review" callback (the v1 interim; REF-005B persists it). `ArgumentBubbleActions.tsx` `flag` chip relabeled "Flag" → "Request review". **The wall (§7):** the moderation concern is a third object, walled from machine Observations and gameplay Allegations — `requestReview` imports nothing from `nodeLabels/` or `moveMetadataLedger`, emits no `NodeLabelMark`, and `needs_source`/`needs_quote` exist as BOTH a `ReviewConcernType` and a `ManualTagCode` but are distinct objects from distinct modules (pinned). **Precision finding (§2.6):** the soft-dismiss convention in scope is the REAL live-schema one — `status = 'dismissed'` + `resolved_by`/`resolved_at` (NOT the drifted CLAUDE.md `dismissed`/`reviewed_by`/`reviewed_at`, which the live schema does not have); v1 introduces no delete path. 5 NEW suites / **+61 tests** (`requestReviewModel.test.ts` — required fields + the full 6×6 matrix + visibility pins; `requestReviewVisibility.test.tsx` — renders the composer via `@testing-library/react-native`, person-directed→moderator-visible readout + no public/target-node surface, submit gating, person-directed+`hide_pending_review`→`onSendForModeratorReview`-only with no auto-hide, own-bubble never offered; `requestReviewCopyBanList.test.ts` — 16 tokens over every string + no snake_case + exactly one free-text field; `requestReviewFlagsConventions.test.ts` — no new argument_flags write/delete, select-only stays in `argumentsApi.ts`, real soft-dismiss names; `requestReviewWall.test.ts`). **Full jest: 733 suites / 30042 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 30043 total), up from the 728 / 29981 (29982 total) baseline (+5 suites / +61 tests), exit 0.** `npx tsc --noEmit` exit 0; scoped `eslint` on the 3 new + 3 modified src files + 5 test files clean; anchor suites green (`actPopoutModel`, `actPopoutComponent`, `oneBoxCopyBanList`, all REF-003 + disagreement-loop REF-004 suites, `boardActPopoutMountSite`, `timelineNodeActionDockSelectionExclusion`, `uxOneOneFiveReadOnlyBoundary`); `grep ANTHROPIC_API_KEY\|SERVICE_ROLE src/features/requestReview/` empty. **ONE §8 deferral (recorded as an Implementer note in `docs/designs/REF-005.md`):** the §8 relabel of the `flag` ActEntry in `oneBox/actPopoutModel.ts` could NOT land — `uxOneOneFiveReadOnlyBoundary.test.ts` pins that file to a zero-diff against main, and editing a failing guard is a never-self-approve action; the relabel was reverted (file byte-identical to main) while the behavioral rebind (board-Act `flag` opens the structured composer) is fully delivered. Net effect: the board-Act + timeline-popover entries still *read* their existing labels ("Send for review" / "Flag") but open the structured composer; the bubble chip reads "Request review". The note recommends the codebase's established pattern (remove the file from the boundary with a NOTE citing REF-005, per the AppHeader/designTokens precedents) as the REF-005-follow-up, since the file's contract is already pinned by `actPopoutModel.test.ts` + `oneBoxCopyBanList.test.ts`. **Automerge NOT eligible** (moderation-visibility surface in play — design §16). **NO operator follow-up** — pure `src` UI change; no migration, no `db push`, no `functions deploy`, no Edge, no env var, no service-role, no new dependency. Merge is not a deploy. See `docs/designs/REF-005.md` (+ its Implementer note).

**2026-06-12 — REF-004 (Act / Inspect / Go integration: one disagreement loop; DERIVED-ONLY UI, no persistence/Edge/migration/AI):** Closes REF-001's ratified loop — **Act changes the issue · Inspect explains the issue · Go moves around the issue map** — by wiring REF-003's Referee Card zone-3 buttons + two new secondary affordances into the three already-shipped one-box popouts, with a **provably gate-neutral** diff (no file in the engine/role gating path is touched). **(1) Act routing (handler swap):** extracted the board-Act `handleBoardActSelectBoxType` body into a single shared `enterBoxForActEntry(entryId)` useCallback in `ArgumentGameSurface.tsx` (`actEntryToQuickAction(entryId)` → `quickActionToPreset` → `handleAction('reply', activeMessageId ?? '', preset)` — the EXISTING composer-open path, byte-equivalent to the prior inline `if (activeMessageId) … else handleAction('reply','',…)`); BOTH the board Act path and the swapped `handleRefereeMove` now delegate to it, so "Act changes the issue" is ONE code path, not two. The v1 `null`-quickAction early-return is dropped (every constructive zone-3 entry has a non-null quick action; the `reply`-class entries correctly open the composer with no forced preset, identical to the board path). **(2) Inspect handoff:** NEW `buildInspectOpenIssueDetail(issue)` + `InspectOpenIssueDetail` interface in `oneBox/inspectContentBuilder.ts` (additive; existing `buildInspectContent` untouched) + NEW sibling overlay `cardView/InspectOpenIssueDetail.tsx` mounted exactly like `NodeLabelInspectGroups` / `MetadataDiffInspector` (gated on `inspectVisible && activeMessageId && refereeCardIssue != null`). It is the SINGLE home for the issue's raw codes — a plain-language block (relation · axis · burden · state, from REF-002's frozen label maps) + a clearly-diagnostic **"Raw provenance (Inspect only)"** block (`issue.id` + each Observation/Allegation `sourceCode`, or a one-line "No raw provenance on this issue." note). A **"View details"** card affordance opens the existing Inspect popout; the fixed 7-section InspectPopout core is unchanged. **(3) Go handoff:** NEW `issueStateToGoLens(state)` + frozen `ISSUE_STATE_TO_GO_LENS` in `oneBox/goPopoutModel.ts` mapping the 8 `IssueState`s onto the three EXISTING Go lenses (`open→unresolved`; `source_requested`/`quote_requested→evidence`; the rest→`active_path`) — NO new `FocusLensId`, no `timelineDensityLensModel` change; a **"Focus on board"** affordance (rendered iff `targetNodeId != null`) jumps to the target node and applies the lens via the shipped `setGoLens` path. **Dims-never-hides preserved by construction** (the shipped `applyTimelineLens` `LensEmphasis` is only `'bright' | 'dimmed'`). `IssueState` is a TYPE-ONLY import in goPopoutModel (no runtime cycle); inspectContentBuilder value-imports the label maps over an acyclic edge (tsc-verified). **(4) Recovery surface:** engine-invalid intended moves never render active — the card renders only REF-002's `nextBestMoves` (already engine+role survivors), and a gate-removed `narrow` surfaces the `branch_tangent` recovery (its normal Act label, never "invalid"/"rejected") which routes through the SAME `enterBoxForActEntry('branch_tangent')` into an engine-valid box (no parallel mechanism, no bypass). **Plumbing:** one additive optional `onRefereeNavigate?: (verb: RefereeNavVerb)` prop threaded through the SAME 4-level chain REF-003 established (`ArgumentBubbleStack` → `ArgumentBubbleCard` → `CardDetailPanel` → `RefereeCardView`), forwarded to the active card only; omitted → the secondary affordance row does not render (byte-equivalent to REF-003, pinned by the green REF-003 suites). **Doctrine pins (test-proven):** raw keys/`sourceCode`/`issue.id` appear ONLY in the new Inspect overlay (the card stays raw-free, including the new affordance row — REF-003's `refereeCardNoRawCodes` re-pinned for the REF-004 surface); routing reads only `move.actEntryId` and the lens reads only the procedural `IssueState` (no heat/popularity/strength signal); no submit/fetch/Edge/Supabase/service-role path added (Act routing reuses the composer-open path); a11y per `accessibility-targets` (both affordances ≥44×44 via `TOUCH_TARGET.minSizePx` + `hitSlopAll`, role/label/state; the Inspect popout owns the shipped focus-trap + return; static/reduce-motion-safe; no key badge). **Two design-recorded scope resolutions:** (a) Act routing is **direct-into-box**, not pre-focused Act (the live `ActPopout` chassis has no pre-focus prop; the card button already encodes the surviving entry); (b) **no Open Issues rail is shipped** — a repo scan confirms only a `gameCopy.ts` comment names it — so this card scopes to the card buttons + Go filtering and names the rail as the **REF-006-RAIL** follow-up (the "Rail → Act → box → post → card update" acceptance line is delivered for the card-button→Act→box half; the post→card-update half is REF-003's existing post-not-blocked regression). 4 NEW test suites / **60 tests** (`disagreementLoopRouting.test.tsx` — the handoff matrix row by row: per-entry `actEntryToQuickAction` + `opensBoxType`, button-press → `onMove` payload, every move enabled, affordance-row gating, board-path single-bridge source scan; `disagreementLoopGoFilter.test.ts` — the 8-state lens map total/frozen, each result a `TIMELINE_LENS_IDS` member or null, `applyTimelineLens` dims-never-hides + zero-match all-bright; `disagreementLoopRecoveryRoutes.test.ts` — invalid never active, `branch_tangent` recovery surfaces + is a `buildActPopout` survivor, observer/own-bubble empty; `disagreementLoopInspectRawKeys.test.tsx` — raw keys present in the overlay + absent from the card, ban-list + no-snake-case scan of the user-facing copy). **Full jest: 728 suites / 29981 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 29982 total), up from the 724 / 29921 (29922 total) baseline (+4 suites / +60 tests), exit 0.** `npx tsc --noEmit` exit 0; scoped `eslint` on the 7 modified + 1 new src files + 4 test files clean; anchor suites green (`actPopoutModel`, `actPopoutComponent`, `inspectPopoutModel`, `goPopoutModel`, `tangentRoutingModel`, all four REF-003 suites, `boardActPopoutMountSite`, `openIssueModel.*`); `grep ANTHROPIC_API_KEY\|SERVICE_ROLE` in the new files empty. **NO operator follow-up** — pure `src` UI change; no migration, no `db push`, no `functions deploy`, no Edge, no env var, no service-role, no new dependency. Merge is not a deploy. See `docs/designs/REF-004.md`.

**2026-06-12 — REF-003 (Referee Card surface in active node detail; DERIVED-ONLY UI, no persistence/Edge/migration/AI):** Renders REF-001's ratified three-zone Referee Card from REF-002's shipped `DisagreementContract` derivation, on the ACTIVE node's detail surface inside the existing `CardDetailPanel` chassis. NEW `src/features/arguments/cardView/RefereeCardView.tsx` — pure presentational React component (no state, no network, no AI, no `selectBanner`): consumes a `DisagreementContract` and derives its `RefereeCardViewModel` in-component via the shipped `buildRefereeCardViewModel` (REF-002 stays the single derivation owner); renders the optional verbatim "point under dispute" anchor, zone 1 (referee note + non-color tone glyph from `BANNER_TONE_GLYPH_CHAR`, **or** the neutral teaching state `'No referee notes yet on this move.'` when there is no banner / no observation and the relation is the `replies` fallback), zone 2 (open-task burden+axis **or** the terminal-state line), zone 3 (the 0–3 constructive next-move buttons, collapses when empty), and the reused imported advisory caption. NEW `src/features/arguments/cardView/refereeCardAssembly.ts` — the pure `buildRefereeCardInput(bundle): BuildOpenIssueInput | null` (no fetch, no Edge, no classifier call, no persistence; value-imports only `OPEN_EVIDENCE_DEBT_STATUSES` to OPEN-filter node debts). MODIFIED `CardDetailPanel.tsx` (new `refereeCard` / `onRefereeMove` props + a full-width `refereeCardSlot` rendering `<RefereeCardView>` inserted BETWEEN the parent-bubble slot and the render-order regions — above the raw classifier strip in both layouts; **#504's five zones byte-unchanged**, slot renders nothing when omitted), `ArgumentBubbleCard.tsx` + `ArgumentBubbleStack.tsx` (additive props forwarded to the ACTIVE card only, mirroring `activeCardDetail` / `onRailAction`), and `ArgumentGameSurface.tsx` (a memoized `refereeCardIssue` built via `buildRefereeCardInput(...)` → `buildOpenIssue(...)` keyed on the same upstream maps as `activeCardDetail` + the constitution rules + the `refereeBanner` prop, so a late classifier row / banner re-derives the card; a `handleRefereeMove` deep-link handler that routes a zone-3 move through the EXISTING composer entry point — `actEntryToQuickAction` → `quickActionToPreset` → `handleAction('reply', …, preset)` — no new composer path, REF-004 swaps this handler). **Doctrine pins (test-proven):** the card renders ONLY `RefereeCardViewModel` plain-language strings (Family-J gating is impossible to bypass — the component never maps over raw marks); zone 1 ABSORBS the banner meaning — the component never imports/renders `RefereeBannerView` and never calls `selectBanner` (the panel gains zero new banner elements; pinned by an absent `testID="referee-banner-view"` + an import-scan); raw `snake_case` / family IDs / rawKeys / `issue.id` / `actEntryId` / numeric confidence never reach the surface (verbatim user `targetQuote` is excluded from the code scan); posting NEVER blocks on card data (the card is render-time enrichment, never in any submit closure — regression-pinned); a11y per `accessibility-targets` (each move ≥44×44 via `TOUCH_TARGET.minSizePx` + `hitSlopAll`, role/label/state; one note-block screen-reader sentence; the tone glyph hidden from the a11y tree; color-independent shape+text; reduce-motion no-op). 4 NEW test suites / **45 tests** (`RefereeCardView.test.tsx` — zones, teaching/empty/terminal states, observer/own-bubble collapsed zone 3, anchor present/absent, late-update re-derivation, move-press payload, the no-second-banner pin, the post-not-blocked regression; `refereeCardBanList.test.ts` — every rendered string vs the 16 prohibited tokens with the sanctioned "not a verdict" caption negation stripped + the drift-guard; `refereeCardNoRawCodes.test.ts` — no internal code on the surface, `issue.id`/`actEntryId`/`recoveredFromCode`/`sourceCode` absent, Family-J mark gated, underscore user-quote rendered verbatim; `refereeCardA11y.test.tsx` — targets/roles/labels/state, glyph hidden, color-independence, reduce-motion no-op, reading order). **Full jest: 724 suites / 29921 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 29922 total), up from the 720 / 29876 baseline (+4 suites / +45 tests), exit 0.** `npm run typecheck` exit 0; scoped `eslint` on the 2 new src files + 4 modified src files + 4 test files clean; `grep ANTHROPIC_API_KEY\|SERVICE_ROLE` in the new files empty. **NO operator follow-up** — pure `src` UI change; no migration, no `db push`, no `functions deploy`, no Edge, no env var. The surface-level banner (`ArgumentGameSurface.tsx`) is left untouched (relocating it is #504 slice 4's job); REF-004 swaps the `onMove` surface handler. See `docs/designs/REF-003.md`.

**2026-06-12 — REF-002 (Open Issue model — pure-TS Disagreement Contract derivation; DERIVED-ONLY, no persistence/Edge/migration):** Implements REF-001's ratified `DisagreementContract` derivation as a single pure model. NEW `src/features/refereeLoop/openIssueModel.ts` (+ `index.ts` barrel): total, deterministic, JSON-serializable, non-mutating; no React/Supabase/fetch/`Date.now`/provider imports. `buildOpenIssue(input)` derives `relation · axis · burden · state · referee observation · next-best moves` for one selected node and returns `null` when room/target is null; the individually-tested helpers `deriveOpenIssueAxis` (6-tier), `deriveOpenIssueRelation` (5-tier + reply-neutrality), `deriveOpenIssueBurden`, `deriveOpenIssueState` (19-row lifecycle map + open-debt override), and `deriveNextBestMoves` (map → intersect → recovery route → pad) are all exported, plus `openIssueToRefereeCard` (issue #585 name) with the `buildRefereeCardViewModel` alias. **Doctrine encoded:** the model is CONSULTATIVE (runs after storage, never the submission gate); `nextBestMoves` is always a SUBSET of `buildActPopout` engine+role survivors (a gate-removed suggestion surfaces the `branch_tangent` recovery route, never the invalid action, or is dropped); `replies` is the internal FALLBACK relation only (never preferred over a more specific procedural relation, never clears a debt); no Open Issue axis is ever literally `fact` (the three `fact` sources map to `evidence`/`framing` by remedy/debt); Family J (`sensitive_composer`) is re-asserted OUT of `refereeObservations` even when a J mark is in the input, via the DERIVED `OPEN_ISSUE_PRODUCTION_FAMILIES` complement of the `HUB_NON_PRODUCTION_FAMILIES` mirror (never hardcoded); machine Observations and user Allegations stay separate fields; no field reads heat/popularity/standing. MODIFIED `src/features/arguments/gameCopy.ts` — ONE added key `moved_on: 'Moved on'` so all 8 IssueState codes resolve through `toPlainLanguageOrSuppress` (the other 7 already resolved via the LIFE-001 block). 5 NEW test suites / **256 tests** (`openIssueModel.test.ts` — all derivation rows incl. the three distinct `fact` sources + the full 19-row lifecycle map + the four REF-001 worked traces field-for-field; `.banlist.test.ts` — every emitted string scanned for the 16 verdict/person tokens + no snake_case + 8-code plain-language coverage pinning `moved_on`; `.actGateParity.test.ts` — `nextBestMoves ⊆ survivors` property across roles × parents × stages + recovery-route + observer/own-bubble empty + exhaustive `SuggestedMoveCode → ActEntryId` map; `.sensitiveSurface.test.ts` — Family-J exclusion even when a J mark is present + roster-from-mirror + Allegation visibility/disjointness; `refereeLoopForbiddenImports.test.ts` — mirrors `metadataForbiddenImports`, bans `selectBanner`/banner-priority/React/Supabase/fetch/`Date.now`/secrets/`console.log`, asserts the permitted `buildActPopout`+`deriveSuggestedMoves` joins). **Full jest: 720 suites / 29876 passed (1 pre-existing skip; the one full-suite failure is the documented `moveMetadataLedger` META-001 60ms wall-clock flake under parallel load — passes isolated 49/49, not in this diff), up from the 715 / 29620 baseline (+5 suites / +256 tests).** `npm run typecheck` exit 0; scoped `eslint` on `src/features/refereeLoop` + the five test files + `gameCopy.ts` clean; `grep ANTHROPIC_API_KEY\|SERVICE_ROLE src/features/refereeLoop/` empty. **Implementation reconciliation (recorded per REF-002's own "REF-001 wins and the divergence is recorded" rule):** REF-001 specifies the intersection is PADDED with the next Act survivors (so the card shows 2–3 moves); REF-002's Step-2 prose omitted padding — padding is implemented. REF-001's worked-trace button arrays are illustrative (inputs are "abbreviated"; padding order/count depends on the unstated parent type), so the worked-trace tests assert the binding contract fields field-for-field and assert `nextBestMoves` via the invariants REF-001 guarantees (subset; first move = top suggested mapped entry; real Act labels). **NO operator follow-up** — pure `src` change; no migration, no `db push`, no `functions deploy`, no Edge, no UI (REF-003 renders the card). See `docs/designs/REF-002.md`.

**2026-06-12 — OPS-MCP-BAN-SCAN-NORMALIZATION (evasion-resistant ban scans — raw-OR-normalized union matcher at all eleven call sites; DENO-DEPLOY-BEARING / scan-tightening):** Closed the four regex-evasion gaps the #578 honesty corpus pinned (Cyrillic/Greek homoglyph, diacritic, leetspeak, + zero-width insertion added by this card) by normalizing the SCANNED TEXT ONLY before pattern-testing — detection-time, never persisted (spans stay verbatim). NEW pure-TS `mcp-server/lib/banScanNormalize.ts`: `normalizeForBanScan` (5 fixed steps — lowercase → explicit zero-width strip {U+200B/C/D, U+2060, U+FEFF} → NFKD + `\p{M}` combining-mark strip → explicit commented Cyrillic/Greek homoglyph table → adjacency-gated leet map {0→o,1→l,3→e,4→a,5→s,7→t,@→a,$→s}) and the shared `banScanMatches(text, patterns)` (operator-confirmed D2 raw-OR-normalized union — raw scan always runs first so no prior catch is ever lost; normalized scan additive; null-safe). ALL ELEVEN call sites routed through the one matcher choke point: the ten `scanFamily<X>BooleanResponseForBanList` functions (evidenceSpan + serverName + classifierSetVersion) + `findUncleanEvidenceSpanKeys`; the post-drop re-scan routes through it by construction (no dispatcher edit). **TIGHTEN-ONLY, never relaxing:** `DOCTRINE_BAN_PATTERNS` and every `FAMILY_<X>_BAN_PATTERNS` are byte-unchanged (git diff: zero pattern-array hunks; family-scan diffs are import + loop→matcher routing only; `path` strings + field order preserved). NEW `mcp-server/tests/banScanNormalize.test.ts` (39 Deno: mapping classes, fullwidth/ligature, adjacency-gating false-positive guards `Model 3`/`2019`/`Section 230`/`$5`/`co2`/`café`/`naïve`/`résumé`/Cyrillic word, tighten-only + monotonicity incl. the boundary-faking `ro`+ZWSP+`bot` raw catch, idempotence, null/empty, disguised-token collector⇔scan agreement A/H/J, persisted-content-verbatim). The 3 #578 EDGE pins (homoglyph/diacritic/leet) flipped `false→true` with boundary-moved notes + 2 new EDGE pins (zero-width, fullwidth); the case-insensitive and strict-boundary-asymmetry pins (`troll`≠`trolling`; `astroturf\w*` matches `astroturfing`) stay. **SURV tripwire re-verified (not edited): all 20 soft exemplars survive the union matcher; 10 hard controls still caught — no STOP finding.** **mcp-server Deno suite: 1741 passed / 0 failed (+41 vs the 1700 baseline), exit 0. Full jest: 715 suites / 29620 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 29621 total), exit 0 — UNCHANGED (jest does not touch the Deno scan).** `npm run typecheck` exit 0 (`tsconfig` excludes `mcp-server`); scoped `deno lint` + `deno check` on all new/touched files clean. No validator/schema/Edge/`src`/migration/`engine`/`package.json` change. **OPERATOR FOLLOW-UP (Deno-deploy-bearing, no provider spend):** on merge the GitHub integration auto-builds `cdiscourse-mcp-server` on Deno Deploy — confirm the build succeeded + hit `/health` (expect 200); no `db push`, no `functions deploy`, no env var, no MCP-boolean smoke (no new booleans/prompts). See `docs/designs/OPS-MCP-BAN-SCAN-NORMALIZATION.md`.

**2026-06-11 — OPS-MCP-MODELINFO-SHAPE-REINFORCEMENT (prompt-side `modelInfo` emission-shape reinforcement A–J; DENO-DEPLOY-BEARING / prompt-only):** The named Phase-5/Disposition-(c) follow-up to the OPS-MCP-SOFT-PARAPHRASE-LIVE-PROBE audit, re-scoped from "non-echo" to "emission-shape" after source verification corrected the dead-letter mechanism (the bare `validator_path: 'modelInfo'` has ONE producer — the structural `must be plain object` check at `mcpBooleanObservationSchemaMirror.ts:279-281` — not a ban-scan hit; `modelInfo` has no free text; reproduced deterministically via Edge admin_validation run `3ff1ba04`, 5th consecutive failure on `2c6d9ca1`×`disagreement_axis`, family B). NEW `mcp-server/lib/modelInfoEmissionDirective.ts` exports a single shared, family-agnostic `MODEL_INFO_EMISSION_DIRECTIVE` (RESPONSE-ENVELOPE RULE: always emit the literal `modelInfo` envelope even on hostile/incoherent/empty/long/fully-uncertain input — uncertainty goes to `confidence`/false observations, never to dropping or altering the envelope). It is interpolated VERBATIM into all ten `family*Prompt.ts` user prompts immediately before the `The object MUST conform to this shape:` anchor (in J, after the existing FINAL CHECK block) — ADDITIVE ONLY: one import + one interpolation each, the response-shape JSON examples (incl. each `classifierSetVersion: "family-x-v1"`) byte-unchanged. **The validator gate is NEVER relaxed — fail-closed stays the guard; the prompt only reduces how often it must fire.** No validator/scan/schema/Edge/`src`/migration/`keyLevelFailClosed.ts` change. The OPS-MCP-SOFT-PARAPHRASE-LIVE-PROBE audit gained an Amendment (verdict PASS + fail-closed conclusion unchanged; Phase-5 mechanism re-typed; audit-lint 0 findings). +26 Deno tests (20 per-family presence/position/example-unchanged + 6 shared-constant: load-bearing fragments, zero DOCTRINE_BAN_PATTERNS, shape-not-content, no weakening qualifier, family-agnostic, BINDING block). **mcp-server Deno suite: 1700 passed / 0 failed (+26 vs the 1674 baseline), exit 0. Full jest: 715 suites / 29620 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 29621 total), exit 0 — UNCHANGED (no `src/` or root `__tests__/` change).** `deno lint` clean (22 files); scoped `deno check` on all touched files clean; root `npm run typecheck` exit 0 (`tsconfig` excludes `mcp-server`). **OPERATOR FOLLOW-UP (Deno-deploy-bearing):** merge auto-builds `cdiscourse-mcp-server` on Deno Deploy (no `db push`, no `functions deploy`, no env/routing change); then re-run the single failing cell via Edge admin_validation (arg `2c6d9ca1` × family B `disagreement_axis`, 1 classification) — expect success (no `validation_failed` at `path:'modelInfo'`); if it still fails, fail-closed remains correct and the next escalation is model-side structured-output enforcement (a separate future card — NOT this one). See `docs/designs/OPS-MCP-MODELINFO-SHAPE-REINFORCEMENT.md`.

**2026-06-11 — OPS-MCP-EVIDENCE-SPAN-QUOTATION-FRAMING (frame the classifier evidence span as a marked quotation of the move's own words; DISPLAY/COPY-ONLY):** The cross-family list-union ADR's named Option-3 follow-up (the honest weakness it found at investigation #5): a classifier chip's verbatim `evidenceSpan` was prefixed `"Why this fired:"` and unquoted, so a bare hostile token the span legitimately quotes (`…astroturfed…`) read marginally like a machine-stated reason rather than a marked quote of the author's own text. This card sharpens ONLY that framing — no scan/suppression/gate/migration/Edge change, no behavior change to what persists or loads. (1) `CARD_CLASSIFIER_EVIDENCE_PREFIX` changed `'Why this fired:'` → `'From this move’s text:'` (curly U+2019); new exported `EVIDENCE_QUOTE_OPEN`/`EVIDENCE_QUOTE_CLOSE` (curly U+201C/U+201D, mirroring the parent-bubble `“${quote}”` precedent); new additive `evidenceSpanFramed: string | null` field on `CardClassifierChip`, built in `markToChip` (the SINGLE source shared by the capped strip + uncapped hub) as `${PREFIX} “${span}”` — the raw `evidenceSpan` stays verbatim/unaltered (inner quotes + trailing ellipsis preserved); `ClassifierLabel` now renders `chip.evidenceSpanFramed` and its unused prefix import was removed. (2) Screen-reader hint reframed `'. Tap to see why this fired.'` → `'. Tap to see the quoted text from this move.'`. (3) Comment-only stale-comment cleanup at 8 sites (`CardDetailPanel.tsx` :227/:302/:849, `cardDetailModel.ts` :148/:338, `cardMappingSectionModel.ts` :26/:130, `cardClassifierStripModel.ts` :310) correcting the stale "A–G / H,I,J-frozen" framing to the live "A–I production roster, Family J only frozen". Doctrine: the frame is attributive (provenance), never a verdict — a ban-list test pins the FRAME copy carries zero verdict tokens, and a dedicated test proves a verbatim hostile token survives inside the quotes while the frame around it stays clean (the ADR Option 0 keeps the span). +9 additive tests (8 in `cardClassifierProvenance.test.ts`, 1 render in `cardViewRefine.test.tsx`). **Full jest: 715 suites / 29620 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 29621 total), exit 0** (+9 vs the 29611 baseline; no new suite). Typecheck exit 0; per-file eslint clean on all 6 touched files. NO operator follow-up — pure display/copy change; no migration, no `functions deploy`, no env var, no `mcp-server/` redeploy. See `docs/designs/OPS-MCP-EVIDENCE-SPAN-QUOTATION-FRAMING.md`.

**2026-06-11 — OPS-MCP-SOFT-PARAPHRASE-ADVERSARIAL-FIXTURE (characterize-and-pin the soft-paraphrase survivor boundary A–J; TEST-ONLY, build-bearing-only):** Honesty-as-tests follow-up to OPS-MCP-KEY-LEVEL-FAIL-CLOSED-WIDENING (PR #577). Key-level fail-closed lets an unclean `evidenceSpan` key die ALONE while clean siblings survive; that surfaced a residual the parent design recorded plainly — a sibling span that is regex-CLEAN but SOFT-PARAPHRASE (a person/intent characterization the byte-unchanged ban-scan does not catch, e.g. "the author seems biased", "they clearly have an agenda") now survives on a clean sibling, rendering PUBLICLY on A–I (timeline_node / rendered_now) and composer-only on J. No regex closes it and the validator/ban-scan must never be relaxed, so this card CHARACTERIZES and PINS rather than closing anything. **ZERO production-file change** — the entire diff is 4 new test files + 2 doc edits; every `mcp-server/lib/**`, `mcp-server/tools/**`, `supabase/functions/**`, `src/**`, migration, `package.json`, and `scripts/**` is byte-identical to main. NEW `mcp-server/tests/softParaphraseSurvivorCorpus.test.ts` (24 Deno: SURV-shape ×3 + the TRIPWIRE SURV-1 ×10 pinning a named 5-class / 20-exemplar soft-survivor taxonomy CLEAN against every family stack + SURV-2 ×10 hard-control caught with E–J family-extension-fired proof + SURV-union ×1 cross-family guarantee; header carries the verbatim tripwire convention — a SURV-1 failure is a BOUNDARY-MOVED signal to re-assess, never a regression to fix by editing/deleting the exemplar). NEW `mcp-server/tests/softParaphraseMixedPacketReachability.test.ts` (6 Deno: MIX-[A/D/H/I/J] one hard key dropped + one soft sibling survives / re-validates / re-scans clean with a `null` sibling exercising the null-skip path; MIX-banned-token-probe the soft words reach the wire, the hard token never does). NEW `mcp-server/tests/softParaphraseRegexBoundaryHonesty.test.ts` (6 Deno: EDGE-case-insensitive positive control; homoglyph / diacritic / leet / strict-boundary-asymmetry pinned as CURRENT behavior, out of scope to change — closing them is a pattern-engine card). NEW `__tests__/softParaphrasePublicSurfaceBoundary.test.ts` (4 jest: PSB-public A renders timeline_node/rendered_now ×2; PSB-composer-only J's 3 sensitive keys are composer_only; PSB-edge-no-ban-scan the Edge sanitizer references no `scanFamily`/`DOCTRINE_BAN_PATTERNS`). The optional dispatcher full-envelope J pin (+ its JSON fixture) was OMITTED by operator choice (strict literal test-only scope); the lib-level MIX-J pin covers item (c) fully. No exemplar substitutions needed — all 20 soft exemplars verified clean against the union of all ten stacks, all 10 hard controls verified caught. **Deno server suite: 1674 passed / 0 failed (+36 vs the 1638 baseline), exit 0. Full jest: 715 suites / 29611 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 29612 total), exit 0 (+1 suite / +4 tests vs the 714 / 29607 baseline).** Typecheck exit 0; `deno lint tests/softParaphrase*.test.ts` clean; per-file `npx eslint` on the jest file clean. **NO OPERATOR FOLLOW-UP:** test-only — merging triggers a behavior-inert Deno Deploy build of `mcp-server` (a `tests/` change with no `lib`/`tools`/`main.ts` delta), so it is build-bearing only, NOT deploy-bearing; no migration, no `functions deploy`, no env var, no routing arm. The soft-paraphrase residual remains OPEN by design (no regex catches the soft class; the drop-rate observability is blind to it since a survivor is never dropped; the human L5 audit is the sole live backstop). See `docs/designs/OPS-MCP-SOFT-PARAPHRASE-ADVERSARIAL-FIXTURE.md`.

**2026-06-11 — OPS-MCP-KEY-LEVEL-FAIL-CLOSED-WIDENING (extend key-level fail-closed J-only → ALL TEN families A–J; MIGRATION-BEARING + DENO-DEPLOY-BEARING + EDGE-BEARING):** Lifted the J-only bound on the live-proven key-level fail-closed mechanism (PR #576) to the production families (A–I), the follow-up the OPS-MCP-KEY-LEVEL-FAIL-CLOSED design explicitly deferred. **Production semantics are IDENTICAL to J's** — every span is still scanned against the byte-unchanged ban-list, no unclean span ever reaches the wire/Edge/DB/client, and the change is fail-CLOSED (an unclean key is OMITTED, never coerced into a fabricated finding). The only delta for A–I is that an unclean key now dies ALONE (clean siblings survive) instead of failing the whole packet, and the production queue-drainer path persists the drop names on its SUCCESS branch (omission + alert-never-gate observability now covers production volumes). **THE GATE:** `mcp-server/lib/keyLevelFailClosed.ts` — `KEY_LEVEL_FAIL_CLOSED_FAMILIES` extended from `{sensitive_composer}` to all ten registered slugs; `banPatternsForKeyLevelFamily(family)` now returns each family's REAL pattern stack — A–D → `[...DOCTRINE_BAN_PATTERNS]` (those four scan modules export no family-specific array — none exists), E–J → `[...DOCTRINE_BAN_PATTERNS, ...FAMILY_<X>_BAN_PATTERNS]` (each `FAMILY_<X>_BAN_PATTERNS` already exported; imported unchanged) — the SAME stack each family's `scanFamily<X>BooleanResponseForBanList` composes, so the per-key drop decision and the whole-packet scan can NEVER diverge. **No `family*BanListScan.ts` file was touched** (every needed pattern was already exported); all ten scan modules + `doctrineBanList.ts` are byte-IDENTICAL to main (`git hash-object` proven), and `engine.ts`/registry/routing are untouched (registry families keep their exact `productionEnabled` flags — J still admin-validation-only). The dispatcher Step-5 branch in `classifyArgumentBooleanObservations.ts` was already family-agnostic (gates on `KEY_LEVEL_FAIL_CLOSED_FAMILIES.has(resolvedFamily)`) — verified, not re-implemented. **THE QUEUE PATH (production-bearing core):** NEW sequential migration `20260611000002_ops_mcp_key_level_fail_closed_widening_finalizer.sql` re-creates `public.finalize_classifier_job` from the CURRENT 9-arg body (`20260602000001`) with ONE additive trailing param `p_dropped_unclean_span_keys text[] DEFAULT NULL`, written to `dropped_unclean_span_keys` on the SUCCESS branch ONLY; the TERMINAL-FAILURE branch is byte-faithful (still `failure_detail = p_failure_detail`, does NOT touch the new column); NO ALTER TABLE (the column already exists from `20260611000001`); full OPS-001 four-class header; WRITTEN NOT APPLIED. `classifierDrainerClassify.ts` now carries `keysDroppedForUncleanSpan` (NAMES only, NULL on zero drops) off the sanitized response into its `classified` result; `classifierDrainerCore.ts` threads it on the SUCCESS finalize → the RPC's `p_dropped_unclean_span_keys` (terminal/argument_missing/defensive finalizes do NOT pass it). The Edge DIRECT path (`classifyArgumentCore` → `persistRun`) was already family-agnostic + threaded by the J ship — pinned. Q18 observability (`scripts/ops/sql/18-…`) already groups by family + admin-health `byUncleanSpanKeyDrop` bucket already family-agnostic — verified, no change. **Tests:** NEW `mcp-server/tests/keyLevelFailClosedWidening.test.ts` (41 Deno tests: ten-family membership; per-family no-divergence — `banPatternsForKeyLevelFamily` === the scan stack by source+flags AND collector⇔scan agree on a dirty span; E–J family-only-token wiring proofs; representative A/D/H drop tests); the J-only membership test in `familyJKeyLevelFailClosed.test.ts` RETARGETED to assert all-ten membership (a designed behavior change — the J key-drop behavior tests stay green); `__tests__/keyLevelFailClosedWideningFinalizerMigration.test.ts` (26 migration-shape tests); `__tests__/keyLevelFailClosedWideningDrainerThread.test.ts` (13 drainer-threading + direct-path-family-agnostic source-scan tests); `booleanObservationBatching.test.ts` +1 explicit D 22→16+6 batching-union test (the existing 22-key KLF-1 already covered it). **Deno server suite: 1638 passed / 0 failed (+41 vs the 1597 baseline), exit 0. Full jest: 714 suites / 29607 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 29608 total), exit 0 (+2 suites / +40 tests vs the 712 / 29567 baseline).** Typecheck clean; per-file eslint clean (eslint-scoped files) + deno lint clean (mcp-server files; `mcp-server/**` is eslint-ignored); the unfiltered `npm run lint` exit 1 is pre-existing `.claude/worktrees/` sibling-worktree noise (gitignored, not on this branch). **OPERATOR FOLLOW-UP (migration-bearing deploy chain):** (1) `npx supabase db push --linked` to apply the 10-arg finalizer re-create BEFORE merging the drainer code that calls it; verify the 10-arg overload exists; THEN merge. (2) On merge the Supabase GitHub integration auto-redeploys registered Edge Functions + the mcp-server auto-builds on Deno Deploy; re-run a production-family (A–I) drainer drain and confirm a SUCCESS run that dropped ≥1 unclean key records the NAMES in `dropped_unclean_span_keys` with the clean siblings persisted. **The §10a doctrine-review merge gate from the J card carries forward; NO family posture flip — J stays admin-validation-only.** See `docs/designs/OPS-MCP-KEY-LEVEL-FAIL-CLOSED.md` § "Widening (A–J)".

**2026-06-11 — OPS-MCP-KEY-LEVEL-FAIL-CLOSED (key-level fail-closed with span-omission semantics; J-only; MIGRATION-BEARING + DENO-DEPLOY-BEARING + EDGE-BEARING):** Replaced the packet-level death penalty in the mcp-server boolean-observation response validator with KEY-LEVEL death. Previously ONE Family J `evidenceSpan` tripping the doctrine ban-scan failed the WHOLE response packet (`validation_failed` / `doctrine_ban_list`), discarding the clean sibling keys' Observations (the deterministic, retry-immune packet-residual documented in `docs/audits/MCP-SERVER-011-FAMILY-J-SMOKE-2026-06-11.md` Amendment). Now the unclean key is OMITTED — it never returns and never persists its span — while the clean siblings (independently verified clean against the SAME byte-unchanged ban-list) survive. **Relaxes NOTHING:** the ban-scan patterns are byte-unchanged (`familyJBanListScan.ts` sha256 `026d3728…`, `doctrineBanList.ts` sha256 `811ac700…` — both unchanged vs main); no unclean span ever reaches the wire/Edge/DB/client; it is a fail-CLOSED change (default for an unclean key is non-emission) and the deterministic server-side BACKSTOP for the model-side narrow-or-set-false instruction. **Omission, not coerce-to-false** (coerce-to-false would fabricate a finding the model never made — §1 distortion; rejected). NEW `mcp-server/lib/keyLevelFailClosed.ts` shared dispatcher helper (`KEY_LEVEL_FAIL_CLOSED_FAMILIES = {sensitive_composer}`; `findUncleanEvidenceSpanKeys(spans, patterns)`; `banPatternsForKeyLevelFamily`; `dropUncleanEvidenceSpanKeys`) — built from the SAME `[...DOCTRINE_BAN_PATTERNS, ...FAMILY_J_BAN_PATTERNS]` stack the scan uses so the drop decision and whole-packet scan can never diverge. Step 5 of `classifyArgumentBooleanObservations.ts`: for in-set families an `evidenceSpan.*` ban-hit becomes a key-level drop (collect → drop → re-validate → re-scan kept; populate optional `keysDroppedForUncleanSpan` with NAMES only; all-dirty → empty-success; re-scan surfacing a `modelInfo.*` hit falls through to the unchanged packet-fail); out-of-set families A–I + modelInfo hits keep BYTE-IDENTICAL packet-fail posture. Optional `keysDroppedForUncleanSpan?: string[]` threaded through the server mirror + both Edge schema parser twins (validated absent from observations/confidence/evidenceSpan/checkedRawKeys — the anti-resurrection invariant) + sanitizer passthrough + `mergeBatchResponses` union + `classifyArgumentCore` → `persistRun` (SUCCESS + ≥1-drop only; conditional spread → byte-equal INSERT when omitted). **`checkedRawKeys` REDEFINED "attempted" → "cleanly assessed"** (`checkedRawKeys ∪ keysDroppedForUncleanSpan = attempted`); CONSUMER INVENTORY confirmed no consumer reads it alone as an attempted-count (see below). NEW additive-nullable migration `20260611000001_ops_mcp_key_level_fail_closed_dropped_keys.sql` (`dropped_unclean_span_keys text[]`, mirrors the `20260602000001` precedent: no RLS/index, no backfill, no finalizer re-create — J is admin-validation-only + direct-dispatch). Admin classifier-health: `byUncleanSpanKeyDrop` count bucket in BOTH `classifierHealthModel` twins (value-parity enforced) + Edge `RUN_COLUMNS`/`toModelRow` + the health-tab advisory card + CSV line (NAMES + counts only; advisory drop-RATE never gates). NEW ops Q-file `scripts/ops/sql/18-unclean-span-key-drops-by-family.sql` + SECTIONS entry; the `.sql` count-pin bumped 18→19 across SqlSafety (`:59`), NoServiceRoleNoSecrets, ReportShape (SECTIONS + ids + json), EmptyDbSafety, and the D/G/I coverage pins; runbook intro 18→19 queries / 17→18 questions + a Q18 section. `engine.ts`, the ban-pattern files, the family registry (J stays `productionEnabled:false`), and all A–I packet-fail behavior are byte-unchanged (test-pinned). **Deno server suite: 1597 passed / 0 failed (+14 vs the 1583 baseline), exit 0. Full jest: 712 suites / 29567 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 29568 total), exit 0 (+1 suite / +18 tests vs the 711 / 29549 baseline).** Typecheck clean; per-file eslint clean on every touched file (the unfiltered `npm run lint` exit 1 is pre-existing `.claude/worktrees/` sibling-worktree noise — gitignored, not on this branch); secret scan of `app/`+`src/` clean (the only hits are pre-existing doc-comments + the dev-fixture's own detection regex). **OPERATOR FOLLOW-UP (migration-bearing deploy chain):** (1) `npx supabase db push --linked` to apply the additive `dropped_unclean_span_keys` column BEFORE merging the Edge code that writes it; verify the column exists; THEN merge. (2) On merge the Supabase GitHub integration auto-redeploys registered Edge Functions and the mcp-server auto-builds on Deno Deploy — re-run the J E3 admin_validation smoke (the existential person-shift input that failed in Phase 4b): expect run `status='success'` with `needs_pre_send_pause` named in `dropped_unclean_span_keys` AND `shifts_to_person_or_intent` (+ clean siblings) persisted. **NO production-enable implied — J stays admin-validation-only; the implement card carries an explicit §10a doctrine-review merge gate.** See `docs/designs/OPS-MCP-KEY-LEVEL-FAIL-CLOSED.md`.

**2026-06-11 — OPS-MCP-FAMILY-J-PROMPT-ORDER-ROBUSTNESS (J prompt iteration 2; per-key span reminders + pre-emit final check; Deno-deploy-bearing):** Follow-up to the PARTIAL outcome of OPS-MCP-FAMILY-J-SPAN-SHAPE-REINFORCEMENT (PR #572). The post-merge E3 replay still returned an unclean `needs_pre_send_pause` span, and the #388 (2026-06-11) diagnosis matrix found the residual is **order-sensitive**, not a content failure: the global SPAN-SELECTION RULE sits at the END of the user prompt, so with `shifts_to_person_or_intent` asked FIRST (the Edge registry order) the rule loses salience by the time `needs_pre_send_pause` is answered and that span contaminates with the input's slur sentence (3/3 deterministic at the 30s in-body budget); with the pause question asked earlier (alphabetical) it narrows cleanly (2/2). FIX (prompt-side only; chosen candidates (a)+(c) from the #388 record — **server-side key reordering (b) REJECTED** because the rawKey registry order is a cross-family request contract and reordering would touch all families / risk parity drift for no validator benefit): (1) `mcp-server/lib/familyJPrompt.ts` — NEW exported `FAMILY_J_SPAN_REMINDERS` map; `buildFamilyJUserPrompt` appends a SHORT bracketed per-key span reminder to EACH question line (same line, never a new line — the questions block stays exactly one line per rawKey), strongest on `needs_pre_send_pause` (`[span: SHORTEST clean fragment only — for reactive markers anchor ONLY the typographic burst, e.g. "WRONG WRONG WRONG!!!", never any fragment containing a person label; if no clean fragment exists, answer false]`); analogous one-liners for the other four keys (each carries the shared `SHORTEST clean fragment only` + narrow-or-false spine). (2) NEW binding **FINAL CHECK** block inserted as the lead-in to the response-shape instruction (after the questions/definitions/cross-key-note, immediately before `The object MUST conform to this shape:`) so it is among the LAST instructions read before emitting JSON: re-scan EVERY evidenceSpan against the banned person-directed terms; narrow or flip to false; an output with even one unclean span is rejected whole. The Part 1 global SPAN-SELECTION RULE block survives verbatim at the end (the FINAL CHECK precedes it; both legs coexist); the function's structure doc-comment list renumbered to name both additions. **BYTE-EQUAL (never relaxed):** `familyJBanListScan.ts` `git hash-object` = `d9913ca4c99c3f7b0085e6ca7c3a4d9f927ac9b4` (unchanged); `familyJKeys.ts` untouched (reminders are prompt-only — no guard sentence needed mirroring); no Edge / `supabase/**`; no `src/**`; families A–I untouched. +7 Deno tests in `familyJPrompt.test.ts` (reminder-map shape + shared spine; each reminder lands on its question line; the pause reminder is strongest + carries the WRONG-burst example; subset request only emits requested-key reminders; questions block stays exactly 5 lines with no spilled newlines; FINAL CHECK present + positioned after the questions and before the response shape with the Part 1 SPAN-SELECTION RULE still following it; reminders + FINAL CHECK carry zero banned person-directed tokens). **Deno server suite: 1583 passed / 0 failed (+7 vs the 1576 Part-1 baseline), exit 0. Full jest: 711 suites / 29549 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 29550 total), exit 0 (unchanged — Deno-only change; a transient first-run failure in the repo-wide `dockerfileShape.test.ts` *.json walk was confirmed a `--maxWorkers=4` parallel-load flake — passes isolated, not in this branch's diff).** Typecheck clean; secret/ban scan of the diff clean (banned tokens appear only in negation/instruction context; the system-prompt negation-scan test still passes). **DENO-DEPLOY-BEARING ON MERGE:** the GitHub integration redeploys `cdiscourse-mcp-server` to Deno Deploy automatically (Supabase merge auto-deploy does NOT propagate `mcp-server/`); no migration, no Vault, no routing arm, no `functions deploy`. OPERATOR FOLLOW-UP: after the Deno redeploy, re-run the E3 admin_validation smoke on the existential person-shift input and confirm `needs_pre_send_pause` anchors a clean typographic fragment (or answers false) — no `validation_failed` at that span; **NO production-enable implied** — J stays admin-validation-only (E4 ceiling stands); the probabilistic-outcome ladder still applies. See `docs/designs/OPS-MCP-FAMILY-J-SPAN-SHAPE-REINFORCEMENT.md` § "Part 2 — order-robustness".

**2026-06-11 — OPS-MCP-ADMIN-VALIDATION-BODY-BUDGET (Part 2 of the admin timeout-hierarchy fix; in-body MODEL budget; EDGE-bearing):** Closed the residual `validation_failed` on the ADMIN-gated `classify-argument-boolean-observations` path that PR #570 (Part 1, the caller-side `AbortSignal.timeout` 15s→30s widen) did NOT resolve. ROOT CAUSE (2026-06-11 byte-exact replay, deterministic 4/4): there is a SECOND, distinct timeout lever — the request **body**'s `timeoutMs` field, set by `buildBooleanObservationRequestForArgument` and defaulting to `DEFAULT_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS = 12_000` (`booleanObservationRequestBuilder.ts:38`). That in-body value is the MCP server's own MODEL deliberation budget (read server-side), NOT a transport abort. `classifyArgumentCore.ts`'s build call omitted `timeoutMs`, so admin_validation bodies carried 12s; Part 1 gave the *transport* 30s of room but the *model* was still cut at 12s. Replay: body `timeoutMs:12000` → model truncates deliberation, emits a slur-echoing span → `validation_failed` at `evidenceSpan.needs_pre_send_pause`/`doctrine_ban_list`; body `timeoutMs:30000` → clean narrowed span → success. The DRAINER already passes `DRAINER_MCP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS` (30s) IN-BODY (`classifierDrainerClassify.ts` header §2 / :138) in addition to the adapter option, so this aligns admin_validation with the drainer on BOTH levers.
