FIX (small diff, never `git add -A`): (1) `classifyArgumentCore.ts` — import `ADMIN_VALIDATION_MCP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS` from the adapter core (the constant PR #570 added) and pass `timeoutMs: mode === 'admin_validation' ? ADMIN_VALIDATION_MCP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS : undefined` into the builder call; `undefined` → the builder's 12s default, so **production / auto-trigger / submit** request bodies stay BYTE-EQUIVALENT (fast-fail hot path BY DESIGN). (2) Extended the constant's doc-comment in `booleanObservationMcpAdapterCore.ts` with the SECOND-LEVER finding (in-body model budget; replay 12s→truncated→fail vs 30s→clean; drainer-parity citation). INVARIANTS (test-proven): production/auto-trigger/submit in-body budget = 12s unchanged; drainer in-body 30s untouched; Part 1 caller-side levers untouched; `engine.ts`, migrations, registry, routing, `mcp-server/**` untouched. +8 tests in `__tests__/adminValidationTimeoutHierarchy.test.ts` (AVTH-13..20: import-scan; mutation-resistant ternary source-scan — fails if the field is removed, the gate inverted, the branches swapped, or the named constant inlined; production-gets-undefined / no-ungated-pass scan; behavioural via the Jest bridge — admin body=30000, production-omitted=12000, undefined-else=12000; drainer-parity admin in-body===drainer in-body===30000). **Full jest: 711 suites / 29549 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 29550 total) / exit 0** (+8 tests vs the 29541 baseline; no new suite). Typecheck + per-file eslint clean; secret scan of the diff clean (the only `src/` ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/SERVICE_ROLE hits are pre-existing doc-comments + the dev-fixture validator's own scan pattern — untouched). **EDGE-BEARING ON MERGE:** the `_shared/booleanObservations` module is shared with `submit-argument`, so the registered Edge Functions redeploy via the Supabase GitHub integration — no migration, no Vault, no routing arm, no `mcp-server/` redeploy. OPERATOR FOLLOW-UP: after merge auto-deploy, re-run the Family-J E3 admin_validation smoke on the existential person-shift input and confirm a clean span + success (no `validation_failed` at `needs_pre_send_pause`); NO production-enable implied — J stays admin-validation-only. See `docs/designs/OPS-MCP-ADMIN-VALIDATION-TIMEOUT-HIERARCHY.md` § "Part 2 — the in-body budget".

**2026-06-11 — OPS-MCP-FAMILY-J-SPAN-SHAPE-REINFORCEMENT (Card 3 of J-chain; prompt-side span non-echo; Deno-deploy-bearing):** Closed the E3-amendment gap surfaced in `docs/audits/MCP-SERVER-011-FAMILY-J-SMOKE-2026-06-11.md` (Amendment): with the Edge-shaped input (full parent + thread context), the model fired `needs_pre_send_pause` on the existential person-shift text and anchored its span on the reactive sentence — which carries the input's slurs ("You're such a troll … you're toxic — …") — so `familyJBanListScan` rejected the packet closed (`validation_failed` at `evidenceSpan.needs_pre_send_pause` / `doctrine_ban_list`, deterministic 4/4, fail-closed — nothing dirty persisted). GAP: `familyJPrompt.ts` gave `shifts_to_person_or_intent` a concrete clean-anchor example but `needs_pre_send_pause` named WHAT to anchor (all-caps bursts, repeated punctuation) with NO sub-span selection rule for when those markers are INTERLEAVED with person labels. FIX (the #421/#423 RAWKEY-SHAPE REINFORCEMENT precedent, `git show 9ae3c7a`; **prompt-side only — the validator gate is NEVER relaxed**): (1) `mcp-server/lib/familyJPrompt.ts` — NEW BINDING **SPAN-SELECTION RULE** block in the user prompt applying to ALL J keys: (a) SHORTEST SUB-SPAN, (b) SELF-SCAN the candidate span for banned person-directed terms, (c) NARROW TO EXCLUDE (reactive markers → anchor ONLY the typographic fragment, concrete example `"WRONG WRONG WRONG!!!"`; focus-shift → anchor "because you work for…", never the slur), (d) NARROW-OR-FALSE (a clean narrow span ALWAYS exists for a true feature; if none can be produced, answer false rather than emit an unclean span). The `needs_pre_send_pause` per-key paragraph in `FAMILY_J_SYSTEM_PROMPT` strengthened with the interleaved-slur concrete escape; the doc-comment numbered-section list updated to name the block. (2) `mcp-server/lib/familyJKeys.ts` — `needs_pre_send_pause` `falsePositiveGuards` extended with the sub-span/non-echo constraint (mirrors the axis-partner key's slur-non-echo constraint); prior "MUST NOT contain: unhinged, hostile, aggressive, losing it" enumeration preserved verbatim. (3) NEW fixture `classify-argument-boolean-observations.family-j-pause-adversarial-request.json` — reactive markers interleaved inside a slur sentence (the case the existing clean pause fixture did not cover). (4) +8 Deno tests: `familyJPrompt.test.ts` (+5: SPAN-SELECTION RULE + 4 sub-rules, WRONG-burst example + validator-path anchor, system-prompt escape, prompt-entry guard delta, questions-block-undisturbed) + `familyJAdversarialDoctrine.test.ts` (+3: new interleaved fixture shape; clean narrowed `WRONG WRONG WRONG!!!` span PASSES the ban scan; slur-bearing full-sentence span FAILS at `evidenceSpan.needs_pre_send_pause` — **validator posture UNCHANGED**). **BYTE-EQUAL (never relaxed):** `familyJBanListScan.ts` `git hash-object` = `d9913ca4c99c3f7b0085e6ca7c3a4d9f927ac9b4` (unchanged); no Edge / `supabase/**`; no `src/**` (the parity test compares only rawKey/source/disposition metadata, not guard text); families A–I untouched; `mcp-server-001-smoke.sh` J checks unchanged. **Deno server suite: 1576 passed / 0 failed (+8 vs the 1568 baseline), exit 0. Full jest: 711 suites / 29541 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 29542 total), exit 0 (unchanged — Deno-only change).** Typecheck clean; project-tree eslint green (the unfiltered exit 1 is pre-existing `.claude/worktrees/` sibling-worktree noise — `mcp-server/**` is eslint-ignored); secret scan of the diff clean; banned tokens appear only in negation/instruction context (the shared-ban-pattern system-prompt negation test still passes). **EDGE/DENO-DEPLOY-BEARING ON MERGE:** the GitHub integration redeploys `cdiscourse-mcp-server` to Deno Deploy automatically (Supabase merge auto-deploy does NOT propagate `mcp-server/`); no migration, no Vault, no routing arm, no `functions deploy`. OPERATOR FOLLOW-UP: after the Deno redeploy, re-run the E3 admin_validation smoke on the existential person-shift input and confirm `needs_pre_send_pause` anchors a clean typographic fragment (or answers false) — no `validation_failed` at that span; **NO production-enable implied** — J stays admin-validation-only (E4 ceiling stands). See `docs/designs/OPS-MCP-FAMILY-J-SPAN-SHAPE-REINFORCEMENT.md`.

**2026-06-11 — OPS-MCP-ADMIN-VALIDATION-TIMEOUT-HIERARCHY (admin_validation caller patience 15s → 30s):** Corrected the inverted Edge→MCP timeout hierarchy on the ADMIN-gated `classify-argument-boolean-observations` path, mirroring the ARCH-001 Card 2 DRAINER precedent exactly. ROOT CAUSE (verified, from the merged `docs/audits/MCP-SERVER-011-FAMILY-J-SMOKE-2026-06-11.md` Phase 4b typed finding): the admin Edge handler injected the bare `runBooleanObservationMcpAdapter`, so its caller-side `AbortSignal.timeout` defaulted to `MCP_BOOLEAN_OBSERVATION_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS = 15_000` (`booleanObservationMcpAdapterCore.ts:58`) — TIGHTER than the MCP server's own 25s model budget (`MCP_SERVER_MODEL_TIMEOUT_MS`, default `25_000` at `mcp-server/lib/anthropicCall.ts:32`). The Family-J existential person-shift input drove longer model deliberation (16-25s, valid work) that clipped the 15s caller abort → `mcp_api_error` 3/3 at the Edge while the DIRECT hosted call on the same content succeeded (nothing dirty persisted; fail-closed). FIX (small diff, never `git add -A`): (1) NEW `export const ADMIN_VALIDATION_MCP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS = 30_000` next to the drainer's constant in `booleanObservationMcpAdapterCore.ts`, comment carrying the hierarchy rule (caller patience must EXCEED the 25s server budget; 30s = 25s + 5s headroom), the J E3 motivating incident, and the batch-interaction bound (`MAX_ARGUMENTS_PER_CALL=10` × 30s = 300s worst-case sequential vs the Edge wall clock; observed per-arg ~4-6s, 30s ceiling only for deliberation-heavy sensitive inputs; recommend ≤5-arg admin batches — no enforcement change). (2) ONE injection-site wrap in `supabase/functions/classify-argument-boolean-observations/index.ts`: the handler builds a mode-aware adapter once per request — `body.mode === 'admin_validation'` → a 1-arg closure passing `{ timeoutMs: ADMIN_VALIDATION_MCP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS }`; `production` mode keeps the bare reference (15s). INVARIANTS (test-proven byte-unchanged): the 15s default constant, `DRAINER_MCP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS = 30_000` + the drainer path (`classifierDrainerClassify.ts`), and `autoTriggerDispatcher.ts` (the submit-path direct-dispatch caller still injects the bare 1-arg adapter → 15s) are all untouched; `engine.ts`, migrations, registry, routing, and `mcp-server/**` untouched. +12 tests in NEW `__tests__/adminValidationTimeoutHierarchy.test.ts` (AVTH-1..12: constant=30000, hierarchy >25s+5s headroom, matches drainer 30s, 15s default still tighter-by-design, admin path passes the 30s options gated on admin_validation, auto-trigger/drainer/default byte-unchanged) + a `EDGE_ADMIN_VALIDATION_MCP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS` / `EDGE_DRAINER_MCP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS` Jest-bridge re-export. **Full jest: 711 suites / 29541 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 29542 total) / exit 0** (+1 suite / +12 tests vs the 710 / 29529 baseline). Typecheck + per-file eslint clean; secret scan clean. **EDGE-BEARING ON MERGE:** the `classify-argument-boolean-observations` Edge Function auto-redeploys via the Supabase GitHub integration (registered in `config.toml`) — no migration, no Vault, no routing arm, no `mcp-server/` redeploy. OPERATOR FOLLOW-UP: after merge auto-deploy, re-run the Family-J E3 admin_validation smoke on the existential input and confirm it classifies at the Edge boundary within the 30s ceiling (no `mcp_api_error`); NO production-enable implied — J stays admin-validation-only. See `docs/designs/OPS-MCP-ADMIN-VALIDATION-TIMEOUT-HIERARCHY.md`.

**2026-06-11 — MCP-SERVER-011-FAMILY-J (Card 1 of J-chain; `sensitive_composer` admin_validation classifier; Deno MCP server build):** Built the server-side `sensitive_composer` (Family J) boolean-observation classifier on the Deno MCP server, mirroring the Family I Card-1 structure (`4b9dabd`), so the hosted server can classify the 5 existing UX-001.5A sensitive keys (`shifts_to_person_or_intent`, `contains_unplayable_insult_only`, `needs_pre_send_pause`, `uses_popularity_as_evidence`, `uses_satire_as_evidence`) at **admin_validation depth only**. **This is the MOST sensitive prompt in the system** (4 of 5 keys verdict-adjacent; 3 person/intent-directed): the §10a-constrained prompt + ban-list are engineered so the model output detects whether the TEXT exhibits a structural feature and NEVER characterizes the author, with `shifts_to_person_or_intent` carrying the maximal axis-partner guard. J is **source-uniform** `semantic_referee` — NO excluded-deterministic list and NO `MCP_SERVER_SUPPORTED_FAMILY_SOURCES` entry (full passthrough; the inverse of the mixed-source D/G/I). 5 new `mcp-server/lib` modules (`familyJKeys.ts` with source+disposition metadata, `familyJPrompt.ts`, `familyJAnthropic.ts`, `familyJBanListScan.ts` — the 18-pattern largest family-local person/intent list, `familyJFixtureProvider.ts`) + 10 fixtures (incl. the EXISTENTIAL person-shift-adversarial fixture: slur in INPUT, clean OUTPUT) + 6 dedicated Deno suites + registry/dispatcher wiring. **`sensitive_composer` retargeted as the canonical "unsupported family" example across 6 Deno test files to a synthetic unregistered string (`__unregistered_family_for_test__`)** since J was the last held-out family; envelope counts grew 9→10. New jest tripwire `__tests__/mcpOneTwoOneCEdgeFamilyRegistryFamilyJ.test.ts` (FJ-1..FJ-8) locks the HELD-OUT Edge shape (`productionEnabled:false`, NOT in production list, index 9). **ADMIN-VALIDATION-ONLY CEILING (E4): NO production-enable card in this chain** — a future flip requires a fresh `cdiscourse-doctrine` §10a doctrine review. NO `src/` change (RO-16 `familyJ.ts` byte-equal stays green), NO Edge `familyRegistry.ts` / `booleanObservationRequestBuilder.ts` change, NO migration, NO `audit-lint-rules.cjs` change (L5 `family_j` mechanization is a SEPARATE follow-up Card 2). **Deno server suite: 1568 passed / 0 failed (+158 vs 1410 baseline). Full jest: 710 suites / 29518 passed (1 pre-existing skip; 29519 total) / exit 0 (+1 suite / +8 tests vs the 709 / 29510 baseline).** Typecheck clean; per-file eslint clean on the touched jest file. > **MCP-I-D2 — Family I (`thread_topology`) production-enable (2026-06-10).** Per the operator-authorized **MCP-021C-EDGE-FAMILY-I-ENABLE** card (#394), executed against the binding D1 runbook (`docs/designs/MCP-021C-EDGE-FAMILY-I-PRODUCTION-ENABLE-D1.md`) after the H-stable precondition cleared (Lane C/C4), `thread_topology.productionEnabled` is flipped `false → true` in `supabase/functions/_shared/booleanObservations/familyRegistry.ts` (line 111; `adminValidationEnabled` stays `true`). The production roster is now **A–I = 9 families**; **J (`sensitive_composer`) stays held out (`productionEnabled:false`)** and **H (`claim_clarity`) stays `true`**. Exactly one production-code line changed. Family I is **mixed-source** (8 auto_metadata + 7 lifecycle + 6 ai_classifier): the Edge subset entry `MCP_SERVER_SUPPORTED_FAMILY_SOURCES['thread_topology'] = {'ai_classifier'}` (shipped Card 1A #550) stays **byte-identical** and mode-agnostic, so production-mode I sends exactly the 6 `ai_classifier` keys (`introduces_new_issue, references_prior_agreement, introduces_sub_axis, returns_to_prior_issue, references_external_context, compares_options`) and zero of the 15 deterministic keys → no `mcp_validation_failed`. New binding `__tests__/edgeFamilyIProductionEnable.test.ts` (IIE-1..IIE-19, G-style subset-PRESENT inverse) + re-baseline of 15 roster/count/frozen-set suites (the 5 sibling enable suites, the registry/enablement/admin-mode/auto-trigger/routing-predicate gates, the ARCH-001 burst proof to the 9-family N=72 burst with `C=3` preserved, and the two `__tests__/docs/` readiness-ledger guards — frozen set {I,J}→{J}, plus a NEW I-true drift guard). Client/admin I re-scope (`HUB_NON_PRODUCTION_FAMILIES` / `FROZEN_NON_PRODUCTION_FAMILIES` {I,J}→{J}) is a **separate operator follow-up** (§4.3), NOT this card — so I stays off the production user surface. No `mcp-server/`, migration, `booleanObservationRequestBuilder.ts`, routing-flag, or `engine.ts` change. NOT deployed by Claude — Edge auto-deploys on merge; operator runs the D3 production-enable smoke (`docs/designs/MCP-021C-EDGE-FAMILY-I-PRODUCTION-ENABLE-D1.md` §6) + D4 window. **29455 tests / 707 suites passing** (was 29438 / 706; net +17 = +20 IIE binding minus 3 from frozen-set loop re-baselines; +1 suite). Typecheck + lint clean.

> **MCP-H-002 — Family H (`claim_clarity`) production re-enable (2026-06-10).** Per operator approval **E#7(b)**, the Family H production-mode flip (originally PR #405 `488d105`, reverted at PR #408 `722f17b` after a smoke FAIL whose cause — provider reliability at 8-family load — has since been fixed and re-proven, see #472 closing comment) is **re-applied**. `claim_clarity.productionEnabled` is `true` again in `supabase/functions/_shared/booleanObservations/familyRegistry.ts`. The production roster is now **A–H = 8 families**; **I/J (`thread_topology` / `sensitive_composer`) remain held out (`productionEnabled:false`)**. One-character registry flip + revert-of-revert re-baseline of the sibling enablement tests to the 8-family roster + restore of `__tests__/edgeFamilyHProductionEnable.test.ts` (HHE-1..HHE-18) + re-baseline of the ARCH-001 burst regression proof to the 8-family production burst (N=64). No subset-filter entry for H (uniform `ai_classifier`, 12 keys — HALT-13 honored). No mcp-server/, migration, routing-flag, or engine.ts change. NOT deployed by Claude — Edge auto-deploys on merge; operator runs the production-enable smoke (`docs/audits/MCP-021C-EDGE-FAMILY-H-ENABLE-SMOKE-template.md`).

> **MCP cutover gate semantics are normalized in `docs/designs/OPS-MCP-CUTOVER-GATE-CRITERIA-CONSOLIDATION.md` (2026-06-03).** Where this file's older cutover wording conflicts, that canonical doc controls. Key invariants: PASS-LOAD = **0 terminal dead-letters at N=56**; the percentage ramp is **launch-time, operator-card-gated — not a scheduled ladder**; `PASS-STAGE-1-PLUMBING / INSUFFICIENT-ORGANIC-VOLUME` is **strictly weaker** than an organic `PASS-STAGE-1` and **does not authorize 5%**; the 62/63 Family-F run is a **target-mitigation pass, not a global PASS-LOAD**; I/J stay `productionEnabled:false` (**H re-enabled 2026-06-10 per MCP-H-002 / E#7(b)** — see the MCP-H-002 note above; A–H = 8 production families).

Per the APPROVED design `docs/designs/ARCH-001-CARD3-PRODUCTION-SMOKE-STAGED-ROLLOUT.md`. NOTHING deployed/applied/armed by this card — apply/Vault-seed/arm/smoke are operator GATE-C. FOUR artifacts written: (1) NEW migration `supabase/migrations/20260608000001_arch_001_card3_cron_drain_tick.sql` — adds EXACTLY ONE `cron.schedule('arch-001-classifier-drain-tick','* * * * *', …)` (the 60s Channel-1 drain safety tick) whose DO-block body reads the drainer URL + shared secret from Supabase Vault (`vault.decrypted_secrets`) AT TICK RUNTIME and `net.http_post`s the drainer. The one improvement over Card 2's bare commented example: a NULL-URL GUARD (`IF v_url IS NULL OR v_secret IS NULL OR length=0 THEN RETURN`) makes a pre-Vault-seed apply a SILENT no-op (no noisy failed `net.http_post`, no `cron.job_run_details` error every minute during the seed window) — APPLY-SAFE / no-op-until-seeded. pg_cron + pg_net were `CREATE EXTENSION`'d by Card 1 (`20260528000021`); this migration does NOT re-create them. NOT applied to any DB. Reuses the SAME pure `runWithBoundedConcurrency` the drainer feeds C into; drives 56 synthetic jobs (8 args × 7 families) and asserts `maxObserved <= C=3` throughout + REACHES exactly 3 (not under-bounding), with the CONTRAST that unbounded `Promise.all` over the same 56 saturates at `maxObserved === 56` (the ~76-wide RCA saturation the queue prevents). C is derived FROM the drainer source (`DRAINER_PROVIDER_CONCURRENCY`, single value-pin `=3`, `<=` MCP cap 5). (3) NEW runbook `docs/runbooks/ARCH-001-CARD3-staged-arm-runbook.md` — the operator-gated Vault-seed step + staged-arm protocol (Step-1 `ENABLED=true`+`PERCENTAGE=0` smoke-tag-only → PASS-LOAD → PASS-LOAD-CONFIRM → 1%→5→25→50→100, each a separate operator card on real organic evidence) + the §3 smoke poll-to-settle + disarm/rollback; leak-safe, no real secrets, documents the protocol WITHOUT executing it. (4) NEW smoke harness `scripts/arch-001-card3-smoke/` (README plan + 6 read-only smoke-scoped SQL snapshots: queue-health A / failure-class B / last-drain C / stuck-lease D / canary-completeness / burst-completeness) — drives the §3 canary → N=56 burst, PASS-LOAD = 0 terminal dead-letters at N=56 (bar NOT lowered). <!-- Latest implementer card: MCP-MAPPING Build 2g — Family G (resolution_progress) +3 booleans + their fireable mapping rows; LAST Build-2 family, batched (21 keys → 2 batches of 16+5) — Build 2g of the ratified GATE-A design (`docs/designs/MCP-OBSERVATION-MAPPING-REFACTOR-DESIGN-001.md` + its Build-2 families manifest §6), STACKED on Family D HEAD `0c7ac8c` (branch `feat/mcp-build2-family-g`), NOT main, so the shared count/manifest/gameCopy/ops-SQL files accrete without conflict and G inherits the full B/A/C/E/F + batching-infra + D stack. DEPLOY-BEARING (Edge classifier mirror + standalone MCP-server prompt) → GATE-C. POST-STORAGE, DISPLAY-ONLY; `engine.ts` stays the sole submission gate. CAP / BATCHING: Family G is a SUBSET family — its mcp-server ai_classifier Subset goes 18 → 21, the SECOND family (after D) to EXCEED `MAX_FLAGS_PER_RESPONSE = 20`, consuming MCP-BOOLEAN-BATCHING-INFRA-001 UNCHANGED: the Edge `chunkRawKeys` lexicographically splits the 21-key set into 2 batches (16 + 5), serves each as a normal ≤16-key single-family request (each passes the UNCHANGED validator), and merges the 2 responses into one 21-key result under one run_id. Adding G's keys required NO batching-infra change (BATCH_SIZE=16 / threshold=20 byte-fixed). (A) THE 3 BOOLEANS — added as `MachineObservationDefinition`s to BOTH the client `src/features/nodeLabels/machineObservationDefinitions/familyG.ts` AND the byte-equal Edge mirror `supabase/functions/_shared/booleanObservations/machineObservationDefinitions/familyG.ts` (Family G full taxonomy 30 → 33; the ai_classifier Subset 18 → 21; all `ai_classifier`, `inspect` surface, `future_source` disposition, never `visibleByDefault`): `records_remaining_disagreement` ("Records remaining disagreement" — does the move record the SET of what remains in dispute, a roundup distinct from isolating one open point?), `defines_next_evidence_needed` ("Defines next evidence needed" — does it define the SPECIFIC evidence that would resolve the open point next?), `separates_normative_from_empirical` ("Separates normative from empirical" — does it separate a values dispute from a factual one?). EVIDENCE-DOCTRINE FENCE (manifest §6 + evidence-doctrine skill; G2): `defines_next_evidence_needed` names a next evidence step (aligns with the evidence-debt model — a primary_record_needed / source_needed-style next step), is advisory, and NEVER grants or denies factual standing/truth and NEVER judges the author — its `falsePositiveGuards` + `doctrineNotes` carry the explicit "NEVER grant or deny factual standing" + "anti-amplification module untouched" fence. None of the 3 is verdict-adjacent (manifest §6: "lowest-risk family alongside D"); all labels verdict-free (no truth/standing/who-won tokens). Each carries a question entry in the standalone MCP prompt `mcp-server/lib/familyGKeys.ts` (`FAMILY_G_RAW_KEYS` + `FAMILY_G_PROMPT_ENTRIES` 18 → 21) + `familyGPrompt.ts` count/batching narrative (the prompt builder already filters to `requestedRawKeys`, so it serves a ≤16-key batch per call with NO builder change). NO per-family classifier-set version bump (`FAMILY_G_CLASSIFIER_SET_VERSION` stays `family-g-v1` — EXACTLY as D left its `-v1`). NO schema-version bump (`MCP_BOOLEAN_OBSERVATION_SCHEMA_VERSION` stays `mcp-021.machine-observations.boolean.v1`, byte-equal). NO `familyRegistry` change (G already `productionEnabled:true`). NO DDL. NO batching-infra change. The Edge subset filter is source-based (`MCP_SERVER_SUPPORTED_FAMILY_SOURCES['resolution_progress'] = {ai_classifier}`), so the 3 new ai_classifier defs flow through with NO key-list change. (A.5) CANONICAL + PER-BATCH FIXTURES — mirroring D: `mcp-server/fixtures/` gains `family-g-batch0-{request,response}.json` (16 keys) + `family-g-batch1-{request,response}.json` (5 keys) — the actual ≤20 wire units, each passing the mcp-server validator; the existing `family-g-canonical-{request,response}.json` are re-cast as the FULL 21-key pre-chunk request + the MERGED 21-key result (the Edge merge target; >20 by design, so NOT validated by the 20-cap, asserted as the disjoint union of the 2 batches). The fixture provider now serves the valid 16-key batch-0 response. (B) THE MAPPING ROWS — 15 resolution-progress rows adopted into `observationMappingRegistry.ts` from the candidate CSV's deferred `resolution_progress` rows whose required flags are EXACTLY these 3 (6 singles MBOM-00101..00106 + 9 intra-3 pairs MBOM-01136..01144, covering single_true / single_false / pair_true_true / pair_true_false / pair_false_true), re-authored into clean verdict-free move-level labels, every flag now a deployed A-G rawKey so each rule fires. The CSV's other 60+ `resolution_progress` pair rows that ALSO need a PLANNED partner flag remain DEFERRED to Build 3. `OBSERVATION_MAPPING_ADOPTION_MANIFEST` gains `build2gFamilyGAdoptedRules: 15`; `totalActiveRules` 104 → 119. Labels route through `gameCopy.toPlainLanguageOrSuppress` — 15 new verdict-free, snake-free plain-language entries; composite-supersedes-singles respected. Tests: new `__tests__/mcpBuild2gFamilyG.test.ts` (client≡Edge deep-equal parity, unique rawKeys, A-G reconciliation, no-schema-bump regression incl. Edge mirror, verdict-free + describes-MOVE-not-author over the 3 defs + their rows with word-boundary matching incl. the G resolution↔verdict tokens, EVIDENCE-DOCTRINE FENCE for G2, rules fire + supersede, plain-language routing, AND the §0.5 BATCHING PROOF — the REAL 21-key Family G Subset → 2 batches (16 + 5) → merged 21-key result, batching infra UNCHANGED, each batch ≤20, disjoint+union=21, deterministic/lexicographic-stable, no batch metadata on the wire) + new mcp-server Deno `tests/familyGFixtureParity.test.ts` (per-batch validator + ban-list + the merged-21 = union-of-2-batches proof). Updated count assertions (familyG 30 → 33 / Subset 18 → 21 / registry 190 → 193 / per-family CASE resolution_progress 18 → 21 / Edge-request 18 → 21 / multi-family 37 → 40 / familyRegistryInit 18 → 21 / familyGKeys 18 → 21 / familyGKeysParity 30 → 33 + 18 → 21 / familyGAdversarialDoctrine canonical-response now merged-21 + provider batch0-16). Deploy-bearing ops SQL `scripts/ops/sql/14-per-family-per-mode-signal-density.sql` per-family CASE `resolution_progress` 18 → 21 + `scripts/ops/sql/16-family-g-subset-coverage.sql` Subset list 18 → 21 (21-vs-33 header) + their `opsMcpObservabilityFamilyGCoverage` mirror count test. RELAXED the stale MCP-021B byte-equal-vs-main guard RO-13 (familyG.ts) to an additive/well-formedness check, following the documented RO-7..RO-12 precedent in the same file; full `mcpOneTwoOneBReadOnlyBoundary` suite re-run green (no other RO-N tripped). Test delta (FULL Jest suite, RECONCILED onto main, captured exit 0): **703 → 704 suites / 29389 passing + 1 pre-existing skip (29390 total) (+1 suite / new mcpBuild2gFamilyG suite + per-rule-parameterized assertions across edited suites)**. mcp-server Deno suite: 1410 passed / 0 failed (+15 vs D's 1395 — the new familyGFixtureParity suite). Typecheck + lint clean (exit 0). OPERATOR DEPLOY (deploy-bearing): merge → the Edge `classify-argument-boolean-observations` auto-deploys (registered in `supabase/config.toml`); the standalone MCP server (`mcp-server/`, Deno Deploy) is NOT auto-deployed — the operator must redeploy it then run the hosted MCP smoke against the `*.deno.net` base URL exercising the 2-batch split with ZERO terminal dead-letters (N=56 / 0-dead-letter bar); until that redeploy lands the 3 new `booleanQuestion`s are NOT asked. At admin_validation sign-off, the 3 Family G booleans run admin-validation-first; confirm the 21-key family splits into 2 batches under one run_id in the hosted smoke. NO migration, NO DDL, NO global schema-version bump, NO batching-infra change, NO `engine.ts`, NO `familyRegistry` productionEnabled change, NO anti-amplification change, NO HiTODS / H-I-J / Build-3 cross-flag rows, NO routing/percentage flip, NO new dependency, NO `inactive_reason` exposure, NO classifier/provider/AI invocation by Claude, NO push/PR/merge/deploy by Claude, NO secret printed. -->

<!-- Latest implementer card: MCP-BOOLEAN-BATCHING-INFRA-001 — request batching for >20-key families (PLUMBING ONLY; NO family keys, NO new booleans) — STACKED on Build-2 Family-F HEAD `2d4fa70` (branch `feat/mcp-boolean-batching-infra`). DEPLOY-BEARING (Edge classifier mirror + standalone MCP-server) → GATE-C. POST-STORAGE, DISPLAY-ONLY; `engine.ts` (the sole submission gate) UNTOUCHED. THE PROBLEM: a family whose source-filtered classified-key set exceeds `MAX_FLAGS_PER_RESPONSE = 20` (Build-2 Family D = 22, Family G = 21) cannot be served by a single MCP request — the 22/21-entry `observations` map (true + false counted) trips `flag_count_too_high` on the Edge parser and `validation_failed` on the mcp-server mirror. This card adds the deterministic CHUNKING + MERGE plumbing that dissolves the blocker WITHOUT touching the cap, the schema, or the wire shape; D/G consume it in later cards. (A) THE PURE CORE — new `supabase/functions/_shared/booleanObservations/booleanObservationBatching.ts` (Deno-free, directly Jest-loadable): `BATCH_SIZE = 16` (headroom 4 under the 20 cap), `BATCH_SPLIT_THRESHOLD = 20` (a VERBATIM mirror of the per-response cap, NOT a redefinition), `chunkRawKeys(rawKeys, batchSize?)` (L ≤ 20 → EXACTLY 1 batch in input order = byte-identical to today; L > 20 → ceil(L/16) batches of ≤16 from a LEXICOGRAPHICALLY pre-sorted copy so a given rawKey always lands in the same batch = idempotent re-runs; D=22 → 16+6, G=21 → 16+5), `buildBatchRequestFromFull(base, batch)` (per-batch request narrowed to the slice; carries NO batchIndex/batchTotal — out-of-band only), `mergeBatchResponses(outcomes, fallbackNodeId)` (unions observations/confidence/evidenceSpan; first-wins collision guard; nodeId+modelInfo from the first success), `firstBatchFailureDetail` (leak-safe `{batchIndex,batchTotal,reason}` projection — integers/enums only). (B) THE ORCHESTRATION — `classifyArgumentCore.ts` (direct/auto-trigger path) and `classifierDrainerClassify.ts` (queue/drainer path) now CHUNK the family's full rawKey set → loop the adapter ONCE PER BATCH (sequential, no concurrency-product increase) → MERGE → the SAME unchanged sanitize/persist tail. ONE logical run per (argument, family): all batches share one run_id (direct path persists once; drainer path returns the merged positives for the atomic `finalize_classifier_job`). FAILURE SEMANTICS: ALL-OR-NOTHING on both paths — when any batch fails the run is marked `status:'failed'` (`mcp_batch_partial_failure`) with a leak-safe `failure_detail {batchIndex,batchTotal,reason}` and NO positive rows are persisted (direct path aligned to the drainer per the reconcile/545 CONCERN: the production results-read consumers `machineObservationPersistenceQuery.ts` Source-6 + `argumentsApi.ts` filter run_mode but NOT status, so a positive under a failed run would surface + double-count the retry's fresh success run); the drainer (atomic finalize) returns the first failed batch's typed `unavailable` so the EXISTING retry policy re-runs the WHOLE (argument, family) job (family-granularity retry; idempotent chunk assignment — design Q1 confirmed in `classifierDrainerRetryPolicy.ts`). §2.5 ASYNC-PATH CONFIRMATION: batched classification runs ONLY on the POST-storage paths — the auto-trigger (fire-and-forget via `EdgeRuntime.waitUntil`, off the submit isolate) and the classifier drainer (30s/90s budget) — NEVER the synchronous submit path, so N sequential MCP calls for a >20 family never hit the 15s submit timeout; `engine.ts` UNTOUCHED. BACKWARD-COMPAT: A=19/B=17/C=20/E=19/F=17 all ≤ 20 → exactly 1 batch whose per-batch request is byte-identical to the pre-batching builder output (proven against the REAL request builder); the >20 path is proven with a SYNTHETIC fixture (the infra adds NO family key). NO-SCHEMA-BUMP: `MCP_BOOLEAN_OBSERVATION_SCHEMA_VERSION` byte-equal; batch metadata asserted absent from the serialized request. mcp-server NEEDS NO functional change (the prompt builders already filter to `requestedRawKeys`; the validator accepts subsets) — the Edge owns all chunking; NO optional server-side mirror chunker created (avoids two chunkers drifting). Tests: 3 new suites — `booleanObservationBatching.test.ts` (34: chunk determinism/lexicographic stability, split threshold incl. 33-synthetic, disjointness, per-batch ≤20, merge union/first-wins/partial-failure, batch-metadata-never-on-the-wire), `booleanObservationBatchingBackwardCompat.test.ts` (18: real A/B/C/E/F 1-batch byte-identity + Family-I/#550 6-key subset, 21-entry single response STILL rejects = cap intact, no-schema-bump), `booleanObservationBatchingOrchestration.test.ts` (20: Deno-only wiring source scans + engine.ts untouched). Updated the now-stale source-scan anchors in `archOneCardTwoDrainerCore.test.ts` (DC-26: `adapter(mcpRequest`→`adapter(batchRequest`), `mcpOneTwoOneCAutoTriggerFailureMode.test.ts` (FAIL-24: `adapterResult`→`unavailable`), `mcpOneTwoOneCFailureSubreasonThreading.test.ts` (THR-4: failure-field counts/anchors), `opsMcpClassifierFailureDetailAutoTriggerFill.test.ts` (FILL-9/10/12/13/15: branch guard `if (!anySuccess)` + `unavailable` alias + the shared success/partial persistRun) — INTENT-PRESERVING per the design's "intended update, not a relaxation" note (the leak-safe builder reuse, success byte-equality, and INSERT-only writer guarantees are all re-asserted against the new structure). The full `mcpOneTwoOneBReadOnlyBoundary` suite stays GREEN (42/42 — the infra touches NO `family*.ts` definition and NO schema version, so no RO-N guard was relaxed). Test delta (FULL Jest suite, captured exit 0): **702 suites / 27089 passing + 1 pre-existing skip (27090 total)** (FULL Jest on reconcile/545-onto-main, exit 0; absolute totals exceed the original branch-relative figures because this card is reconciled onto current main, which already carries Build-2 families A/B/C/E/F). This card adds +3 batching suites / +72 batching tests (booleanObservationBatching 34 / BackwardCompat 18 / Orchestration 20; includes +6 reconcile/545 bar-raising tests — E/F backward-compat + Family-I/#550 6-key subset) plus the direct-path ALL-OR-NOTHING partial-failure CONCERN correction (no positive rows persisted on any batch failure). mcp-server Deno suite: 1388 passed / 0 failed. Typecheck + lint clean (exit 0). OPERATOR DEPLOY (deploy-bearing, but INERT until a family exceeds 20): merge → the Edge `classify-argument-boolean-observations` auto-deploys (registered in `supabase/config.toml`); the standalone MCP server (`mcp-server/`, Deno Deploy) is NOT auto-deployed — the operator redeploys it (no functional change this card, so the redeploy is a no-op for behavior but keeps the trees in sync). The batching loop is dormant until a family's key count exceeds 20 (D/G cards). NO migration, NO DDL, NO schema-version bump, NO new family keys / NO new booleans, NO `engine.ts`, NO `familyRegistry` productionEnabled change, NO HiTODS / H-I-J, NO routing/percentage flip, NO new dependency, NO classifier/provider/AI invocation by Claude, NO push/PR/merge/deploy by Claude, NO secret printed. -->

<!-- Latest implementer card: MCP-MAPPING Build 2f — Family F (critical_question) +3 booleans + their fireable mapping rows — Build 2f of the ratified GATE-A design (`docs/designs/MCP-OBSERVATION-MAPPING-REFACTOR-DESIGN-001.md` + its Build-2 families manifest §5), STACKED on Build 2e (Family E, branch base `4adbcad`; Families D and G are SKIPPED — both hit the 20-flag MAX_FLAGS_PER_RESPONSE cap) so the shared count/manifest/gameCopy files accrete without conflict. DEPLOY-BEARING (Edge classifier mirror + standalone MCP-server prompt) → GATE-C. POST-STORAGE, DISPLAY-ONLY; `engine.ts` stays the sole submission gate. CAP CHECK: Family F ends at 17 classified keys ≤ MAX_FLAGS_PER_RESPONSE 20 → OK. (A) THE 3 BOOLEANS — added as `MachineObservationDefinition`s to BOTH the client `src/features/nodeLabels/machineObservationDefinitions/familyF.ts` AND the byte-equal Edge mirror `supabase/functions/_shared/booleanObservations/machineObservationDefinitions/familyF.ts` (Family F 14 → 17; all `ai_classifier`, `inspect` surface, `future_source` disposition, never `visibleByDefault`): `question_names_uncertainty` ("Question names a source of uncertainty" — does the question this move poses name a specific source of uncertainty?), `question_separates_claim_evidence` ("Question separates claim from evidence" — does the question separate the claim from the evidence for it?), `question_invites_revision` ("Question leaves room to refine" — does the question invite revision rather than demand closure?). PREFIX NOTE: the 3 new keys use a NEW `question_*` prefix for this family (manifest §5.2 — reads naturally, no collision with the existing `*_missing`/`*_unclear`/`*_available` keys). POLARITY NOTE: unlike the 14 baseline critical-question keys (which flag an unanswered question — an absence/gap), the 3 new keys describe a POSITIVE structural feature of the QUESTION the move poses. `question_invites_revision` (F3) is VERDICT-ADJACENT and is fenced "invites-revision-not-a-verdict": its `falsePositiveGuards` state it describes the collaborative stance of the MOVE's question never the author, that it NEVER asserts the parent is wrong/weak or NEEDS revision as a verdict (cdiscourse-doctrine §1: an open question is an invitation to refine, not a judgment that refinement is required), and that presence/absence is never a criticism; "weak"/"wrong"/"flawed"/"invalid"/"fallacy" are banned from the F3 label + diagnostic + all F3 rows. Adversarial fixtures (a gotcha/closure-framed question "So you admit X?" → FALSE; an open improvement-seeking question → TRUE) confirm move-level-not-author phrasing. Each carries a question entry in the standalone MCP prompt `mcp-server/lib/familyFKeys.ts` (`FAMILY_F_RAW_KEYS` + `FAMILY_F_PROMPT_ENTRIES` 14 → 17) and `mcp-server/lib/familyFPrompt.ts` count comments + system-prompt framing (14 → 17; F3 prompt guard carries the invites-revision-not-a-verdict + describes-the-question-not-the-author fence); the canonical request + response fixtures add the 3 keys (all false/low/null). NO per-family classifier-set version bump (`FAMILY_F_CLASSIFIER_SET_VERSION` stays `family-f-v1` — EXACTLY as Families A/B/C/E left their `-v1`). NO schema-version bump (`MCP_BOOLEAN_OBSERVATION_SCHEMA_VERSION` stays `mcp-021.machine-observations.boolean.v1`, byte-equal — vocabulary expansion, not a wire change). NO `familyRegistry` change (F already `productionEnabled:true`). NO DDL (the store is `raw_key`-row key-value). (B) THE MAPPING ROWS — 16 critical-question rows adopted into `observationMappingRegistry.ts` from the candidate CSV's deferred `critical_question` rows whose required flags are EXACTLY these 3 (6 singles MBOM-00143..00148 + 9 intra-3 pairs MBOM-01334..01342 + 1 curated_triple MBOM-01343 — Family F is the ONLY family whose unlock set carries a curated_triple, and all 3 of its flags are the new F keys, so it is seeded here per the fireability rule), re-authored into clean verdict-free move-level labels, every flag now a deployed A-G rawKey so each rule fires. The CSV's other 60+ `critical_question` pair rows that ALSO need a PLANNED partner flag (poses_question, requests_evidence, addresses_parent_reasoning, …) remain DEFERRED to Build 3. `OBSERVATION_MAPPING_ADOPTION_MANIFEST` gains `build2fFamilyFAdoptedRules: 16`; `totalActiveRules` 73 → 89. Labels route through `gameCopy.toPlainLanguageOrSuppress` — 16 new verdict-free, snake-free plain-language entries; composite-supersedes-singles respected (the curated_triple supersedes its consumed singles; the intra-3 pairs co-render). Tests: new `__tests__/mcpBuild2fFamilyF.test.ts` (client≡Edge deep-equal parity, unique rawKeys, question_* prefix, A-G reconciliation, no-schema-bump regression incl. Edge mirror, verdict-free + describes-MOVE-not-author over the 3 defs + their rows, F3 invites-revision-not-a-verdict fence + adversarial gotcha/open-question fixtures, the curated_triple fires + supersedes the consumed singles, rules fire + supersede, plain-language routing). Updated count assertions (14 → 17 / 184 → 187 / per-family CASE adds `critical_question` 17 / Edge-request 14 → 17 / familyRegistryInit 14 → 17) across the existing Jest + mcp-server Deno suites (NOT relaxations — the per-family key count IS 17 now). Deploy-bearing ops SQL `scripts/ops/sql/14-per-family-per-mode-signal-density.sql` per-family CASE gains `when 'critical_question' then 17` + header note + a mirror count assertion in the Family-D coverage Q14 test. RELAXED the stale MCP-021B byte-equal-vs-main guard RO-12 (familyF.ts) to an additive/well-formedness check (asserts `export const FAMILY_F_DEFINITIONS` + 3 baseline rawKeys still present), following the documented RO-7/RO-8/RO-9/RO-11 precedent in the same file; full `mcpOneTwoOneBReadOnlyBoundary` suite re-run green (no other RO-N tripped). Test delta (FULL Jest suite, captured exit 0): **698 → 699 suites / 27017 passing + 1 pre-existing skip (27018 total) (+1 suite / new mcpBuild2fFamilyF suite + per-rule-parameterized assertions across edited suites)**. mcp-server Deno suite: 1388 passed / 0 failed. Typecheck + lint clean (exit 0). OPERATOR DEPLOY (deploy-bearing): merge → the Edge `classify-argument-boolean-observations` auto-deploys (registered in `supabase/config.toml`); the standalone MCP server (`mcp-server/`, Deno Deploy) is NOT auto-deployed — the operator must redeploy it then run the hosted MCP smoke against the `*.deno.net` base URL; until that redeploy lands the 3 new `booleanQuestion`s are NOT asked. At admin_validation sign-off, the F3 (`question_invites_revision`) invites-revision-not-a-verdict framing passes the 5-layer defense (system-prompt doctrine block · per-key falsePositiveGuards · word-boundary ban-list scan · adversarial fixtures · live smoke audit). NO migration, NO DDL, NO schema-version bump, NO `engine.ts`, NO `familyRegistry` productionEnabled change, NO HiTODS / H-I-J / Build-3 cross-flag rows, NO routing/percentage flip, NO new dependency, NO `inactive_reason` exposure, NO classifier/provider/AI invocation by Claude, NO push/PR/merge/deploy by Claude, NO secret printed. -->

<!-- Latest implementer card: MCP-MAPPING Build 2e — Family E (argument_scheme) +3 booleans + their fireable mapping rows — Build 2e of the ratified GATE-A design (`docs/designs/MCP-OBSERVATION-MAPPING-REFACTOR-DESIGN-001.md` + its Build-2 families manifest §4), STACKED on Build 2c (Family C, branch base `6357666`; Family D is SKIPPED — blocked on the 20-flag MAX_FLAGS_PER_RESPONSE cap) so the shared count/manifest/gameCopy files accrete without conflict. DEPLOY-BEARING (Edge classifier mirror + standalone MCP-server prompt) → GATE-C. POST-STORAGE, DISPLAY-ONLY; `engine.ts` stays the sole submission gate. CAP CHECK: Family E ends at 19 classified keys ≤ MAX_FLAGS_PER_RESPONSE 20 → OK. (A) THE 3 BOOLEANS — added as `MachineObservationDefinition`s to BOTH the client `src/features/nodeLabels/machineObservationDefinitions/familyE.ts` AND the byte-equal Edge mirror `supabase/functions/_shared/booleanObservations/machineObservationDefinitions/familyE.ts` (Family E 16 → 19; all `ai_classifier`, `inspect` surface, `future_source` disposition, never `visibleByDefault`): `linked_premise_structure` ("Premises that depend on each other" — does the move use linked premises that each need the others and fail together?), `convergent_premise_structure` ("Premises that each stand alone" — does it use convergent premises that each independently support the conclusion?), `enthymeme_gap_detected` ("Relies on an unstated step" — does it rely on an unstated load-bearing premise?). THEORY-TERM RULE (GATE-A §8.2 rule 4): the argumentation-theory terms `linked` / `convergent` / `enthymeme` stay INTERNAL (rawKey + classifier prompt + internal metadata); the user-facing label/shortLabel/description carry ONLY plain language ("Premises that depend on each other" / "Premises that each stand alone" / "Relies on an unstated step"), never the raw theory term — enforced by a dedicated word-boundary theory-term-not-surfaced test over defs + mapping rows + gameCopy. `enthymeme_gap_detected` (E3) is VERDICT-ADJACENT and is fenced "gap-is-not-a-verdict": its `falsePositiveGuards` state it describes a structural feature of the MOVE's inference never the author, that a gap is an invitation to state the premise NOT a defeat (cdiscourse-doctrine §1), and that presence/absence is never a criticism ("this person reasons sloppily" is explicitly forbidden); "weak"/"wrong"/"flawed"/"invalid"/"fallacy" are banned from the E3 label + diagnostic + all E3 rows. Adversarial fixtures (a fully-stated-premise move → FALSE / only the absence-rule fires; an unstated-load-bearing-premise move → TRUE) confirm move-level-not-author phrasing. Each carries a question entry in the standalone MCP prompt `mcp-server/lib/familyEKeys.ts` (`FAMILY_E_RAW_KEYS` + `FAMILY_E_PROMPT_ENTRIES` 16 → 19) and `mcp-server/lib/familyEPrompt.ts` count comments + system-prompt framing (16 → 19; E3 prompt guard carries the gap-is-not-a-verdict + MUST-NOT-call-it-weak/wrong fence); the canonical request + response fixtures add the 3 keys (all false/low/null). NO per-family classifier-set version bump (`FAMILY_E_CLASSIFIER_SET_VERSION` stays `family-e-v1` — EXACTLY as Families A/B/C left their `-v1`). NO schema-version bump (`MCP_BOOLEAN_OBSERVATION_SCHEMA_VERSION` stays `mcp-021.machine-observations.boolean.v1`, byte-equal — vocabulary expansion, not a wire change). NO `familyRegistry` change (E already `productionEnabled:true`). NO DDL (the store is `raw_key`-row key-value). (B) THE MAPPING ROWS — 15 argument-scheme rows adopted into `observationMappingRegistry.ts` from the candidate CSV's deferred `argument_scheme` rows whose required flags are EXACTLY these 3 (6 singles MBOM-00119..00124 + 9 intra-3 pairs MBOM-01325..01333, covering single_true / single_false / pair_true_true / pair_true_false / pair_false_true), re-authored into clean verdict-free, theory-term-free move-level labels, every flag now a deployed A-G rawKey so each rule fires. The CSV's other 60+ `argument_scheme` pair/triple rows that ALSO need a PLANNED partner flag (has_premise_conclusion, cause_effect_language, goal_action_reasoning, …) remain DEFERRED to Build 3. `OBSERVATION_MAPPING_ADOPTION_MANIFEST` gains `build2eFamilyEAdoptedRules: 15`; `totalActiveRules` 58 → 73. Labels route through `gameCopy.toPlainLanguageOrSuppress` — 15 new verdict-free, snake-free, theory-term-free plain-language entries; composite-supersedes-singles respected. Tests: new `__tests__/mcpBuild2eFamilyE.test.ts` (client≡Edge deep-equal parity, unique rawKeys, A-G reconciliation, no-schema-bump regression incl. Edge mirror, verdict-free + describes-MOVE-not-author over the 3 defs + their rows, THEORY-TERM-NOT-SURFACED word-boundary test over defs + rows + gameCopy [+ a sanity test proving the terms ARE present in the classifier-facing definitions], E3 gap-is-not-a-verdict fence + adversarial stated/unstated-premise fixtures, rules fire + supersede, plain-language routing). Updated count assertions (16 → 19 / 181 → 184 / per-family CASE adds `argument_scheme` 19 / Edge-request 16 → 19 / canonical-fixture 16 → 19 / validator full-19-key acceptance) across the existing Jest + mcp-server Deno suites (NOT relaxations — the per-family key count IS 19 now). Deploy-bearing ops SQL `scripts/ops/sql/14-per-family-per-mode-signal-density.sql` per-family CASE gains `when 'argument_scheme' then 19` + header note (E backfill is a separate queued ops card; E has 0 MCP runs today) + a mirror count assertion in the Family-D coverage Q14 test. Relaxed the stale MCP-021B byte-equal-vs-main guard RO-11 (familyE.ts) to an additive/well-formedness check, and RO-9 (familyC.ts, inherited from the Build-2c stacked base) likewise, following the documented RO-7/RO-8 precedent in the same file. Test delta (FULL Jest suite, RECONCILED onto main @ 48b221c, captured exit 0): **697 → 698 suites / 25814 passing + 1 pre-existing skip (25815 total) (+1 suite / new mcpBuild2eFamilyE suite + per-rule-parameterized assertions across edited suites)**. mcp-server Deno suite: 1383 passed / 0 failed. Typecheck + lint clean (exit 0). OPERATOR DEPLOY (deploy-bearing): merge → the Edge `classify-argument-boolean-observations` auto-deploys (registered in `supabase/config.toml`); the standalone MCP server (`mcp-server/`, Deno Deploy) is NOT auto-deployed — the operator must redeploy it then run the hosted MCP smoke against the `*.deno.net` base URL; until that redeploy lands the 3 new `booleanQuestion`s are NOT asked. At admin_validation sign-off, the E3 (`enthymeme_gap_detected`) gap-is-not-a-verdict framing passes the 5-layer defense (system-prompt doctrine block · per-key falsePositiveGuards · word-boundary ban-list scan · adversarial fixtures · live smoke audit). NO migration, NO DDL, NO schema-version bump, NO `engine.ts`, NO `familyRegistry` productionEnabled change, NO theory term surfaced raw, NO HiTODS / H-I-J / Build-3 cross-flag rows, NO routing/percentage flip, NO new dependency, NO `inactive_reason` exposure, NO classifier/provider/AI invocation by Claude, NO push/PR/merge/deploy by Claude, NO secret printed. -->

<!-- Latest implementer card: MCP-MAPPING Build 2c — Family C (misunderstanding_repair) +3 booleans + their fireable mapping rows — Build 2c of the ratified GATE-A design (`docs/designs/MCP-OBSERVATION-MAPPING-REFACTOR-DESIGN-001.md` + its Build-2 families manifest §2), STACKED on Build 2b (Family A, branch base `9824c98`) so the shared count/manifest/gameCopy files accrete without conflict. DEPLOY-BEARING (Edge classifier mirror + standalone MCP-server prompt) → GATE-C. POST-STORAGE, DISPLAY-ONLY; `engine.ts` stays the sole submission gate. (A) THE 3 BOOLEANS — added as `MachineObservationDefinition`s to BOTH the client `src/features/nodeLabels/machineObservationDefinitions/familyC.ts` AND the byte-equal Edge mirror `supabase/functions/_shared/booleanObservations/machineObservationDefinitions/familyC.ts` (Family C 17 → 20; all `ai_classifier`, `inspect` surface, `future_source` disposition, never `visibleByDefault`): `offers_repair_path` ("Offers a way to resolve" — does the move propose a concrete way to resolve a misunderstanding, a path not just a flag?), `names_ambiguity_source` ("Names the ambiguity" — does it name the specific term/reference that is ambiguous and why?), `accepts_correction` ("Takes up an offered point" — does it accept a correction a prior move offered?). `accepts_correction` (C3) is VERDICT-ADJACENT and is fenced "repair-not-defeat": its `falsePositiveGuards` state it describes the repair MOVE never the author, that it never frames taking up an offered point as defeat or concession of the whole (cdiscourse-doctrine §1: concession is a scoring repair, not a defeat), and that ABSENCE is never a criticism. The user-facing label/shortLabel/description are deliberately "Takes up an offered point" (NOT "Accepts correction") so no standalone "correct" verdict token appears; the rawKey alone carries "correct". The verdict-free ban-list test uses WORD-BOUNDARY matching (`\bcorrect\b`), proving the benign "correction" substring does NOT false-positive AND a real standalone "correct" WOULD be caught; "correct"/"true"/"wins" are banned from the C3 label + diagnostic + all C3 rows. Adversarial fixtures (a correction-REJECTING move → FALSE; a genuine acceptance → TRUE) confirm move-level-not-author phrasing. Each carries a question entry in the standalone MCP prompt `mcp-server/lib/familyCKeys.ts` (`FAMILY_C_RAW_KEYS` + `FAMILY_C_PROMPT_ENTRIES` 17 → 20) and `mcp-server/lib/familyCPrompt.ts` count comments (17 → 20); the canonical request + response fixtures add the 3 keys. NO per-family classifier-set version bump (`FAMILY_C_CLASSIFIER_SET_VERSION` stays `family-c-v1` — EXACTLY as Families A/B left their `-v1`). NO schema-version bump (`MCP_BOOLEAN_OBSERVATION_SCHEMA_VERSION` stays `mcp-021.machine-observations.boolean.v1`, byte-equal — vocabulary expansion, not a wire change). NO `familyRegistry` change (C already `productionEnabled:true`). NO DDL (the store is `raw_key`-row key-value). (B) THE MAPPING ROWS — 15 misunderstanding-repair rows adopted into `observationMappingRegistry.ts` from the candidate CSV's deferred `misunderstanding_repair` rows whose required flags are EXACTLY these 3 (6 singles MBOM-00071..00076 + 9 intra-3 pairs MBOM-00929..00937, covering single_true / single_false / pair_true_true / pair_true_false / pair_false_true), re-authored into clean verdict-free move-level labels, every flag now a deployed A-G rawKey so each rule fires. The CSV's other 30+ `misunderstanding_repair` pair rows that ALSO need a PLANNED partner flag (signals_confusion, asks_for_clarification, restates_other_position, …) remain DEFERRED to Build 3. `OBSERVATION_MAPPING_ADOPTION_MANIFEST` gains `build2cFamilyCAdoptedRules: 15`; `totalActiveRules` 43 → 58. Labels route through `gameCopy.toPlainLanguageOrSuppress` — 15 new verdict-free, snake-free plain-language entries; the C3 rows are repair-not-defeat and word-boundary clean of "correct"; composite-supersedes-singles respected. Tests: new `__tests__/mcpBuild2cFamilyC.test.ts` (client≡Edge deep-equal parity, unique rawKeys, A-G reconciliation, no-schema-bump regression incl. Edge mirror, verdict-free + describes-MOVE-not-author over the 3 defs + their rows, C3 repair-not-defeat fence + word-boundary "correct" handling [proves benign "correction" passes AND standalone "correct" is caught] + adversarial accept/reject fixtures, rules fire + supersede, plain-language routing). Deploy-bearing ops SQL `scripts/ops/sql/14-per-family-per-mode-signal-density.sql` per-family CASE updated (misunderstanding_repair 17 → 20) + its 2 coverage tests + the multi-family fixture (density recomputed 3/(2×20)=0.075). <!-- Latest implementer card: MCP-MAPPING Build 2b — Family A (parent_relation) +3 booleans + their fireable mapping rows — Build 2b of the ratified GATE-A design (`docs/designs/MCP-OBSERVATION-MAPPING-REFACTOR-DESIGN-001.md` + its Build-2 families manifest §1), STACKED on Build 2a (Family B, branch base `e91a964`) so the shared count/manifest/gameCopy files accrete without conflict. DEPLOY-BEARING (Edge classifier mirror + standalone MCP-server prompt) → GATE-C. POST-STORAGE, DISPLAY-ONLY; `engine.ts` stays the sole submission gate. (A) THE 3 BOOLEANS — added as `MachineObservationDefinition`s to BOTH the client `src/features/nodeLabels/machineObservationDefinitions/familyA.ts` AND the byte-equal Edge mirror `supabase/functions/_shared/booleanObservations/machineObservationDefinitions/familyA.ts` (Family A 16 → 19; all `ai_classifier`, `inspect` surface, `future_source` disposition, never `visibleByDefault`): `acknowledges_parent_strength` ("Grants a point before disagreeing" — does the move grant a strength of the parent before disagreeing?), `compares_parent_to_sibling_branch` ("Compares to a sibling branch" — does it compare the parent to a sibling branch in the same thread?), `identifies_parent_scope_limit` ("Names a scope limit" — does it name a specific scope limit on the parent's claim?). `acknowledges_parent_strength` (A1) is VERDICT-ADJACENT and is fenced: its `falsePositiveGuards` state it describes the MOVE never the author, that it never says the parent IS strong/right, and that ABSENCE is never a criticism; "correct"/"true"/"wins" are banned from its label + diagnostic; adversarial fixtures (a bare-attack/no-acknowledgement move → FALSE; a strength-acknowledging disagreement → TRUE) confirm move-level-not-author phrasing. `compares_parent_to_sibling_branch` (A2) carries a RELIABILITY CARRY-FORWARD doctrineNote: cross-branch reasoning is a harder classifier task, so the operator must verify admin_validation precision at audit and MAY DEFER trusting it on the production card surface if noisy (all 3 shipped; A2 not dropped). Each carries a question entry in the standalone MCP prompt `mcp-server/lib/familyAKeys.ts` (`FAMILY_A_RAW_KEYS` + `FAMILY_A_PROMPT_ENTRIES` 16 → 19) and `mcp-server/lib/familyAPrompt.ts` count comments (16 → 19); the canonical fixture `mcp-server/fixtures/classify-argument-boolean-observations.family-a-canonical-response.json` adds the 3 keys. NO per-family classifier-set version bump (`FAMILY_A_CLASSIFIER_SET_VERSION` stays `family-a-v1` — EXACTLY as Family B left `family-b-v1`). NO schema-version bump (`MCP_BOOLEAN_OBSERVATION_SCHEMA_VERSION` stays `mcp-021.machine-observations.boolean.v1`, byte-equal — vocabulary expansion, not a wire change). NO `familyRegistry` change (A already `productionEnabled:true`). NO DDL (the store is `raw_key`-row key-value). (B) THE MAPPING ROWS — 15 parent-relation rows adopted into `observationMappingRegistry.ts` from the candidate CSV's deferred `parent_relation` rows whose required flags are EXACTLY these 3 (6 singles MBOM-00019..00024 + 9 intra-3 pairs MBOM-00458..00466, covering single_true / single_false / pair_true_true / pair_true_false / pair_false_true), re-authored through the design's review pass into clean verdict-free move-level labels, every flag now a deployed A-G rawKey so each rule fires. The CSV's other ~30 `parent_relation` pair rows that ALSO need a PLANNED partner flag (references_parent_claim, quotes_parent_text, …) remain DEFERRED to Build 3. `OBSERVATION_MAPPING_ADOPTION_MANIFEST` gains `build2bFamilyAAdoptedRules: 15`; `totalActiveRules` 28 → 43. Labels route through `gameCopy.toPlainLanguageOrSuppress` — 15 new verdict-free, snake-free plain-language entries; composite-supersedes-singles respected. Tests: new `__tests__/mcpBuild2bFamilyA.test.ts` (client≡Edge deep-equal parity for the 3 defs, unique rawKeys, A-G reconciliation, no-schema-bump regression incl. Edge mirror, verdict-free + describes-MOVE-not-author over the 3 defs + their rows, A1 adversarial/fence fixtures + "correct/true/wins" ban over A1 label+diagnostic+rows, A2 reliability-carry-forward note, rules fire + supersede, plain-language routing). Updated count assertions (16 → 19 / 175 → 178 / per-family CASE 16 → 19 / mixed-family totals) across the existing Jest + mcp-server Deno suites (NOT relaxations — the per-family key count IS 19 now). Relaxed the stale MCP-021B byte-equal-vs-main guard RO-7 (familyA.ts) to an additive/well-formedness check, following the documented RO-8/RO-28/RO-36/37 precedent in the same file. Deploy-bearing ops SQL `scripts/ops/sql/14-per-family-per-mode-signal-density.sql` per-family CASE updated (parent_relation 16 → 19) + its 3 coverage tests + the multi-family fixture (density recomputed 12/(4×19)=0.1579). Test delta (FULL Jest suite, RECONCILED onto main @ 88425cf, captured exit 0): **696 suites / 23567 total (23566 passing + 1 pre-existing skip unchanged)** — new mcpBuild2bFamilyA suite + per-rule-parameterized assertions across edited suites, plus the cross-PR SFI-11 count update (#550 multi-family thread_topology+parent_relation 22→25 for Family A 16→19). mcp-server Deno suite: 1379 passed / 0 failed. Typecheck + lint clean (exit 0). OPERATOR DEPLOY (deploy-bearing): merge → the Edge `classify-argument-boolean-observations` auto-deploys (registered in `supabase/config.toml`); the standalone MCP server (`mcp-server/`, Deno Deploy) is NOT auto-deployed — the operator must redeploy it then run the hosted MCP smoke against the `*.deno.net` base URL; until that redeploy lands the 3 new `booleanQuestion`s are NOT asked. At admin_validation sign-off, verify A2 (`compares_parent_to_sibling_branch`) precision and DEFER it on the production card surface if noisy. NO migration, NO DDL, NO schema-version bump, NO `engine.ts`, NO `familyRegistry` productionEnabled change, NO HiTODS / H-I-J / Build-3 cross-flag rows, NO routing/percentage flip, NO new dependency, NO `inactive_reason` exposure, NO classifier/provider/AI invocation by Claude, NO push/PR/merge/deploy by Claude, NO secret printed. -->

<!-- Latest implementer card: MCP-OBSERVATION-MAPPING-EXPANSION-001 (Build 1 / Slice B — wire the observation-mapping evaluator into the card classifier display) — Track 1 of the ratified GATE-A design #530 (`docs/designs/MCP-OBSERVATION-MAPPING-REFACTOR-DESIGN-001.md`), on top of Slice A's pure engine. POST-STORAGE, DISPLAY-ONLY. Pure `src/**` + `__tests__/**` + this status note. NO migration, NO Edge, NO `supabase/**`, NO mcp-server change, NO new boolean, NO `engine.ts`/`familyRegistry.ts` edit, NO schema-version bump (`MCP_BOOLEAN_OBSERVATION_SCHEMA_VERSION` stays `v1`, byte-equal). (1) PER-NODE WIRING — `ArgumentGameSurface.tsx` now, for the ACTIVE node only, derives the node's set of POSITIVE persisted rawKeys (`persistedObservationsByArgumentId[activeMessageId].map(r => r.rawKey)` — absence of a row IS the negative per the persistence schema), calls `evaluateObservationMapping(positiveRawKeys, OBSERVATION_MAPPING_REGISTRY, { surface: 'card' })`, and formats the results via the new `buildCardMappingSection` into a memoized `activeMappingSection` (keyed on activeMessageId + the persisted-observations map). The evaluator reads already-persisted booleans; it NEVER calls the classifier/network and is NEVER in the submit/acceptance path (`engine.ts` stays the sole gate). The section is threaded Surface → `ArgumentBubbleStack` (`activeMappingSection`) → `ArgumentBubbleCard` (`mappingSection`) → `CardDetailPanel` (`mappingSection`), mirroring the existing card-detail prop chain; forwarded to the ACTIVE card only. (2) NEW PURE RENDER-PREP MODEL — `src/features/arguments/cardView/cardMappingSectionModel.ts`: `buildCardMappingSection(ObservationMappingResult[]) → { chips, heading 'Combination observations', advisoryCaption 'What the referee noticed — advisory, not a verdict.', emptyStateCopy, hasSignals }`. Each chip carries label (shortLabel) + longLabel (displayLabel, gameCopy-routed by the evaluator) + diagnosticSentence + confidencePips (low=1/med=2/high=3, NEVER a number) + confidenceLabel + a11y label. DEFENSIVE guards: drops any result whose displayLabel/shortLabel still `looksLikeInternalCode` (no raw snake_case echo, §9/§10a) and any result whose `familyKey` (either cross-family half) is a frozen H/I/J family (A-G only). Pure: no React/Supabase/network/Date.now/Math.random/mutation. (3) NEW DISPLAY-ONLY SECTION ON THE CARD — `CardDetailPanel.tsx` renders a "Combination observations" zone (`testID="card-detail-mapping-zone"`) inside the classifier column, BELOW (additive to) the existing per-observation classifier strip (Slice 2/3 — NOT regressed). Each chip is `accessibilityRole="text"` (NO onPress, NO button role — the mapping chips are observations, not user moves), reuses the shared `ConfidencePips` dots, shows the advisory caption + diagnostic. Visible by default on the active card (#14 — no tap/disclosure); empty/none → teaching empty state (`card-detail-mapping-empty`). composite-supersedes-singles is already applied by the evaluator — the card renders exactly what it returns (a consumed single's label does not also appear). A-G only; H/I/J never; no `inactive_reason`. Tests: `__tests__/cardMappingSectionModel.test.ts` (19 — happy path, composite-supersedes, empty/degenerate, pips-not-numbers, defensive H/I/J + snake_case guards, verdict-token ban-list) + `__tests__/CardMappingSection.test.tsx` (12 — section renders by default, N labels for N triggering keys, empty state, composite-supersedes on the card, display-only/no-button/pips/advisory caption, A-G-only/H-I-J-never, classifier strip NOT regressed, byte-equivalent when prop omitted, active-card-only threading). Existing `CardDetailPanel` / `CardDetailHubPanel` / `cardViewRefine*` / `argumentDetailModel` / `observationMapping*` suites unchanged + green (no card-detail test relaxed). Test delta (FULL suite, captured exit 0): **690 → 692 suites / 21280 → 21311 total (21279 → 21310 passing + 1 pre-existing skip unchanged)** (+2 suites / +31 tests). Typecheck + lint clean (exit 0). Files: `src/features/arguments/ArgumentGameSurface.tsx`, `src/features/arguments/ArgumentBubbleStack.tsx`, `src/features/arguments/ArgumentBubbleCard.tsx`, `src/features/arguments/cardView/CardDetailPanel.tsx`, `src/features/arguments/cardView/cardMappingSectionModel.ts` (new), `__tests__/{cardMappingSectionModel.test.ts, CardMappingSection.test.tsx}` (new), this status note. NO migration, NO Edge, NO supabase/**, NO mcp-server change, NO new boolean, NO schema-version bump, NO engine.ts, NO familyRegistry.ts, NO routing/productionEnabled flip, NO classifier/provider/AI invocation, NO H/I/J surfaced, NO inactive_reason exposure, NO new dependency, NO push/PR/merge by Claude, NO secret printed. -->

<!-- Earlier implementer card: MCP-OBSERVATION-MAPPING-EXPANSION-001 (Build 1 / Slice A — pure observation-mapping evaluator + reviewed existing-boolean A-G registry; composite-supersedes-singles) — Track 1 of the ratified GATE-A design #530 (`docs/designs/MCP-OBSERVATION-MAPPING-REFACTOR-DESIGN-001.md`). POST-STORAGE, DISPLAY-ONLY. Pure `src/**` + `__tests__/**` + optional `scripts/**` + this status note. NO migration, NO Edge, NO `supabase/**`, NO mcp-server change, NO new boolean, NO `engine.ts`/`familyRegistry.ts` edit, NO card wiring (Slice B). (1) THE PURE EVALUATOR — new `src/features/nodeLabels/observationMapping/observationMappingEvaluator.ts`: `evaluateObservationMapping(positiveRawKeys: ReadonlySet<string>|string[], rules, { surface: 'card'|'timeline' }) → ObservationMappingResult[]`. Pure, deterministic, JSON-serializable; no React/Supabase/network/Date.now/Math.random/mutation. Runs over a node's already-persisted POSITIVE boolean rawKeys (NEVER calls the classifier; never in the submit/acceptance path — `engine.ts` at `src/domain/constitution/engine.ts` stays the sole gate). Implements all rule kinds: `single_true`, `single_false` (required_false — fires only when the flag is ABSENT, "not observed" never "author failed"), `pair_true_true`, `pair_true_false`/`pair_false_true` (asymmetric), `curated_triple`, `cross_family`. OQ-c (ratified §13c): composites SUPERSEDE the singles whose POSITIVE rawKeys they fully consume (triple/cross-family > pair > single) — a consumed single_true does not also render; uncovered singles still render; single_false negatives are never suppressed. Output carries labelShort + diagnosticSentence + displayPriority + confidence as a PIP level (low/med/high, NEVER a number) + observationCode routed through `gameCopy.toPlainLanguageOrSuppress` (unmapped → fall back to the rule's verdict-free `labelNeutral`; a raw snake_case code can never leak). Ordering: displayPriority asc, then mappingId. (2) THE REVIEWED + RECONCILED REGISTRY — new `observationMappingRegistry.ts` (checked-in declarative data the evaluator reads, A-G ONLY; H/I/J NEVER). RECONCILIATION FINDING: the candidate CSV (`docs/designs/mcp-observation-mapping/mcp_boolean_observation_mapping_expanded_1000plus.csv`) has 957 `fits_existing_or_planned_boolean_answers` rows in A-G families, but they are built on a PLANNED flag vocabulary (65 distinct flags such as `references_parent_claim`, `addresses_parent_reasoning`, `targets_central_parent_point`) that the DEPLOYED classifier (familyA..familyG definitions) does NOT return. Only **2** CSV A-G rows reference exclusively deployed rawKeys (the `provides_evidence` single_true + single_false) — ADOPTED directly (familyKey corrected from the CSV's `disagreement_axis` to the deployed Family-D `evidence_source_chain`). The other **955** are DEFERRED (planned vocabulary → Build 2/3 once the new booleans deploy). To deliver genuine combination-aware labels NOW + keep the registry extensible, the file ALSO authors **11** CURATED genuinely-fireable rules DIRECTLY over deployed A-G rawKeys (every flag ∈ the live A-G rawKey set), exercising every rule kind incl. a curated_triple that supersedes its singles + a `disagreement_axis+evidence_source_chain` cross_family. ADOPTION MANIFEST (`OBSERVATION_MAPPING_ADOPTION_MANIFEST`, verifiable via `scripts/mcp-observation-mapping/analyzeReconciliation.js`): csvFitsExistingAgRows 957 · adoptedFromCsv 2 · csvAgRowsDeferredPlannedVocab 955 · plannedNotDeployedDistinctFlags 65 · curatedFireableRules 11 · totalActiveRules 13 · frozenFamiliesAdopted 0. New `deployedAgRawKeys.ts` derives the deployed-A-G rawKey set from the live MCP-021A definition registry (so the reconciliation test stays in sync). New gameCopy entries (13) map every adopted observationCode to a verdict-free plain-language sentence; unmapped codes still suppress. (3) PROGRAMMATIC REVIEW GATE — `observationMappingRegistry.test.ts` scans EVERY adopted rule's labelShort/labelNeutral/diagnosticSentence for the verdict-token ban-list (winner/loser/correct/true/false/liar/dishonest/bad faith/manipulative/extremist/propagandist/stupid/idiot/troll/astroturfer, word-boundary), author-labeling ("this person/user…"), asserts safety_note present + family ∈ A-G + every flag is a deployed rawKey (reconciliation) + no `inactive_reason` (§10a). `observationMappingEvaluator.test.ts` covers every rule kind, absence semantics, composite-supersedes-singles (consumed/uncovered/negative-never-suppressed), determinism (JSON-equal), non-mutation, empty/null/unknown input, surface filter, pips-not-numbers. `observationMappingPostStorageOnly.test.ts` proves engine.ts + composerSubmit.ts never import the evaluator + purity (no React/Supabase/network/Date.now/Math.random) + no classifier/provider call + no MachineObservationDefinition authored. Test delta (FULL suite, captured exit 0): **687 → 690 suites / 20337 → 21280 total (20336 → 21279 passing + 1 pre-existing skip unchanged)** (+3 suites / +943 tests — the registry+evaluator tests are per-rule parameterized). Typecheck + lint clean (exit 0).
