<!-- Latest implementer card: ARCH-001 Card 3 — DRAINER TUNING + KICK COALESCING + STAGED-ROLLOUT KNOB (no migration; closes the ARCH-001 architecture chain). PR #383 merged at `d42d6da`; smoke audit PR #385 merged at `b974b25`. Four scoped changes on top of Card 2: (1) `DRAINER_MAX_ATTEMPTS` 3 → 4 in `classifierDrainerRetryPolicy.ts` — extra retry budget for the `provider_server_error` tail the Card 2 smoke flagged (2.86% dead-letter rate on Anthropic {isError} overload). Card 1's `reclaim_stale_leases` cap (3) is unchanged by design — the live-retry cap and the lease-expiry reclaim cap are different ceilings. (2) NEW export `DRAINER_PROVIDER_SERVER_ERROR_BACKOFF_SECONDS=[60,180,360]` applied ONLY when `reason==='api_error' AND subReason==='provider_server_error'`; default `DRAINER_RETRY_BACKOFF_SECONDS=[30,120]` PRESERVED exactly for every other retryable class (network_error, rate_limited, contract failures). Worst-case provider_server_error retry budget 60+180+360=600s ≈ 10 min, ≈ 2× Card-2's ~300s observed overload window. (3) Kick coalescing in `enqueueClassifierJobs` — replaced 7 sequential `enqueue_classifier_job` RPC calls with ONE multi-row `.from('argument_machine_observation_runs').insert(rows)` against the table directly. `arch_001_kick_classifier_drainer_trg` is `FOR EACH STATEMENT`, so 1 statement = 1 kick per submit (vs Card-2's 7 kicks/submit observed as ~106 `skipped_single_flight` rows across 15 burst args). No migration needed: existing trigger fire mode + partial-unique-indexes + service-role RLS already support the multi-row form; idempotency comes from the DB partial unique indexes #4/#5 (one success per cell + one active job per cell) + structural uniqueness of fresh argument_ids. No ON CONFLICT in the multi-row path — the partial-index predicate cannot be expressed via supabase-js `.from().insert()`, and a bare `ON CONFLICT (cols)` does not match a partial unique index. (4) NEW `CLASSIFIER_QUEUE_ROUTING_PERCENTAGE_ENV` staged-rollout knob. `parseRoutingPercentage(raw)` (fail-closed neg/NaN/missing → 0, clamp-up >100 → 100, floor fractional); `stableHashArgumentId(id)` djb2-style 32-bit deterministic hash. `shouldRouteToQueue` signature gains optional 4th param `percentage = 0` (backward-compatible with Card 2 callers). Routing predicate: `enabled === true AND (smoke-tag prefix OR hash(argument_id) % 100 < percentage)`. Smoke-tag override remains active at percentage=0 — the path the Card-3 smoke uses. `submit-argument/index.ts` reads the env via `parseRoutingPercentage` and threads it through. Production stays default-disabled: strict-true gate on master flag + `parseRoutingPercentage(undefined)=0` means no routing without explicit operator action. **Invariants preserved (verified by Phase C 8-agent fan-out + roadmap-reviewer APPROVE + 3 adversarial Explore NO_REFUTATION):** `finalize_classifier_job` signature byte-unchanged; single-flight acquire+finally-release pattern unchanged; `DRAINER_PROVIDER_CONCURRENCY=3`, `DRAINER_WALL_CLOCK_BUDGET_MS=90000`, `DRAINER_LEASE_TTL_SECONDS=130` all unchanged (C not lowered to 2 per Card 2 audit recommendation #1 — out of scope); `classifierDrainerCore.ts` UNTOUCHED; cron `* * * * *`, MCP cap=5, A-G family scope, H/I/J `productionEnabled=false` all unchanged; no `package.json` change; no MCP server provider-path change. Test delta 18,674 → 18,713 tests (+39: 36 new in `archOneCardThreeTuningAndRollout.test.ts` covering MAX_ATTEMPTS=4 cap + provider_server_error schedule selection + parseRoutingPercentage edge cases + stableHashArgumentId determinism/distribution + shouldRouteToQueue percentage routing at 0/50/99/100 + smoke-tag override + master-flag gating; +3 updated retry-policy cases; ENQ-* tests INVERTED to expect single `.from().insert()` not 7 `.rpc()`; `uxOneOneFiveReadOnlyBoundary.test.ts` `DISPATCH_TAIL_TOKENS` extended with Card 3 identifiers). 592 suites pass; typecheck + lint clean. PR commits: `cb57aba` (implementation) + `6685817` (review APPROVE) + `68a146b` (one non-blocking doc-drift fix from adversarial Adv-1: stale module-header comment that referenced MAX_ATTEMPTS=3). **Smoke PASS verdict** at `docs/audits/ARCH-001-CARD3-SMOKE-2026-05-31.md`; review at `docs/reviews/ARCH-001-CARD3-SMOKE.md` (APPROVE + 5/5 metrics MATCH live DB + 0 doctrine/secret findings). Smoke re-bursted 16 args (1 canary + 3 waves × 5) via the throwaway harness `.claude-tmp/queue-smoke-submit.cjs` (reused unchanged from Card 2). 42 verification agents (9 + 10 + 12 + 8 + 1 + 2 adversarial). **Key deltas vs Card 2:** dead-letter rate 2.86% (3/105) → **0.893% (1/112)** (−1.97 pts; target <1% met); `skipped_single_flight` 112 → 17 across 16 args (**−84.82%**; 1.06/submit vs ~7/submit); retry recoveries 4 → 6 (4 at attempt 2 + 2 at attempt 3); dead-letter row lifetime 303-418s → **795s** (direct evidence the new [60,180,360]s schedule + MAX=4 were used); sustained RPM 0.79 → 1.44; peak-minute 30 → 44 (both under Tier-1 50 ceiling); submit p95 warm ~2.4s → 1.8s. 100% grid coverage (112/112 terminal); 0 H/I/J rows; 0 duplicate-success rows; 0 overlapping drain runs across 56 drains; 0 direct-dispatch leakage; 0 banned verdict tokens across 185 evidence spans; 0 auth-mismatch. The 1 Card 3 dead-letter is the long-tail `provider_server_error` class operating exactly as Card 2's audit predicted (persistent upstream transient that doesn't heal even with the longer retry budget). **Phase X closeout: `CLASSIFIER_QUEUE_ROUTING_ENABLED=false` via management CLI exit 0 — production returned to fully inert.** ARCH-001 architecture chain **CLOSED**. Staged percentage rollout (`CLASSIFIER_QUEUE_ROUTING_PERCENTAGE` 0 → 1 → 5 → 25 → 100) is a separate operational sequence (each step is one operator `secrets set` + observation window), NOT a follow-up Card. Family H planning is a separate decision; H/I/J remain frozen. See `docs/audits/ARCH-001-CARD3-SMOKE-2026-05-31.md` and `docs/reviews/ARCH-001-CARD3-TUNING-AND-ROLLOUT.md`. -->
<!-- Latest implementer card: ARCH-001 Card 2 — DRAINER + ENQUEUE behind smoke-only routing (the largest card in the chain; CALLs the Card-1/2A SQL functions, never redefines them). (1) NEW migration `supabase/migrations/20260528000023_arch_001_card2_enqueue_kick.sql` (WRITTEN, NOT APPLIED — operator `npx supabase db push --linked` after merge): the statement-level `AFTER INSERT` enqueue-KICK trigger `arch_001_kick_classifier_drainer` on `argument_machine_observation_runs` — fires ONE `net.http_post` to the drainer per INSERT statement that inserted ≥1 queue row (state=pending, family NOT NULL), guarded by `pg_try_advisory_xact_lock` so concurrent submits coalesce, reading the drainer URL + shared secret from Supabase Vault (`vault.decrypted_secrets`); an unseeded secret SKIPS the kick silently (the periodic tick is the safety net) and NEVER fails the INSERT (OPS-001 ordering). The 60s `cron.schedule` safety tick is a COMMENTED operator step (NOT scheduled in-migration — §A.11 step 1; scheduling before deploy+Vault would POST a null URL). No table/column/index/RLS/policy change; no CREATE EXTENSION (Card 1 did it); no secret literal; never logs URL/secret/Authorization/response. (2) NEW Edge Function `supabase/functions/classifier-drainer/` (`[functions.classifier-drainer] verify_jwt = false` in config.toml) — validates the shared secret (`CLASSIFIER_DRAIN_SHARED_SECRET` env, equal to the Vault value) on the Authorization header BEFORE any work (401 otherwise; never logged). Orchestration core `classifierDrainerCore.ts`: `acquire_drain_lease` (single-flight → audit `skipped_single_flight` + exit if not acquired), `reclaim_stale_leases()` FIRST, then a bounded loop — `claim_classifier_jobs(20, owner, '120 seconds')` → classify per family at ≥30s → on success `finalize_classifier_job(run_id, owner, 'succeeded','success', null,null,null, observations)`; on RETRYABLE failure a run-row-ONLY UPDATE to `retry_scheduled` (available_at=now()+backoff, lease cleared, guarded on lease_owner+state='leased' — NOT finalize, NOT an in-request wait); on terminal `finalize_classifier_job(..., 'failed_terminal'|'dead_letter','failed', reason, sub_reason, dl_reason, null)`. Bounded by C=3 / T=90s / batch-cap 20 / per-invocation cap 60; lease TTL 130s ≥ T(90)+call(30)+margin(10); `release_drain_lease` in a finally; one `classifier_drain_audit` row (no secret). Carry-forward #1: a FALSE `finalize_classifier_job` return = lost lease → counted as `jobsLostLease`, NOT success / NOT retry-as-succeeded / NOT double-counted (the job re-runs later). #6: consistent (terminal_state,status) pairs. `classifierDrainerClassify.ts` reuses `loadArgumentContext` + the request builder + the adapter (no persist — the job IS the run row) and maps positive observations to `finalize_classifier_job`'s `p_observations` jsonb `[{raw_key,family,confidence,evidence_span}]`. `classifierDrainerRetryPolicy.ts` (pure §A.9 table; MAX_ATTEMPTS=3 matching the Card-1 reclaim cap; backoff +30s/+120s; contract failures bounded to 1 retry then terminal, never dead_letter; config failures terminal). (3) ROUTING in submit-argument: `classifierQueueRouting.ts` `shouldRouteToQueue(arg, debate, enabled)` — pure, DEFAULT DISABLED (false for everything unless the operator master flag `CLASSIFIER_QUEUE_ROUTING_ENABLED='true'` AND the debate title starts with `[arch-001-queue-smoke]`); `enqueueClassifierJobs` calls `enqueue_classifier_job` per production family A–G (idempotent ON CONFLICT via index #5; H/I/J never enqueued). The dispatch site is now a MUTUALLY-EXCLUSIVE if/else (enqueue XOR `dispatchAutoTriggerForArgument` under waitUntil); the non-smoke ELSE arm is BYTE-UNCHANGED; submit stays nonblocking. (4) `findExistingRun` (autoTriggerDispatcher.ts) is now ACTIVE-QUEUE-AWARE: the SELECT also reads `state`; a cell in `pending`/`leased`/`retry_scheduled` is treated as in-flight (no re-dispatch) — the `status='success'` check is preserved (minimal+additive; rest of the direct path untouched). (5) ADAPTER timeout PARAMETERIZED (additive): `runBooleanObservationMcpAdapter` gains an optional `{ timeoutMs }`; the 15s submit-path default is UNCHANGED (byte-equal for the 1-arg caller); the drainer passes `DRAINER_MCP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS=30000` (≥ the 25s MCP model budget — corrects the §A.6 inversion). (6) Monitoring SQL + runbooks in the SIBLING dir `scripts/arch-001-card2-sql/` (NOT under scripts/ops/): `classifier-queue-monitoring.sql` (§A.10 read-only signals), `RUNBOOK.md` (prerequisites + alert thresholds + triage + rollback), `smoke-runbook.md` (canary → 3×5 burst → poll-to-settle → completeness + the two-psql-session reclaim-vs-finalize race). +124 tests (16 retry-policy + 21 routing-predicate pure unit; 34 drainer-core + 17 submit-routing + 21 drainer-edge + 21 kick-migration scan). Full suite 18552 -> 18676 tests; typecheck + lint clean. No Supabase write / no migration applied / no deploy by Claude; no MCP-server change; no Family H; no production rollout (Card 3). Operator prerequisites to run the smoke: apply the Card-2 migration, deploy the drainer, set `CLASSIFIER_DRAIN_SHARED_SECRET`, seed the two Vault secrets, schedule the 60s cron tick, restore `MCP_SERVER_MAX_PROVIDER_CONCURRENCY=5`, set `CLASSIFIER_QUEUE_ROUTING_ENABLED=true`, then run `scripts/arch-001-card2-sql/smoke-runbook.md`. See `docs/designs/ARCH-001-CARD2-DRAINER-ENQUEUE-intent.md` + the parent design §A.2/§A.4/§A.5/§A.6/§A.9/§A.11. -->
<!-- Latest implementer card: ARCH-001 Card 2A — ATOMIC QUEUE FINALIZER (migration only). New sequenced migration `supabase/migrations/20260528000022_arch_001_card2a_atomic_finalizer.sql` (WRITTEN, NOT APPLIED — operator applies via `npx supabase db push --linked` after merge, then runs the verify SQL). Defines ONLY `public.finalize_classifier_job(p_run_id, p_owner, p_terminal_state, p_status, p_failure_reason, p_failure_sub_reason, p_dead_letter_reason, p_observations) RETURNS boolean`, LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY INVOKER. CORRECTS parent design §A.3 non-atomic finalization (persistResults INSERT + a separate UPDATE runs = two PostgREST calls): the result-row INSERT and the run-row UPDATE now run in ONE plpgsql transaction (no COMMIT/autonomous/dblink in the body). Ownership guard FIRST — SELECT ... WHERE id=p_run_id AND lease_owner=p_owner AND state=leased FOR UPDATE; NOT FOUND -> RETURN false (no results, nothing updated) so a stale / wrong / reclaimed owner cannot finalize. Success (succeeded): INSERT result rows from p_observations (debate_id/argument_id/schema_version read from the locked run row; column mapping mirrors persistResults exactly) with ON CONFLICT (run_id, raw_key) DO NOTHING (column inference against the named non-partial constraint amor_unique_run_rawkey, NOT ON CONSTRAINT) -> duplicate-safe on retry; then UPDATE run state=succeeded/status, completed_at=now(), lease cleared; RETURN true. Terminal failure (failed_terminal | dead_letter): no result rows; UPDATE run state/status/completed_at/failure_reason/failure_sub_reason, dead_letter_reason only when dead_letter, lease cleared; RETURN true. Invalid p_terminal_state raises; retry_scheduled NOT handled here. No table/column/index/RLS/policy change; ONE function + ONE COMMENT. persistenceWriter.ts / autoTriggerDispatcher.ts / MCP untouched. Tests: +62 (40 migration shape incl. atomicity + ownership-guard-first + ON CONFLICT inference + persistResults column parity; 22 verify-script). Operator post-merge verify SQL `scripts/arch-001-card2a-sql/verify-finalize-classifier-job.sql` (BEGIN...ROLLBACK, reuses an existing argument): Cases 1-5 LIVE (success / duplicate-safe / wrong-owner / stale-owner / terminal-failure), Case 6 (direct-dispatch unchanged) shape-only. Full suite 18490 -> 18552 tests; typecheck + lint clean. No Supabase write by Claude; migration NOT applied. -->
<!-- Latest implementer card: ARCH-001 Card 1 — DATABASE SUBSTRATE ONLY (civil-discourse classifier queue). New sequenced migration `supabase/migrations/20260528000021_arch_001_classifier_queue_substrate.sql` (WRITTEN, NOT APPLIED — operator applies via `npx supabase db push --linked` after merge; the two `CREATE EXTENSION pg_cron`/`pg_net` statements are HALT-class because `max_worker_processes=6` is tight). OPS-001 four-class ordering. Extends `public.argument_machine_observation_runs` to BE the job row (design §A.3 Option 2): 9 additive `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` columns (`family`, `state` 6-value CHECK DEFAULT `'succeeded'`, `attempt_count`, `available_at`, `lease_expires_at`, `lease_owner`, `failure_sub_reason`, `dead_letter_reason`, `last_attempt_at`), `ALTER COLUMN status DROP NOT NULL` (existing CHECK unchanged — NULL satisfies it; a pre-terminal queue row carries `status` NULL), one-shot `state` backfill (`'succeeded'` where `status='success'`, else `'failed_terminal'`). Four partial indexes #4–#7 every one predicated `... AND family IS NOT NULL` so historical NULL-family rows are excluded: #4 `amor_one_success_per_cell_idx` UNIQUE `(argument_id, family, run_mode, schema_version) WHERE state='succeeded'`; #5 `amor_one_active_job_per_cell_idx` UNIQUE same cols `WHERE state IN ('pending','leased','retry_scheduled')`; #6 `amor_claimable_idx (state, available_at) WHERE state IN ('pending','retry_scheduled')`; #7 `amor_stale_lease_idx (state, lease_expires_at) WHERE state='leased'`. Two new service-role-only tables (`classifier_drain_lock`, `classifier_drain_audit`) — RLS ENABLED, NO client write policy, NO SELECT policy (operator reads via service-role monitoring SQL §A.10; `'skipped_single_flight'` outcome value fixed). Claim/lease/recovery + the idempotent-enqueue form as SQL FUNCTIONS (NEVER PostgREST chains): `claim_classifier_jobs(batch_size, owner, lease)` (CTE `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` → UPDATE to `leased` + attempt bump → RETURNING), `acquire_drain_lease(owner, ttl)` (`ON CONFLICT (lock_key) DO UPDATE … WHERE classifier_drain_lock.expires_at < now()` — only steal an EXPIRED lease), `release_drain_lease(owner)` (own-only DELETE, returns row count), `reclaim_stale_leases()` (expired `leased` → `retry_scheduled`, or `dead_letter` at `attempt_count >= 3`), `enqueue_classifier_job(...)` (DEFINED + TESTED here, WIRED in Card 2) using the LOAD-BEARING `ON CONFLICT (argument_id, family, run_mode, schema_version) WHERE <index #5 predicate> DO NOTHING` — column-inference + predicate, NOT `ON CONSTRAINT` (which cannot target a partial index). No secret literal (Vault drainer credential is a runbook NOTE only). NO `cron.schedule`, NO `net.http_post`, NO enqueue wiring, NO drainer Edge Function, NO feature flag, NO submit-path routing, NO reader change (`autoTriggerDispatcher.ts` / `machineObservationPersistenceQuery.ts` UNTOUCHED — reader fixes LOGGED for Card 2), NO MCP-server / Family H / prompt / taxonomy / schema-mirror / `package.json` change. Docker was unavailable in the implementer env, so the 6 behavioral cases (index #4/#5 violations, claim/SKIP-LOCKED, lease race, reclaim cap, pending-row compatibility) run under the operator's POST-MERGE verification via `scripts/arch-001-card1-sql/verify-classifier-queue-substrate.sql` (BEGIN … ROLLBACK; reuses one existing argument; secret-free; two-session concurrency procedure documented in Section F); the Jest suites lock the migration TEXT/SHAPE + function definitions + the ON CONFLICT form. **18490 tests / 583 suites passing** (+77 across `archOneClassifierQueueSubstrateMigration.test.ts`, `archOneClassifierQueueFunctionsShape.test.ts`, `archOneClassifierQueueVerifyScript.test.ts`). Typecheck + lint clean. Migration NOT applied. -->
<!-- Latest implementer card: OPS-MCP-SERVER-CAPACITY-INVESTIGATION (#371) — per-isolate bounded provider-concurrency cap in `mcp-server/` (server-cap ONLY: NO retry-after protocol, NO Edge change, NO Family H, NO prompt/taxonomy/family-key/schema-mirror/Source-6/Edge-flag/audit-lint change, NO migration, NO `package.json`/`deno.json` change). RCA: `mcp-server/lib/anthropicCall.ts` `callAnthropic` fans out UNTHROTTLED concurrent Anthropic fetches (1 fetch/call, no semaphore/queue/retry); a cross-argument burst (~5 args × up-to-2 families = ~10 concurrent Edge→MCP requests) → ~10 concurrent unthrottled provider calls → 429 (`rate_limited`) / 25s timeout (`model_timeout`) / 5xx (`api_error`) `{isError}` envelopes. The Edge `MAX_AUTO_TRIGGER_CONCURRENT_FAMILIES=2` is per-ARGUMENT so it cannot bound cross-arg fan-in — the throttle must live at the server. NEW pure module `mcp-server/lib/providerConcurrency.ts`: `createBoundedSemaphore(cap)` (counting semaphore — FIFO queue, direct slot-handoff on release, `inFlight`/`waiting` getters, IDEMPOTENT `ReleaseFn` via a captured `released` flag so a double-release is a no-op, `RangeError` for cap<1/non-int), `readEnvMaxProviderConcurrency()` (env `MCP_SERVER_MAX_PROVIDER_CONCURRENCY`, default `DEFAULT_MAX_PROVIDER_CONCURRENCY=5`, validated finite int ≥1 else default), exported `RESOLVED_MAX_PROVIDER_CONCURRENCY` + the PER-ISOLATE singleton `providerConcurrencyGate`. Gate core is PURE (no Deno.env/fetch/console/Date.now in acquire/release/handoff — the env read is isolated in the reader; source-scan-enforced). `callAnthropic` now does `const release = await providerConcurrencyGate.acquire();` AFTER the `key_missing` early-return (missing key consumes no slot) and BEFORE the fetch, wrapping the entire fetch + status-branches + drain + `response.json()` + extract/parse + success region in `try { … } finally { release(); }` so EVERY return path releases. Headers/body/signal/log events/every `reason`/every return shape are BYTE-EQUAL (the only behavioral diff is the wrap + import; deno fmt also reflowed one pre-existing ternary — formatting-only). TOPOLOGY HONESTY: per-isolate cap, NOT a global cap (the hosted server may run multiple isolates); the post-merge smoke (separate SPEND gate) decides whether per-isolate suffices, else `OPS-MCP-SERVER-RETRY-AFTER-PROTOCOL` is filed. NO unbounded queue (the waiter queue is naturally bounded by the isolate's in-flight request count; no wait-timeout that would manufacture failures). **mcp-server Deno tests: 1022 → 1041 (+19); 0 failed.** `deno check` + `deno fmt --check` clean on the 4 touched files; existing `anthropicNoLogging` + family A–G + parity + toolDispatch suites stay green. NO Anthropic/xAI/X API/Supabase write by Claude; the change is entirely server-side Deno (`mcp-server/`). Files: NEW `mcp-server/lib/providerConcurrency.ts`, MODIFIED `mcp-server/lib/anthropicCall.ts`, NEW `mcp-server/tests/providerConcurrency.test.ts` + `mcp-server/tests/anthropicCallProviderCap.test.ts`. See `docs/designs/OPS-MCP-SERVER-CAPACITY-INVESTIGATION.md`. -->

<!-- Latest implementer card: OPS-MCP-RESULT-VALIDATION-BURST-HARDENING — Phase 1 (TYPE) (the typed validation sub-reason at the failure point + the RETURN/log surface + the sanitizer; NON-SPEND, type-only, ADDITIVE, NO migration / NO DB column / NO retry change / NO concurrency change / NO Family H). Origin: the OPS-MCP-AUTO-TRIGGER-PARALLELIZATION smoke (PARTIAL) found 4/35 family runs `mcp_validation_failed` under a burst (peak 7–8 concurrency, ~4–8s → result-side). The adapter used to DISCARD the validator's granular `{reason, details}` and collapse everything to a single opaque `validation_failed`. Phase 1 stops the discard. NEW pure module `supabase/functions/_shared/booleanObservations/booleanObservationFailureSubreason.ts`: exports `BooleanObservationFailureSubreason` (15-value controlled union — the durable `request_`/`response_`/`provider_` split), `ALL_BOOLEAN_OBSERVATION_FAILURE_SUBREASONS`, `BooleanObservationFailureDetail`, `mapToFailureSubreason(adapterReason, validatorReason?)` (pure, total, exhaustive `never`-checked switch — validator `not_json→response_not_json` / `wrong_schema_version→response_wrong_schema_version` / `wrong_shape→response_wrong_shape` / `missing_required_field→response_missing_required_field` / `flag_count_too_high→response_flag_count_too_high` / `duplicate_node_id→unknown`; adapter `parse_failure→response_not_json` / `network_error→provider_network_error` / `rate_limited→provider_rate_limited` / `api_error→provider_api_error`; `url_missing`/`token_missing`→`undefined`), and `buildFailureDetail(input)` — the migration-grade SANITIZER (cdiscourse-doctrine §6): named-args-only allowlist (`validatorReason/path/expected/receivedType/receivedKeys/checkedRawKey/schemaVersion/family`; NO free-text/`extra`/`message`/`details` entry point), `receivedType` = `typeof` (never the value), `receivedKeys` capped ≤32 × ≤64 chars identifier-shaped only, `path` static-allowlist-gated, `checkedRawKey` registry-validated, defense-in-depth secret-shape scrub (regexes assembled from fragments — `sk`+`-ant-`/`xai`+`-`/`sb`+`_secret_`/JWT/`Bea`+`rer`/`Authorization`/`SERVICE_ROLE`), 2000-char serialized cap with graceful degradation (drop receivedKeys → truncate → `{validatorReason,schemaVersion}`). Zero `Deno.`/`fetch`/`console`; type-only import of `McpBooleanObservationParseFailureReason` (the schema file is NOT modified — HALT-7). MODIFIED: `booleanObservationMcpAdapterCore.ts` (the `unavailable` `BooleanObservationAdapterResult` variant gains optional `subReason?`+`detail?`; union byte-equal); `booleanObservationMcpAdapter.ts` (each unavailable return populates `subReason` (+`detail` where a safe field exists) per the per-site table; the validation_failed collapse delegates to `mapToFailureSubreason('validation_failed', parsed.reason)` + `buildFailureDetail({validatorReason: parsed.reason, schemaVersion, received: extracted, receivedKeysFrom: extracted})` — RE-DERIVED from `extracted`, **`parsed.details` is NEVER read**; the `:168` schema-version guard sets `response_wrong_schema_version` directly; `reason:'validation_failed'` PRESERVED at BOTH collapse sites; `url_missing`/`token_missing` byte-equal, no subReason); `classifyArgumentCore.ts` (`PerArgumentSummary` gains optional `failureSubReason?`+`failureDetail?`, set ONLY in the unavailable branch from `adapterResult.subReason`/`.detail`; `failureReason` = `unavailableReasonToFailureReason(...)` byte-equal; success/persist/argument_not_found set neither); `autoTriggerLog.ts` (`AutoTriggerLogFields` gains optional `failure_sub_reason?`+`failure_detail?`; `emitAutoTriggerLog` body unchanged); `autoTriggerDispatcher.ts` (ONLY the terminal-failure `emitAutoTriggerLog` site passes `failure_sub_reason`/`failure_detail` off the `PerArgumentSummary`; `RETRYABLE_FAILURE_REASONS`/`MAX_ATTEMPTS`/retry loop/bounded-concurrency dispatch/`findExistingRun`/idempotency/return type all byte-equal — HALT-1/HALT-5). **HALT-9 preserved: `reason:'validation_failed'` + `failure_reason='mcp_validation_failed'` still emit.** Adapter never reads `parsed.details`; schema file + its 2 mirrors + migrations + `familyRegistry.ts` + `booleanObservationRequestBuilder.ts` + `boundedConcurrencyRunner.ts` + `autoTriggerConcurrency.ts` + `package.json` NOT in the diff. NEW tests: `__tests__/booleanObservationFailureSubreason.test.ts` (34 behavioral — mapping coverage incl. the `duplicate_node_id→unknown` edge, vocabulary integrity + the cdiscourse-doctrine §1 verdict-free ban-list, the HOSTILE-fixture sanitizer battery proving every banned class — Bearer/sk-ant/sb_secret/xai/JWT/Authorization/5000-char body — is dropped, the 2000-char cap with receivedKeys-dropped-first, identifier-shaped key caps, and receivedType-never-carries-a-value) via new bridge `__tests__/_helpers/booleanObservationFailureSubreasonDeno.ts`; `__tests__/mcpOneTwoOneCFailureSubreasonThreading.test.ts` (7 — behavioral duration-discriminator on the pure map + source-scan of the PerArgumentSummary / AutoTriggerLogFields / dispatcher surface; the Deno adapter + classifyArgumentCore are NOT Jest-loadable per the repo's coverage wall, so the wiring is source-scanned). EDITED source-scans: `mcpOneTwoOneCEdgeAdapterSourceScan.test.ts` (+SCAN-21..25 — adapter imports the two helpers, carries `reason:'validation_failed'`, never reads `parsed.details` in executable code, the new module has no console/`Deno.`/fetch + no secret-shaped literal via the extended SCAN-17 loop + the SCAN-19 fence); `mcpOneTwoOneCAutoTriggerFailureMode.test.ts` (+FAIL-23..25 — the unavailable branch reads/sets the new fields; FAIL-1..7 failure_reason mapping unchanged). <!-- Latest implementer card: OPS-MCP-RESULT-VALIDATION-BURST-HARDENING — Phase 3 (FIX) (#365). Detects the operator-hosted MCP server's OWN `{ isError, reason, path, detail }` error envelope on the EXTRACTED response BEFORE the MCP-021A schema validator and types it correctly. Phase 2 reproduced the ~11% production burst live and proved EVERY failure was this envelope returned under concurrent load and MIS-TYPED by the Edge adapter as `response_wrong_schema_version` (it lacks a `schemaVersion`, so the validator's step-2 rejected it). Two production-code edits, both under `supabase/functions/_shared/booleanObservations/`: (1) `booleanObservationMcpAdapter.ts` gains a pure, total `isServerErrorEnvelope(extracted)` guard (STRICT `extracted.isError === true` — truthiness is forbidden; `isError:1`/`'true'`/an object must NOT trip it) + a detection `return` block inserted BETWEEN the `extracted === null` guard and `parseMcpBooleanObservationResponse(extracted)`; the block returns `{ kind:'unavailable', reason:'api_error', subReason:'provider_server_error', detail: buildFailureDetail({serverReason, path, receivedKeysFrom: extracted}) }`. Because `api_error` maps (UNCHANGED) to `failure_reason='mcp_api_error'`, which is ALREADY in `RETRYABLE_FAILURE_REASONS`, the server-side transient self-heals via the EXISTING bounded retry (MAX_ATTEMPTS=2 = one retry; backoff 2s/8s; concurrency 2) with ZERO dispatch-config edit. (2) `booleanObservationFailureSubreason.ts` (pure) adds `provider_server_error` to the union + `ALL_…` (provider/transport group, 15→16) and a `serverReason?: string` field to `FailureDetailInput` + `BooleanObservationFailureDetail`, sanitized through the EXISTING `looksSecret` scrub + 200-char cap + defense-in-depth delete + tier-2 re-truncate — UNTRUSTED server input forwarded-with-scrub exactly like `expected`/`schemaVersion`; the raw envelope `detail` blob is NEVER forwarded (no `detail:` arg exists). `provider_api_error` (HTTP-level) and `provider_server_error` (body-level `{isError}`) stay distinct siblings. NO dispatcher/core/log/schema-mirror/parser edit (the fix detects the envelope BEFORE the parser — it never teaches the parser about `isError`); `autoTriggerDispatcher.ts` + `classifyArgumentCore.ts` + `mcpBooleanObservationSchema.ts` + its 2 mirrors are byte-equal. NO migration, NO DB column, NO prompt/taxonomy/family-key/Source-6/flag/audit-lint/package.json change; concurrency stays 2; Family H frozen. `provider_server_error`/`serverReason` are transport/server-side facts (ban-list test extended to 16 values — verdict-free); operator/diagnostic only, never user-facing. New `__tests__/mcpOneTwoOneCServerErrorEnvelope.test.ts` (SEV-1..15: detection-precedes-parser ordering + the bug reproduced behaviorally; `provider_server_error` typed; `api_error` carrier; retryable; recover-to-success composition; bounded-retry terminal typed+sanitized; ordinary `response_*` unchanged + the strict-`===true` discriminator; HOSTILE serverReason wall). Edited `booleanObservationFailureSubreason.test.ts` (VOCAB-1 → 16-value ordered list + serverReason sanitizer cases + 16-value ban-list), `mcpOneTwoOneCEdgeAdapterSourceScan.test.ts` (SCAN-26..28), `mcpOneTwoOneCAutoTriggerFailureMode.test.ts` (FAIL-26 RETRYABLE_FAILURE_REASONS byte-equal). +26 tests (18387 → 18413; 579 → 580 suites). Typecheck + lint clean. No Anthropic / xAI / X API / Supabase write by Claude in this stage. Auto-deploys on merge (Supabase GitHub integration redeploys Edge Functions; no migration → no db push). Phase 4 (gated SPEND verify) is a separate operator-approved step. See `docs/designs/OPS-MCP-RESULT-VALIDATION-BURST-HARDENING-PHASE3.md` + `-intent.md`. -->

NO migration; NO Supabase write by Claude; NO service-role; runtime change ships via the Supabase GitHub merge auto-deploy; the latency lib is a local operator script. Faithful G-replica of the shipped Family F doctrine-risk card. Converts the Family G (`resolution_progress`) doctrine proof — Card 1's Phase 4b resolution↔verdict existential (Fixture C input named "won"/"lost"/"beat me" three times; the `concedes_broader_point` classifier's persisted `evidence_span` anchored "I withdraw the broad claim and stand on the narrow scope only" and did NOT echo any verdict word) — from operator discipline into mechanical L5 enforcement. ONE pure-DATA edit: appends `resolution_progress`, `family_g`, and `concedes_broader_point` to the `DOCTRINE_RISK_FAMILIES` Set in `scripts/ops/audit-lint-rules.cjs` (after F's 3) so L5 mechanically requires persisted `evidence_span` inspection for any future doctrine-risk Family G audit — the same teeth already enforced for Families E + F. `family_g` is the LOAD-BEARING alias: `detectFamily()` emits `family_g` for a `MCP-SERVER-NNN-FAMILY-G` title (`mapFamilyLetterToName` has no G case → default branch → `family_g`); adding only `resolution_progress` would be a silent no-op for the real G docs. `mapFamilyLetterToName` (LOGIC) is NOT touched; `audit-lint.mjs` and `audit-lint-lib.cjs` are NOT touched. A.2 outcome = DATA-ONLY (zero HALT triggers): the real on-main G PARTIAL smoke (`MCP-SERVER-008-FAMILY-G-SMOKE-2026-05-29.md` @ 1c19d11) still lints EXIT 0 (it names `evidence_span`, so `hasInspection` is true and L5 does not fire — consistent-PARTIAL preserved by MENTION, not by verdict-awareness). 3 NEW fixtures: `family-g-original-PARTIAL.md` (static copy of on-main G PARTIAL @ 1c19d11, body byte-identical → exit 0), `family-g-amendment-PASS.md` (HAND-AUTHORED representative G hosted-completion amendment with persisted `evidence_span` readback; real one operator-deferred → exit 0), and `family-g-IMPROPER-PASS-no-evidence-span.md` (SYNTHETIC G-amendment shape with every `evidence_span` trigger stripped → exit 1, finding [L5] ONLY — the TEETH proof; negative control: WITHOUT `family_g` it wrongly passes exit 0, WITH it fails). The 7 existing audit-lint fixtures (4 hardening + 3 F) stay byte-equal and still produce exits 1,0,0,0,0,0,1; the 10-fixture matrix direct-invokes to 1,0,0,0,0,0,1,0,0,1; the real on-main G smoke lints exit 0. **opsAuditLint suite: 147 → 158 (+11), EXIT 0** (3 membership tests, 1 E+F-preserved additive guard, 1 `detectFamily → family_g` A.1-trap pin, 3 L5 firing/non-firing tests for `family_g` incl. a consistent-PARTIAL-for-G case, 3 fixture self-validation incl. the L5-only teeth assertion); `FIXTURE_FILES` 7→10 and `fixture count is exactly 7`→`10`. typecheck + lint clean. No migration, no Edge Function, no DB, no `.mjs`/lib logic, no `package.json`, no `.github/workflows`, no Anthropic/xAI/X, no service-role, no client AI. The G production flip is Card 3 — NOT started. Post-merge operator/implementer authors `docs/audits/OPS-MCP-AUDIT-LINT-RULES-FAMILY-G-DOCTRINE-RISK-SMOKE-2026-05-29.md` (5-phase smoke per intent §8; must carry `Audit-Lint: v1` and self-lint clean). See `docs/designs/OPS-MCP-AUDIT-LINT-RULES-FAMILY-G-DOCTRINE-RISK.md` + `docs/ops/AUDIT-LINT.md` § "Adding a doctrine-risk family". -->

<!-- Latest implementer card: MCP-SERVER-006-FAMILY-E (Argument Scheme Boolean Observation Classifier — 16-key uniform ai_classifier per design §1; 5th MCP server family alongside A/B/C/D; admin_validation-only posture per Edge familyRegistry entry at familyRegistry.ts:89-93; Stage 2B NOT REQUIRED — uniform ai_classifier, 16/16 keys, MAX_TOKENS=1500 unchanged from Family A/B/C, no compound-key schema change). Six commits land the full Family E implementation. Server-side mirror under `mcp-server/lib/`: `familyEKeys.ts` (FAMILY_E_RAW_KEYS=16 Walton (1995, 2008) argumentation schemes [causal_reasoning_present, analogy_reasoning_present, example_reasoning_present, authority_reasoning_present, consequence_reasoning_present, principle_reasoning_present, definition_reasoning_present, classification_reasoning_present, precedent_reasoning_present, means_end_reasoning_present, tradeoff_reasoning_present, abductive_explanation_present, exception_reasoning_present, slippery_slope_reasoning_present, cost_benefit_reasoning_present, risk_reasoning_present] + 16 verbose FAMILY_E_PROMPT_ENTRIES with per-key doctrine guards + FAMILY_E_CLASSIFIER_SET_VERSION='family-e-v1'). `familyEPrompt.ts` mirrors Family A/B/C/D's 7 absolute rules verbatim, adds scheme-as-descriptive doctrine framing, and pins 3 doctrine-risk anchors in the system prompt: (1) slippery_slope_reasoning_present is a SCHEME, never a fallacy — output MUST NOT call this fallacy/fallacious/weak/invalid/bad reasoning/logical error/flawed/wrong/proof of anything; (2) abductive_explanation_present (Peirce: inference to best explanation) is a SCHEME, not a fallacy; (3) analogy_reasoning_present (Walton's analogy scheme) is a SCHEME, not a fallacy. The system prompt explicitly names the corresponding Family F critical questions (consequence_probability_unclear, analogy_mapping_missing) as the place where scheme-quality is probed; Family E only detects the PATTERN. User prompt includes the amendment §2.2 adversarial-fallacy-word handling instruction ("If the move's text itself contains the word 'fallacy' ... your output evidenceSpan MUST NOT echo the fallacy framing — anchor on the structural pattern"). FAMILY_E_MAX_TOKENS=1500 (matches Family A/B/C; NO bump from the Family D 1800 envelope per design §2; ~60-token headroom on 16 keys × ~85 tokens). FAMILY_E_TEMPERATURE=0; FAMILY_E_MAX_BODY_FIELD_LEN=8000. `familyEAnthropic.ts` wraps shared callAnthropic with FAMILY_E_MAX_TOKENS=1500 + classify_argument_boolean_observations log tag. `familyEBanListScan.ts` is the existential doctrine-binding layer: stacks the shared DOCTRINE_BAN_PATTERNS with FAMILY_E_BAN_PATTERNS (12 amendment §3 BINDING tokens — fallacy/fallacious/invalid/flawed/wrong/weak argument/invalid argument/bad reasoning/flawed reasoning/logical error/informal fallacy/proof of). Family E-specific patterns are scoped to THIS file only; shared DOCTRINE_BAN_PATTERNS in doctrineBanList.ts is byte-equal preserved (Family-E-scope keeps "invalid" / "wrong" usable in Family B disputes_validity / Family C acknowledges_misread evidenceSpans). `familyEFixtureProvider.ts` loads the canonical Family E response from `classify-argument-boolean-observations.family-e-canonical-response.json` (MCP_SERVER_USE_FIXTURE_PROVIDER=true). Registry registration: ONE-line addition to `familyRegistryInit.ts` registers `argument_scheme` after Family D; `getSupportedFamilies()` returns ['parent_relation', 'disagreement_axis', 'misunderstanding_repair', 'evidence_source_chain', 'argument_scheme'] in declaration order. Dispatcher: `classifyArgumentBooleanObservations.ts` `pickFamilyProviders` adds the `argument_scheme` branch routing to runAnthropicFamilyEClassifier / loadFixtureFamilyEPacket / scanFamilyEBooleanResponseForBanList; tool description updated to advertise Family E + the 16 Walton schemes with explicit slippery-slope reference. Family A/B/C/D lib + fixture + dispatch code all byte-equal preserved. NEW audit template `docs/audits/MCP-SERVER-005-FAMILY-D-SMOKE-template.md` for operator-driven 8-phase smoke checklist post-merge (Phase 1 pre-flight; Phase 2 local Deno regression ~614; Phase 3 hosted MCP smoke 15/15; Phase 4 Edge admin_validation against 3 seeded args with raw_keys ⊆ Subset assertion + 0-positives PARTIAL acceptable per Decision 9 + doctrine-risk-key evidenceSpan spot-checks; Phase 5 unsupported E/F/G/H/I/J rejection regression; Phase 6 targeted Jest+Deno regression; Phase 7 OPS observations including 4-families-operational + token-budget observation + doctrine-risk-key calibration + anti-amplification calibration; Phase 8 verdict + authorization grants on PASS). Tests: **mcp-server Deno 467 → 614 tests / 0 failed (+147 across this card)**, **Jest 17,924 → 17,932 tests passing (+8 in C6 for Edge Family D registry test)**, typecheck + lint clean. Local fixture-mode smoke gate not exercised in implementer session (operator runs Phase 1 in post-merge smoke). **All 24 conditional HALT triggers from intent brief CLEAN**: Card 1 SMOKE PASS verified at ac66b2e; Family D rawKey list = 19 ai_classifier Subset verified against upstream familyD.ts; no Family E-J registration; Family A/B/C lib byte-equal preserved (no changes to familyA*.ts / familyB*.ts / familyC*.ts); unsupported_family rejection envelope for E/F/G/H/I/J intact; schema mirror response shape unchanged (no compound keys); no new taxonomy keys; MCP schema version unchanged; no Family A/B/C prompt changes; no client-side MCP call; no MCP bearer / Anthropic / service-role secret exposure (callAnthropic + log helpers preserve secrets); no raw argument body / prompt / response / bearer / API key in any log line; Subset filter matches Stage 2B exactly (19 keys; the 8 deterministic excluded); Full-27 path NOT chosen (Subset locked by Stage 2B); MAX_TOKENS=1800 within Stage 2B approval; NO compound-key response shape; Edge Family D entry productionEnabled=false (Jest FD-2 + existing FR-7 assert it); prompt frames evidence-source moves as descriptive only (no anecdote-as-weakness, no burden-as-verdict, no evidence-gap-as-failure framing); no verdict / winner / fallacy tokens in user-facing strings; working tree at PR-create time contains only the 10 documented operator-territory files; test delta +155 well under +300 ceiling. Doctrine ban-list scan CLEAN across all new source + production fixture files (the intentionally-malformed family-d-malformed-response.json and the intentionally-ban-list family-d-ban-list-response.json are excluded as negative-test fixtures). Client-secret boundary scan CLEAN: zero match on MCP_URL / MCP_TOKEN / SEMANTIC_REFEREE_MCP / EXPO_PUBLIC_*MCP / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in src/ or app/. MCP-018/021A/021B/021C-EDGE boundary diff: 0 lines changed. Family A/B/C `mcp-server/lib/` boundary: 0 lines changed. Root package boundary: 0 lines changed in package.json or package-lock.json. **Operator follow-up** (per `docs/audits/MCP-SERVER-005-FAMILY-D-SMOKE-template.md` 8-phase plan): no migration, no Edge deploy required — Deno Deploy auto-deploys the post-merge build and Supabase Edge Function auto-deploys the byte-equal Edge layer. (1) Phase 1 pre-flight; (2) Phase 2 local Deno regression `cd mcp-server && deno test --allow-net --allow-env --allow-read` expecting ~614 / 0; (3) Phase 3 hosted MCP smoke `bash scripts/mcp-server-001-smoke.sh --base-url https://cdiscourse-mcp-server.civildiscourse.deno.net --token <hosted-bearer-token>` expecting 15/15 PASS; (4) Phase 4 Edge admin_validation smoke with requestedFamilies=['evidence_source_chain'] against the 3 seeded args from Card 1's smoke (Decision 9: 0-positives is PARTIAL-acceptable; pipeline verification + raw_keys ⊆ Subset is the binding PASS gate); (5) Phase 5 unsupported E/F/G/H/I/J rejection regression (6 envelopes); (6) Phase 6 targeted Jest + Deno regression; (7) Phase 7 OPS observations (4 families now operational on hosted MCP — Family A+B+C production + Family D admin_validation); (8) Phase 8 verdict — on PASS, `MCP-021C-EDGE-FAMILY-D-ENABLE` AUTHORIZED to design (production-mode flip for Family D) + `MCP-SERVER-006-FAMILY-E` AUTHORIZED to begin + `OPS-MCP-OBSERVABILITY-FAMILY-D-COVERAGE` recommended for 4-family-state observability. **Operator-deferred decisions** (per design §12 Decision provenance ledger): (a) post-PASS revisit of the 8-deferred deterministic keys — file `MCP-021C-EDGE-FAMILY-D-DETERMINISTIC-KEYS` now or defer to post-Family-D-smoke OPS review; (b) whether the +300 MAX_TOKENS bump produces measurable truncation under real corpus load (telemetry from Phase 4 + future admin_validation calls informs); (c) whether the doctrine-risk-key real-corpus calibration on anecdote_used / burden_request_present / evidence_gap_present produces any framing that should trigger Family-D-specific ban patterns (future OPS observability card); (d) whether to file `OPS-MCP-OBSERVABILITY-FAMILY-D-COVERAGE` for 4-family state observability. See `docs/designs/MCP-SERVER-005-FAMILY-D.md` § "Stage 2B" for the operator-approved scope + recorded binding decisions. -->
<!-- Latest implementer card: MCP-SERVER-001 (Operator-hosted MCP Server Foundation and Contract Compatibility — first of the MCP-SERVER-* track, distinct from MCP-021; one new co-located server tree at `mcp-server/` + one local smoke script + one runbook + one audit template + one CDiscourse-side presence test; NO new Supabase migration, NO new Supabase Edge Function modification, NO client-side AI call, NO service-role usage, NO new dependency in root package.json, NO change to MCP-018/021A/021B/021C trees, NO change to root visual primitives or design tokens). New directory `mcp-server/` houses a Deno-based HTTP server: `main.ts` entry, `server.ts` routing skeleton with `runAuthGate` middleware shared between `/mcp` and `/mcp/adapter-compat`, `bootstrap.ts` side-effect route registration (avoids circular import), `routes/health.ts` unauthenticated capability endpoint, `routes/mcp.ts` official JSON-RPC 2.0 endpoint (initialize / notifications/initialized / tools/list / tools/call / ping methods; GET returns 405 by design), `routes/adapterCompat.ts` simplified `{tool, input}` envelope endpoint (the shape MCP-018 + MCP-021C-EDGE currently send; preserves Option C from design §5). Common lib: `lib/jsonRpc.ts` (envelope helpers + parse + standard error codes), `lib/auth.ts` (bearer middleware with constant-time compare; envelope shaper for both endpoint shapes), `lib/origin.ts` (allow-list with empty-list = open mode), `lib/protocolVersion.ts` (`2025-11-25` targeted, echoed in response headers; warns on older known + unknown versions), `lib/logging.ts` (structured JSON sink with secret redaction — Bearer, sk-ant-, JWT-shape, and a forbidden-field-name list always redact to `[REDACTED]`), `lib/responseHelpers.ts` (Content-Type + MCP-Protocol-Version + X-Request-Id headers on every response), `lib/toolRegistry.ts` (`buildInitializeResult` + `buildToolsListResult`), `lib/toolDispatch.ts` (`invokeToolByName` switch dispatched by both endpoints). Two tools registered: `classify_semantic_move` (the verbatim `MCP_CLASSIFY_TOOL_NAME` constant from `supabase/functions/_shared/semanticReferee/mcpAdapterCore.ts:38`) and `classify_argument_boolean_observations` (the verbatim `MCP_BOOLEAN_OBSERVATION_TOOL_NAME` constant from `supabase/functions/_shared/booleanObservations/booleanObservationMcpAdapterCore.ts:44-45`). `classify_semantic_move` is fully implemented: input validation via `lib/semanticRefereePacketSchema.ts` (pure-TS structural-subset validator — no zod; enforces additionalProperties: false, snake_case reason codes, scoreHint range, enum membership, body length bounds, and runs the doctrine ban-list across every string field via `lib/doctrineBanList.ts`); seed prompt assembly via `lib/seedPrompt.ts` (mirror of `supabase/functions/_shared/semanticReferee/seedPrompt.ts` + `anthropicClassifierCore.ts` — parity test `tests/seedPromptParity.test.ts` enforces byte-equal absolute rules + 35-id catalog v1); Anthropic call via `lib/anthropic.ts` (Messages API client; ANTHROPIC_API_KEY read at call time; AbortSignal.timeout 25s; NEVER logs Authorization / x-api-key / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY — source-text scan in `tests/anthropicNoLogging.test.ts` enforces it); fixture-provider path via `lib/fixtureProvider.ts` (opt-in via `MCP_SERVER_USE_FIXTURE_PROVIDER=true` env flag for offline smoke). Tool returns BOTH `content[text]` (JSON-serialized packet for MCP-018 back-compat parsing) AND `structuredContent` (typed packet for modern MCP clients) per Decision 12. `classify_argument_boolean_observations` is SCAFFOLDED: registered, `tools/list` returns its metadata, invocation returns the documented `{isError: true, content: [{type: "text", text: "Tool scaffolded for MCP-SERVER-002; not yet implemented"}], structuredContent: {reason: "not_implemented", scaffoldedFor: "MCP-SERVER-002"}}` envelope. The scaffolded handler does NOT import anthropic, does NOT reference `api.anthropic.com`, does NOT call `runAnthropic*`, does NOT call `fetch()` — verified by source-text scan in `tests/classifyArgumentBooleanObservations.test.ts`. Tools / lib / routes / fixtures all under `mcp-server/`; tests under `mcp-server/tests/` run via `deno task test` and DO NOT count against CDiscourse Jest budget (Jest testMatch is `**/__tests__/**/*.test.(ts|tsx)` — the `tests/` directory does not match). New `scripts/mcp-server-001-smoke.sh` Bash script with 9 checks (per design §19.3): health unauthenticated; adapter-compat no-auth + bad-token reject; adapter-compat valid semantic + valid boolean scaffold; /mcp initialize + tools/list + tools/call (semantic) + tools/call (boolean scaffold). Script accepts `--base-url <url> --token <bearer> [--verbose]`; NEVER prints the token argument value verbatim (prints `[REDACTED]` for diagnostics). Six fixtures under `mcp-server/fixtures/`: `classify-semantic-move.response.json` (derived from `SEMANTIC_REFEREE_FIXTURES['fixture-content-hash-mainline']`; identity fields stripped — server doesn't emit them), `classify-semantic-move.request.json` (constructed to satisfy `buildMcpToolRequestBody`), `classify-semantic-move.adapter-compat-request.json` (simplified `{tool, input}` envelope), `classify-semantic-move.malformed-response.json` (smuggled `verdict: "correct"` for schema rejection test), `classify-argument-boolean-observations.request.json` (constructed to satisfy `buildBooleanObservationToolRequestBody`; synthetic move + family_evidence + 2 rawKeys), `classify-argument-boolean-observations.scaffolded-response.json` (documented not_implemented envelope). New deployment runbook `docs/deployment/mcp-server-001-runbook.md` covers Phase 1-5 (local smoke → Deno Deploy deploy → hosted smoke → Supabase secret wiring with `/mcp/adapter-compat` URL suffix → MCP-018 integration verification) + token rotation + logs access + rollback + the 8 operator-deferred decisions. New audit template `docs/audits/MCP-SERVER-001-smoke-template.md` for operator-driven smoke checklist post-merge with failure_reason interpretation table. New CDiscourse-side presence test `__tests__/mcpServerOnePresence.test.ts` (+26 tests) asserts mcp-server/deno.json tasks, tool name pins matching shipped Edge Function adapter constants, smoke script structure + 9 checks + no-token-leak, fixture parse + structural shape + synthetic-only ([fixture] prefix) + doctrine ban-list scan. Bounded edits to root config: `tsconfig.json` exclude list gains `mcp-server` (so root tsc --noEmit does not try to compile Deno-syntax files); `eslint.config.js` ignores list gains `mcp-server/**` (same reason). NO root `package.json` dependency change. NO new visual primitive, no new design token, no new component. NO change to any MCP-018 / MCP-021A / MCP-021B / MCP-021C-EDGE file. **17,540 → 17,566 tests / 541 → 542 suites passing (+26 tests / +1 suite).** Typecheck + lint clean. Doctrine ban-list scan CLEAN across all new source + fixture files (the intentionally-malformed fixture is excluded from the production-fixture ban scan). Client-secret boundary scan CLEAN: zero match on MCP_URL / MCP_TOKEN / SEMANTIC_REFEREE_MCP / EXPO_PUBLIC_*MCP / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in src/ or app/. MCP-018/021A/021B/021C boundary diff: 0 lines changed. Root package boundary: 0 lines changed in package.json or package-lock.json. Hosting platform: **Deno Deploy** confirmed (5/5 criteria met). Server location: **co-located** at `mcp-server/`. MCP lifecycle option: **C (both endpoints)**. classify_semantic_move wire compatibility verified via fixtures + the adapter-compat-request.json that matches the simplified envelope MCP-018 + MCP-021C-EDGE adapters send today. Model id: **`claude-haiku-4-5`** (matches `DEFAULT_SEMANTIC_REFEREE_MODEL` constant in `anthropicClassifierCore.ts:36`). **Operator follow-up** (per `docs/deployment/mcp-server-001-runbook.md`): (1) Phase 1 — local smoke via `bash scripts/mcp-server-001-smoke.sh --base-url http://localhost:8080 --token <token>` (operator runs `cd mcp-server && deno task dev` first). (2) Phase 2 — create Deno Deploy project `cdiscourse-mcp-server` (or operator's chosen name); set env vars: MCP_SERVER_BEARER_TOKEN + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + MODEL_PROVIDER=anthropic + MCP_SERVER_ENV=prod; deploy `mcp-server/main.ts`. (6) Record outcome in `docs/audits/MCP-SERVER-001-SMOKE-<date>.md` per the template. **Operator-deferred decisions** (per `docs/designs/MCP-SERVER-001.md` §24.14): hosting platform confirmation, server location confirmation (co-located vs separate repo), model id pinning policy (alias vs dated snapshot), allowed-origins policy, default URL suffix for Supabase secret, Deno Deploy project name, CI/deploy workflow (deferred to follow-up), MCP-Protocol-Version pinning policy. **MCP-SERVER-002 readiness**: Tool registry already includes `classify_argument_boolean_observations`. MCP-SERVER-002 swaps the handler body for the real implementation without an architecture pivot. See `docs/designs/MCP-SERVER-001.md`. -->
<!-- Latest implementer card: MCP-SERVER-002 (Family A Boolean Observation Tool — promotes `classify_argument_boolean_observations` from scaffolded `not_implemented` envelope to real Family A classifier; folds in `actorRole='moderator'` enum addition + 3 docs corrections; no new Supabase migration, no new Edge Function, no client-side AI call, no service-role usage, no new root package.json dependency, no change to MCP-018/021A/021B/021C trees, no change to `mcp-server/tools/classifySemanticMove.ts` or `mcp-server/lib/seedPrompt.ts` — semantic-move tool stays byte-equal (Trigger 14 protected)). The MCP server tool flow: validate input → select provider (fixture or Anthropic) → validate response against MCP-021A wire shape (server-side mirror) → doctrine ban-list scan → return tool result with content[text] + structuredContent. The tool is single-prompt (16 keys per Anthropic call; ~4-5k input tokens, MAX_TOKENS=1500, temperature=0). Server-side mirror modules under `mcp-server/lib/`: `familyAKeys.ts` (FAMILY_A_RAW_KEYS=16 binding rawKeys [supports_parent, challenges_parent, refines_parent, extends_parent, distinguishes_parent, reframes_parent, questions_parent, summarizes_parent, acknowledges_parent, corrects_parent_detail, contrasts_with_parent, answers_parent_question, has_rebuttal, has_counter_rebuttal, rebutted, quote_anchors_parent] + FAMILY_A_PROMPT_ENTRIES + FAMILY_A_CLASSIFIER_SET_VERSION='family-a-v1'), `familyAPrompt.ts` (FAMILY_A_SYSTEM_PROMPT verbatim mirrors semantic-move's 7 absolute rules + Family-A-specific structural framing + conservative-positives bias + buildFamilyAUserPrompt builds the user prompt with 16 booleanQuestions + 16 verbose definition blocks + auto_metadata/lifecycle warning + response-shape instruction), `familyABooleanRequestSchema.ts` (validateFamilyABooleanRequest rejects schemaVersion mismatch with invalid_params, non-`parent_relation` requestedFamilies with unsupported_family, non-Family-A rawKeys with unsupported_rawKey, timeoutMs outside [1,60000] with invalid_params), `familyABanListScan.ts` (scanFamilyABooleanResponseForBanList scans every evidenceSpan + modelInfo.serverName + modelInfo.classifierSetVersion against DOCTRINE_BAN_PATTERNS), `anthropicCall.ts` (shared HTTP/timeout/parse skeleton; semantic-move and Family A both route through it; `callAnthropic({system, userPrompt, maxTokens, temperature, toolNameForLogging, requestId, perToolTimeoutMs, fetchImpl})` returns {ok:true, packet} or {ok:false, reason} with 6 failure surfaces: key_missing / model_timeout / api_error / rate_limited / network_error / parse_failure), `anthropic.ts` REFACTORED to use the shared helper while keeping public exports byte-equal (`runAnthropicSemanticReferee` + the 4 named constants + `extractAnthropicContentText` + `parseJsonFromContent` signatures unchanged), `familyAAnthropic.ts` (runAnthropicFamilyAClassifier wrapper around callAnthropic with per-tool timeoutMs passthrough + classify_argument_boolean_observations log tag), `familyAFixtureProvider.ts` (loads `classify-argument-boolean-observations.family-a-canonical-response.json` when MCP_SERVER_USE_FIXTURE_PROVIDER=true), `mcpBooleanObservationSchemaMirror.ts` (validateMcpBooleanObservationResponse server-side STRUCTURAL validator + MCP_BOOLEAN_OBSERVATION_SCHEMA_VERSION + MAX_EVIDENCE_SPAN_CHARS=240 + MAX_FLAGS_PER_RESPONSE=20 + 6 failure-reason enum). Real tool implementation `mcp-server/tools/classifyArgumentBooleanObservations.ts` rewritten from scaffold; description no longer references "scaffolded" or "not_implemented"; the input/output schemas already had the right shape from MCP-SERVER-001. `actorRole='moderator'` (folded-in MCP-SERVER-001-SMOKE follow-up): 3-line surgical change to `mcp-server/lib/semanticRefereePacketSchema.ts` — ALL_ACTOR_ROLES gains 'moderator' as 5th entry, ClassifyMoveRoomContext.actorRole TS type gains | 'moderator', validator error message updated. `mcp-server/tools/classifySemanticMove.ts` inputSchema enum stays at 4 entries (read-only boundary; operator-deferred reconciliation noted in MCP-SERVER-002 smoke template). New migration `20260526000019_mcp_021c_edge_run_mode.sql` adds `run_mode text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'production' CHECK IN ('production', 'admin_validation')` to `argument_machine_observation_runs` plus an index on the new column; the migration is additive (no DROP, no DELETE, no TRUNCATE, no new RLS policy); existing 9 MCP-021B smoke-seed rows backfill to 'production' via the DEFAULT. New Edge Function `supabase/functions/classify-argument-boolean-observations/index.ts` (verify_jwt = true; requireAdmin(req) gates BOTH production and admin_validation modes per Decision 7; service-role bypasses RLS only AFTER admin auth; for each argumentId: loads move + parent + ancestor-3 thread context via service client, filters families via familyRegistry.filterFamiliesForMode, builds the MCP request via booleanObservationRequestBuilder, invokes runBooleanObservationMcpAdapter, on success sanitizes at INSPECT confidence floor and writes a 'success' run + per-positive-rawKey result rows, on unavailable writes a 'failed' run with stable mcp_<reason> failure_reason; returns sanitized PerArgumentSummary array — NO model token, NO URL, NO Authorization header, NO admin email; production mode rejects requests with zero eligible families at 422). Bounded edits: `src/features/nodeLabels/machineObservationPersistenceTypes.ts` (adds `MachineObservationRunMode` type + `ALL_MACHINE_OBSERVATION_RUN_MODES` frozen array + `isMachineObservationRunMode` guard + `runMode` field on `MachineObservationRunRow`); `src/features/nodeLabels/machineObservationPersistenceQuery.ts` (SELECT now uses PostgREST `!inner` join on `argument_machine_observation_runs` and applies `.eq('argument_machine_observation_runs.run_mode', 'production')` — admin-validation rows remain persisted but never reach Source 6 rendering; mapRawRow drops the joined runs object so result row shape is byte-equal to MCP-021B). <!-- Latest implementer card: MCP-021B (Persisted Machine Observation Classifier Results — second of MCP-021A → 021B → 021C three-card sequence; one migration + RLS + 3 new pure-TS persistence files + 8 bounded additive edits + 9 test files; no Edge Function, no service-role in client, no new dependency, no AI call, no live MCP call, no new visual primitive, no new design token, no display cap change, no taxonomy key added). Adds 2 new tables to `public.`: `argument_machine_observation_runs` (per-classifier-run audit row; columns: id, debate_id, argument_id, schema_version, requested_families text[], provider_key, model_name, input_hash, status CHECK(success|failed|fallback), failure_reason, started_at, completed_at, created_at; FKs ON DELETE CASCADE from debates + arguments) and `argument_machine_observation_results` (per-positive-observation row; columns: id, run_id FK to runs ON DELETE CASCADE, debate_id, argument_id, schema_version, raw_key, family, confidence CHECK(low|medium|high), evidence_span, created_at; UNIQUE(run_id, raw_key)). Three indexes: `(argument_id, schema_version, completed_at DESC NULLS LAST)` on runs, `(argument_id, schema_version, raw_key)` on results, `(run_id)` on results. RLS enabled on both with read-only client policies: `amor_runs_select_via_argument` uses single-table `EXISTS into public.arguments` (META-1A delegation pattern) — inherits the QOL-039 canonical arm set (author / moderator-admin / posted-public via `is_debate_open_or_locked_public` / participant-private via `is_debate_participant`). The brief proposed an explicit JOIN debates + JOIN debate_participants shape; the design diverges to the canonical META-1A pattern because the brief's shape would re-introduce the cross-table recursion that QOL-039's SECURITY DEFINER helpers were created to avoid (divergence explicitly authorized by intent brief line 204). `amor_results_select_via_run` uses single-table `EXISTS into runs` (inherit-via-run chain: results → runs → arguments → SECURITY DEFINER helpers; each hop targets a distinct table; recursion-free). NO `CREATE POLICY ... FOR INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE` on either table — MCP-021C will write via a service-role Edge Function. OPS-001 four-class header walk explicit in the migration top-of-file comment block. NO `COMMENT ON storage.*` statements (PR-003 SQLSTATE 42501 boundary preserved). Source 6 contract changes ADDITIVELY: `adaptRawClassifierBinarySource(input)` transitions from `return [];` unconditionally to `return [] OR mapPersistedObservationRowsToNodeLabelMarks(input.persistedClassifierRows, ...)` when `input.persistedClassifierRows` is supplied. Pre-MCP-021B callers (callers that don't pass the new optional input) continue to receive `[]` byte-equal — the MCP-021A `mcpOneTwoOneASourceSixInvariance.test.ts` (8 cases) continues to pass unchanged. New file `src/features/nodeLabels/machineObservationPersistenceTypes.ts` (pure-TS row interfaces `MachineObservationRunRow` + `MachineObservationResultRow` + 5 type guards `isMachineObservationConfidence` / `isMachineObservationRunStatus` / `isMachineObservationFamily` / `isWellFormedResultRow` / `isWellFormedRunRow`). New file `src/features/nodeLabels/machineObservationPersistenceAdapter.ts` (pure-TS `mapPersistedObservationRowsToNodeLabelMarks(rows, options)` applies four filters in order: schema_version === MCP_BOOLEAN_OBSERVATION_SCHEMA_VERSION → rawKey ∈ MACHINE_OBSERVATION_DEFINITIONS_BY_RAW_KEY → confidence ≥ per-surface floor via MachineObservationDefinition.confidenceEligibility → evidence_span truncated to 240 chars; every dropped row is silently discarded with no log, no echo; plain-language fields sourced from MCP-021A definition registry, never echoed from the raw row; emitted marks always carry `kind: 'machine_observation'` and `id: 'machine_observation:persisted:${row.id}:${argumentId}'`). New file `src/features/nodeLabels/machineObservationPersistenceQuery.ts` (read-only Supabase SELECT helper `fetchPersistedObservationsForArguments(argumentIds)` batched up to 1000 ids via `.in()`; mirrors META-1A's `fetchPointTagsForArguments` precedent; shared authed client, no service-role; SUPABASE_CONFIGURED false short-circuits offline-safe). Bounded edits: `nodeLabelSourceAdapters.ts` (RawClassifierBinaryAdapterInput + adaptAllSourcesForNode gain optional persistedClassifierRows + surface fields; backwards-compat preserved), `nodeLabels/index.ts` (exports 3 new persistence helpers + types; header comment updated), `NodeLabelStrip.tsx` + `NodeLabelInspectGroups.tsx` (each accepts optional persistedClassifierRows prop, forwards into adaptAllSourcesForNode with surface 'timeline_node' / 'inspect' respectively; computeNodeLabelStripDescriptors + computeNodeLabelInspectGroups helpers gain matching optional parameter), `argumentsApi.ts` (fetchArgumentRelations Promise.all gains 5th SELECT against argument_machine_observation_results; ArgumentRelations type gains persistedObservations field), `useArgumentRoomMessages.ts` (accumulates persistedObservationsByArgumentId map and exposes on hook return shape), `ArgumentTreeScreen.tsx` (destructures and passes through), `ArgumentGameSurface.tsx` (accepts optional persistedObservationsByArgumentId prop and threads activeMessage's slice into both NodeLabelStrip + NodeLabelInspectGroups mounts). Defensive type fixes: composerHandoff.ts + useArgumentViewport.ts + __tests__/argumentCache.test.ts add persistedObservations: [] to their literal ArgumentRelations constructions. UX-001.5A display caps (Timeline 1+1+overflow, Selected Context 3+3+overflow, Inspect unbounded grouped) absorb the new input transparently — verified by 21-test stress fixture with 100+ persisted rows threaded through the full pipeline (adaptAllSourcesForNode → combinePerNodeMarks → filterMarksBySurface → dedupePerNodeMarks → enforceTimelineNodeDisplayCap / enforceSelectedContextDisplayCap / enforceInspectGroupedView). Composer-only entries from Family J (shifts_to_person_or_intent, contains_unplayable_insult_only, needs_pre_send_pause) never surface on Timeline/Selected/Inspect — the composer_only disposition gate filters them at every surface even when the adapter emits them. 9 new test files / 220 new tests: `mcpOneTwoOneBPersistenceTypes.test.ts` (34 — row guards + enum validators), `mcpOneTwoOneBPersistedRowAdapter.test.ts` (32 — adapter happy path + silent drops + confidence floor + evidence_span truncation + input boundaries), `mcpOneTwoOneBPersistenceMigration.test.ts` (22 — migration text scan + OPS-001 four-class header walk + statement-order checks), `mcpOneTwoOneBRlsPolicy.test.ts` (15 — RLS policy text scan + no client-write policy + service-role-free client source scan), `mcpOneTwoOneBSourceSixAdapter.test.ts` (24 — backwards-compat invariance + persisted-row happy path + surface gating + aggregator pass-through), `mcpOneTwoOneBIntegrationFlow.test.ts` (6 — NodeLabelStrip / NodeLabelInspectGroups consume persistedClassifierRows correctly), `mcpOneTwoOneBDisplayCapPreservation.test.ts` (21 — 100+ row stress fixtures), `mcpOneTwoOneBDoctrine.test.ts` (24 — verdict-token ban-list scan on source files + migration; behavioral doctrine; no engagement/popularity/heat columns or sort; no console.log; no external AI imports; no point_tags reads; plain-language scan), `mcpOneTwoOneBReadOnlyBoundary.test.ts` (42 — git diff main..HEAD zero-byte assertions on UX-001.5A model files + all 11 MCP-021A registry files + userAllegationRegistry + all 5 UX-001.6 cross-device QA test files + all 9 MCP-021A test files + package.json/lock + useSemanticReferee + designTokens + RefereeBannerView; bounded-edit assertion on NodeLabelStrip + NodeLabelInspectGroups). **16,909 → 17,129 tests / 512 → 521 suites passing (+220 tests / +9 suites).** Typecheck + lint clean. **Read-only API boundary verified clean** via git diff main..HEAD: zero byte change on nodeLabelPresentationModel.ts, nodeLabelPriorityModel.ts, nodeLabelDescriptorAdapter.ts, machineObservationDefinitions.ts, all 10 machineObservationDefinitions/family{A..J}.ts, mcpBooleanObservationSchema.ts, userAllegationRegistry.ts, machineObservationRegistry.ts, threadTopologyAutoMetadata.ts, nodeLabelTypes.ts, all 5 __tests__/uxOneOneSix*.test.{ts,tsx} files, all 9 __tests__/mcpOneTwoOneA*.test.ts files, package.json, package-lock.json, useSemanticReferee.ts, designTokens.ts, RefereeBannerView.tsx. UX-001.2 offset acceptance 11/11 passing unchanged. UX-001.6 cross-device QA matrix all 5 suites passing (2937/2937 unchanged). UX-001.5A regression all 5 suites passing (174/174 unchanged). MCP-021A regression all 9 suites passing (114/114 unchanged) — the load-bearing mcpOneTwoOneASourceSixInvariance.test.ts continues to pass (Source 6 byte-equal for pre-MCP-021B inputs). **All 21 conditional HALT triggers CLEAN**: 15 binding from intent brief (no live MCP call, no AI provider call path, no new taxonomy key, no new visual primitive or token, no display cap change, no client-JWT INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE policy, no COMMENT ON storage.*, test count delta +220 ≤ +500 ceiling, migration ordering safe, no MCP-021A taxonomy / schema / UX-001.5A presentation modification outside bounded edit list, Source 6 backwards-compat preserved, canonical visibility predicate identified, context window clean, zero blocking interpretive judgments, Phase A reconciliation anticipated divergence authorized by brief line 204) + 6 implementer-specific (migration apply deferred to operator since Docker not available locally, Source 6 adapter signature additive backwards-compatible, UX-001.6 matrix passes, MCP-021A regression passes, display cap preservation passes at 100+ rows, working tree contains only the 5 pre-existing untracked files). **Operator follow-up**: (1) `npx supabase db push --linked` after merge — Supabase GitHub integration auto-applies; verify with `npx supabase db status` + `npx supabase db lint`. (2) Smoke seed per `docs/decisions/MCP-021-sequencing.md` §"Required follow-up after MCP-021B" — seed 5-10 fake persisted Machine Observation result rows via service-role SQL against the bot-seeded rooms, verify Source 6 adapter consumes them, verify display caps hold, verify second-account visibility follows RLS. **Operator-deferred review items**: (a) RLS recursion fallback shape (design §11.2: duplicate-predicate available if MCP-021C smoke surfaces plan-cost issues on results read path); (b) Realtime channel for persisted observations deferred to a future card mirroring pointTagsRealtime.ts; (c) Family-sharded batched MCP execution is MCP-021C territory — schema accommodates per-family requested_families array on runs row. **MCP-021C readiness**: persistence schema set up exactly for MCP-021C's write path; service-role Edge Function will build MCP request via buildMcpBooleanObservationRequest (MCP-021A), call MCP transport, parse + sanitize via parseMcpBooleanObservationResponse + sanitizeMcpBooleanObservationResponse (MCP-021A), and write one runs row + N positive results rows in a transaction. Schema version constant `mcp-021.machine-observations.boolean.v1` preserved verbatim across MCP-021A/B/C. See `docs/designs/MCP-021B.md`. -->
<!-- Latest implementer card: MCP-021A (Maximal Boolean Machine Observation Taxonomy — first of MCP-021A → 021B → 021C three-card sequence; pure-TS taxonomy + schema + tests; no runtime behavior change; no migration; no Edge Function; no new dependency; no AI call; no service-role; no display-cap change). Expands the Machine Observation registry from 65 to 172 entries (+107 NEW) across 10 families: A parent_relation (16), B disagreement_axis (14; umbrella + Inspect-only subtypes per Decision 4), C misunderstanding_repair (17), D evidence_source_chain (27), E argument_scheme (16; all Walton schemes), F critical_question (14; all paired with Family E), G resolution_progress (30; largest family — note Family G has 21 retroactive entries not 20 per design §3.7 self-correction, hence 172 total instead of forecast 171), H claim_clarity (12), I thread_topology (21; Decision 7 source split: 4 auto_metadata + 3 ai_classifier), J sensitive_composer (5 UNCHANGED per Trigger 10). Parallel-registry implementation choice (design §4 option b): NodeLabelMark stays byte-equal; NEW `MachineObservationDefinition` interface carrying 8 verbose fields lives in `src/features/nodeLabels/machineObservationDefinitions.ts` + 10 per-family files under `machineObservationDefinitions/familyA..familyJ.ts`. Every entry — 172/172 — carries: `family`, `booleanQuestion`, `positiveDefinition`, `negativeDefinition`, `positiveExamples` (≥1), `negativeExamples` (≥1), `falsePositiveGuards` (≥1), `doctrineNotes` (≥1), `confidenceEligibility` (3 per-surface thresholds). New file `src/features/nodeLabels/mcpBooleanObservationSchema.ts` — pure-TS MCP wire contract: `McpBooleanObservationRequest`, `McpBooleanObservationResponse` (refinement 1: confidence REQUIRED), schema version constant `'mcp-021.machine-observations.boolean.v1'`, 4 validators (`parseMcpBooleanObservationResponse` handling 7 failure modes incl. flag_count_too_high at 20; `sanitizeMcpBooleanObservationResponse` drops unknown rawKeys silently + applies per-surface confidence floor + truncates evidenceSpan to 240 chars; `buildMcpBooleanObservationRequest`; `mcpResponseToNodeLabelMarks` bridge). New file `src/features/nodeLabels/threadTopologyAutoMetadata.ts` — Decision 7 NEW no-op stub derivers (`deriveSplitsThread`, `deriveMergesThread`, `deriveReferencesSiblingNode`, `deriveReferencesAncestorNode`, `deriveAllThreadTopologyAutoMetadata`) — all return [] unconditionally; real implementation is MCP-021C territory. Decision 5 collapses honored: brief `disagreement_evidence_applicability` → existing #49 `disputes_evidence_applicability`; brief `requests_clarification_present` → existing #38; brief `narrow_concession_present` → existing #45; brief `source_chain_gap` → existing #43; brief `source_requested`/`quote_requested` → existing #5/#6 + #22/#23; brief `branch_recommended` → existing #34; brief `changes_subject` → existing #36 `introduces_new_issue`. NO aliases added. Trigger 10 DROPs honored: 6 candidates dropped (`repeats_prior_point` doctrine-risk; 5 Family J extras `hostile_generalization_present`/`identity_group_reference_present`/`sarcasm_or_mockery_present`/`excessive_heat_present`/`moderation_boundary_near` — all flagged for sequel-audit consideration outside MCP-021A scope). 8 new test files (test categories per design §8): `mcpOneTwoOneAFamilyContracts.test.ts` (14 — family type contracts), `mcpBooleanObservationSchema.test.ts` (36 — MCP schema parse/sanitize/build/bridge), `mcpOneTwoOneAThreadTopologyStubs.test.ts` (12 — no-op stub verification), `mcpOneTwoOneARegistrySize.test.ts` (33 — registry count + family assignment + Decision 7 source split + Family J cap), `mcpOneTwoOneADefinitionCompleteness.test.ts` (10 — Trigger 12 all 8 verbose fields), `mcpOneTwoOneANoDuplicateAliases.test.ts` (10 — Decision 5 collapses + Trigger 10 DROPs), `mcpOneTwoOneASurfacePolicy.test.ts` (11 — Decision 4 Family B umbrella + subtype split; Family E/F all inspect; Family J never Timeline), `mcpOneTwoOneADisplayCapPreservation.test.ts` (4 — synthetic 150-positive stress test; UX-001.5A caps unchanged), `mcpOneTwoOneALabelDoctrine.test.ts` (10 — verdict-token ban + Family E never-fallacy + Family F never-unwarranted), `mcpOneTwoOneASourceSixInvariance.test.ts` (10 — Trigger 1 BINDING: adaptRawClassifierBinarySource returns [] for all inputs including simulated MCP-021C payloads). Aggregate-test approach per design §8.9 implementer choice (per-entry expansion would have ballooned test count beyond +1,000 ceiling). **16,759 → 16,909 tests / 502 → 512 suites passing (+150 tests / +10 suites).** Typecheck + lint clean. **Read-only API boundary verified clean**: zero diff on `src/features/nodeLabels/nodeLabelSourceAdapters.ts` (Source 6 byte-equal), `src/features/nodeLabels/userAllegationRegistry.ts`, `src/features/nodeLabels/nodeLabelPresentationModel.ts`, `src/features/nodeLabels/nodeLabelPriorityModel.ts`, `src/features/nodeLabels/NodeLabelStrip.tsx`, `src/features/nodeLabels/NodeLabelInspectGroups.tsx`, all `__tests__/uxOneOneSix*.test.{ts,tsx}` (5 files preserved), all UX-001.{1-7} read-only files outside bounded list (visual primitives, design tokens, RefereeBannerView, composer, oneBox, Timeline, score tracker, useSemanticReferee), all `supabase/migrations/` + `supabase/functions/`, `package.json`/`package-lock.json`. <!-- Latest implementer card: UX-001.5A (Node Labels: Machine Observations and User Allegations — adjacent follow-through to closed UX-001 epic; pure UI / pure-TS, no migration, no Edge Function, no new dependency, no AI call, no service-role). Ships a new `src/features/nodeLabels/` module (10 files) consuming the read-only UX-001.5 visual primitives — 65 Machine Observation entries (16 auto-metadata + 19 lifecycle + 25 AI classifier + 5 sensitive composer-only; lifecycle union is 19 values per `ALL_POINT_LIFECYCLE_STATES` — design §4.5's 18/64 forecast was an off-by-one arithmetic correction, documented as §19 Implementer note) and 10 User Allegation entries (1 per `ManualTagCode`). Registry keyed by COMPOUND `${source}:${rawKey}` so overlap-shared rawKeys (`source_requested`, `quote_requested` shared between `auto_metadata` and `lifecycle`) remain individually addressable; a byRawKey companion map exposes the highest-priority entry per rawKey for dedupe / descriptor paths.
