## OPS-MCP-SMOKE-DOCTRINE-HARDENING — audit-lint enforcement for direct-proof obligations (2026-05-28)

**Status:** Implementer phase complete; operator post-merge smoke pending per `docs/designs/OPS-MCP-SMOKE-DOCTRINE-HARDENING.md` §12 (5-phase smoke; PARTIAL expected per CI deferral). Linter operational on 4-fixture self-validation (centerpiece). CI wiring DEFERRED to follow-on `OPS-MCP-SMOKE-LINT-CI-WIRING` per HALT trigger 9 (`.github/workflows/` absent in repo; introducing it would be a non-additive shared-config change). The card converts the operator-stated audit-integrity rules R1-R4 from prose into a mechanical read-only linter that catches the class of defect that produced the `29f30b0` Family E improper-PASS audit (Phase 3 hosted MCP NOT-RUN; verdict PASS; justification "covered indirectly via Phase 4 success"). Six rules L1-L6 implemented in `scripts/ops/audit-lint-lib.cjs`: L1 required-phase NOT-RUN + verdict PASS fails; L2 indirect-proof phrase in NOT-RUN direct-proof phase fails (negative-lookahead distinguishes sole vs supplemental unit-test claims); L3 production-enable audits distinguish three success levels (dispatch + targeted classifier-signal + read-path); L4 production-enable targeted-signal requires positive result-row evidence; L5 doctrine-risk audits (argument_scheme / slippery_slope alias) inspect persisted `evidence_span`; L6 amendment / hosted-completion / verdict-upgrade docs carry full provenance (priorVerdict + missingProof + newlySuppliedProof). Rules data in `scripts/ops/audit-lint-rules.cjs` is pure CommonJS DATA (no fs / spawn / network). 4 fixtures under `__tests__/fixtures/audit-lint/` extracted via `git show` at motivating SHAs (29f30b0 / b1829f5 / bccb0c2 / current main); each carries an `<!-- AUDIT-LINT-FIXTURE: ... -->` HTML marker; directory `README.md` declares the intentional-negative-fixture contract for doctrine ban-list scanners to exclude. 4-fixture self-validation: original-family-e-IMPROPER-PASS.md FAILS (L1 + L2 + L5 trip); family-e-amendment-PARTIAL.md PASSES; family-e-hosted-completion-PASS.md PASSES; family-d-strengthened-amendment-PASS.md PASSES (model audit). Smoke-template additive updates (Family C / D / E): `Audit-Lint: v1` marker near top + required-final-step section pointing operator at `node scripts/ops/audit-lint.mjs <doc>`; existing required-phase substance unchanged. New `docs/ops/AUDIT-LINT.md` operator reference covers usage / exit codes / six rules / audit-type detection / phase-id normalization / marker mechanism / CI deferral / how to add a doctrine-risk family or indirect-proof phrase / fixture directory contract / what the linter is NOT. **18,121 tests / 570 suites passing** (+105 opsAuditLint tests added; baseline 18,016 → 18,121 net delta). Typecheck + lint clean. Deno 792 unchanged (no `mcp-server/*` touched). No runtime code change. No registry / prompt / taxonomy change. No existing audit doc under `docs/audits/` modified or deleted (the three templates are SEPARATE template files; their substance is preserved). `package.json` `audit-lint` npm script reverted in C3 (MCP-021B `RO-36 — package.json unchanged` test conflict; runner remains invokable via full `node scripts/ops/audit-lint.mjs <path>`; smoke templates use the full form). Authorizations granted on PASS (or PARTIAL with CI deferred): `MCP-SERVER-007-FAMILY-F` AUTHORIZED to begin under linter from Phase 1; `MCP-021C-EDGE-FAMILY-E-ENABLE` AUTHORIZED with production-enable audit subject to L3 + L4 from authoring; `OPS-MCP-SMOKE-LINT-CI-WIRING` AUTHORIZED to file as follow-on. See `docs/designs/OPS-MCP-SMOKE-DOCTRINE-HARDENING.md` and `docs/ops/AUDIT-LINT.md`.

## OPS-MCP-SMOKE-LINT-CI-WIRING — audit-lint GitHub Actions workflow (lifts PARTIAL cap from OPS-MCP-SMOKE-DOCTRINE-HARDENING) (2026-05-28)

**Status:** Implementer phase complete. CI workflow operational at `.github/workflows/audit-lint.yml` (first `.github/` entry in this repo). Five-commit cadence (C1 pure classifier + truth-table tests → C2 CLI surface + marker single-source → C3 workflow YAML → C4 workflow-shape tests + `docs/ops/AUDIT-LINT.md` update → C5 final gates + handoff). The card lifts the PARTIAL cap from OPS-MCP-SMOKE-DOCTRINE-HARDENING by wiring the L1-L6 linter into GitHub Actions on every PR that touches a smoke audit doc OR the linter source / fixtures. The classifier is **shared between CI and Jest tests** — no YAML-side scoping drift possible. Truth table (per intent §4): status `A` (added smoke audit) → ALWAYS in scope (closes "submit new audit without marker" evasion); status `M` (modified) → in scope iff `Audit-Lint: v1` marker present at HEAD; `D`/`R`/`C` → out of scope; non-audit paths → out of scope; templates → refused. Implementation: pure `classifyChangedFiles(entries, readMarkerAtHead)` in `scripts/ops/audit-lint-lib.cjs` (preserves the lib's no-fs / no-spawn / no-network discipline by accepting an injected reader closure); CLI surface `--classify-changed --base <sha> --head <sha>` (plus `--changed-list-stdin` operator-debug seam) in `scripts/ops/audit-lint.mjs`; the marker reader closure lives in the `.mjs` and sources `rules.MARKER_STRING` from `audit-lint-rules.cjs` (no literal duplication anywhere — verified by 3 single-source tests). Workflow shape: `on.pull_request.paths` filters to 5 patterns verbatim (smoke audit glob + 3 linter source files + fixture dir); `actions/checkout@v4` with `fetch-depth: 0`; `actions/setup-node@v4` with Node 20.x; resolves `BASE_SHA=${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}` and `HEAD_SHA=${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}` — **NEVER `HEAD~1`, NEVER `origin/main`** (asserted by workflow-shape tests); shells out to the classifier once, then lints each in-scope path with aggregate exit-code; `permissions: contents: read` (minimal); concurrency group keyed by PR number with `cancel-in-progress: true`. 69-line workflow file; single-purpose `name: audit-lint`. **`docs/ops/AUDIT-LINT.md` replaces the "CI deferred" section with "CI enforcement (GitHub Actions)"** plus a historical note tying back to the PARTIAL cap. **18,153 tests / 570 suites passing** (+32 opsAuditLint tests added; baseline 18,121 → 18,153 net delta — 11 truth-table + 3 single-source + 6 CLI parsing + 10 workflow-shape + 2 lib-export/purity-discipline extension). Typecheck + lint clean. Deno 792/0 unchanged (no `mcp-server/*` touched). 4 historical fixtures still self-validate with unchanged verdicts: `original-family-e-IMPROPER-PASS.md` → exit 1 / L1+L2+L5 trip; `family-e-amendment-PARTIAL.md` → exit 0 / 0 findings; `family-e-hosted-completion-PASS.md` → exit 0 / 0 findings; `family-d-strengthened-amendment-PASS.md` → exit 0 / 0 findings. `package.json` UNCHANGED vs main (RO-36 ratchet preserved; verified by `git diff main -- package.json` empty + RO-36 test green). No linter rule change (L1-L6 unchanged; `audit-lint-rules.cjs` UNCHANGED). No runtime code change. No global historical corpus lint. No service-role, no AI call, no secrets — `permissions: contents: read` is the only token access. Doctrine-vacuous: this card is OPS process tooling; no scoring surface, no user-facing copy, no AI moderation surface. See `docs/designs/OPS-MCP-SMOKE-LINT-CI-WIRING.md` (1145-line designer spec) and `docs/ops/AUDIT-LINT.md` § "CI enforcement (GitHub Actions)".





## MCP-SERVER-007-FAMILY-F — Critical-Question Boolean Observation Classifier (Card 1 of three-card chain) (2026-05-28)

**Status:** Implementer phase complete; operator post-merge smoke pending per `docs/audits/MCP-SERVER-007-FAMILY-F-SMOKE-template.md`. Six commits land Family F admin_validation-only on the hosted MCP server. Server-side mirror under `mcp-server/lib/`: `familyFKeys.ts` (FAMILY_F_RAW_KEYS=14 Walton (1995, 2008) + Toulmin (1958) + Peirce critical questions [missing_warrant, unstated_assumption, authority_basis_missing, causal_mechanism_missing, analogy_mapping_missing, example_representativeness_unclear, consequence_probability_unclear, definition_boundary_unclear, criterion_weighting_unclear, alternative_explanation_available, counterexample_available, scope_limit_unstated, qualification_missing, comparison_baseline_missing] + 14 verbose FAMILY_F_PROMPT_ENTRIES with per-key doctrine guards + FAMILY_F_CLASSIFIER_SET_VERSION='family-f-v1'). `familyFPrompt.ts` mirrors Family A/B/C/D/E's 7 absolute rules verbatim and adds CQ-as-productive-probe doctrine framing; the system prompt pins consequence_probability_unclear as the highest-doctrine-risk CQ partnering with Family E's slippery_slope_reasoning_present and explicitly forbids the output from labeling the slippery-slope inference a fallacy / fallacious / weak / invalid / bad reasoning / flawed / wrong / proves wrong / refutes / invalidates / unmet-means-fallacy / any verdict on argument quality. `familyFAnthropic.ts` mirrors familyEAnthropic.ts byte-for-byte for shape; MAX_TOKENS=1500 unchanged from Family E (intent §4 D8 NOT FIRED — 14 keys × ~85 tokens ≈ 1190 with ~310 token headroom; lighter load than Family E's 16 keys with ~60 token headroom). `familyFBanListScan.ts` stacks the shared `DOCTRINE_BAN_PATTERNS` with the 12 intent §4 D5 BINDING Family-F-LOCAL tokens (`unmet-means-fallacy`, `proves wrong`, `invalidates`, `refutes`, `fallacy`, `fallacious`, `flawed`, `wrong`, `weak argument`, `invalid argument`, `bad reasoning`, `proof of`) — F-LOCAL because `invalidates` and `refutes` may legitimately appear in Family B `disputes_validity` evidenceSpans; promoting them to shared would break Family A/B/C/D outputs. Family-A/B/C/D/E lib byte-equal preserved (0 lines diff vs main); shared `doctrineBanList.ts` byte-equal preserved. `familyFFixtureProvider.ts` loads the canonical Family F response fixture for smoke checks 18+19. Registry registration: `familyRegistryInit.ts` adds `register('critical_question', { rawKeys, classifierSetVersion: 'family-f-v1' })` after Family E; sixth registration in canonical order. Dispatcher routing: `classifyArgumentBooleanObservations.ts` adds `critical_question` branch to `pickFamilyProviders()` and extends the `FamilyProviders.anthropic` union to include `ValidatedFamilyFRequest`; tool description text advertises Family F's 14 critical questions with explicit doctrine framing ("CQs are descriptive structural probes on absence/gap, never adjudications of argument quality; an unmet CQ NEVER means the partner scheme is a fallacy"). Stage 2B NOT REQUIRED per design §1: uniform `ai_classifier` source for all 14 keys (T1 disposed; no Subset filter), no cross-family rawKey collision (T2 disposed), doctrine-translation peril structurally addressable via the Family E 5-layer defense precedent (T3 disposed), MAX_TOKENS=1500 sufficient (T4 disposed), structurally independent of Family E at the classifier level (T5 disposed). **5-LAYER DOCTRINE DEFENSE:** (1) system prompt 7 absolute rules byte-equal to A/B/C/D/E + CQ-as-productive-probe doctrine block forbidding verdict framing; (2) per-key falsePositiveGuards on 6 doctrine-risk keys (consequence_probability_unclear HIGH + analogy_mapping_missing / alternative_explanation_available / causal_mechanism_missing / authority_basis_missing / missing_warrant MEDIUM) with verbatim "MUST NOT contain words like 'fallacy'" enumerations; (3) `FAMILY_F_BAN_PATTERNS` 12-token runtime scanner; (4) 3 mandatory + 2 optional adversarial fixtures targeting the E↔F doctrine boundary (Fixture A: CQ-unmet on slippery-slope text [reuses Family E's slippery-slope-clear input verbatim]; Fixture B: CQ-met baseline with explicit probability anchors; Fixture C: input contains "fallacy" TWICE — existential adversarial proof; Fixture D: multi-CQ mixed states; Fixture E: verdict-baiting bait); (5) Phase 4b smoke verification on persisted `evidence_span` rows. **First family-ship PR under live audit-lint CI** per intent §4 D12 BINDING; smoke template at `docs/audits/MCP-SERVER-007-FAMILY-F-SMOKE-template.md` carries `Audit-Lint: v1` marker on line 3, includes the verbatim D12 enforcement-loop provenance subsection ("First-enforcement provenance: this is the first family-ship PR to be linted by audit-lint CI with a non-empty in-scope set..."), and includes the required final `node scripts/ops/audit-lint.mjs <doc>` step. **Card 1 of three-card chain:** Card 2 = `MCP-021C-EDGE-FAMILY-E-ENABLE` (Family E production flip; AUTHORIZED on Phase 4b PASS); Card 3 = `MCP-021C-EDGE-FAMILY-F-ENABLE` (Family F production flip; Edge familyRegistry.ts:95-97 productionEnabled=false this card; Card 3 flips). Smoke script `scripts/mcp-server-001-smoke.sh` extended to 19 checks: Check 18 (compat-boolean-family-f) + Check 19 (mcp-tools-call-boolean-family-f) — both POST Family F requests against the slippery-slope text using consequence_probability_unclear + missing_warrant + alternative_explanation_available rawKeys and assert family-f-v1 in modelInfo.classifierSetVersion. **Test delta: Jest 18,153 unchanged / Deno 871 / 0 (was 792 pre-Family-F; +79 net new Family F tests).** Breakdown: familyFKeys.test.ts (14) + familyFPrompt.test.ts (22) + familyFAnthropic.test.ts (11) + familyFAdversarialDoctrine.test.ts (26) + +3 familyRegistryInit tests + +0 net from stale-unsupported retargeting in 6 dispatch test files (renamed to Family G/H/I as canonical first-unsupported). Test delta band +95-130 forecast not met because the +95 lower bound was inclusive of files NOT shipped in this card (familyFKeysParity / familyFBanListScan dedicated tests / familyFFixtureParity / familyFResponseValidator / familyFDispatch / familyFDoctrineFixtures / Edge familyRegistry F Jest test) — the doctrine-binding test file (familyFAdversarialDoctrine 26 tests covering all 12 D5 ban-list tokens + 3 E↔F cross-checks + reviewer matrix sub-check) carries the existential constraints. Typecheck + lint clean. Cross-family byte-equal verification: A-E lib files 0 lines diff; `src/` 0 lines diff; `supabase/` 0 lines diff. Doctrine-boundary verification on `familyFPrompt.ts`: every banned-token occurrence appears within negation context (MUST NOT / never / does NOT / NEVER / "is fatal, the inference is fallacious" only inside "The CQ does NOT assert..." negation). 4 historical audit-lint fixtures still self-validate with unchanged verdicts. No Anthropic / xAI / X API / Supabase write / service-role usage in this card. **Operator follow-up:** None — Deno Deploy auto-deploys on merge to main; Edge familyRegistry F entry already has productionEnabled=false (Card 1 boundary). Operator runs 19-check smoke + Phase 4b live adversarial CQ verification + Phase 7 enforcement-loop provenance subsection extraction (CI run ID + in_scope count + linter exit from PR Actions tab) per `docs/audits/MCP-SERVER-007-FAMILY-F-SMOKE-template.md`. **On smoke PASS:** Gate A unblocks Card 2 (`MCP-021C-EDGE-FAMILY-E-ENABLE` design; Family E production flip — FIRST production-enable card under L3+L4+L5 audit-lint enforcement). See `docs/designs/MCP-SERVER-007-FAMILY-F.md` (1010-line designer spec) and `docs/designs/MCP-SERVER-007-FAMILY-F-intent.md` (operator-authored intent brief with 12 binding decisions + 26 HALT triggers).

## OPS-MCP-LATENCY-BUDGET — Auto-trigger latency budget (measure + codify) (2026-05-28)

**Status:** Implementer phase complete (resolved on the operator's Option-B decision); operator post-merge smoke pending per `docs/designs/OPS-MCP-LATENCY-BUDGET.md`. **MEASURES + CODIFIES only** — no dispatch change, no parallelization, no family flip (design D9). The card answers the operative question for Family G: *can G enter production under the current sequential dispatcher, or must a parallelization card precede it?* It adds a read-only operator report (`scripts/ops/mcp-latency-report.mjs` + pure lib `scripts/ops/mcp-latency-report-lib.cjs`) that runs two read-only SQL files, computes per-family + wall-clock-background percentiles, classifies the budget, and projects the family count at which sequential dispatch crosses the warning / FAIL lines.

**Binding clock + thresholds.** The budget binds to **ONE** clock: `wall_clock_background` p95 = `max(completed_at) − min(started_at)` per argument over its production-success runs (design D2 — strictly ≥ the per-family sum because it includes inter-family dispatch gaps). Classification (`classifyLatencyBudget(wallClockBackgroundP95Seconds, submitBlocked)`): **`submitBlocked` checked FIRST → FAIL** (D3 — a blocked submit is an immediate FAIL regardless of background timing), then `>= 45s` → FAIL, `>= 30s` → PARTIAL, else PASS; non-finite/negative throws RangeError. `WARN_SECONDS = 30`, `FAIL_SECONDS = 45`. Latency is a **system-performance** metric, never a gameplay/truth/heat signal (cdiscourse-doctrine §1, §2); it **never** blocks posting (`submit-argument` is fire-and-forget via `EdgeRuntime.waitUntil`); a FAIL projection is a signal to file `OPS-MCP-AUTO-TRIGGER-PARALLELIZATION`, never an instruction to drop a family or block a submit. The operator-facing markdown is doctrine-ban-list scanned (no verdict/quality token) and carries no argument body / `evidence_span` field.

**Directory ownership (operator decision — Option B: relocate).** The original implementation placed the SQL at `scripts/ops/sql/16,17`, which broke 3 assertions in the two observability SQL-safety suites (they assert an exact 16-file count over the observability-owned dir). Per Option B, the SQL was relocated **out of `scripts/ops/sql/`** via `git mv` and locally renumbered to `scripts/ops-latency-sql/01,02`; SQL bodies are byte-identical to the design's Q16/Q17 (only header `--file` paths changed). `scripts/ops/sql/` is back to exactly 16 observability files (`git diff main..HEAD -- scripts/ops/sql/` is 0 lines) and the two observability test files are **byte-equal / 0-diff** and green again. **One documented deviation from the literal Option-B spec:** the dedicated dir is a **sibling** `scripts/ops-latency-sql/` rather than the literally-named nested `scripts/ops/latency-sql/`, because `__tests__/opsMcpObservabilityNoServiceRoleNoSecrets.test.ts` collects `.sql` files **recursively** under `scripts/ops/` and asserts exactly 16 — a nested dir would still count 18 (verified empirically) and leave that suite red. A sibling outside `scripts/ops/` is the only layout that satisfies every operator verification gate (obs green + obs tests untouched + latency SQL out of `scripts/ops/sql/`). See `docs/ops/LATENCY-BUDGET.md` § "Directory ownership" and the design's § "Implementer note: RESOLVED".

**Tests.** Full suite **18,269 / 574 suites green** (was red on 3 observability assertions in the blocked state; now all green). The card adds `__tests__/opsMcpLatencyBudget.test.ts` (47 — pure-helper boundary/projection/percentile/aggregate/ban-list/purity coverage, committed in the prior implementer commit) and this session's new `__tests__/opsMcpLatencySqlSafety.test.ts` (+20 — latency-dir-scoped safety scans: no DDL, no `select *`, no bare body/`evidence_span`, no secrets/Authorization/Bearer/JWT, terminating `;`, `OPS-MCP-LATENCY-BUDGET` header, sibling-dir assertion). The only existing-test edit is one assertion in the card's **own** `opsMcpLatencyBudget.test.ts` (`LATENCY_SECTIONS` sqlFile names `16`/`17` → `01`/`02`). Typecheck + lint clean. Read-only `node scripts/ops/mcp-latency-report.mjs --no-write` runs end-to-end and exits 0.

**Operator follow-up:** None required for merge — pure code + read-only SQL + docs (no migration, no Edge Function, no env var, no `package.json` change; the Supabase GitHub auto-deploy is a no-op for this card). Post-merge: run the read-only report against the linked DB to capture the binding fresh-N=5 `wall_clock_background` p95 + the G projection, and record the smoke per the design. **Patterns a future parallelization card consumes:** the projection's `failCrossingFamilyCount` is the trigger condition for `OPS-MCP-AUTO-TRIGGER-PARALLELIZATION` (filed only if a near-term family crosses the 45s FAIL line); the binding clock + thresholds + classifier are reusable as-is. See `docs/designs/OPS-MCP-LATENCY-BUDGET.md` and `docs/ops/LATENCY-BUDGET.md`.
